ولبثوا في كهفهم ثلاث مائة سنين وازدادوا: 25. And they stayed in their Cave three hundred (solar) years, adding nine (for lunar years). (Tafsir Al-Qurtubi) (18:25)
قل الله اعلم بما لبثوا له غيب السماوات و: 26. Say: "Allâh knows best how long they stayed. With Him is (the knowledge of) the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. How clearly He sees, and hears (everything)! They have no Walî (Helper, Disposer of affairs, Protector) other than Him, and He makes none to share in His Decision and His Rule." (18:26)
واتل ما اوحي اليك من كتاب ربك لا مبدل لك: 27. And recite what has been revealed to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) of the Book (the Qur’ân) of your Lord (i.e. recite it, understand and follow its teachings and act on its orders and preach it to men). None can change His Words, and none will you find as a refuge other than Him. (18:27)
واصبر نفسك مع الذين يدعون ربهم بالغداة و: 28. And keep yourself (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) patiently with those who call on their Lord (i.e. your companions who remember their Lord with glorification, praising in prayers, and other righteous deeds) morning and afternoon, seeking His Face; and let not your eyes overlook them, desiring the pomp and glitter of the life of the world; and obey not him whose heart We have made heedless of Our Remembrance, and who follows his own lusts, and whose affair (deeds) has been lost. (18:28)
وقل الحق من ربكم فمن شاء فليؤمن ومن شاء: 29. And say: "The truth is from your Lord." Then whosoever wills, let him believe; and whosoever wills, let him disbelieve. Verily, We have prepared for the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers.), a Fire whose walls will be surrounding them (disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh). And if they ask for help (relief, water), they will be granted water like boiling oil, that will scald their faces. Terrible is the drink, and an evil Murtafaq (dwelling, resting place.)! (18:29)
ان الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات انا لا نض: 30. Verily as for those who believed and did righteous deeds, certainly We shall not make the reward of anyone who does his (righteous) deeds in the most perfect manner to be lost. (18:30)
اولئك لهم جنات عدن تجري من تحتهم الانهار: 31. These! For them will be ‘Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens); wherein rivers flow underneath them; therein they will be adorned with bracelets of gold, and they will wear green garments of fine and thick silk. They will recline therein on raised thrones. How good is the reward, and what an excellent Murtafaq (dwelling, resting place.)! (18:31)
واضرب لهم مثلا رجلين جعلنا لاحدهما جنتين: 32. And put forward to them the example of two men: unto one of them We had given two gardens of grapes, and We had surrounded both with date-palms; and had put between them green crops (cultivated fields). (18:32)
كلتا الجنتين اتت اكلها ولم تظلم منه شيئا: 33. Each of those two gardens brought forth its produce, and failed not in the least therein, and We caused a river to gush forth in the midst of them. (18:33)
وكان له ثمر فقال لصاحبه وهو يحاوره انا ا: 34. And he had property (or fruit) and he said to his companion, in the course of mutual talk: "I am more than you in wealth and stronger in respect of men." (Tafsir Al-Qurtubî). (18:34)
ودخل جنته وهو ظالم لنفسه قال ما اظن ان ت: 35. And he went into his garden while in a state (of pride and disbelief) unjust to himself. He said: "I think not that this will ever perish. (18:35)
وما اظن الساعة قائمة ولئن رددت الى ربي ل: 36. "And I think not the Hour will ever come, and if indeed I am brought back to my Lord, (on the Day of Resurrection), I surely shall find better than this when I return to Him." (18:36)
قال له صاحبه وهو يحاوره اكفرت بالذي خلقك: 37. His companion said to him during the talk with him: "Do you disbelieve in Him Who created you out of dust (i.e. your father Adam), then out of Nutfah (mixed semen drops of male and female discharge), then fashioned you into a man? (18:37)
لكنا هو الله ربي ولا اشرك بربي احدا: 38. "But as for my part, (I believe) that He is Allâh, my Lord, and none shall I associate as partner with my Lord. (18:38)
ولولا اذ دخلت جنتك قلت ما شاء الله لا قو: 39. "It was better for you to say, when you entered your garden: ‘That which Allâh wills (will come to pass)! There is no power but with Allâh[2]!’ If you see me less than you in wealth, and children, (18:39)
فعسى ربي ان يؤتين خيرا من جنتك ويرسل علي: 40. "It may be that my Lord will give me something better than your garden, and will send on it Husbân (torment, bolt) from the sky, then it will be a barren slippery earth. (18:40)
او يصبح ماؤها غورا فلن تستطيع له طلبا: 41. "Or the water thereof (of the gardens) becomes deep-sunken (underground) so that you will never be able to seek it." (18:41)
واحيط بثمره فاصبح يقلب كفيه على ما انفق: 42. So his fruits were encircled (with ruin). And he remained clapping his hands (with sorrow) over what he had spent upon it, while it was all destroyed on its trellises, and he could only say: "Would that I had ascribed no partners to my Lord!" [Tafsir Ibn Kathîr] (18:42)
ولم تكن له فئة ينصرونه من دون الله وما ك: 43. And he had no group of men to help him against Allâh, nor could he defend (or save) himself. (18:43)
هنالك الولاية لله الحق هو خير ثوابا وخير: 44. There (on the Day of Resurrection), Al-Walâyah (protection, power, authority and kingdom) will be for Allâh (Alone), the True God. He (Allâh) is the Best for reward and the Best for the final end. (Lâ ilâha illallâh- none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh). (18:44)
واضرب لهم مثل الحياة الدنيا كماء انزلناه: 45. And put forward to them the example of the life of this world: it is like the water (rain) which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mingles with it, and becomes fresh and green. But (later) it becomes dry and broken pieces, which the winds scatter. And Allâh is Able to do everything. (18:45)
المال والبنون زينة الحياة الدنيا والباقي: 46. Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world. But the good righteous deeds,[3] that last, are better with your Lord for rewards and better in respect of hope. (18:46)
ويوم نسير الجبال وترى الارض بارزة وحشرنا: 47. And (remember) the Day We shall cause the mountains to pass away (like clouds of dust), and you will see the earth as a levelled plain, and we shall gather them all together so as to leave not one of them behind. (18:47)
وعرضوا على ربك صفا لقد جئتمونا كما خلقنا: 48. And they will be set before your Lord in (lines as) rows, (and Allâh will say): "Now indeed, you have come to Us as We created you the first time. Nay, but you thought that We had appointed no meeting for you (with Us)." (18:48)
ووضع الكتاب فترى المجرمين مشفقين مما فيه: 49. And the Book (one’s Record) will be placed (in the right hand for a believer in the Oneness of Allâh, and in the left hand for a disbeliever in the Oneness of Allâh), and you will see the Mujrimûn (criminals, polytheists, sinners), fearful of that which is (recorded) therein. They will say: "Woe to us! What sort of Book is this that leaves neither a small thing nor a big thing, but has recorded it with numbers!" And they will find all that they did, placed before them, and your Lord treats no one with injustice. (18:49)
واذ قلنا للملائكة اسجدوا لادم فسجدوا الا: 50. And (remember) when We said to the angels: "Prostrate yourselves unto Adam." So they prostrated themselves except Iblîs (Satan). He was one of the jinn; he disobeyed the Command of his Lord. Will you then take him (Iblîs) and his offspring as protectors and helpers rather than Me while they are enemies to you? What an evil is the exchange for the Zâlimûn (polytheists, and wrong-doers). (18:50)
ما اشهدتهم خلق السماوات والارض ولا خلق ا: 51. I (Allâh) made them (Iblîs and his offspring) not to witness (nor took their help in) the creation of the heavens and the earth and not (even) their own creation, nor was I (Allâh) to take the misleaders as helpers. (18:51)
ويوم يقول نادوا شركائي الذين زعمتم فدعوه: 52. And (remember) the Day He will say: "Call those (so-called) partners of Mine whom you pretended." Then they will cry unto them, but they will not answer them, and We shall put a Maubiq (barrier)[4] between them. (18:52)
وراى المجرمون النار فظنوا انهم مواقعوها: 53. And the Mujrimûn (criminals, polytheists, sinners) shall see the Fire and apprehend that they have to fall therein. And they will find no way of escape from there. (18:53)
ولقد صرفنا في هذا القران للناس من كل مثل: 54. And indeed We have put forth every kind of example in this Qur’ân, for mankind. But, man is ever more quarrelsome than anything. (18:54)
وما منع الناس ان يؤمنوا اذ جاءهم الهدى و: 55. And nothing prevents men from believing, (now) when the guidance (the Qur’ân) has come to them, and from asking Forgiveness of their Lord, except that the ways of the ancients be repeated with them (i.e. their destruction decreed by Allâh), or the torment be brought to them face to face? (18:55)
وما نرسل المرسلين الا مبشرين ومنذرين ويج: 56. And We send not the Messengers except as givers of glad tidings and warners. But those who disbelieve, dispute with false argument, in order to refute the truth thereby. And they treat My Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and that with which they are warned, as a jest and mockery! (18:56)
ومن اظلم ممن ذكر بايات ربه فاعرض عنها ون: 57. And who does more wrong than he who is reminded of the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of his Lord, but turns away from them, forgetting what (deeds) his hands have sent forth. Truly, We have set veils over their hearts lest they should understand this (the Qur’ân), and in their ears, deafness. And if you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) call them to guidance, even then they will never be guided. (18:57)
وربك الغفور ذو الرحمة لو يؤاخذهم بما كسب: 58. And your Lord is Most Forgiving, Owner of Mercy. Were He to call them to account for what they have earned, then surely, He would have hastened their punishment. But they have their appointed time, beyond which they will find no escape. (18:58)
وتلك القرى اهلكناهم لما ظلموا وجعلنا لمه: 59. And these towns (population, - ‘Âd, Thamûd) We destroyed them when they did wrong. And We appointed a fixed time for their destruction. (18:59)
واذ قال موسى لفتاه لا ابرح حتى ابلغ مجمع: 60. And (remember) when Mûsâ (Moses) said to his boy-servant: "I will not give up (travelling) until I reach the junction of the two seas or (until) I spend years and years in travelling."[5] (18:60)
فلما بلغا مجمع بينهما نسيا حوتهما فاتخذ: 61. But when they reached the junction of the two seas, they forgot their fish, and it took its way through the sea as in a tunnel. (18:61)
فلما جاوزا قال لفتاه اتنا غداءنا لقد لقي: 62. So when they had passed further on (beyond that fixed place), Mûsâ (Moses) said to his boy-servant: "Bring us our morning meal; truly, we have suffered much fatigue in this, our journey." (18:62)
قال ارايت اذ اوينا الى الصخرة فاني نسيت: 63. He said: "Do you remember when we betook ourselves to the rock? I indeed forgot the fish; none but Shaitân (Satan) made me forget to remember it. It took its course into the sea in a strange (way)!" (18:63)
قال ذلك ما كنا نبغ فارتدا على اثارهما قص: 64. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "That is what we have been seeking." So they went back retracing their footsteps. (18:64)
فوجدا عبدا من عبادنا اتيناه رحمة من عندن: 65. Then they found one of Our slaves, on whom We had bestowed mercy from Us, and whom We had taught knowledge from Us. (18:65)
قال له موسى هل اتبعك على ان تعلمن مما عل: 66. Mûsâ (Moses) said to him (Khidr): "May I follow you so that you teach me something of that knowledge (guidance and true path) which you have been taught (by Allâh)?" (18:66)
قال انك لن تستطيع معي صبرا: 67. He (Khidr) said: "Verily you will not be able to have patience with me! (18:67)
وكيف تصبر على ما لم تحط به خبرا: 68. "And how can you have patience about a thing which you know not?" (18:68)
قال ستجدني ان شاء الله صابرا ولا اعصي لك: 69. Mûsâ (Moses) said: "If Allâh wills, you will find me patient, and I will not disobey you in aught." (18:69)
قال فان اتبعتني فلا تسالني عن شيء حتى اح: 70. He (Khidr) said: "Then, if you follow me, ask me not about anything till I myself mention of it to you." (18:70)
فانطلقا حتى اذا ركبا في السفينة خرقها قا: 71. So they both proceeded, till, when they embarked the ship, he (Khidr) scuttled it. Mûsâ (Moses) said: "Have you scuttled it in order to drown its people? Verily, you have committed a thing "Imr" (a Munkar - evil, bad, dreadful thing)." (18:71)
قال الم اقل انك لن تستطيع معي صبرا: 72. He (Khidr) said: "Did I not tell you, that you would not be able to have patience with me?" (18:72)
قال لا تؤاخذني بما نسيت ولا ترهقني من ام: 73. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Call me not to account for what I forgot,[6] and be not hard upon me for my affair (with you)." (18:73)
فانطلقا حتى اذا لقيا غلاما فقتله قال اقت: 74. Then they both proceeded, till they met a boy, and he (Khidr) killed him. Mûsâ (Moses) said: "Have you killed an innocent person who had killed none? Verily, you have committed a thing Nukr (a great Munkar - prohibited, evil, dreadful thing)!" (18:74)
قال الم اقل لك انك لن تستطيع معي صبرا: 75. (Khidr) said: "Did I not tell you that you can have no patience with me?" (18:75)
قال ان سالتك عن شيء بعدها فلا تصاحبني قد: 76. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "If I ask you anything after this, keep me not in your company, you have received an excuse from me." (18:76)
فانطلقا حتى اذا اتيا اهل قرية استطعما اه: 77. Then they both proceeded, till, when they came to the people of a town, they asked them for food, but they refused to entertain them. Then they found therein a wall about to collapse and he (Khidr) set it up straight. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: If you had wished, surely, you could have taken wages for it!" (18:77)
قال هذا فراق بيني وبينك سانبئك بتاويل ما: 78. (Khidr) said: "This is the parting between me and you, I will tell you the interpretation of (those) things over which you were unable to hold patience. (18:78)
اما السفينة فكانت لمساكين يعملون في البح: 79. "As for the ship, it belonged to Masâkîn (poor people) working in the sea. So I wished to make a defective damage in it, as there was a king behind them who seized every ship by force. (18:79)
واما الغلام فكان ابواه مؤمنين فخشينا ان: 80. "And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared lest he should oppress them by rebellion and disbelief. (18:80)
فاردنا ان يبدلهما ربهما خيرا منه زكاة وا: 81. "So we intended that their Lord should change him for them for one better in righteousness and nearer to mercy. (18:81)
واما الجدار فكان لغلامين يتيمين في المدي: 82. "And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the town; and there was under it a treasure belonging to them; and their father was a righteous man, and your Lord intended that they should attain their age of full strength and take out their treasure as a mercy from your Lord. And I did them not of my own accord. That is the interpretation of those (things) over which you could not hold patience." (18:82)
ويسالونك عن ذي القرنين قل ساتلو عليكم من: 83. And they ask you about Dhul-Qarnain. Say: "I shall recite to you something of his story." (18:83)
انا مكنا له في الارض واتيناه من كل شيء س: 84. Verily, We established him in the earth, and We gave him the means of everything. (18:84)
حتى اذا بلغ مغرب الشمس وجدها تغرب في عين: 86. Until, when he reached the setting place of the sun, he found it setting in a spring of black muddy (or hot) water. And he found near it a people. We (Allâh) said (by inspiration): "O Dhul-Qarnain! Either you punish them, or treat them with kindness." (18:86)
قال اما من ظلم فسوف نعذبه ثم يرد الى ربه: 87. He said: "As for him (a disbeliever in the Oneness of Allâh) who does wrong, we shall punish him, and then he will be brought back unto his Lord, Who will punish him with a terrible torment (Hell). (18:87)
واما من امن وعمل صالحا فله جزاء الحسنى و: 88. "But as for him who believes (in Allâh’s Oneness) and works righteousness, he shall have the best reward, (Paradise), and we (Dhul-Qarnain) shall speak unto him mild words (as instructions)." (18:88)
حتى اذا بلغ مطلع الشمس وجدها تطلع على قو: 90. Until, when he came to the rising place of the sun, he found it rising on a people for whom We (Allâh) had provided no shelter against the sun. (18:90)
كذلك وقد احطنا بما لديه خبرا: 91. So (it was)! And We knew all about him (Dhul-Qarnain). (18:91)
حتى اذا بلغ بين السدين وجد من دونهما قوم: 93. Until, when he reached between two mountains, he found, before (near) them (those two mountains), a people who scarcely understood a word. (18:93)
قالوا يا ذا القرنين ان ياجوج وماجوج مفسد: 94. They said: "O Dhul-Qarnain! Verily Ya’jûj and Ma’jûj (Gog and Magog)[7] are doing great mischief in the land. Shall we then pay you a tribute in order that you might erect a barrier between us and them?" (18:94)
قال ما مكني فيه ربي خير فاعينوني بقوة اج: 95. He said: "That (wealth, authority and power) in which my Lord had established me is better (than your tribute). So help me with strength (of men), I will erect between you and them a barrier. (18:95)
اتوني زبر الحديد حتى اذا ساوى بين الصدفي: 96. "Give me pieces (blocks) of iron;" then, when he had filled up the gap between the two mountain-cliffs, he said: "Blow;" then when he had made them (red as) fire, he said: "Bring me molten copper to pour over them." (18:96)
فما اسطاعوا ان يظهروه وما استطاعوا له نق: 97. So they [Ya’jûj and Ma’jûj (Gog and Magog)] could not scale it or dig through it. (18:97)
قال هذا رحمة من ربي فاذا جاء وعد ربي جعل: 98. (Dhul-Qarnain) said: "This is a mercy from my Lord, but when the Promise of my Lord comes, He shall level it down to the ground. And the Promise of my Lord is ever true." (18:98)
وتركنا بعضهم يومئذ يموج في بعض ونفخ في ا: 99. And on that Day [i.e. the Day Ya’jûj and Ma’jûj (Gog and Magog) will come out], We shall leave them to surge like waves on one another; and the Trumpet will be blown, and We shall collect them (the creatures) all together. (18:99)
وعرضنا جهنم يومئذ للكافرين عرضا: 100. And on that Day We shall present Hell to the disbelievers, plain to view - (18:100)
الذين كانت اعينهم في غطاء عن ذكري وكانوا: 101. (To) those whose eyes had been under a covering from My Reminder (this Qur’ân), and who could not bear to hear (it). (18:101)
افحسب الذين كفروا ان يتخذوا عبادي من دون: 102. Do then those who disbelieved think that they can take My slaves [i.e., the angels, Allâh’s Messengers, ‘Îsâ (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary)] as Auliyâ’ (lords, gods, protectors) besides Me? Verily, We have prepared Hell as an entertainment for the disbelievers (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism)[8]. (18:102)
قل هل ننبئكم بالاخسرين اعمالا: 103. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Shall We tell you the greatest losers in respect of (their) deeds? (18:103)
الذين ضل سعيهم في الحياة الدنيا وهم يحسب: 104. "Those whose efforts have been wasted in this life while they thought that they were acquiring good by their deeds.[9] (18:104)
اولئك الذين كفروا بايات ربهم ولقائه فحبط: 105. "They are those who deny the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of their Lord and the Meeting with Him (in the Hereafter). So their works are in vain, and on the Day of Resurrection, We shall assign no weight for them. (18:105)
ذلك جزاؤهم جهنم بما كفروا واتخذوا اياتي: 106. "That shall be their recompense, Hell; because they disbelieved and took My Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) and My Messengers by way of jest and mockery. (18:106)
ان الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات كانت لهم: 107. "Verily those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous deeds, shall have the Gardens of Al-Firdaus (Paradise) for their entertainment. (18:107)
خالدين فيها لا يبغون عنها حولا: 108. "Wherein they shall dwell (forever). No desire will they have for removal therefrom." (18:108)
قل لو كان البحر مدادا لكلمات ربي لنفد ال: 109. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to mankind): "If the sea were ink for (writing) the Words of my Lord, surely, the sea would be exhausted before the Words of my Lord would be finished, even if We brought (another sea) like it for its aid." (18:109)
قل انما انا بشر مثلكم يوحى الي انما الهك: 110. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I am only a man like you. It has been revealed to me that your Ilâh (God) is One Ilâh (God - i.e. Allâh). So whoever hopes for the Meeting[10] with his Lord, let him work righteousness and associate none as a partner in the worship of his Lord." (18:110)
كهيعص: 1. Kâf-Hâ-Yâ-‘Aîn-Sâd. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Qur’ân, and none but Allâh (Alone) knows their meanings]. (19:1)
ذكر رحمت ربك عبده زكريا: 2. (This is) a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His slave Zakariyyâ (Zachariah). (19:2)
اذ نادى ربه نداء خفيا: 3. When he called out his Lord (Allâh) a call in secret. (19:3)
قال رب اني وهن العظم مني واشتعل الراس شي: 4. He said: "My Lord! Indeed my bones have grown feeble, and grey hair has spread on my head, And I have never been unblest in my invocation to You, O my Lord! (19:4)
واني خفت الموالي من ورائي وكانت امراتي ع: 5. "And verily I fear my relatives after me, and my wife is barren. So give me from Yourself an heir. (19:5)
يرثني ويرث من ال يعقوب واجعله رب رضيا: 6. "Who shall inherit me, and inherit (also) the posterity of Ya‘qûb (Jacob) (inheritance of the religious knowledge and Prophethood, not of wealth.). And make him, my Lord, one with whom You are Well-Pleased!" (19:6)
يا زكريا انا نبشرك بغلام اسمه يحيى لم نج: 7. (Allâh said) "O Zakariyyâ (Zachariah)! Verily, We give you the glad tidings of a son, whose name will be Yahyâ (John). We have given that name to none before (him)." (19:7)
قال رب انى يكون لي غلام وكانت امراتي عاق: 8. He said: "My Lord! How can I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I have reached the extreme old age." (19:8)
قال كذلك قال ربك هو علي هين وقد خلقتك من: 9. He said: "So (it will be). Your Lord says: It is easy for Me. Certainly I have created you before, when you had been nothing!" (19:9)
قال رب اجعل لي اية قال ايتك الا تكلم الن: 10. [Zakariyyâ (Zachariah)] said: "My Lord! Appoint for me a sign." He said: "Your sign is that you shall not speak unto mankind for three nights, though having no bodily defect." (19:10)
فخرج على قومه من المحراب فاوحى اليهم ان: 11. Then he came out to his people from Al-Mihrâb (a praying place or a private room), and he told them by signs to glorify Allâh’s Praises in the morning and in the afternoon. (19:11)
يا يحيى خذ الكتاب بقوة واتيناه الحكم صبي: 12. (It was said to his son): "O Yahyâ (John)! Hold fast the Scripture [the Taurât (Torah)]." And We gave him wisdom while yet a child. (19:12)
وحنانا من لدنا وزكاة وكان تقيا: 13. And (made him) sympathetic to men as a mercy (or a grant) from Us, and pure from sins [i.e. Yahyâ (John)] and he was righteous, (19:13)
وبرا بوالديه ولم يكن جبارا عصيا: 14. And dutiful towards his parents, and he was neither arrogant nor disobedient (to Allâh or to his parents). (19:14)
وسلام عليه يوم ولد ويوم يموت ويوم يبعث ح: 15. And Salâm (peace) be on him the day he was born, and the day he dies, and the day he will be raised up to life (again)! (19:15)
واذكر في الكتاب مريم اذ انتبذت من اهلها: 16. And mention in the Book (the Qur’ân, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم the story of) Maryam (Mary), when she withdrew in seclusion from her family to a place facing east. (19:16)
فاتخذت من دونهم حجابا فارسلنا اليها روحن: 17. She placed a screen (to screen herself) from them; then We sent to her Our Ruh [angel Jibrîl (Gabriel)], and he appeared before her in the form of a man in all respects. (19:17)
قالت اني اعوذ بالرحمن منك ان كنت تقيا: 18. She said: "Verily! I seek refuge with the Most Gracious (Allâh) from you, if you do fear Allâh." (19:18)
قال انما انا رسول ربك لاهب لك غلاما زكيا: 19. (The angel) said: "I am only a messenger from your Lord, (to announce) to you the gift of a righteous son." (19:19)
قالت انى يكون لي غلام ولم يمسسني بشر ولم: 20. She said: "How can I have a son, when no man has touched me, nor am I unchaste?" (19:20)
قال كذلك قال ربك هو علي هين ولنجعله اية: 21. He said: "So (it will be), your Lord said: ‘That is easy for Me (Allâh): And (We wish) to appoint him as a sign to mankind and a mercy from Us (Allâh), and it is a matter (already) decreed, (by Allâh).’ " (19:21)
فحملته فانتبذت به مكانا قصيا: 22. So she conceived him,[1] and she withdrew with him to a far place (i.e. Bethlehem valley about 4-6 miles from Jerusalem). (19:22)
فاجاءها المخاض الى جذع النخلة قالت يا لي: 23. And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a date-palm. She said: "Would that I had died before this, and had been forgotten and out of sight!" (19:23)
فناداها من تحتها الا تحزني قد جعل ربك تح: 24. Then [the babe ‘Îsâ (Jesus) or Jibrîl (Gabriel)] cried unto her from below her, saying: "Grieve not: your Lord has provided a water stream under you. (19:24)
وهزي اليك بجذع النخلة تساقط عليك رطبا جن: 25. "And shake the trunk of date-palm towards you, it will let fall fresh ripe-dates upon you." (19:25)
فكلي واشربي وقري عينا فاما ترين من البشر: 26. "So eat and drink and be glad. And if you see any human being, say: ‘Verily! I have vowed a fast unto the Most Gracious (Allâh) so I shall not speak to any human being this day.’" (19:26)
فاتت به قومها تحمله قالوا يا مريم لقد جئ: 27. Then she brought him (the baby) to her people, carrying him. They said: "O Mary! Indeed you have brought a thing Fariyy (a mighty thing). [Tafsir Al-Tabari] (19:27)
يا اخت هارون ما كان ابوك امرا سوء وما كا: 28. "O sister (i.e. the like) of Hârûn (Aaron)[2]! Your father was not a man who used to commit adultery, nor your mother was an unchaste woman." (19:28)
فاشارت اليه قالوا كيف نكلم من كان في الم: 29. Then she pointed to him. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle?[3]" (19:29)
قال اني عبد الله اتاني الكتاب وجعلني نبي: 30. "He [‘Îsâ (Jesus)] said: "Verily I am a slave of Allâh, He has given me the Scripture and made me a Prophet;[4]" (19:30)
وجعلني مباركا اين ما كنت واوصاني بالصلاة: 31. "And He has made me blessed wheresoever I be, and has enjoined on me Salât (prayer), and Zakât, as long as I live." (19:31)
وبرا بوالدتي ولم يجعلني جبارا شقيا: 32. "And dutiful to my mother, and made me not arrogant, unblest. (19:32)
والسلام علي يوم ولدت ويوم اموت ويوم ابعث: 33. "And Salâm (peace) be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive!" (19:33)
ذلك عيسى ابن مريم قول الحق الذي فيه يمتر: 34. Such is ‘Îsâ (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary). (It is) a statement of truth, about which they doubt (or dispute). (19:34)
ما كان لله ان يتخذ من ولد سبحانه اذا قضى: 35. It befits not (the Majesty of) Allâh that He should beget a son [this refers to the slander of Christians against Allâh, by saying that ‘Îsâ (Jesus) is the son of Allâh]. Glorified (and Exalted) be He (above all that they associate with Him). When He decrees a thing, He only says to it: "Be!" - and it is.[5] (19:35)
وان الله ربي وربكم فاعبدوه هذا صراط مستق: 36. [‘Îsâ (Jesus) said]: "And verily Allâh is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him (Alone). That is the Straight Path. (Allâh’s religion of Islâmic Monotheism which He did ordain for all of His Prophets)." [Tafsir At-Tabarî] (19:36)
فاختلف الاحزاب من بينهم فويل للذين كفروا: 37. Then the sects differed [i.e. the Christians about ‘Îsâ (Jesus) عليه السلام], so woe unto the disbelievers [those who gave false witness by saying that ‘Îsâ (Jesus) is the son of Allâh] from the Meeting of a great Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection, when they will be thrown in the blazing Fire)[6]. (19:37)
اسمع بهم وابصر يوم ياتوننا لكن الظالمون: 38. How clearly will they (polytheists and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh) see and hear the Day when they will appear before Us! But the Zalimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers) today are in plain error. (19:38)
وانذرهم يوم الحسرة اذ قضي الامر وهم في غ: 39. And warn them (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) of the Day of grief and regrets, when the case has been decided, while (now) they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not[7]. (19:39)
انا نحن نرث الارض ومن عليها والينا يرجعو: 40. Verily We will inherit the earth and whatsoever is thereon. And to Us they all shall be returned, (19:40)
واذكر في الكتاب ابراهيم انه كان صديقا نب: 41. And mention in the Book (the Qur’ân) Ibrâhîm (Abraham). Verily he was a man of truth, a Prophet. (19:41)
اذ قال لابيه يا ابت لم تعبد ما لا يسمع و: 42. When he said to his father: "O my father! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and cannot avail you in anything? (19:42)
يا ابت اني قد جاءني من العلم ما لم ياتك: 43. "O my father! Verily there has come to me of the knowledge that which came not unto you. So follow me, I will guide you to the Straight Path. (19:43)
يا ابت لا تعبد الشيطان ان الشيطان كان لل: 44. "O my father! Worship not Shaitân (Satan). Verily Shaitân (Satan) has been a rebel against the Most Gracious (Allâh). (19:44)
يا ابت اني اخاف ان يمسك عذاب من الرحمن ف: 45. "O my father! Verily I fear lest a torment from the Most Gracious (Allâh) should overtake you, so that you become a companion of Shaitân (Satan) (in the Hell-fire)." [Tafsir Al-Qurtubî] (19:45)
قال اراغب انت عن الهتي يا ابراهيم لئن لم: 46. He (the father) said: "Do you reject my gods, O Ibrâhîm (Abraham)? If you stop not (this), I will indeed stone you. So get away from me safely (before I punish you)." (19:46)
قال سلام عليك ساستغفر لك ربي انه كان بي: 47. Ibrâhîm (Abraham) said: "Peace be on you! I will ask Forgiveness of my Lord for you. Verily He is unto me Ever Most Gracious. (19:47)
واعتزلكم وما تدعون من دون الله وادعو ربي: 48. "And I shall turn away from you and from those whom you invoke besides Allâh. And I shall call upon my Lord and I hope that I shall not be unblest in my invocation to my Lord." (19:48)
فلما اعتزلهم وما يعبدون من دون الله وهبن: 49. So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allâh, We gave him Ishâq (Isaac) and Ya‘qûb (Jacob), and each one of them We made a Prophet. (19:49)
ووهبنا لهم من رحمتنا وجعلنا لهم لسان صدق: 50. And We gave them of Our Mercy (a good provision in plenty), and We granted them honour on the tongues (of all the nations, i.e. everybody remembers them with a good praise).[8] (19:50)
واذكر في الكتاب موسى انه كان مخلصا وكان: 51. And mention in the Book (this Qur’ân) Mûsâ (Moses). Verily he was chosen and he was a Messenger (and) a Prophet. (19:51)
وناديناه من جانب الطور الايمن وقربناه نج: 52. And We called him from the right side of the Mount, and made him draw near to Us for a talk with him [Mûsâ (Moses)]. (19:52)
ووهبنا له من رحمتنا اخاه هارون نبيا: 53. And We granted him his brother Hârûn (Aaron), (also) a Prophet, out of Our Mercy. (19:53)
واذكر في الكتاب اسماعيل انه كان صادق الو: 54. And mention in the Book (the Qur’ân) Ismâ‘îl (Ishmael). Verily he was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet. (19:54)
وكان يامر اهله بالصلاة والزكاة وكان عند: 55. And he used to enjoin on his family and his people As-Salât (the prayers) and the Zakât and his Lord was pleased with him. (19:55)
واذكر في الكتاب ادريس انه كان صديقا نبيا: 56. And mention in the Book (the Qur’ân) Idrîs (Enoch).Verily he was a man of truth, (and) a Prophet. (19:56)
ورفعناه مكانا عليا: 57. And We raised him to a high station. (19:57)
اولئك الذين انعم الله عليهم من النبيين م: 58. Those were they unto whom Allâh bestowed His Grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nûh (Noah), and of the offspring of Ibrâhîm (Abraham) and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Verses of the Most Gracious (Allâh) were recited unto them, they fell down prostrate and weeping.[9] (19:58)
فخلف من بعدهم خلف اضاعوا الصلاة واتبعوا: 59. Then, there has succeeded them a posterity who have given up As-Salât (the prayers) [i.e. made their Salât (prayers) to be lost, either by not offering them or by not offering them perfectly or by not offering them in their proper fixed times] and have followed lusts.[10] So they will be thrown in Hell. (19:59)
الا من تاب وامن وعمل صالحا فاولئك يدخلون: 60. Except those who repent and believe (in the Oneness of Allâh and His Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), and work righteousness.[11] Such will enter Paradise and they will not be wronged in aught. (19:60)
جنات عدن التي وعد الرحمن عباده بالغيب ان: 61. (They will enter) ‘Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), which the Most Gracious (Allâh) has promised to His slaves in the Unseen: Verily His Promise must come to pass. (19:61)
لا يسمعون فيها لغوا الا سلاما ولهم رزقهم: 62. They shall not hear therein (in Paradise) any Laghw (dirty, false, evil vain talk), but only Salâm (salutations of peace). And they will have therein their sustenance, morning and afternoon. [See (V.40:55)]. (19:62)
تلك الجنة التي نورث من عبادنا من كان تقي: 63. Such is the Paradise which We shall give as an inheritance to those of Our slaves who have been Al-Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2). (19:63)
وما نتنزل الا بامر ربك له ما بين ايدينا: 64. And we (angels) descend not except by the Command of your Lord (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). To Him belongs what is before us and what is behind us, and what is between those two; and your Lord is never forgetful - (19:64)
رب السماوات والارض وما بينهما فاعبده واص: 65. Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, so worship Him (Alone) and be constant and patient in His worship. Do you know of any who is similar to Him? (of course none is similar or co-equal or comparable to Him, and He has none as partner with Him). [There is nothing like Him and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer].[12] (19:65)
ويقول الانسان ااذا ما مت لسوف اخرج حيا: 66. And man (the disbeliever) says: "When I am dead, shall I then be raised up alive?" (19:66)
اولا يذكر الانسان انا خلقناه من قبل ولم: 67. Does not man remember that We created him before, while he was nothing? (19:67)
فوربك لنحشرنهم والشياطين ثم لنحضرنهم حول: 68. So by your Lord, surely, We shall gather them together, and (also) the Shayâtîn (devils) (with them), then We shall bring them round Hell on their knees. (19:68)
ثم لننزعن من كل شيعة ايهم اشد على الرحمن: 69. Then indeed We shall drag out from every sect all those who were worst in obstinate rebellion against the Most Gracious (Allâh). (19:69)
ثم لنحن اعلم بالذين هم اولى بها صليا: 70. Then, verily, We know best those who are most worthy of being burnt therein. (19:70)
وان منكم الا واردها كان على ربك حتما مقض: 71. There is not one of you but will pass over it (Hell): this is with your Lord; a Decree which must be accomplished.[13] (19:71)
ثم ننجي الذين اتقوا ونذر الظالمين فيها ج: 72. Then We shall save those who used to fear Allâh and were dutiful to Him. And We shall leave the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrongdoers) therein (humbled) to their knees (in Hell). (19:72)
واذا تتلى عليهم اياتنا بينات قال الذين ك: 73. And when Our Clear Verses are recited to them, those who disbelieve (the rich and strong among the pagans of Quraish who live a life of luxury) say to those who believe (the weak, poor companions of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم who have a hard life): "Which of the two groups (i.e. believers or disbelievers) is best in (point of) position and as regards station (place of council for consultation)." (19:73)
وكم اهلكنا قبلهم من قرن هم احسن اثاثا ور: 74. And how many a generation (past nations) have We destroyed before them, who were better in wealth, goods and outward appearance? (19:74)
قل من كان في الضلالة فليمدد له الرحمن مد: 75. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) whoever is in error, the Most Gracious (Allâh) will extend (the rope) to him, until, when they see that which they were promised, either the torment or the Hour, they will come to know who is worst in position, and who is weaker in forces. [This is the answer to the question in Verse No.19:73] (19:75)
ويزيد الله الذين اهتدوا هدى والباقيات ال: 76. And Allâh increases in guidance those who walk aright.[14] And the righteous good deeds that last, are better with your Lord, for reward and better for resort. (19:76)
افرايت الذي كفر باياتنا وقال لاوتين مالا: 77. Have you seen him who disbelieved in Our Ayât (this Qur’ân and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) and said: "I shall certainly be given wealth and children [if I will be alive (again)]." (19:77)
اطلع الغيب ام اتخذ عند الرحمن عهدا: 78. Has he known the Unseen or has he taken a covenant from the Most Gracious (Allâh)? (19:78)
كلا سنكتب ما يقول ونمد له من العذاب مدا: 79. Nay, We shall record what he says, and We shall increase his torment (in the Hell); (19:79)
ونرثه ما يقول وياتينا فردا: 80. And We shall inherit from him (at his death) all that he talks of (i.e. wealth and children which We have bestowed upon him in this world), and he shall come to Us alone. (19:80)
واتخذوا من دون الله الهة ليكونوا لهم عزا: 81. And they have taken (for worship) âlihah (gods) besides Allâh, that they might give them honour, power and glory (and also protect them from Allâh’s punishment). (19:81)
كلا سيكفرون بعبادتهم ويكونون عليهم ضدا: 82. Nay, but they (the so-called gods) will deny their worship of them, and become opponents to them (on the Day of Resurrection). (19:82)
الم تر انا ارسلنا الشياطين على الكافرين: 83. See you not that We have sent the Shayâtîn (devils) against the disbelievers to push them to do evil. (19:83)
فلا تعجل عليهم انما نعد لهم عدا: 84. So make no haste against them; We only count out to them a (limited) number (of the days of the life of this world and delay their term so that they may increase in evil and sins). (19:84)
يوم نحشر المتقين الى الرحمن وفدا: 85. The Day We shall gather the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2) unto the Most Gracious (Allâh), like a delegation (presented before a king for honour). (19:85)
ونسوق المجرمين الى جهنم وردا: 86. And We shall drive the Mujrimûn (polytheists, sinners, criminals, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh) to Hell, in a thirsty state (like a thirsty herd driven down to water). (19:86)
لا يملكون الشفاعة الا من اتخذ عند الرحمن: 87. None shall have the power of intercession, but such a one as has received permission (or promise) from the Most Gracious (Allâh). (19:87)
وقالوا اتخذ الرحمن ولدا: 88. And they say: "The Most Gracious (Allâh) has begotten a son (or offspring or children) [as the Jews say: ‘Uzair (Ezra) is the son of Allâh, and the Christians say that He has begotten a son [‘Îsâ (Jesus) عليه السلام], and the pagan Arabs say that He has begotten daughters (angels and others.)]." (19:88)
لقد جئتم شيئا ادا: 89. Indeed you have brought forth (said) a terrible evil thing. (19:89)
تكاد السماوات يتفطرن منه وتنشق الارض وتخ: 90. Whereby the heavens are almost torn, and the earth is split asunder, and the mountains fall in ruins, (19:90)
ان دعوا للرحمن ولدا: 91. That they ascribe a son (or offspring or children) to the Most Gracious (Allâh). (19:91)
وما ينبغي للرحمن ان يتخذ ولدا: 92. But it is not suitable for (the Majesty of) the Most Gracious (Allâh) that He should beget a son (or offspring or children). (19:92)
ان كل من في السماوات والارض الا اتي الرح: 93. There is none in the heavens and the earth but comes unto the Most Gracious (Allâh) as a slave. (19:93)
لقد احصاهم وعدهم عدا: 94. Verily He knows each one of them, and has counted them a full counting. (19:94)
وكلهم اتيه يوم القيامة فردا: 95. And everyone of them will come to Him alone on the Day of Resurrection (without any helper, or protector or defender). (19:95)
ان الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات سيجعل لهم: 96. Verily, those who believe [in the Oneness of Allâh and in His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم)] and work deeds of righteousness, the Most Gracious (Allâh) will bestow love for them[15] (in the hearts of the believers). (19:96)
فانما يسرناه بلسانك لتبشر به المتقين وتن: 97. So We have made this (the Qur’ân) easy in your own tongue (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), only that you may give glad tidings to the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2), and warn with it the Ludd[16] (most quarrelsome) people. (19:97)
وكم اهلكنا قبلهم من قرن هل تحس منهم من ا: 98. And how many a generation before them have We destroyed! Can you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) find a single one of them or hear even a whisper of them? (19:98)
ما انزلنا عليك القران لتشقى: 2. We have not sent down the Qur’ân unto you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) to cause you distress, (20:2)
الا تذكرة لمن يخشى: 3. But only as a Reminder to those who fear (Allâh). (20:3)
تنزيلا ممن خلق الارض والسماوات العلى: 4. A revelation from Him (Allâh) Who has created the earth and high heavens. (20:4)
الرحمن على العرش استوى: 5. The Most Gracious (Allâh) rose over (Istawâ) the (Mighty) Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty). (20:5)
له ما في السماوات وما في الارض وما بينهم: 6. To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth, and all that is between them, and all that is under the soil. (20:6)
وان تجهر بالقول فانه يعلم السر واخفى: 7. And if you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) speak (the invocation) aloud, then verily, He knows the secret and that which is yet more hidden. (20:7)
الله لا اله الا هو له الاسماء الحسنى: 8. Allâh! Lâ ilâha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He)! To Him belong the Best Names.[1] (20:8)
وهل اتاك حديث موسى: 9. And has there come to you the story of Mûsâ (Moses)? (20:9)
اذ راى نارا فقال لاهله امكثوا اني انست ن: 10. When he saw a fire, he said to his family: "Wait! Verily, I have seen a fire; perhaps I can bring you some burning brand therefrom, or find some guidance at the fire." (20:10)
فلما اتاها نودي يا موسى: 11. And when he came to it (the fire), he was called by name: "O Mûsâ (Moses)! (20:11)
اني انا ربك فاخلع نعليك انك بالواد المقد: 12. "Verily I am your Lord! So take off your shoes; you are in the sacred valley, Tuwa. (20:12)
وانا اخترتك فاستمع لما يوحى: 13. "And I have chosen you. So listen to that which will be revealed (to you). (20:13)
انني انا الله لا اله الا انا فاعبدني واق: 14. "Verily! I am Allâh! Lâ ilâha illa Ana (none has the right to be worshipped but I), so worship Me, and perform As-Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât) for My Remembrance. (20:14)
ان الساعة اتية اكاد اخفيها لتجزى كل نفس: 15. "Verily, the Hour is coming - and I am almost hiding it from Myself - that every person may be rewarded for that which he strives. (20:15)
فلا يصدنك عنها من لا يؤمن بها واتبع هواه: 16. "Therefore, let not the one who believes not therein (i.e. in the Day of Resurrection, Reckoning, Paradise and Hell.), but follows his own lusts,[2] divert you therefrom, lest you perish. (20:16)
وما تلك بيمينك يا موسى: 17. "And what is that in your right hand, O Mûsâ (Moses)?" (20:17)
قال هي عصاي اتوكا عليها واهش بها على غنم: 18. He said: "This is my stick, whereon I lean, and wherewith I beat down branches for my sheep, and wherein I find other uses." (20:18)
فالقاها فاذا هي حية تسعى: 20. He cast it down, and behold! It was a snake, moving quickly. (20:20)
قال خذها ولا تخف سنعيدها سيرتها الاولى: 21. Allâh said: "Grasp it and fear not; We shall return it to its former state, (20:21)
واضمم يدك الى جناحك تخرج بيضاء من غير سو: 22. "And press your (right) hand to your (left) side: it will come forth white (and shining), without any disease as another sign, (20:22)
لنريك من اياتنا الكبرى: 23. "That We may show you (some) of Our Greater Signs. (20:23)
اذهب الى فرعون انه طغى: 24. "Go to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)! Verily, he has transgressed (all bounds in disbelief and disobedience, and has behaved as an arrogant and as a tyrant)." (20:24)
قال رب اشرح لي صدري: 25. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "O my Lord! Open for me my chest (grant me self-confidence, contentment, and boldness). (20:25)
واحلل عقدة من لساني: 27. "And loose the knot (the defect) from my tongue, (i.e. remove the incorrectness from my speech) [That occurred as a result of a brand of fire which Mûsâ (Moses) put in his mouth when he was an infant]. (Tafsir At-Tabarî). (20:27)
واجعل لي وزيرا من اهلي: 29. "And appoint for me a helper from my family, (20:29)
واشركه في امري: 32. "And let him share my task (of conveying Allâh’s Message and Prophethood), (20:32)
انك كنت بنا بصيرا: 35. "Verily You are Ever a Well-Seer of us." (20:35)
قال قد اوتيت سؤلك يا موسى: 36. (Allâh) said: "You are granted your request, O Mûsâ (Moses)! (20:36)
ولقد مننا عليك مرة اخرى: 37. "And indeed We conferred a favour on you another time (before). (20:37)
اذ اوحينا الى امك ما يوحى: 38. "When We inspired your mother with that which We inspired. (20:38)
ان اقذفيه في التابوت فاقذفيه في اليم فلي: 39. "Saying: ‘Put him (the child) into the Tabût (a box or a case or a chest) and put it into the river (Nile); then the river shall cast it up on the bank, and there, an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his shall take him.’ And I endued you with love from Me, in order that you may be brought up under My Eye. (20:39)
اذ تمشي اختك فتقول هل ادلكم على من يكفله: 40. "When your sister went and said: ‘Shall I show you one who will nurse him?’ So We restored you to your mother, that she might cool her eyes and she should not grieve. Then you did kill a man, but We saved you from great distress and tried you with a heavy trial. Then you stayed a number of years with the people of Madyan (Midian). Then you came here according to the fixed term which I ordained (for you), O Mûsâ (Moses)! (20:40)
واصطنعتك لنفسي: 41. "And I have chosen you,[3] for Myself. (20:41)
اذهب انت واخوك باياتي ولا تنيا في ذكري: 42. "Go you and your brother with My Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and do not, you both, slacken and become weak in My Remembrance. (20:42)
اذهبا الى فرعون انه طغى: 43. "Go, both of you, to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), verily, he has transgressed (all bounds in disbelief and disobedience and behaved as an arrogant and as a tyrant). (20:43)
فقولا له قولا لينا لعله يتذكر او يخشى: 44. "And speak to him mildly, perhaps he may accept admonition or fear (Allâh)." (20:44)
قالا ربنا اننا نخاف ان يفرط علينا او ان: 45. They said: "Our Lord! Verily we fear lest he should hasten to punish us or lest he should transgress (all bounds against us)." (20:45)
قال لا تخافا انني معكما اسمع وارى: 46. He (Allâh) said: "Fear not, verily I am with you both, hearing and seeing. (20:46)
فاتياه فقولا انا رسولا ربك فارسل معنا بن: 47. "So go you both to him, and say: ‘Verily, we are Messengers of your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with us, and torment them not; indeed, we have come with a sign from your Lord! And peace will be upon him who follows the guidance! (20:47)
انا قد اوحي الينا ان العذاب على من كذب و: 48. "Truly, it has been revealed to us that the torment will be for him who denies [believes not in the Oneness of Allâh, and in His Messengers.], and turns away (from the truth and obedience of Allâh)." (20:48)
قال فمن ربكما يا موسى: 49. Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) said: "Who then, O Mûsâ (Moses), is the Lord of you two?" (20:49)
قال ربنا الذي اعطى كل شيء خلقه ثم هدى: 50. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Our Lord is He Who gave to each thing its form and nature, then guided it aright." (20:50)
قال فما بال القرون الاولى: 51. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "What about the generations of old?" (20:51)
قال علمها عند ربي في كتاب لا يضل ربي ولا: 52. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord, in a Record. My Lord neither errs nor He forgets." (20:52)
الذي جعل لكم الارض مهدا وسلك لكم فيها سب: 53. Who has made earth for you like a bed (spread out); and has opened roads (ways and paths) for you therein, and has sent down water (rain) from the sky. And We have brought forth with it various kinds of vegetation. (20:53)
كلوا وارعوا انعامكم ان في ذلك لايات لاول: 54. Eat and pasture your cattle (therein); verily, in this are Ayât (proofs and signs) for men of understanding. (20:54)
منها خلقناكم وفيها نعيدكم ومنها نخرجكم ت: 55. Thereof (the earth) We created you, and into it We shall return you, and from it We shall bring you out once again. (20:55)
ولقد اريناه اياتنا كلها فكذب وابى: 56. And indeed We showed him [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] all Our Ayât (signs and evidence), but he denied and refused. (20:56)
قال اجئتنا لتخرجنا من ارضنا بسحرك يا موس: 57. He [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "Have you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Mûsâ (Moses)? (20:57)
فلناتينك بسحر مثله فاجعل بيننا وبينك موع: 58. "Then verily, we can produce magic the like thereof; so appoint a meeting between us and you, which neither we nor you shall fail to keep, in an open place where both shall have a just and equal chance (and beholders could witness the competition)." (20:58)
قال موعدكم يوم الزينة وان يحشر الناس ضحى: 59. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Your appointed meeting is the day of the festival, and let the people assemble when the sun has risen (forenoon)." (20:59)
فتولى فرعون فجمع كيده ثم اتى: 60. So Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) withdrew, devised his plot and then came back. (20:60)
قال لهم موسى ويلكم لا تفتروا على الله كذ: 61. Mûsâ (Moses) said to them: "Woe unto you! Invent not a lie against Allâh, lest He should destroy you completely by a torment. And surely, he who invents a lie (against Allâh) will fail miserably." (20:61)
فتنازعوا امرهم بينهم واسروا النجوى: 62. Then they debated one with another what they must do, and they kept their talk secret. (20:62)
قالوا ان هذان لساحران يريدان ان يخرجاكم: 63. They said: "Verily these are two magicians. Their object is to drive you out from your land with magic, and overcome your chiefs and nobles. (20:63)
فاجمعوا كيدكم ثم ائتوا صفا وقد افلح اليو: 64. "So devise your plot, and then assemble in line. And whoever overcomes this day will be indeed successful." (20:64)
قالوا يا موسى اما ان تلقي واما ان نكون ا: 65. They said: "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Either you throw first or we be the first to throw?" (20:65)
قال بل القوا فاذا حبالهم وعصيهم يخيل الي: 66. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Nay, throw you (first)!" Then behold! their ropes and their sticks, by their magic, appeared to him as though they moved fast. (20:66)
فاوجس في نفسه خيفة موسى: 67. So Mûsâ (Moses) conceived fear in himself. (20:67)
قلنا لا تخف انك انت الاعلى: 68. We (Allâh) said: "Fear not! Surely, you will have the upper hand. (20:68)
والق ما في يمينك تلقف ما صنعوا انما صنعو: 69. "And throw that which is in your right hand! It will swallow up that which they have made. That which they have made is only a magician’s trick, and the magician will never be successful, to whatever amount (of skill) he may attain." (20:69)
فالقي السحرة سجدا قالوا امنا برب هارون و: 70. So the magicians fell down prostrate. They said: "We believe in the Lord of Hârûn (Aaron) and Mûsâ (Moses)." (20:70)
قال امنتم له قبل ان اذن لكم انه لكبيركم: 71. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "Believe you in him [Mûsâ (Moses)] before I give you permission? Verily he is your chief who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you on the trunks of date-palms, and you shall surely know which of us [I (Fir‘aun - Pharaoh) or the Lord of Mûsâ (Moses) (Allâh)] can give the severe and more lasting torment." (20:71)
قالوا لن نؤثرك على ما جاءنا من البينات و: 72. They said: "We prefer you not over what have come to us of the clear signs and to Him (Allâh) Who created us. So decree whatever you desire to decree, for you can only decree (regarding) this life of the world. (20:72)
انا امنا بربنا ليغفر لنا خطايانا وما اكر: 73. "Verily we have believed in our Lord, that He may forgive us our faults, and the magic to which you did compel us. And Allâh is better as regards reward in comparison to your [Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh)] reward, and more lasting (as regards punishment in comparison to your punishment)." (20:73)
انه من يات ربه مجرما فان له جهنم لا يموت: 74. Verily whoever comes to his Lord as a Mujrim (criminal, polytheist, sinner, disbeliever in the Oneness of Allâh and His Messengers), then surely, for him is Hell, wherein he will neither die nor live. (20:74)
ومن ياته مؤمنا قد عمل الصالحات فاولئك له: 75. But whoever comes to Him (Allâh) as a believer (in the Oneness of Allâh), and has done righteous good deeds, for such are the high ranks (in the Hereafter), - (20:75)
جنات عدن تجري من تحتها الانهار خالدين في: 76. ‘Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), under which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever: such is the reward of those who purify themselves (by abstaining from all kinds of sins and evil deeds which Allâh has forbidden and by doing all that Allâh has ordained). (20:76)
ولقد اوحينا الى موسى ان اسر بعبادي فاضرب: 77. And indeed We revealed to Mûsâ (Moses) (saying): "Travel by night with Ibâdi (My slaves) and strike a dry path for them in the sea, fearing neither to be overtaken [by Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] nor being afraid (of drowning in the sea)." (20:77)
فاتبعهم فرعون بجنوده فغشيهم من اليم ما غ: 78. Then Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) pursued them with his hosts, but the sea-water completely overwhelmed them and covered them up. (20:78)
واضل فرعون قومه وما هدى: 79. And Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) led his people astray, and he did not guide them. (20:79)
يا بني اسرائيل قد انجيناكم من عدوكم وواع: 80. O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount, and We sent down to you Al-Manna [4] and quails, (20:80)
كلوا من طيبات ما رزقناكم ولا تطغوا فيه ف: 81. (Saying) eat of the Tayyibât (good lawful things) wherewith We have provided you, and commit no transgression or oppression therein, lest My Anger should justly descend on you. And he on whom My Anger descends, he is indeed perished. [Tafsir At-Tabari] (20:81)
واني لغفار لمن تاب وامن وعمل صالحا ثم اه: 82. And verily, I am indeed forgiving to him who repents, believes (in My Oneness, and associates none in worship with Me) and does righteous good deeds, and then remains constant in doing them, (till his death). (20:82)
وما اعجلك عن قومك يا موسى: 83. "And what made you hasten from your people, O Mûsâ (Moses)?" (20:83)
قال هم اولاء على اثري وعجلت اليك رب لترض: 84. He said: "They are close on my footsteps: and I hastened to You, O my Lord, that You might be pleased." (20:84)
قال فانا قد فتنا قومك من بعدك واضلهم الس: 85. (Allâh) said: "Verily We have tried your people in your absence, and As-Sâmirî has led them astray." (20:85)
فرجع موسى الى قومه غضبان اسفا قال يا قوم: 86. Then Mûsâ (Moses) returned to his people in a state of anger and sorrow. He said: "O my people! Did not your Lord promise you a fair promise? Did then the promise seem to you long in coming? Or did you desire that wrath should descend from your Lord on you, that you broke your promise to me (i.e disbelieving in Allâh and worshipping the calf)?" (20:86)
قالوا ما اخلفنا موعدك بملكنا ولكنا حملنا: 87. They said: "We broke not the promise to you, of our own will, but we were made to carry the weight of the ornaments of the [Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh)] people, then we cast them (into the fire), and that was what As-Sâmirî suggested." (20:87)
فاخرج لهم عجلا جسدا له خوار فقالوا هذا ا: 88. Then he took out (of the fire) for them (a statue of) a calf which seemed to low. They said: "This is your ilâh (god), and the ilâh (god) of Mûsâ (Moses), but he [Mûsâ (Moses)] has forgotten (his god).’" (20:88)
افلا يرون الا يرجع اليهم قولا ولا يملك ل: 89. Did they not see that it could not return them a word (for answer), and that it had no power either to harm them or to do them good? (20:89)
ولقد قال لهم هارون من قبل يا قوم انما فت: 90. And Hârûn (Aaron) indeed had said to them beforehand: "O my people! You are being tried in this, and verily, your Lord is (Allâh) the Most Gracious, so follow me and obey my order." (20:90)
قالوا لن نبرح عليه عاكفين حتى يرجع الينا: 91. They said: "We will not stop worshipping it (i.e. the calf), until Mûsâ (Moses) returns to us." (20:91)
قال يا هارون ما منعك اذ رايتهم ضلوا: 92. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "O Hârûn (Aaron)! What prevented you when you saw them going astray; (20:92)
الا تتبعن افعصيت امري: 93. "That you followed me not (according to my advice to you)? Have you then disobeyed my order?" (20:93)
قال يا ابن ام لا تاخذ بلحيتي ولا براسي ا: 94. He [Hârûn (Aaron)] said: "O son of my mother! Seize (me) not by my beard, nor by my head! Verily, I feared lest you should say: ‘You have caused a division among the Children of Israel, and you have not respected my word!’ " (20:94)
قال فما خطبك يا سامري: 95. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "And what is the matter with you. O Sâmirî? (i.e. why did you do so?)" (20:95)
قال بصرت بما لم يبصروا به فقبضت قبضة من: 96. (Sâmirî) said: "I saw what they saw not, so I took a handful (of dust) from the (hoof) print of the messenger [Jibrîl’s (Gabriel) horse] and threw it [into the fire in which were put the ornaments of Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh) people, or into the calf]. Thus my inner-self suggested to me." (20:96)
قال فاذهب فان لك في الحياة ان تقول لا مس: 97. Mûsâ (Moses) said: "Then go away! And verily, your (punishment) in this life will be that you will say: "Touch me not (i.e. you will live alone exiled away from mankind); and verily (for a future torment), you have a promise that will not fail. And look at your ilâh (god) to which you have been devoted. We will certainly burn it, and scatter its particles in the sea." (20:97)
انما الهكم الله الذي لا اله الا هو وسع ك: 98. Your Ilâh (God) is only Allâh, (the One) Lâ ilâha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). He has full knowledge of all things. (20:98)
كذلك نقص عليك من انباء ما قد سبق وقد اتي: 99. Thus We relate to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) some information of what happened before. And indeed We have given you from Us a Reminder (this Qur’ân). (20:99)
من اعرض عنه فانه يحمل يوم القيامة وزرا: 100. Whoever turns away from it (this Qur’ân - i.e. does not believe in it, nor acts on its orders), verily, they will bear a heavy burden (of sins) on the Day of Resurrection,[5] (20:100)
خالدين فيه وساء لهم يوم القيامة حملا: 101. They will abide in that (state in the Fire of Hell) - and evil indeed will it be that load for them on the Day of Resurrection; (20:101)
يوم ينفخ في الصور ونحشر المجرمين يومئذ ز: 102. The Day when the Trumpet will be blown (the second blowing): that Day, We shall gather the Mujrimûn (criminals, polytheists, sinners, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh) blue or blind-eyed with thirst.[6] (20:102)
يتخافتون بينهم ان لبثتم الا عشرا: 103. They will speak in a very low voice to each other (saying): "You stayed not longer than ten (days)." (20:103)
نحن اعلم بما يقولون اذ يقول امثلهم طريقة: 104. We know very well what they will say, when the best among them in knowledge and wisdom will say: "You stayed no longer than a day!" (20:104)
ويسالونك عن الجبال فقل ينسفها ربي نسفا: 105. And they ask you concerning the mountains: say, "My Lord will blast them and scatter them as particles of dust. (20:105)
فيذرها قاعا صفصفا: 106. "Then He shall leave them as a level smooth plain. (20:106)
لا ترى فيها عوجا ولا امتا: 107. "You will see therein nothing crooked or curved." (20:107)
يومئذ يتبعون الداعي لا عوج له وخشعت الاص: 108. On that Day mankind will follow strictly (the voice of) Allâh’s caller, no crookedness (that is without going to the right or left of that voice) will they show him (Allâh’s caller). And all voices will be humbled for the Most Gracious (Allâh), and nothing shall you hear but the low voice of their footsteps. (20:108)
يومئذ لا تنفع الشفاعة الا من اذن له الرح: 109. On that day no intercession shall avail, except the one for whom the Most Gracious (Allâh) has given permission and whose word is acceptable to Him. (20:109)
يعلم ما بين ايديهم وما خلفهم ولا يحيطون: 110. He (Allâh) knows what happens to them (His creatures) in this world, and what will happen to them (in the Hereafter) but they will never compass anything of His Knowledge. (20:110)
وعنت الوجوه للحي القيوم وقد خاب من حمل ظ: 111. And (all) faces shall be humbled before (Allâh), Al-Hayyul-Qayyum (the Ever Living, the One Who sustains and protects all that exists). And he who carried (a burden of) wrongdoing (i.e. he who disbelieved in Allâh, ascribed partners to Him, and did deeds of His disobedience), will be indeed a complete failure (on that Day). (20:111)
ومن يعمل من الصالحات وهو مؤمن فلا يخاف ظ: 112. And he who works deeds of righteousness, while he is a believer (in Islâmic Monotheism), then he will have no fear of injustice, nor of any curtailment (of his reward). (20:112)
وكذلك انزلناه قرانا عربيا وصرفنا فيه من: 113. And thus We have sent it down as a Qur’ân in Arabic, and have explained therein in detail the warnings, in order that they may fear Allâh, or that it may cause them to have a lesson from it (or to have the honour for believing and acting on its teachings). (20:113)
فتعالى الله الملك الحق ولا تعجل بالقران: 114. Then High above all be Allâh, the True King. And be not in haste (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) with the Qur’ân before its revelation is completed to you, and say: "My Lord! Increase me in knowledge." (20:114)
ولقد عهدنا الى ادم من قبل فنسي ولم نجد ل: 115. And indeed We made a covenant with Adam before, but he forgot, and We found on his part no firm will-power. (20:115)
واذ قلنا للملائكة اسجدوا لادم فسجدوا الا: 116. And (remember) when We said to the angels: "Prostrate yourselves to Adam." They prostrated themselves (all) except Iblîs (Satan) he refused. (20:116)
فقلنا يا ادم ان هذا عدو لك ولزوجك فلا يخ: 117. Then We said: "O Adam! Verily, this is an enemy to you and to your wife. So let him not get you both out of Paradise, so that you will be distressed. (20:117)
ان لك الا تجوع فيها ولا تعرى: 118. Verily, you have (a promise from Us) that you will never be hungry therein nor naked. (20:118)
وانك لا تظما فيها ولا تضحى: 119. And you (will) suffer not from thirst therein nor from the sun’s heat. (20:119)
فوسوس اليه الشيطان قال يا ادم هل ادلك عل: 120. Then Shaitân (Satan) whispered to him, saying: "O Adam! Shall I lead you to the Tree of Eternity and to a kingdom that will never waste away?" (20:120)
فاكلا منها فبدت لهما سواتهما وطفقا يخصفا: 121. Then they both ate of the tree, and so their private parts became manifest to them, and they began to cover themselves with the leaves of the Paradise for their covering. Thus did Adam disobey his Lord, so he went astray. (20:121)
ثم اجتباه ربه فتاب عليه وهدى: 122. Then his Lord chose him, and turned to him with forgiveness, and gave him guidance. (20:122)
قال اهبطا منها جميعا بعضكم لبعض عدو فاما: 123. He (Allâh) said: "Get you down (from the Paradise to the earth), both of you, together, some of you are an enemy to some others. Then if there comes to you guidance from Me, then whoever follows My Guidance he shall neither go astray, nor shall be distressed. (20:123)
ومن اعرض عن ذكري فان له معيشة ضنكا ونحشر: 124. "But whosoever turns away from My Reminder (i.e. neither believes in this Qur’ân nor acts on its teachings.) verily, for him is a life of hardship, and We shall raise him up blind on the Day of Resurrection." (20:124)
قال رب لم حشرتني اعمى وقد كنت بصيرا: 125. He will say: "O my Lord! Why have you raised me up blind, while I had sight (before)." (20:125)
قال كذلك اتتك اياتنا فنسيتها وكذلك اليوم: 126. (Allâh) will say: "Like this: Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) came unto you, but you disregarded them (i.e. you left them, did not think deeply in them, and you turned away from them), and so this Day, you will be neglected (in the Hell-fire, away from Allâh’s Mercy)." (20:126)
وكذلك نجزي من اسرف ولم يؤمن بايات ربه ول: 127. And thus do We requite him who transgresses beyond bounds [i.e. commits the great sins and disobeys his Lord (Allâh) and believes not in His Messengers, and His revealed Books, like this Qur’an], and believes not in the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of his Lord; and the torment of the Hereafter is far more severe and more lasting. (20:127)
افلم يهد لهم كم اهلكنا قبلهم من القرون ي: 128. Is it not a guidance for them (to know) how many generations We have destroyed before them, in whose dwellings they walk? Verily, in this are signs indeed for men of understanding. (20:128)
ولولا كلمة سبقت من ربك لكان لزاما واجل م: 129. And had it not been for a Word that went forth before from your Lord, and a term determined, (their punishment) must necessarily have come (in this world). (20:129)
فاصبر على ما يقولون وسبح بحمد ربك قبل طل: 130. So bear patiently (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) what they say, and glorify the praises of your Lord before the rising of the sun, and before its setting, and during some hours of the night, and at the ends of the day (an indication for the five compulsory congregational prayers), that you may become pleased (with the reward which Allâh shall give you). (20:130)
ولا تمدن عينيك الى ما متعنا به ازواجا من: 131. And strain not your eyes in longing for the things We have given for enjoyment to various groups of them (polytheists and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh), the splendour of the life of this world, that We may test them thereby. But the provision (good reward in the Hereafter) of your Lord is better and more lasting. (20:131)
وامر اهلك بالصلاة واصطبر عليها لا نسالك: 132. And enjoin As-Salât (the prayer) on your family, and be patient in offering them [i.e. the Salât (prayers)]. We ask not of you a provision (i.e. to give Us something: money): We provide for you. And the good end (i.e. Paradise) is for the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2). (20:132)
وقالوا لولا ياتينا باية من ربه اولم تاته: 133. They say: "Why does he not bring us a sign (proof) from his Lord?" Has there not come to them the proof of that which is (written) in the former papers [Scriptures, i.e. the Taurât (Torah), and the Injeel (Gospel), about the coming of the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم]? (20:133)
ولو انا اهلكناهم بعذاب من قبله لقالوا رب: 134. And if We had destroyed them with a torment before this (i.e. Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم and the Qur’ân), they would surely have said: "Our Lord! If only You had sent us a Messenger, we should certainly have followed Your Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), before we were humiliated and disgraced."[7] (20:134)
قل كل متربص فتربصوا فستعلمون من اصحاب ال: 135. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Each one (believer and disbeliever.) is waiting, so wait you too; and you shall know who are they that are on the Straight and Even Path (i.e. Allâh’s religion of Islâmic Monotheism), and who are they that have let themselves be guided (on the Right Path). (20:135)
اقترب للناس حسابهم وهم في غفلة معرضون: 1. Draws near for mankind their reckoning, while they turn away in heedlessness. (21:1)
ما ياتيهم من ذكر من ربهم محدث الا استمعو: 2. Comes not unto them an admonition (a chapter of the Qur’ân) from their Lord as a recent revelation but they listen to it while they play (21:2)
لاهية قلوبهم واسروا النجوى الذين ظلموا ه: 3. With their hearts occupied (with evil things). Those who do wrong, conceal their private counsels, (saying): "Is this (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) more than a human being like you? Will you submit to magic while you see it?" (21:3)
قال ربي يعلم القول في السماء والارض وهو: 4. He (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) said: "My Lord knows (every) word (spoken) in the heavens and on earth. And He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower." (21:4)
بل قالوا اضغاث احلام بل افتراه بل هو شاع: 5. Nay, they say: "These (revelations of the Qur’ân which are revealed to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are mixed up false dreams! Nay, he has invented them! - Nay, he is a poet! Let him then bring us an Ayâh (sign as a proof) like the ones (Prophets) the former were sent (with)!" (21:5)
ما امنت قبلهم من قرية اهلكناها افهم يؤمن: 6. Not one of the towns (populations), of those which We destroyed, believed before them (though We sent them signs): will they then believe? (21:6)
وما ارسلنا قبلك الا رجالا نوحي اليهم فاس: 7. And We sent not before you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) but men to whom We revealed. So ask the people of the Reminder [Scriptures - the Taurât (Torah), the Injeel (Gospel)] if you do not know. (21:7)
وما جعلناهم جسدا لا ياكلون الطعام وما كا: 8. And We did not create them (the Messengers, with) bodies that ate not food, nor were they immortals. (21:8)
ثم صدقناهم الوعد فانجيناهم ومن نشاء واهل: 9. Then We fulfilled to them the promise. So We saved them and those whom We willed, but We destroyed Al-Musrifûn (i.e. disbelievers in Allah, in His Messengers, extravagants, transgressors of Allâh’s limits by committing crimes, oppressions, polytheism and sins). (21:9)
لقد انزلنا اليكم كتابا فيه ذكركم افلا تع: 10. Indeed, We have sent down for you (O mankind) a Book (the Qur’ân) in which there is Dhikrukum,(your Reminder or an honour for you i.e. honour for the one who follows the teaching of the Qur’ân and acts on its teachings). Will you not then understand? (21:10)
وكم قصمنا من قرية كانت ظالمة وانشانا بعد: 11. How many a town (community) given to wrong-doing, have We destroyed, and raised up after them another people! (21:11)
فلما احسوا باسنا اذا هم منها يركضون: 12. Then, when they perceived (saw) Our Torment (coming), behold, they (tried to) flee from it. (21:12)
لا تركضوا وارجعوا الى ما اترفتم فيه ومسا: 13. Flee not, but return to that wherein you lived a luxurious life, and to your homes, in order that you may be questioned. (21:13)
قالوا يا ويلنا انا كنا ظالمين: 14. They cried: "Woe to us! Certainly we have been Zâlimûn (polytheists, wrong-doers and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh)." (21:14)
فما زالت تلك دعواهم حتى جعلناهم حصيدا خا: 15. And that cry of theirs ceased not, till We made them as a field that is reaped, extinct (dead). (21:15)
وما خلقنا السماء والارض وما بينهما لاعبي: 16. We created not the heavens and the earth and all that is between them for a (mere) play.[1] (21:16)
لو اردنا ان نتخذ لهوا لاتخذناه من لدنا ا: 17. Had We intended to take a pastime (i.e. a wife or a son), We could surely have taken it from Us, if We were going to do (that). (21:17)
بل نقذف بالحق على الباطل فيدمغه فاذا هو: 18. Nay, We fling (send down) the truth (this Qur’ân) against the falsehood (disbelief), so it destroys it, and behold, it (falsehood) is vanished. And woe to you for that (lie) which you ascribe (to Allâh by uttering that Allâh has a wife and a son). (21:18)
وله من في السماوات والارض ومن عنده لا يس: 19. To Him belongs whosoever is in the heavens and on earth. And those who are near Him (i.e. the angels) are not too proud to worship Him, nor are they weary (of His worship). (21:19)
يسبحون الليل والنهار لا يفترون: 20. They (i.e. the angels) glorify His Praises night and day, (and) they never slacken (to do so). (21:20)
ام اتخذوا الهة من الارض هم ينشرون: 21. Or have they taken (for worship) âlihah (gods) from the earth who raise the dead? (21:21)
لو كان فيهما الهة الا الله لفسدتا فسبحان: 22. Had there been therein (in the heavens and the earth) âlihah (gods) besides Allâh, then verily both would have been ruined. Glorified be Allâh, the Lord of the Throne, (High is He) above all that (evil) they associate with Him! (21:22)
لا يسال عما يفعل وهم يسالون: 23. He cannot be questioned as to what He does, while they will be questioned. (21:23)
ام اتخذوا من دونه الهة قل هاتوا برهانكم: 24. Or have they taken for worship (other) âlihah (gods) besides Him? Say: "Bring your proof." This (the Qur’ân) is the Reminder for those with me and the Reminder for those before me. But most of them know not the Truth, so they are averse. (21:24)
وما ارسلنا من قبلك من رسول الا نوحي اليه: 25. And We did not send any Messenger before you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) but We revealed to him (saying): Lâ ilâha illa Ana [none has the right to be worshipped but I (Allâh)], so worship Me (Alone and none else)." (21:25)
وقالوا اتخذ الرحمن ولدا سبحانه بل عباد م: 26. And they say: "The Most Gracious (Allâh) has begotten a son (or children)." Glory to Him! They [whom they call children of Allâh i.e. the angels, ‘Îsâ (Jesus) - son of Maryam (Mary), ‘Uzair (Ezra)], are but honoured slaves. (21:26)
لا يسبقونه بالقول وهم بامره يعملون: 27. They speak not until He has spoken, and they act on His Command. (21:27)
يعلم ما بين ايديهم وما خلفهم ولا يشفعون: 28. He knows what is before them, and what is behind them, and they cannot intercede except for him with whom He is pleased. And they stand in awe for fear of Him. (21:28)
ومن يقل منهم اني اله من دونه فذلك نجزيه: 29. And if any of them should say: "Verily, I am an ilâh (a god) besides Him (Allâh)," such a one We should recompense with Hell. Thus We recompense the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers). (21:29)
اولم ير الذين كفروا ان السماوات والارض ك: 30. Have not those who disbelieve known that the heavens and the earth were joined together as one united piece, then We parted them? And We have made from water every living thing. Will they not then believe? (21:30)
وجعلنا في الارض رواسي ان تميد بهم وجعلنا: 31. And We have placed on the earth firm mountains, lest it should shake with them, and We placed therein broad highways for them to pass through, that they may be guided. (21:31)
وجعلنا السماء سقفا محفوظا وهم عن اياتها: 32. And We have made the heaven a roof, safe and well guarded. Yet they turn away from its signs (i.e. sun, moon, winds, clouds, etc.). (21:32)
وهو الذي خلق الليل والنهار والشمس والقمر: 33. And He it is Who has created the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, each in an orbit floating. (21:33)
وما جعلنا لبشر من قبلك الخلد افان مت فهم: 34. And We granted not to any human being immortality before you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): then if you die, would they live forever? (21:34)
كل نفس ذائقة الموت ونبلوكم بالشر والخير: 35. Everyone is going to taste death, and We shall make a trial of you with evil and with good. And to Us you will be returned. (21:35)
واذا راك الذين كفروا ان يتخذونك الا هزوا: 36. And when those who disbelieved (in the Oneness of Allâh) see you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), they take you not except for mockery (saying): "Is this the one who talks (badly) about your gods?" While they disbelieve at the mention of the Most Gracious (Allâh). [Tafsir Al-Qurtubî]. (21:36)
خلق الانسان من عجل ساريكم اياتي فلا تستع: 37. Man is created of haste. I will show you My Ayât (torments, proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). So ask Me not to hasten (them). (21:37)
لو يعلم الذين كفروا حين لا يكفون عن وجوه: 39. If only those who disbelieved knew (the time) when they will not be able to ward off the Fire from their faces, nor from their backs, and they will not be helped. (21:39)
بل تاتيهم بغتة فتبهتهم فلا يستطيعون ردها: 40. Nay, it (the Fire or the Day of Resurrection) will come upon them all of a sudden and will perplex them, and they will have no power to avert it nor will they get respite. (21:40)
قل من يكلؤكم بالليل والنهار من الرحمن بل: 42. Say: "Who can guard and protect you in the night or in the day from the (punishment of the) Most Gracious (Allâh)?" Nay, but they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord. (21:42)
ام لهم الهة تمنعهم من دوننا لا يستطيعون: 43. Or have they âlihah (gods) who can guard them from Us? They have no power to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us (i.e. from Our Torment). (21:43)
بل متعنا هؤلاء واباءهم حتى طال عليهم الع: 44. Nay, We gave the luxuries of this life to these men and their fathers until the period grew long for them. See they not that We gradually reduce the land (in their control) from its outlying borders? Is it then they who will overcome?[2] (21:44)
قل انما انذركم بالوحي ولا يسمع الصم الدع: 45. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I warn you only by the revelation (from Allâh and not by the opinion of the religious scholars and others). But the deaf (who follow the religious scholars and others blindly) will not hear the call, (even) when they are warned [i.e. one should follow only the Qur’ân and the Sunnah (legal ways, orders, acts of worship, and the statements of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم, as the companions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم did)][3]. (21:45)
ولئن مستهم نفحة من عذاب ربك ليقولن يا وي: 46. And if a breath (minor calamity) of the Torment of your Lord touches them, they will surely cry: "Woe unto us! Indeed we have been Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers)." (21:46)
ونضع الموازين القسط ليوم القيامة فلا تظل: 47. And We shall set up balances of justice on the Day of Resurrection, then none will be dealt with unjustly in anything. And if there be the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it. And Sufficient are We to take account. (21:47)
ولقد اتينا موسى وهارون الفرقان وضياء وذك: 48. And indeed We granted to Mûsâ (Moses) and Hârûn (Aaron) the criterion (of right and wrong), and a shining light [i.e. the Taurât (Torah)] and a Reminder for Al-Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2). (21:48)
الذين يخشون ربهم بالغيب وهم من الساعة مش: 49. Those who fear their Lord without seeing Him, and they are afraid of the Hour. (21:49)
وهذا ذكر مبارك انزلناه افانتم له منكرون: 50. And this is a blessed Reminder (the Qur’ân) which We have sent down: will you then (dare to) deny it?[4] (21:50)
ولقد اتينا ابراهيم رشده من قبل وكنا به ع: 51. And indeed We bestowed aforetime on Ibrâhîm (Abraham) his (portion of) guidance, and We were Well-Acquainted with him (as to his Belief in the Oneness of Allâh). (21:51)
اذ قال لابيه وقومه ما هذه التماثيل التي: 52. When he said to his father and his people: "What are these images, to which you are devoted?" (21:52)
قالوا وجدنا اباءنا لها عابدين: 53. They said: "We found our fathers worshipping them." (21:53)
قال لقد كنتم انتم واباؤكم في ضلال مبين: 54. He said: "Indeed, you and your fathers have been in manifest error." (21:54)
قالوا اجئتنا بالحق ام انت من اللاعبين: 55. They said: "Have you brought us the truth, or are you one of those who play about?" (21:55)
قال بل ربكم رب السماوات والارض الذي فطره: 56. He said: "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them and to that I am one of the witnesses. (21:56)
وتالله لاكيدن اصنامكم بعد ان تولوا مدبري: 57. "And by Allâh, I shall plot a plan (to destroy) your idols after you have gone away and turned your backs." (21:57)
فجعلهم جذاذا الا كبيرا لهم لعلهم اليه ير: 58. So he broke them to pieces, (all) except the biggest of them, that they might turn to it. (21:58)
قالوا من فعل هذا بالهتنا انه لمن الظالمي: 59. They said: "Who has done this to our âlihah (gods)? He must indeed be one of the Zâlimun (wrong-doers)." (21:59)
قالوا سمعنا فتى يذكرهم يقال له ابراهيم: 60. They said: "We heard a young man talking against them, who is called Ibrâhîm (Abraham)." (21:60)
قالوا فاتوا به على اعين الناس لعلهم يشهد: 61. They said: "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may testify." (21:61)
قالوا اانت فعلت هذا بالهتنا يا ابراهيم: 62. They said: "Are you the one who has done this to our gods, O Ibrâhîm (Abraham)?" (21:62)
قال بل فعله كبيرهم هذا فاسالوهم ان كانوا: 63. [Ibrâhîm (Abraham)] said: "Nay, this one, the biggest of them (idols) did it. Ask them, if they can speak!"[5] (21:63)
فرجعوا الى انفسهم فقالوا انكم انتم الظال: 64. So they turned to themselves and said: "Verily, you are the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers)." (21:64)
ثم نكسوا على رءوسهم لقد علمت ما هؤلاء ين: 65. Then they turned to themselves (their first thought and said): "Indeed you [Ibrâhîm (Abraham)] know well that these (idols) speak not!" (21:65)
قال افتعبدون من دون الله ما لا ينفعكم شي: 66. [Ibrâhîm (Abraham)] said: "Do you then worship besides Allâh, things that can neither profit you, nor harm you? (21:66)
اف لكم ولما تعبدون من دون الله افلا تعقل: 67. "Fie upon you, and upon that which you worship besides Allâh! Have you then no sense?" (21:67)
قالوا حرقوه وانصروا الهتكم ان كنتم فاعلي: 68. They said: "Burn him and help your âlihah (gods), if you will be doing." (21:68)
قلنا يا نار كوني بردا وسلاما على ابراهيم: 69. We (Allâh) said: "O fire! Be you coolness and safety for Ibrâhîm (Abraham)!" (21:69)
