And those who expect not a Meeting with Us (i.e. those who deny the Day of Resurrection and the life of the Hereafter) said: "Why are not the angels sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?" Indeed they think too highly of themselves, and are scornful with great pride.[2] (25:21)
يوم يرون الملائكة لا بشرى يومئذ للمجرمين: 22. On the Day they will see the angels - no glad tidings will there be for the Mujrimûn (criminals, disbelievers, polytheists, sinners) that day. And they (angels) will say: "All kinds of glad tidings are forbidden to you," [none will be allowed to enter Paradise except the one who said: Lâ ilâha illallâh, "(none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh) and acted practically on its legal orders and obligations. See the footnote of V.2:193)]. (25:22)
وقدمنا الى ما عملوا من عمل فجعلناه هباء: 23. And We shall turn to whatever deeds they (disbelievers, polytheists, sinners) did, and We shall make such deeds as scattered floating particles of dust. (25:23)
اصحاب الجنة يومئذ خير مستقرا واحسن مقيلا: 24. The dwellers of Paradise (i.e. those who deserved it through their Islamic Monotheistic Faith and their deeds of righteousness) will, on that Day, have the best abode, and have the fairest of places for repose. (25:24)
ويوم تشقق السماء بالغمام ونزل الملائكة ت: 25. And (remember) the Day when the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and the angels will be sent down, with a grand descending. (25:25)
الملك يومئذ الحق للرحمن وكان يوما على ال: 26. The sovereignty on that Day will be the true (sovereignty), belonging to the Most Gracious (Allâh), and it will be a hard Day for the disbelievers (those who disbelieve in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism). (25:26)
ويوم يعض الظالم على يديه يقول يا ليتني ا: 27. And (remember) the Day when the Zâlim (wrong-doer, oppressor, polytheist) will bite at his hands, he will say: "Oh! Would that I had taken a path with the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم).[3] (25:27)
يا ويلتى ليتني لم اتخذ فلانا خليلا: 28. "Ah! Woe to me! Would that I had never taken so-and-so as a Khalîl (an intimate friend)! (25:28)
لقد اضلني عن الذكر بعد اذ جاءني وكان الش: 29. "He indeed led me astray from the Reminder (this Qur’ân) after it had come to me. And Shaitân (Satan) is to man ever a deserter in the hour of need." [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi] (25:29)
وقال الرسول يا رب ان قومي اتخذوا هذا الق: 30. And the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) will say: "O my Lord! Verily, my people deserted this Qur’ân (neither listened to it, nor acted on its laws and teachings). (25:30)
وكذلك جعلنا لكل نبي عدوا من المجرمين وكف: 31. Thus have We made for every Prophet an enemy among the Mujrimûn (disbelievers, polytheists, criminals). But Sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper. (25:31)
وقال الذين كفروا لولا نزل عليه القران جم: 32. And those who disbelieve say: "Why is not the Qur’ân revealed to him all at once?" Thus (it is sent down in parts), that We may strengthen your heart thereby. And We have revealed it to you gradually, in stages. (It was revealed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم in 23 years.). (25:32)
ولا ياتونك بمثل الا جئناك بالحق واحسن تف: 33. And no example or similitude do they bring (to oppose or to find fault in you or in this Qur’ân), but We reveal to you the truth (against that similitude or example), and the better explanation thereof. (25:33)
الذين يحشرون على وجوههم الى جهنم اولئك ش: 34. Those who will be gathered to Hell (prone) on their faces, such will be in an evil state, and most astray from the (Straight) Path.[4] (25:34)
ولقد اتينا موسى الكتاب وجعلنا معه اخاه ه: 35. And indeed We gave Mûsâ (Moses) the Scripture [the Taurât (Torah)], and placed his brother Hârûn (Aaron) with him as a helper; (25:35)
فقلنا اذهبا الى القوم الذين كذبوا باياتن: 36. And We said: "Go you both to the people who have denied Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.)." Then We destroyed them with utter destruction. (25:36)
وقوم نوح لما كذبوا الرسل اغرقناهم وجعلنا: 37. And Nûh’s (Noah) people, when they denied the Messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers). (25:37)
وعادا وثمود واصحاب الرس وقرونا بين ذلك ك: 38. And (also) ‘Âd and Thamûd, and the Dwellers of Ar-Rass, and many generations in between. (25:38)
وكلا ضربنا له الامثال وكلا تبرنا تتبيرا: 39. And for each (of them) We put forward examples (as proofs and lessons), and each (of them) We brought to utter ruin (because of their disbelief and evil deeds). (25:39)
ولقد اتوا على القرية التي امطرت مطر السو: 40. And indeed they have passed by the town [of Prophet Lût (Lot)] on which was rained the evil rain. Did they (disbelievers) not then see it (with their own eyes)? Nay! But they used not to expect any resurrection. (25:40)
واذا راوك ان يتخذونك الا هزوا اهذا الذي: 41. And when they see you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), they treat you only in mockery (saying): "Is this the one whom Allâh has sent as a Messenger? (25:41)
ان كاد ليضلنا عن الهتنا لولا ان صبرنا عل: 42. "He would have nearly misled us from our âlihah (gods), had it not been that we were patient and constant in their worship!" And they will know when they see the torment, who it is that is most astray from the (Right) Path! (25:42)
ارايت من اتخذ الهه هواه افانت تكون عليه: 43. Have you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) seen him who has taken as his ilâh (god) his own vain desire? Would you then be a Wakîl (a disposer of his affairs or a watcher) over him? (25:43)
ام تحسب ان اكثرهم يسمعون او يعقلون ان هم: 44. Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like cattle - nay, they are even farther astray from the Path (i.e. even worse than cattle). (25:44)
الم تر الى ربك كيف مد الظل ولو شاء لجعله: 45. Have you not seen how your Lord spread the shadow. If He willed, He could have made it still - But We have made the sun its guide (i.e. after the sunrise, the shadow shrinks and vanishes at midnoon and then again appears in the afternoon with the decline of the sun, and had there been no sunlight, there would have been no shadow). (25:45)
ثم قبضناه الينا قبضا يسيرا: 46. Then We withdraw it to Us a gradual concealed withdrawal. (25:46)
وهو الذي جعل لكم الليل لباسا والنوم سبات: 47. And it is He Who makes the night a covering for you, and the sleep (as) a repose, and makes the day Nushûr (i.e. getting up and going about here and there for daily work, after one’s sleep at night, or like resurrection after one’s death). (25:47)
وهو الذي ارسل الرياح بشرا بين يدي رحمته: 48. And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy (rain); and We send down pure water from the sky. (25:48)
لنحيي به بلدة ميتا ونسقيه مما خلقنا انعا: 49. That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give to drink thereof many of the cattle and men that We had created. (25:49)
ولقد صرفناه بينهم ليذكروا فابى اكثر النا: 50. And indeed We have distributed it (rain or water) amongst them in order that they may remember (the Grace of Allâh,) but most men (refuse to accept the Truth or Faith and) accept nothing but disbelief or ingratitude. (25:50)
ولو شئنا لبعثنا في كل قرية نذيرا: 51. And had We willed, We would have raised a warner in every town. (25:51)
فلا تطع الكافرين وجاهدهم به جهادا كبيرا: 52. So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them (by preaching) with the utmost endeavour with it (the Qur’ân). (25:52)
وهو الذي مرج البحرين هذا عذب فرات وهذا م: 53. And it is He Who has let free the two seas (kinds of water): this is palatable and sweet, and that is salt and bitter; and He has set a barrier and a complete partition between them. (25:53)
وهو الذي خلق من الماء بشرا فجعله نسبا وص: 54. And it is He Who has created man from water, and has appointed for him kindred by blood, and kindred by marriage. And your Lord is Ever All-Powerful to do what He wills. (25:54)
ويعبدون من دون الله ما لا ينفعهم ولا يضر: 55. And they (disbelievers, polytheists) worship besides Allâh that which can neither profit them nor harm them; and the disbeliever is ever a helper (of the Satan) against his Lord. (25:55)
وما ارسلناك الا مبشرا ونذيرا: 56. And We have sent you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) only as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner. (25:56)
قل ما اسالكم عليه من اجر الا من شاء ان ي: 57. Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this (that which I have brought from my Lord and its preaching), save that whosoever wills may take a Path to his Lord. (25:57)
وتوكل على الحي الذي لا يموت وسبح بحمده و: 58. And put your trust (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) in the Ever Living One Who dies not, and glorify His Praises, and Sufficient is He as the All-Knower of the sins of His slaves, (25:58)
الذي خلق السماوات والارض وما بينهما في س: 59. Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days. Then He (Istawâ ) rose over the Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty). The Most Gracious (Allâh)! Ask Him (O Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم , concerning His Qualities: His rising over His Throne, His creations, etc.), as He is Al-Khabîr (The All-Knower of everything i.e. Allâh). (25:59)
واذا قيل لهم اسجدوا للرحمن قالوا وما الر: 60. And when it is said to them: "Prostrate yourselves to the Most Gracious (Allâh)! they say: "And what is the Most Gracious? Shall we fall down in prostration to that which you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) command us?" And it increases in them only aversion. (25:60)
تبارك الذي جعل في السماء بروجا وجعل فيها: 61. Blessed be He Who has placed in the heaven big stars, and has placed therein a great lamp (sun), and a moon giving light. (25:61)
وهو الذي جعل الليل والنهار خلفة لمن اراد: 62. And He it is Who has put the night and the day in succession, for such who desires to remember or desires to show his gratitude. (25:62)
وعباد الرحمن الذين يمشون على الارض هونا: 63. And the (faithful) slaves of the Most Gracious (Allâh) are those who walk on the earth in humility and sedateness, and when the foolish address them (with bad words) they reply back with mild words of gentleness. (25:63)
والذين يبيتون لربهم سجدا وقياما: 64. And those who spend the night in worship of their Lord, prostrate and standing.[5] (25:64)
والذين يقولون ربنا اصرف عنا عذاب جهنم ان: 65. And those who say: "Our Lord! Avert from us the torment of Hell. Verily its torment is ever an inseparable, permanent punishment." (25:65)
انها ساءت مستقرا ومقاما: 66. Evil indeed it (Hell) is as an abode and as a place to rest in. (25:66)
والذين اذا انفقوا لم يسرفوا ولم يقتروا و: 67. And those who, when they spend, are neither extravagant nor niggardly, but hold a medium (way) between those (extremes). (25:67)
والذين لا يدعون مع الله الها اخر ولا يقت: 68. And those who invoke not any other ilâh (god) along with Allâh, nor kill such person as Allâh has forbidden, except for just cause, nor commit illegal sexual intercourse - and whoever does this shall receive the punishment.[6] (25:68)
يضاعف له العذاب يوم القيامة ويخلد فيه مه: 69. The torment will be doubled to him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein in disgrace; (25:69)
الا من تاب وامن وعمل عملا صالحا فاولئك ي: 70. Except those who repent and believe (in Islâmic Monotheism), and do righteous deeds; for those, Allâh will change their sins into good deeds, and Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.[7] (25:70)
ومن تاب وعمل صالحا فانه يتوب الى الله مت: 71. And whosoever repents and does righteous good deeds; then verily, he repents towards Allâh with true repentance. (25:71)
والذين لا يشهدون الزور واذا مروا باللغو: 72. And those who do not bear witness to falsehood, and if they pass by some evil play or evil talk, they pass by it with dignity. (25:72)
والذين اذا ذكروا بايات ربهم لم يخروا علي: 73. And those who, when they are reminded of the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of their Lord, fall not deaf and blind thereat. (25:73)
والذين يقولون ربنا هب لنا من ازواجنا وذر: 74. And those who say: "Our Lord! Bestow on us from our wives and our offspring the comfort of our eyes, and make us leaders of the Muttaqûn(the pious. See V.2:2)." (25:74)
اولئك يجزون الغرفة بما صبروا ويلقون فيها: 75. Those will be rewarded with the highest place (in Paradise) because of their patience. Therein they shall be met with greetings and the word of peace and respect. (25:75)
خالدين فيها حسنت مستقرا ومقاما: 76. Abiding therein - excellent it is as an abode, and as a place to rest in. (25:76)
قل ما يعبا بكم ربي لولا دعاؤكم فقد كذبتم: 77. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to the disbelievers): "My Lord pays attention to you only because of your invocation to Him. But now you have indeed denied (Him). So the torment will be yours for ever (inseparable, permanent punishment)." (25:77)
تلك ايات الكتاب المبين: 2. These are the Verses of the manifest Book [(this Qur’ân), which was promised by Allâh in the Taurât (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel), makes things clear]. (26:2)
لعلك باخع نفسك الا يكونوا مؤمنين: 3. It may be that you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are going to kill yourself with grief, that they do not become believers [in your Risalah (Messengership) i.e. in your Message of Islâmic Monotheism].[1] (26:3)
ان نشا ننزل عليهم من السماء اية فظلت اعن: 4. If We will, We could send down to them from the heaven a sign, to which they would bend their necks in humility. (26:4)
وما ياتيهم من ذكر من الرحمن محدث الا كان: 5. And never comes there unto them a Reminder as a recent revelation from the Most Gracious (Allâh), but they turn away therefrom. (26:5)
فقد كذبوا فسياتيهم انباء ما كانوا به يست: 6. So they have indeed denied (the truth - this Qur’ân), then the news of what they mocked at will come to them. (26:6)
اولم يروا الى الارض كم انبتنا فيها من كل: 7. Do they not observe the earth - how much of every good kind We cause to grow therein? (26:7)
ان في ذلك لاية وما كان اكثرهم مؤمنين: 8. Verily, in this is an Ayâh (proof or sign), yet most of them (polytheists, pagans who do not believe in Resurrection) are not believers. (26:8)
وان ربك لهو العزيز الرحيم: 9. And verily your Lord, He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful. (26:9)
واذ نادى ربك موسى ان ائت القوم الظالمين: 10. And (remember) when your Lord called Mûsâ (Moses) (saying): "Go to the people who are Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers) - (26:10)
قوم فرعون الا يتقون: 11. "The people of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh): Will they not fear Allâh and become righteous?" (26:11)
قال رب اني اخاف ان يكذبون: 12. He said: "My Lord! Verily, I fear that they will belie me, (26:12)
ويضيق صدري ولا ينطلق لساني فارسل الى هار: 13. "And my breast straitens, and my tongue expresses not well. So send for Hârûn (Aaron) (to come along with me). (26:13)
ولهم علي ذنب فاخاف ان يقتلون: 14. "And they have a charge of crime against me, and I fear they will kill me." (26:14)
قال كلا فاذهبا باياتنا انا معكم مستمعون: 15. (Allâh) said: "Nay! Go you both with Our Signs. Verily We shall be with you, listening. (26:15)
فاتيا فرعون فقولا انا رسول رب العالمين: 16. "And go both of you to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), and say: ‘We are the Messengers of the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists), (26:16)
ان ارسل معنا بني اسرائيل: 17. "So allow the Children of Israel to go with us.’ " (26:17)
قال الم نربك فينا وليدا ولبثت فينا من عم: 18. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said [to Mûsâ (Moses)]: "Did we not bring you up among us as a child? And you did dwell many years of your life with us. (26:18)
وفعلت فعلتك التي فعلت وانت من الكافرين: 19. "And you did your deed, which you did (i.e. the crime of killing a man) while you were one of the ingrates." (26:19)
قال فعلتها اذا وانا من الضالين: 20. Mûsâ (Moses) said: "I did it then, when I was ignorant (as regards my Lord and His Message). (26:20)
ففررت منكم لما خفتكم فوهب لي ربي حكما وج: 21. "So I fled from you when I feared you. But my Lord has granted me Hukm (i.e. religious knowledge, right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood), and made me one of the Messengers. (26:21)
وتلك نعمة تمنها علي ان عبدت بني اسرائيل: 22. "And this is the past favour with which you reproach me: that you have enslaved the Children of Israel." (26:22)
قال فرعون وما رب العالمين: 23. Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) said: "And what is the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)?" (26:23)
قال رب السماوات والارض وما بينهما ان كنت: 24. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, if you seek to be convinced with certainty." (26:24)
قال لمن حوله الا تستمعون: 25. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said to those around: "Do you not hear (what he says)?" (26:25)
قال ربكم ورب ابائكم الاولين: 26. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Your Lord and the Lord of your ancient fathers!" (26:26)
قال ان رسولكم الذي ارسل اليكم لمجنون: 27. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "Verily, your Messenger who has been sent to you is a madman!" (26:27)
قال رب المشرق والمغرب وما بينهما ان كنتم: 28. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Lord of the east and the west, and all that is between them, if you did but understand!" (26:28)
قال لئن اتخذت الها غيري لاجعلنك من المسج: 29. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "If you choose an ilâh (god) other than me, I will certainly put you among the prisoners." (26:29)
قال اولو جئتك بشيء مبين: 30. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Even if I bring you something manifest (and convincing)?" (26:30)
قال فات به ان كنت من الصادقين: 31. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "Bring it forth then, if you are of the truthful!" (26:31)
قال للملا حوله ان هذا لساحر عليم: 34. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said to the chiefs around him: "Verily! This is indeed a well-versed sorcerer. (26:34)
يريد ان يخرجكم من ارضكم بسحره فماذا تامر: 35. "He wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery: what is it then that you command?" (26:35)
قالوا ارجه واخاه وابعث في المدائن حاشرين: 36. They said: "Put him off and his brother (for a while), and send callers to the cities; (26:36)
ياتوك بكل سحار عليم: 37. "To bring up to you every well-versed sorcerer." (26:37)
فجمع السحرة لميقات يوم معلوم: 38. So the sorcerers were assembled at a fixed time on a day appointed. (26:38)
وقيل للناس هل انتم مجتمعون: 39. And it was said to the people: "Are you (too) going to assemble? (26:39)
لعلنا نتبع السحرة ان كانوا هم الغالبين: 40. "That we may follow the sorcerers [who were on Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh) religion of disbelief] if they are the winners." (26:40)
فلما جاء السحرة قالوا لفرعون ائن لنا لاج: 41. So when the sorcerers arrived, they said to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh): "Will there surely be a reward for us if we are the winners?" (26:41)
قال نعم وانكم اذا لمن المقربين: 42. He said: "Yes, and you shall then verily be of those brought near (to myself)." (26:42)
قال لهم موسى القوا ما انتم ملقون: 43. Mûsâ (Moses) said to them: "Throw what you are going to throw!" (26:43)
فالقوا حبالهم وعصيهم وقالوا بعزة فرعون ا: 44. So they threw their ropes and their sticks, and said: "By the might of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), it is we who will certainly win!" (26:44)
فالقى موسى عصاه فاذا هي تلقف ما يافكون: 45. Then Mûsâ (Moses) threw his stick, and behold, it swallowed up all that they falsely showed! (26:45)
فالقي السحرة ساجدين: 46. And the sorcerers fell down prostrate. (26:46)
قال امنتم له قبل ان اذن لكم انه لكبيركم: 49. [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] said: "You have believed in him before I give you leave. Surely, he indeed is your chief, who has taught you magic! So verily, you shall come to know. Verily, I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you all." (26:49)
قالوا لا ضير انا الى ربنا منقلبون: 50. They said: "No harm! Surely, to our Lord (Allâh) we are to return. (26:50)
انا نطمع ان يغفر لنا ربنا خطايانا ان كنا: 51. "Verily we really hope that our Lord will forgive us our sins, as we are the first of the believers [in Mûsâ (Moses) and in the Monotheism which he has brought from Allâh]." (26:51)
واوحينا الى موسى ان اسر بعبادي انكم متبع: 52. And We revealed to Mûsâ (Moses), saying: "Depart by night with My slaves, verily you will be pursued." (26:52)
فارسل فرعون في المدائن حاشرين: 53. Then Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) sent callers to (all) the cities. (26:53)
ان هؤلاء لشرذمة قليلون: 54. (Saying): "Verily these indeed are but a small band. (26:54)
وانهم لنا لغائظون: 55. "And verily, they have done what has enraged us. (26:55)
وانا لجميع حاذرون: 56. "But we are a host all assembled, amply fore-warned." (26:56)
فاخرجناهم من جنات وعيون: 57. So, We expelled them from gardens and springs, (26:57)
وكنوز ومقام كريم: 58. Treasures, and every kind of honourable place. (26:58)
كذلك واورثناها بني اسرائيل: 59. Thus [We turned them (Pharaoh’s people) out] and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit them. (26:59)
فاتبعوهم مشرقين: 60. So they pursued them at sunrise. (26:60)
فلما تراءى الجمعان قال اصحاب موسى انا لم: 61. And when the two hosts saw each other, the companions of Mûsâ (Moses) said: "We are sure to be overtaken." (26:61)
قال كلا ان معي ربي سيهدين: 62. [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "Nay, verily with me is my Lord. He will guide me." (26:62)
فاوحينا الى موسى ان اضرب بعصاك البحر فان: 63. Then We revealed to Mûsâ (Moses) (saying): "Strike the sea with your stick." And it parted, and each separate part (of that sea water) became like huge mountain. (26:63)
وازلفنا ثم الاخرين: 64. Then We brought near the others [Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh) party] to that place. (26:64)
وانجينا موسى ومن معه اجمعين: 65. And We saved Mûsâ (Moses) and all those with him. (26:65)
واتل عليهم نبا ابراهيم: 69. And recite to them the story of Ibrâhîm (Abraham). (26:69)
اذ قال لابيه وقومه ما تعبدون: 70. When he said to his father and his people: "What do you worship?" (26:70)
قالوا نعبد اصناما فنظل لها عاكفين: 71. They said: "We worship idols, and to them we are ever devoted." (26:71)
قال هل يسمعونكم اذ تدعون: 72. He said: "Do they hear you, when you call on (them)? (26:72)
او ينفعونكم او يضرون: 73. "Or do they benefit you or do they harm (you)?" (26:73)
قالوا بل وجدنا اباءنا كذلك يفعلون: 74. They said: "(Nay) but we found our fathers doing so." (26:74)
قال افرايتم ما كنتم تعبدون: 75. He said: "Do you observe that which you have been worshipping - (26:75)
فانهم عدو لي الا رب العالمين: 77. "Verily they are enemies to me, save the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists), (26:77)
الذي خلقني فهو يهدين: 78. "Who has created me, and it is He Who guides me. (26:78)
والذي هو يطعمني ويسقين: 79. "And it is He Who feeds me and gives me to drink. (26:79)
واذا مرضت فهو يشفين: 80. "And when I am ill, it is He who cures me. (26:80)
والذي يميتني ثم يحيين: 81. "And Who will cause me to die, and then will bring me to life (again). (26:81)
والذي اطمع ان يغفر لي خطيئتي يوم الدين: 82. "And Who, I hope, will forgive me my faults on the Day of Recompense, (the Day of Resurrection). (26:82)
رب هب لي حكما والحقني بالصالحين: 83. My Lord! Bestow Hukm (religious knowledge, right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood) on me, and join me with the righteous. (26:83)
واجعل لي لسان صدق في الاخرين: 84. And grant me an honourable mention in later generations. (26:84)
واجعلني من ورثة جنة النعيم: 85. And make me one of the inheritors of the Paradise of Delight. (26:85)
واغفر لابي انه كان من الضالين: 86. And forgive my father, verily he is of the erring. (26:86)
ولا تخزني يوم يبعثون: 87. And disgrace me not on the Day when (all the creatures) will be resurrected. (26:87)
يوم لا ينفع مال ولا بنون: 88. The Day whereon neither wealth nor sons will avail, (26:88)
الا من اتى الله بقلب سليم: 89. Except him who brings to Allâh a clean heart [clean from Shirk (polytheism) and Nifâq (hypocrisy)]." (26:89)
وازلفت الجنة للمتقين: 90. And Paradise will be brought near to the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2). (26:90)
وبرزت الجحيم للغاوين: 91. And the (Hell) Fire will be placed in full view of the erring. (26:91)
وقيل لهم اين ما كنتم تعبدون: 92. And it will be said to them: "Where are those (the false gods whom you used to set up as rivals with Allâh) that you used to worship. (26:92)
من دون الله هل ينصرونكم او ينتصرون: 93. "Instead of Allâh? Can they help you or (even) help themselves?" (26:93)
فكبكبوا فيها هم والغاوون: 94. Then they will be thrown on their faces into the (Fire), they and the Ghâwûn (devils, and those who were in error). (26:94)
وجنود ابليس اجمعون: 95. And the whole hosts of Iblîs (Satan) together. (26:95)
قالوا وهم فيها يختصمون: 96. They will say while contending therein, (26:96)
تالله ان كنا لفي ضلال مبين: 97. By Allâh, we were truly in a manifest error, (26:97)
اذ نسويكم برب العالمين: 98. When We held you (false gods) as equals (in worship) with the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists); (26:98)
وما اضلنا الا المجرمون: 99. And none has brought us into error except the Mujrimûn [Iblîs (Satan) and those of human beings who commit crimes, murderers, polytheists, oppressors]. (26:99)
ولا صديق حميم: 101. Nor a close friend (to help us). (26:101)
فلو ان لنا كرة فنكون من المؤمنين: 102. (Alas!) If we only had a chance to return (to the world), we shall truly be among the believers! (26:102)
كذبت قوم نوح المرسلين: 105. The people of Nûh (Noah) belied the Messengers. (26:105)
اذ قال لهم اخوهم نوح الا تتقون: 106. When their brother Nûh (Noah) said to them: "Will you not fear Allâh and obey Him? (26:106)
اني لكم رسول امين: 107. "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you. (26:107)
فاتقوا الله واطيعون: 108. "So fear Allâh, keep your duty to Him, and obey me. (26:108)
وما اسالكم عليه من اجر ان اجري الا على ر: 109. "No reward do I ask of you for it (my Message of Islâmic Monotheism); my reward is only from the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists). (26:109)
قالوا انؤمن لك واتبعك الارذلون: 111. They said: "Shall we believe in you, when the meanest (of the people) follow you?" (26:111)
قال وما علمي بما كانوا يعملون: 112. He said: "And what knowledge have I of what they used to do? (26:112)
ان حسابهم الا على ربي لو تشعرون: 113. "Their account is only with my Lord, if you could (but) know. (26:113)
وما انا بطارد المؤمنين: 114. "And I am not going to drive away the believers. (26:114)
ان انا الا نذير مبين: 115. I am only a plain warner." (26:115)
قالوا لئن لم تنته يا نوح لتكونن من المرج: 116. They said: "If you cease not, O Nûh (Noah) you will surely be among those stoned (to death)." (26:116)
قال رب ان قومي كذبون: 117. He said: "My Lord! Verily, my people have belied me. (26:117)
فافتح بيني وبينهم فتحا ونجني ومن معي من: 118. Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me." (26:118)
فانجيناه ومن معه في الفلك المشحون: 119. And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship. (26:119)
ثم اغرقنا بعد الباقين: 120. Then We drowned the rest (disbelievers) thereafter. (26:120)
اذ قال لهم اخوهم هود الا تتقون: 124. When their brother Hûd said to them: "Will you not fear Allâh and obey Him? (26:124)
اتبنون بكل ريع اية تعبثون: 128. "Do you build high palaces on every high place, while you do not live in them? (26:128)
وتتخذون مصانع لعلكم تخلدون: 129. "And do you get for yourselves palaces (fine buildings) as if you will live therein for ever.[2] (26:129)
واذا بطشتم بطشتم جبارين: 130. "And when you seize (somebody), seize you (him) as tyrants? (26:130)
واتقوا الذي امدكم بما تعلمون: 132. "And keep your duty to Him, fear Him Who has aided you with all (good things) that you know. (26:132)
امدكم بانعام وبنين: 133. "He has aided you with cattle and children. (26:133)
اني اخاف عليكم عذاب يوم عظيم: 135. "Verily, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day." (26:135)
قالوا سواء علينا اوعظت ام لم تكن من الوا: 136. They said: "It is the same to us whether you preach or be not of those who preach. (26:136)
ان هذا الا خلق الاولين: 137. "This is no other than the false tales and religion of the ancients, [Tafsir At-Tabarî] (26:137)
وما نحن بمعذبين: 138. "And we are not going to be punished." (26:138)
فكذبوه فاهلكناهم ان في ذلك لاية وما كان: 139. So they belied him, and We destroyed them. Verily in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers. (26:139)
اذ قال لهم اخوهم صالح الا تتقون: 142. When their brother Sâlih said to them: "Will you not fear Allâh and obey Him? (26:142)
اتتركون في ما هاهنا امنين: 146. "Will you be left secure in that which you have here? (26:146)
وزروع ونخل طلعها هضيم: 148. And green crops (fields) and date-palms with soft spadix. (26:148)
وتنحتون من الجبال بيوتا فارهين: 149. "And you hew out in the mountains, houses with great skill. (26:149)
ولا تطيعوا امر المسرفين: 151. "And follow not the command of Al-Musrifûn [i.e. their chiefs: leaders who were polytheists, criminals and sinners], (26:151)
الذين يفسدون في الارض ولا يصلحون: 152. "Who make mischief in the land, and reform not." (26:152)
قالوا انما انت من المسحرين: 153. They said: "You are only of those bewitched! (26:153)
ما انت الا بشر مثلنا فات باية ان كنت من: 154. "You are but a human being like us. Then bring us a sign if you are of the truthful." (26:154)
قال هذه ناقة لها شرب ولكم شرب يوم معلوم: 155. He said: "Here is a she-camel: it has a right to drink (water), and you have a right to drink (water) (each) on a day, known. (26:155)
ولا تمسوها بسوء فياخذكم عذاب يوم عظيم: 156. "And touch her not with harm, lest the torment of a Great Day should seize you." (26:156)
فعقروها فاصبحوا نادمين: 157. But they killed her, and then they became regretful. (26:157)
فاخذهم العذاب ان في ذلك لاية وما كان اكث: 158. So the torment overtook them. Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers. (26:158)
كذبت قوم لوط المرسلين: 160. The people of Lût (Lot) (-who dwelt in the town of Sodom in Palestine) belied the Messengers. (26:160)
اذ قال لهم اخوهم لوط الا تتقون: 161. When their brother Lût (Lot) said to them: "Will you not fear Allâh and obey Him? (26:161)
اتاتون الذكران من العالمين: 165. "Go you in unto the males of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind), (26:165)
وتذرون ما خلق لكم ربكم من ازواجكم بل انت: 166. "And leave those whom Allâh has created for you to be your wives? Nay, you are a trespassing people!" (26:166)
قالوا لئن لم تنته يا لوط لتكونن من المخر: 167. They said: "If you cease not O Lût (Lot)! verily, you will be one of those who are driven out!" (26:167)
قال اني لعملكم من القالين: 168. He said: "I am, indeed, of those who disapprove with severe anger and fury your (this evil) action (of sodomy). (26:168)
رب نجني واهلي مما يعملون: 169. "My Lord! Save me and my family from what they do." (26:169)
فنجيناه واهله اجمعين: 170. So We saved him and his family, all, (26:170)
الا عجوزا في الغابرين: 171. Except an old woman (his wife) among those who remained behind. (26:171)
ثم دمرنا الاخرين: 172. Then afterward We destroyed the others. (26:172)
وامطرنا عليهم مطرا فساء مطر المنذرين: 173. And We rained on them a rain (of torment). And how evil was the rain of those who had been warned! (26:173)
كذب اصحاب الايكة المرسلين: 176. The dwellers of Al-Aikah [near Madyan (Midian)] belied the Messengers. (26:176)
اذ قال لهم شعيب الا تتقون: 177. When Shu‘aib said to them: "Will you not fear Allâh (and obey Him)? (26:177)
اوفوا الكيل ولا تكونوا من المخسرين: 181. "Give full measure, and cause no loss (to others). (26:181)
وزنوا بالقسطاس المستقيم: 182. "And weigh with the true and straight balance. (26:182)
ولا تبخسوا الناس اشياءهم ولا تعثوا في ال: 183. "And defraud not people by reducing their things, nor do evil, making corruption and mischief in the land. (26:183)
واتقوا الذي خلقكم والجبلة الاولين: 184. "And fear Him Who created you and the generations of the men of old." (26:184)
وما انت الا بشر مثلنا وان نظنك لمن الكاذ: 186. "You are but a human being like us and verily, we think that you are one of the liars! (26:186)
فاسقط علينا كسفا من السماء ان كنت من الص: 187. "So cause a piece of the heaven to fall on us, if you are of the truthful!" (26:187)
قال ربي اعلم بما تعملون: 188. He said: "My Lord is the Best Knower of what you do." (26:188)
فكذبوه فاخذهم عذاب يوم الظلة انه كان عذا: 189. But they belied him, so the torment of the day of shadow (a gloomy cloud) seized them. Indeed that was the torment of a Great Day. (26:189)
وانه لتنزيل رب العالمين: 192. And truly, this (the Qur’ân) is a revelation from the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists), (26:192)
نزل به الروح الامين: 193. Which the trustworthy Rûh [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] has brought down (26:193)
على قلبك لتكون من المنذرين: 194. Upon your heart (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) that you may be (one) of the warners, (26:194)
وانه لفي زبر الاولين: 196. And verily, it (the Qur’ân, and its revelation to Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is (announced) in the Scriptures [i.e. the Taurât (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] of former people. (26:196)
اولم يكن لهم اية ان يعلمه علماء بني اسرا: 197. Is it not a sign to them that the learned scholars (like ‘Abdullâh bin Salâm رضي الله عنه who embraced Islâm)[3] of the Children of Israel knew it (as true)? (26:197)
ولو نزلناه على بعض الاعجمين: 198. And if We had revealed it (this Qur’ân) unto any of the non-Arabs, (26:198)
فقراه عليهم ما كانوا به مؤمنين: 199. And he had recited it unto them, they would not have believed in it. (26:199)
كذلك سلكناه في قلوب المجرمين: 200. Thus have We caused it (the denial of the Qur’ân) to enter the hearts of the Mûjrimûn (criminals, polytheists, sinners). (26:200)
لا يؤمنون به حتى يروا العذاب الاليم: 201. They will not believe in it until they see the painful torment. (26:201)
فياتيهم بغتة وهم لا يشعرون: 202. It shall come to them of a sudden, while they perceive it not. (26:202)
فيقولوا هل نحن منظرون: 203. Then they will say: "Can we be respited?" (26:203)
افبعذابنا يستعجلون: 204. Would they then wish for Our Torment to be hastened on? (26:204)
افرايت ان متعناهم سنين: 205. Tell Me, (even) if We do let them enjoy for years, (26:205)
ثم جاءهم ما كانوا يوعدون: 206. And afterwards comes to them that (punishment) which they had been promised, (26:206)
ما اغنى عنهم ما كانوا يمتعون: 207. All that with which they used to enjoy shall not avail them. (26:207)
وما اهلكنا من قرية الا لها منذرون: 208. And never did We destroy a township but it had its warners (26:208)
ذكرى وما كنا ظالمين: 209. By way of reminder, and We have never been unjust. (26:209)
وما تنزلت به الشياطين: 210. And it is not the Shayâtîn (devils) who have brought it (this Qur’ân) down. (26:210)
وما ينبغي لهم وما يستطيعون: 211. Neither would it suit them, nor they can (produce it). (26:211)
انهم عن السمع لمعزولون: 212. Verily, they have been removed far from hearing it. (26:212)
فلا تدع مع الله الها اخر فتكون من المعذب: 213. So invoke not with Allâh another ilâh (god) lest you should be among those who receive punishment. (26:213)
وانذر عشيرتك الاقربين: 214. And warn your tribe (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) of near kindred.[4] (26:214)
واخفض جناحك لمن اتبعك من المؤمنين: 215. And be kind and humble to the believers who follow you. (26:215)
فان عصوك فقل اني بريء مما تعملون: 216. Then if they disobey you, say: "I am innocent of what you do." (26:216)
وتوكل على العزيز الرحيم: 217. And put your trust in the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful, (26:217)
الذي يراك حين تقوم: 218. Who sees you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) when you stand up (alone at night for Tahajjud prayers). (26:218)
وتقلبك في الساجدين: 219. And your movements among those who fall prostrate (to Allâh in the five compulsory congregational prayers). (26:219)
انه هو السميع العليم: 220. Verily He, only He, is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower. (26:220)
هل انبئكم على من تنزل الشياطين: 221. Shall I inform you (O people!) upon whom the Shayâtîn (devils) descend? (26:221)
تنزل على كل افاك اثيم: 222. They descend on every lying, sinful person. (26:222)
يلقون السمع واكثرهم كاذبون: 223. Who gives ear (to the devils and they pour what they may have heard of the Unseen from the angels), and most of them are liars. (26:223)
والشعراء يتبعهم الغاوون: 224. As for the poets, the erring ones follow them, (26:224)
الم تر انهم في كل واد يهيمون: 225. See you not that they speak about every subject (praising people - right or wrong) in their poetry? (26:225)
وانهم يقولون ما لا يفعلون: 226. And that they say what they do not do. (26:226)
الا الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات وذكروا ا: 227. Except those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous deeds, and remember Allâh much, and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged [by replying back in poetry to the unjust poetry (which the pagan poets utter against the Muslims)]. And those who do wrong will come to know by what overturning they will be overturned. (26:227)
طس تلك ايات القران وكتاب مبين: 1. Tâ-Sîn. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Qur’ân, and none but Allâh (Alone) knows their meanings]. These are the Verses of the Qur’an, and (it is) a Book (that makes things) clear: (27:1)
هدى وبشرى للمؤمنين: 2. A guide (to the Right Path) and glad tidings for the believers [who believe in the Oneness of Allâh (i.e. Islâmic Monotheism)]. (27:2)
الذين يقيمون الصلاة ويؤتون الزكاة وهم با: 3. Those who perform As-Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât) and give Zakât and they believe with certainty in the Hereafter (resurrection, recompense of their good and bad deeds, Paradise and Hell). (27:3)
ان الذين لا يؤمنون بالاخرة زينا لهم اعما: 4. Verily, those who believe not in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds fair-seeming to them, so that they wander about blindly. (27:4)
اولئك الذين لهم سوء العذاب وهم في الاخرة: 5. They are those for whom there will be an evil torment (in this world). And in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers. (27:5)
وانك لتلقى القران من لدن حكيم عليم: 6. And verily, you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are being taught the Qur’ân from One, All-Wise, All-Knowing. (27:6)
اذ قال موسى لاهله اني انست نارا ساتيكم م: 7. (Remember) when Mûsâ (Moses) said to his household: "Verily I have seen a fire; I will bring you from there some information, or I will bring you a burning brand, that you may warm yourselves." (27:7)
فلما جاءها نودي ان بورك من في النار ومن: 8. But when he came to it, he was called: "Blessed is whosoever is in the fire, and whosoever is round about it! And glorified be Allâh, the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists). (27:8)
يا موسى انه انا الله العزيز الحكيم: 9. "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Verily it is I, Allâh, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. (27:9)
والق عصاك فلما راها تهتز كانها جان ولى م: 10. "And throw down your stick!" But when he saw it moving as if it were a snake, he turned in flight, and did not look back. (It was said:) "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Fear not: verily the Messengers fear not in front of Me. (27:10)
الا من ظلم ثم بدل حسنا بعد سوء فاني غفور: 11. "Except him who has done wrong and afterwards has changed evil for good; then surely, I am Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful. (27:11)
وادخل يدك في جيبك تخرج بيضاء من غير سوء: 12. "And put your hand into your bosom, it will come forth white without hurt. (These are) among the nine signs (you will take) to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) and his people. Verily they are a people who are Fâsiqûn (rebellious, disobedient to Allâh). (27:12)
فلما جاءتهم اياتنا مبصرة قالوا هذا سحر م: 13. But when Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) came to them, clear to see, they said: "This is a manifest magic." (27:13)
وجحدوا بها واستيقنتها انفسهم ظلما وعلوا: 14. And they belied them (those Ayât) wrongfully and arrogantly, though their ownselves were convinced thereof [i.e. those (Ayât ) are from Allâh, and Mûsâ (Moses) is the Messenger of Allâh in truth, but they disliked to obey Mûsâ (Moses), and hated to believe in his Message of Monotheism]. So see what was the end of the Mufsidûn (disbelievers, disobedient to Allâh, evil-doers and liars.). (27:14)
ولقد اتينا داوود وسليمان علما وقالا الحم: 15. And indeed We gave knowledge to Dâwûd (David) and Sulaimân (Solomon), and they both said: "All the praises and thanks be to Allâh, Who has preferred us above many of His believing slaves!" (27:15)
وورث سليمان داوود وقال يا ايها الناس علم: 16. And Sulaimân (Solomon) inherited (the knowledge of) Dâwûd (David). He said: "O mankind! We have been taught the language of birds, and on us have been bestowed all things. This, verily, is an evident grace (from Allâh)." (27:16)
وحشر لسليمان جنوده من الجن والانس والطير: 17. And there were gathered before Sulaimân (Solomon) his hosts of jinn and men, and birds, and they all were set in battle order (marching forward). (27:17)
حتى اذا اتوا على واد النمل قالت نملة يا: 18. Till, when they came to the valley of the ants, one of the ants said: "O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulaimân (Solomon) and his hosts should crush you, while they perceive not." (27:18)
فتبسم ضاحكا من قولها وقال رب اوزعني ان ا: 19. So he [Sulaimân (Solomon)] smiled, amused at her speech[1] and said: "My Lord! Grant me the power and ability that I may be grateful for Your Favours which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may do righteous good deeds that will please You, and admit me by Your Mercy among Your righteous slaves." (27:19)
وتفقد الطير فقال ما لي لا ارى الهدهد ام: 20. He inspected the birds, and said: "What is the matter that I see not the hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees? (27:20)
لاعذبنه عذابا شديدا او لاذبحنه او لياتين: 21. "I will surely punish him with a severe torment, or slaughter him, unless he brings me a clear reason." (27:21)
فمكث غير بعيد فقال احطت بما لم تحط به وج: 22. But the hoopoe stayed not long: he (came up and) said: "I have grasped (the knowledge of a thing) which you have not grasped and I have come to you from Saba’ (Sheba) with true news. (27:22)
اني وجدت امراة تملكهم واوتيت من كل شيء و: 23. "I found a woman ruling over them: she has been given all things that could be possessed by any ruler of the earth, and she has a great throne. (27:23)
وجدتها وقومها يسجدون للشمس من دون الله و: 24. "I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allâh, and Shaitân (Satan) has made their deeds fair-seeming to them, and has barred them from (Allâh’s) Way: so they have no guidance." (27:24)
الا يسجدوا لله الذي يخرج الخبء في السماو: 25. [As Shaitân (Satan) has barred them from Allâh’s Way] so they do not worship (prostrate themselves before) Allâh, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal. [Tafsir At-Tabarî] (27:25)
الله لا اله الا هو رب العرش العظيم: 26. Allâh, Lâ ilâha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), the Lord of the Supreme Throne! (27:26)
قال سننظر اصدقت ام كنت من الكاذبين: 27. [Sulaimân (Solomon)] said: "We shall see whether you speak the truth or you are (one) of the liars. (27:27)
اذهب بكتابي هذا فالقه اليهم ثم تول عنهم: 28. "Go you with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them, then draw back from them, and see what (answer) they return." (27:28)
قالت يا ايها الملا اني القي الي كتاب كري: 29. She said: "O chiefs! Verily! Here is delivered to me a noble letter, (27:29)
انه من سليمان وانه بسم الله الرحمن الرحي: 30. "Verily it is from Sulaimân (Solomon), and verily, it (reads): In the Name of Allâh, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful: (27:30)
الا تعلوا علي واتوني مسلمين: 31. "Be you not exalted against me, but come to me as Muslims (true believers who submit to Allâh with full submission).’ " (27:31)
قالت يا ايها الملا افتوني في امري ما كنت: 32. She said: "O chiefs! Advise me in (this) case of mine. I decide no case till you are present with me (and give me your opinions)." (27:32)
قالوا نحن اولو قوة واولو باس شديد والامر: 33. They said: "We have great strength, and great ability for war, but it is for you to command: so think over what you will command." (27:33)
قالت ان الملوك اذا دخلوا قرية افسدوها وج: 34. She said: "Verily kings, when they enter a town (country), they despoil it and make the most honourable amongst its people the lowest. And thus they do. (27:34)
واني مرسلة اليهم بهدية فناظرة بم يرجع ال: 35. "But verily! I am going to send him a present, and see with what (answer) the messengers return." (27:35)
فلما جاء سليمان قال اتمدونن بمال فما اتا: 36. So when (the messengers with the present) came to Sulaimân (Solomon), he said: "Will you help me in wealth? What Allâh has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay, you rejoice in your gift!" (27:36)
ارجع اليهم فلناتينهم بجنود لا قبل لهم به: 37. [Then Sulaimân (Solomon) said to the chief of her messengers who brought the present]: "Go back to them. We verily shall come to them with hosts that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from there in disgrace, and they will be abased." (27:37)
قال يا ايها الملا ايكم ياتيني بعرشها قبل: 38. He said: "O chiefs! Which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me surrendering themselves in obedience?" (27:38)
قال عفريت من الجن انا اتيك به قبل ان تقو: 39. A ‘Ifrît (strong one) from the jinn said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place (council). And verily, I am indeed strong, and trustworthy for such work." (27:39)
قال الذي عنده علم من الكتاب انا اتيك به: 40. One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture said: "I will bring it to you within the twinkling of an eye!" Then when he [Sulaimân (Solomon)] saw it placed before him, he said: "This is by the Grace of my Lord - to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! And whoever is grateful, truly, his gratitude is for (the good of) his ownself; and whoever is ungrateful, (he is ungrateful only for the loss of his ownself). Certainly my Lord is Rich (Free of all needs), Bountiful." (27:40)
قال نكروا لها عرشها ننظر اتهتدي ام تكون: 41. He said: "Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will be guided (to recognise her throne), or she will be one of those not guided." (27:41)
فلما جاءت قيل اهكذا عرشك قالت كانه هو وا: 42. So when she came, it was said (to her): "Is your throne like this?" She said: "(It is) as though it were the very same." And [Sulaimân (Solomon) said]: "Knowledge was bestowed on us before her, and we were submitted to Allâh (in Islâm as Muslims before her)." (27:42)
وصدها ما كانت تعبد من دون الله انها كانت: 43. And that which she used to worship besides Allâh has prevented her (from Islâm), for she was of a disbelieving people. (27:43)
قيل لها ادخلي الصرح فلما راته حسبته لجة: 44. It was said to her: "Enter As-Sarh" (a glass surface with water underneath it or a palace): but when she saw it, she thought it was a pool, and she (tucked up her clothes) uncovering her legs. (Sulaimân (Solomon)) said: "Verily, it is a Sarh (a glass surface with water underneath it or a palace)." She said: "My Lord! Verily, I have wronged myself, and I submit [in Islâm, together with Sulaimân (Solomon)] to Allâh, the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)." (27:44)
ولقد ارسلنا الى ثمود اخاهم صالحا ان اعبد: 45. And indeed We sent to Thamûd their brother Sâlih, saying: "Worship Allâh (Alone and none else). Then look! They became two parties (believers and disbelievers) quarreling with each other." (27:45)
قال يا قوم لم تستعجلون بالسيئة قبل الحسن: 46. He said: "O my people! Why do you seek to hasten the evil (torment) before the good (Allâh’s Mercy)? Why seek you not the Forgiveness of Allâh, that you may receive mercy?" (27:46)
قالوا اطيرنا بك وبمن معك قال طائركم عند: 47. They said: "We augur ill omen from you and those with you." He said: "Your ill omen is with Allâh; nay, but you are a people that are being tested." (27:47)
وكان في المدينة تسعة رهط يفسدون في الارض: 48. And there were in the city nine men (from the sons of their chiefs), who made mischief in the land, and would not reform. (27:48)
قالوا تقاسموا بالله لنبيتنه واهله ثم لنق: 49. They said: "Swear one to another by Allâh that we shall make a secret night attack on him and his household, and thereafter we will surely say to his near relatives: ‘We witnessed not the destruction of his household, and verily we are telling the truth.’" (27:49)
ومكروا مكرا ومكرنا مكرا وهم لا يشعرون: 50. So they plotted a plot, and We plotted a plot, while they perceived not. (27:50)
فانظر كيف كان عاقبة مكرهم انا دمرناهم وق: 51. Then see how was the end of their plot! Verily! We destroyed them and their nation, all together. (27:51)
فتلك بيوتهم خاوية بما ظلموا ان في ذلك لا: 52. These are their houses in utter ruin, for they did wrong. Verily, in this is indeed an Ayâh (a lesson or a sign) for people who know. (27:52)
وانجينا الذين امنوا وكانوا يتقون: 53. And We saved those who believed, and used to fear Allâh, and keep their duty to Him. (27:53)
ولوطا اذ قال لقومه اتاتون الفاحشة وانتم: 54. And (remember) Lût (Lot)! When he said to his people.[2] Do you commit Al-Fâhishah (evil, great sin, every kind of unlawful sexual intercourse, sodomy) while you see (one another doing evil without any screen)?" (27:54)
ائنكم لتاتون الرجال شهوة من دون النساء ب: 55. "Do you practise your lusts on men instead of women? Nay, but you are a people who behave senselessly." (27:55)
فما كان جواب قومه الا ان قالوا اخرجوا ال: 56. There was no other answer given by his people except that they said: "Drive out the family of Lût (Lot) from your city Verily, these are men who want to be clean and pure!" (27:56)
فانجيناه واهله الا امراته قدرناها من الغ: 57. So We saved him and his family, except his wife. We destined her to be of those who remained behind. (27:57)
قل الحمد لله وسلام على عباده الذين اصطفى: 59. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Praise and thanks be to Allâh, and peace be on His slaves whom He has chosen (for His Message)! Is Allâh better, or (all) that you ascribe as partners (to Him)?" (Of course, Allâh is Better).[3] (27:59)
امن خلق السماوات والارض وانزل لكم من الس: 60. Is not He (better than your gods) Who created the heavens and the earth, and sends down for you water (rain) from the sky, whereby We cause to grow wonderful gardens full of beauty and delight? It is not in your ability to cause the growth of their trees. Is there any ilâh (god) with Allâh? Nay, but they are a people who ascribe equals (to Him)! (27:60)
امن جعل الارض قرارا وجعل خلالها انهارا و: 61. Is not He (better than your gods) Who has made the earth as a fixed abode, and has placed rivers in its midst, and has placed firm mountains therein, and has set a barrier between the two seas (of salt and sweet water)? Is there any ilâh (god) with Allâh? Nay, but most of them know not! (27:61)
امن يجيب المضطر اذا دعاه ويكشف السوء ويج: 62. Is not He (better than your gods) Who responds to the distressed one, when he calls on Him, and Who removes the evil, and makes you inheritors of the earth, generations after generations? Is there any ilâh (god) with Allâh? Little is that you remember! (27:62)
امن يهديكم في ظلمات البر والبحر ومن يرسل: 63. Is not He (better than your gods) Who guides you in the darkness of the land and the sea, and Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy (rain)? Is there any ilâh (god) with Allâh? High Exalted be Allâh above all that they associate as partners (to Him)! (27:63)
امن يبدا الخلق ثم يعيده ومن يرزقكم من ال: 64. Is not He (better than your so-called gods) Who originates creation, and shall thereafter repeat it, and Who provides for you from heaven and earth? Is there any ilâh (god) with Allâh? Say: "Bring forth your proofs, if you are truthful." (27:64)
قل لا يعلم من في السماوات والارض الغيب ا: 65. Say: "None in the heavens and the earth knows the Ghaib (Unseen) except Allâh, nor can they perceive when they shall be resurrected." (27:65)
بل ادارك علمهم في الاخرة بل هم في شك منه: 66. Nay, they have no knowledge of the Hereafter. Nay, they are in doubt about it. Nay, they are in complete blindness about it. (27:66)
وقال الذين كفروا ااذا كنا ترابا واباؤنا: 67. And those who disbelieve say: "When we have become dust - we and our fathers - shall we really be brought forth (again)? (27:67)
لقد وعدنا هذا نحن واباؤنا من قبل ان هذا: 68. "Indeed we were promised this - we and our forefathers before (us), verily, these are nothing but tales of ancients." (27:68)
قل سيروا في الارض فانظروا كيف كان عاقبة: 69. Say to them (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) "Travel in the land and see how has been the end of the Mujrimûn (criminals, those who denied Allâh’s Messengers and disobeyed Allâh)." (27:69)
ولا تحزن عليهم ولا تكن في ضيق مما يمكرون: 70. And grieve you not over them, nor be straitened (in distress) because of what they plot. (27:70)
قل عسى ان يكون ردف لكم بعض الذي تستعجلون: 72. Say: "Perhaps that which you wish to hasten on, may be close behind you. (27:72)
وان ربك لذو فضل على الناس ولكن اكثرهم لا: 73. "Verily, your Lord is full of Grace for mankind, yet most of them do not give thanks." (27:73)
وان ربك ليعلم ما تكن صدورهم وما يعلنون: 74. And verily, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they reveal. (27:74)
وما من غائبة في السماء والارض الا في كتا: 75. And there is nothing hidden in the heaven and the earth but it is in a Clear Book (i.e. Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz). (27:75)
ان هذا القران يقص على بني اسرائيل اكثر ا: 76. Verily, this Qur’ân narrates to the Children of Israel most of that in which they differ. (27:76)
وانه لهدى ورحمة للمؤمنين: 77. And truly, it (this Qur’ân) is a guide and a mercy for the believers. (27:77)
ان ربك يقضي بينهم بحكمه وهو العزيز العلي: 78. Verily, your Lord will decide between them (various sects) by His Judgement. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing. (27:78)
فتوكل على الله انك على الحق المبين: 79. So put your trust in Allâh; surely, you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are on manifest truth. (27:79)
انك لا تسمع الموتى ولا تسمع الصم الدعاء: 80. Verily, you cannot make the dead to hear nor can you make the deaf to hear the call (i.e. benefit them and similarly the disbelievers), when they flee, turning their backs. (27:80)
وما انت بهادي العمي عن ضلالتهم ان تسمع ا: 81. Nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can only make to hear those who believe in Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and who have submitted (themselves to Allâh in Islâm as Muslims). (27:81)
واذا وقع القول عليهم اخرجنا لهم دابة من: 82. And when the Word (of torment) is fulfilled against them, We shall bring out from the earth a beast[4] for them, to speak to them because mankind believed not with certainty in Our Ayât (Verses of the Qur’ân and Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). (27:82)
ويوم نحشر من كل امة فوجا ممن يكذب باياتن: 83. And (remember) the Day when We shall gather out of every nation a troop of those who denied Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and (then) they (all) shall be driven (to the place of reckoning), (27:83)
حتى اذا جاءوا قال اكذبتم باياتي ولم تحيط: 84. Till, when they come (before their Lord at the place of reckoning), He will say: "Did you deny My Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) whereas you comprehended them not by knowledge (of their truth or falsehood), or what (else) was it that you used to do?" (27:84)
ووقع القول عليهم بما ظلموا فهم لا ينطقون: 85. And the Word (of torment) will be fulfilled against them, because they have done wrong, and they will be unable to speak (in order to defend themselves). (27:85)
الم يروا انا جعلنا الليل ليسكنوا فيه وال: 86. See they not that We have made the night for them to rest therein, and the day sight-giving? Verily, in this are Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) for the people who believe. (27:86)
ويوم ينفخ في الصور ففزع من في السماوات و: 87. And (remember) the Day on which the Trumpet will be blown - and all who are in the heavens and all who are on the earth, will be terrified except him whom Allâh will (exempt). And all shall come to Him, humbled. (27:87)
وترى الجبال تحسبها جامدة وهي تمر مر السح: 88. And you will see the mountains and think them solid, but they shall pass away as the passing away of the clouds. The Work of Allâh, Who perfected all things, verily He is Well-Acquainted with what you do. (27:88)
من جاء بالحسنة فله خير منها وهم من فزع ي: 89. Whoever brings a good deed (i.e. Belief in the Oneness of Allâh along with every deed of righteousness), will have better than its worth; and they will be safe from the terror on that Day. (27:89)
ومن جاء بالسيئة فكبت وجوههم في النار هل: 90. And whoever brings an evil deed (i.e. Shirk - polytheism, disbelief in the Oneness of Allâh and every evil sinful deed), they will be cast down (prone) on their faces in the Fire. (And it will be said to them) "Are you being recompensed anything except what you used to do?" (27:90)
انما امرت ان اعبد رب هذه البلدة الذي حرم: 91. I (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) have been commanded only to worship the Lord of this city (Makkah), Who has sanctified it and to Whom belongs everything. And I am commanded to be from among the Muslims (those who submit to Allâh in Islâm).[5] (27:91)
وان اتلو القران فمن اهتدى فانما يهتدي لن: 92. And that I should recite the Qur’ân, then whosoever receives guidance, receives it for the good of his ownself; and whosoever goes astray, say (to him): "I am only one of the warners." (27:92)
وقل الحمد لله سيريكم اياته فتعرفونها وما: 93. And say [(O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) to these polytheists and pagans.]: "All the praises and thanks be to Allâh. He will show you His Ayât (signs, in yourselves, and in the universe or punishments), and you shall recognise them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do." (27:93)
نتلو عليك من نبا موسى وفرعون بالحق لقوم: 3. We recite to you some of the news of Mûsâ (Moses) and Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) in truth, for a people who believe (in this Qur’ân, and in the Oneness of Allâh). (28:3)
ان فرعون علا في الارض وجعل اهلها شيعا يس: 4. Verily, Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) exalted himself in the land and made its people sects, weakening (oppressing) a group (i.e. Children of Israel) among them: killing their sons, and letting their females live. Verily, he was of the Mufsidûn(i.e. those who commit great sins and crimes, oppressors, tyrants). (28:4)
ونريد ان نمن على الذين استضعفوا في الارض: 5. And We wished to do a favour to those who were weak (and oppressed) in the land, and to make them rulers and to make them the inheritors, (28:5)
ونمكن لهم في الارض ونري فرعون وهامان وجن: 6. And to establish them in the land, and We let Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) and Hâmân and their hosts receive from them that which they feared. (28:6)
واوحينا الى ام موسى ان ارضعيه فاذا خفت ع: 7. And We inspired the mother of Mûsâ (Moses): (telling): "Suckle him [Mûsâ (Moses)], but when you fear for him, then cast him into the river and fear not, nor grieve. Verily, We shall bring him back to you, and shall make him one of (Our) Messengers." [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi] (28:7)
فالتقطه ال فرعون ليكون لهم عدوا وحزنا ان: 8. Then the household of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) picked him up, that he might become for them an enemy and a (cause of) grief. Verily, Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), Hâmân and their hosts were sinners. (28:8)
وقالت امرات فرعون قرت عين لي ولك لا تقتل: 9. And the wife of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) said: "A comfort of the eye for me and for you. Kill him not, perhaps he may be of benefit to us, or we may adopt him as a son." And they perceived not (the result of that). (28:9)
واصبح فؤاد ام موسى فارغا ان كادت لتبدي ب: 10. And the heart of the mother of Mûsâ (Moses) became empty [from every thought, except the thought of Mûsâ (Moses)]. She was very near to disclose his (case, i.e. the child is her son), had We not strengthened her heart (with Faith), so that she might remain as one of the believers. (28:10)
وقالت لاخته قصيه فبصرت به عن جنب وهم لا: 11. And she said to his [Mûsâ’s (Moses)] sister: "Follow him." So she (his sister) watched him from a far place secretly, while they perceived not. (28:11)
وحرمنا عليه المراضع من قبل فقالت هل ادلك: 12. And We had already forbidden (other) foster suckling mothers for him, until she (his sister came up and) said: "Shall I direct you to a household who will rear him for you, and look after him in a good manner?" (28:12)
فرددناه الى امه كي تقر عينها ولا تحزن ول: 13. So did We restore him to his mother, that her eye might be comforted, and that she might not grieve, and that she might know that the Promise of Allâh is true. But most of them know not. (28:13)
ولما بلغ اشده واستوى اتيناه حكما وعلما و: 14. And when he attained his full strength, and was perfect (in manhood), We bestowed on him Hukm (Prophethood and right judgement of the affairs) and religious knowledge [of the religion of his forefathers i.e. Islâmic Monotheism]. And thus do We reward the Muhsinûn (i.e. good doers. See the footnote of V.9:120). (28:14)
ودخل المدينة على حين غفلة من اهلها فوجد: 15. And he entered the city at a time of unawareness of its people: and he found there two men fighting, - one of his party (his religion - from the Children of Israel), and the other of his foes. The man of his (own) party asked him for help against his foe, so Mûsâ (Moses) struck him with his fist and killed him. He said: "This is of Shaitan’s (Satan) doing: verily, he is a plain misleading enemy." (28:15)
قال رب اني ظلمت نفسي فاغفر لي فغفر له ان: 16. He said: "My Lord! Verily, I have wronged myself, so forgive me." Then He forgave him. Verily, He is the Oft-Forgiving, the Most Merciful. (28:16)
قال رب بما انعمت علي فلن اكون ظهيرا للمج: 17. He said: "My Lord! For that with which You have favoured me, I will never more be a helper of the Mujrimûn (criminals, disbelievers, polytheists, sinners)!" (28:17)
فاصبح في المدينة خائفا يترقب فاذا الذي ا: 18. So he became afraid, looking about in the city (waiting as to what will be the result of his crime of killing), when behold, the man who had sought his help the day before, called for his help (again). Mûsâ (Moses) said to him: "Verily, you are a plain misleader!" (28:18)
فلما ان اراد ان يبطش بالذي هو عدو لهما ق: 19. Then when he decided to seize the man who was an enemy to both of them, the man said: "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Is it your intention to kill me as you killed a man yesterday? Your aim is nothing but to become a tyrant in the land, and not to be one of those who do right." (28:19)
وجاء رجل من اقصى المدينة يسعى قال يا موس: 20. And there came a man running, from the farthest end of the city. He said: "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Verily, the chiefs are taking counsel together about you, to kill you, so escape. Truly, I am one of the good advisers to you." (28:20)
فخرج منها خائفا يترقب قال رب نجني من الق: 21. So he escaped from there, looking about in a state of fear. He said: "My Lord! Save me from the people who are Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers)!" (28:21)
ولما توجه تلقاء مدين قال عسى ربي ان يهدي: 22. And when he went towards (the land of) Madyan (Midian), he said: "It may be that my Lord guides me to the Right Way." (28:22)
ولما ورد ماء مدين وجد عليه امة من الناس: 23. And when he arrived at the water (a well) of Madyan (Midian) he found there a group of men watering (their flocks), and besides them he found two women who were keeping back (their flocks). He said: "What is the matter with you?" They said: "We cannot water (our flocks) until the shepherds take (their flocks). And our father is a very old man." (28:23)
فسقى لهما ثم تولى الى الظل فقال رب اني ل: 24. So he watered (their flocks) for them, then he turned back to shade, and said: "My Lord! truly, I am in need of whatever good that You bestow on me!" (28:24)
فجاءته احداهما تمشي على استحياء قالت ان: 25. Then there came to him one of the two women, walking shyly. She said: "Verily, my father calls you that he may reward you for having watered (our flocks) for us." So when he came to him and narrated the story, he said: "Fear you not. You have escaped from the people who are Zâlimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, and wrong-doers)." (28:25)
قالت احداهما يا ابت استاجره ان خير من اس: 26. And said one of them (the two women): "O my father! Hire him! Verily, the best of men for you to hire is the strong, the trustworthy." (28:26)
قال اني اريد ان انكحك احدى ابنتي هاتين ع: 27. He said: "I intend to wed one of these two daughters of mine to you, on condition that you serve me for eight years; but if you complete ten years, it will be (a favour) from you. But I intend not to place you under a difficulty. If Allâh wills, you will find me one of the righteous." (28:27)
قال ذلك بيني وبينك ايما الاجلين قضيت فلا: 28. He [Mûsâ (Moses)] said: "That (is settled) between me and you: whichever of the two terms I fulfil, there will be no injustice to me, and Allâh is Surety over what we say." (28:28)
فلما قضى موسى الاجل وسار باهله انس من جا: 29. Then, when Mûsâ (Moses) had fulfilled the term, and was travelling with his family, he saw a fire in the direction of Tûr (Mount). He said to his family: "Wait, I have seen a fire; perhaps I may bring you from there some information, or a burning fire-brand that you may warm yourselves." (28:29)
فلما اتاها نودي من شاطئ الواد الايمن في: 30. So when he reached it (the fire), he was called from the right side of the valley, in the blessed place, from the tree: "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Verily I am Allâh, the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)! (28:30)
وان الق عصاك فلما راها تهتز كانها جان ول: 31. "And throw your stick!" But when he saw it moving as if it were a snake, he turned in flight, and looked not back. (It was said:) "O Mûsâ (Moses)! Draw near, and fear not. Verily, you are of those who are secure. (28:31)
اسلك يدك في جيبك تخرج بيضاء من غير سوء و: 32. "Put your hand in your bosom, it will come forth white without a disease; and draw your hand close to your side to be free from the fear (which you suffered from the snake, and also your hand will return to its original state). These are two Burhâns (signs, miracles, evidence, proofs) from your Lord to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) and his chiefs. Verily, they are the people who are Fâsiqûn (rebellious, disobedient to Allâh). (28:32)
قال رب اني قتلت منهم نفسا فاخاف ان يقتلو: 33. He said: "My Lord! I have killed a man among them, and I fear that they will kill me. (28:33)
واخي هارون هو افصح مني لسانا فارسله معي: 34. "And my brother Hârûn (Aaron) - he is more eloquent in speech than me: so send him with me as a helper to confirm me. Verily I fear that they will belie me." (28:34)
قال سنشد عضدك باخيك ونجعل لكما سلطانا فل: 35. Allâh said: "We will strengthen your arm through your brother, and give you both power, so they shall not be able to harm you: with Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), you two as well as those who follow you will be the victors." (28:35)
فلما جاءهم موسى باياتنا بينات قالوا ما ه: 36. Then when Mûsâ (Moses) came to them with Our Clear Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), they said: "This is nothing but invented magic. Never did we hear of this among our fathers of old." (28:36)
وقال موسى ربي اعلم بمن جاء بالهدى من عند: 37. Mûsâ (Moses) said: "My Lord knows best him who came with guidance from Him, and whose will be the happy end in the Hereafter. Verily, the Zâlimûn (wrong-doers, polytheists and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh) will not be successful." (28:37)
وقال فرعون يا ايها الملا ما علمت لكم من: 38. Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) said: "O chiefs! I know not that you have an ilâh (a god) other than me. So kindle for me (a fire), O Hâmân, to bake (bricks out of) clay, and set up for me a Sarh (a lofty tower, or palace) in order that I may look at (or look for) the Ilâh (God) of Mûsâ (Moses); and verily, I think that he [Mûsâ (Moses)] is one of the liars." (28:38)
واستكبر هو وجنوده في الارض بغير الحق وظن: 39. And he and his hosts were arrogant in the land, without right, and they thought that they would never return to Us. (28:39)
فاخذناه وجنوده فنبذناهم في اليم فانظر كي: 40. So We seized him and his hosts, and We threw them all into the sea (and drowned them). So behold (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) what was the end of the Zâlimûn [wrong-doers, polytheists and those who disbelieved in the Oneness of their Lord (Allâh), or rejected the advice of His Messenger Mûsâ (Moses) عليه السلام]. (28:40)
وجعلناهم ائمة يدعون الى النار ويوم القيا: 41. And We made them leaders inviting to the Fire: and on the Day of Resurrection, they will not be helped. (28:41)
واتبعناهم في هذه الدنيا لعنة ويوم القيام: 42. And We made a curse to follow them in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection, they will be among Al-Maqbuhûn (those who are prevented from receiving Allâh’s Mercy or any good; despised or destroyed). (28:42)
ولقد اتينا موسى الكتاب من بعد ما اهلكنا: 43. And indeed We gave Mûsâ (Moses) - after We had destroyed the generations of old -the Scripture [the Taurât (Torah)] as an enlightenment for mankind, and a guidance and a mercy, that they might remember (or receive admonition). (28:43)
وما كنت بجانب الغربي اذ قضينا الى موسى ا: 44. And you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) were not on the western side (of the Mount), when We made clear to Mûsâ (Moses) the commandment, and you were not among the witnesses. (28:44)
ولكنا انشانا قرونا فتطاول عليهم العمر وم: 45. But We created generations [after generations i.e. after Mûsâ (Moses) عليه السلام], and long were the ages that passed over them. And you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) were not a dweller among the people of Madyan (Midian), reciting Our Verses to them. But it is We Who kept sending (Messengers). (28:45)
وما كنت بجانب الطور اذ نادينا ولكن رحمة: 46. And you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) were not at the side of the Tûr (Mount) when We did call: [it is said that Allâh called the followers of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم, and they answered His Call, or that Allâh called Mûsâ (Moses)]. But (you are sent) as a mercy from your Lord, to give warning to a people to whom no warner had come before you: in order that they may remember or receive admonition. [Tafsir At-Tabarî]. (28:46)
ولولا ان تصيبهم مصيبة بما قدمت ايديهم في: 47. And if (We had) not (sent you to the people of Makkah) - in case a calamity should seize them for (the deeds) that their hands have sent forth, they would have said: "Our Lord! Why did You not send us a Messenger? We would then have followed Your Ayât (Verses of the Qur’ân) and would have been among the believers." (28:47)
فلما جاءهم الحق من عندنا قالوا لولا اوتي: 48. But when the truth (i.e. Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم with his Message) has come to them from Us, they say: "Why is he not given the like of what was given to Mûsâ (Moses)? Did they not disbelieve in that which was given to Mûsâ (Moses) of old? They say: "Two kinds of magic [the Taurât (Torah) and the Qur’an], each helping the other!" And they say: "Verily in both we are disbelievers." (28:48)
قل فاتوا بكتاب من عند الله هو اهدى منهما: 49. Say (to them, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Then bring a Book from Allâh, which is a better guide than these two [the Taurât (Torah) and the Qur’an], that I may follow it, if you are truthful." (28:49)
فان لم يستجيبوا لك فاعلم انما يتبعون اهو: 50. But if they answer you not (i.e. do not bring the Book nor believe in your doctrine of Islâmic Monotheism), then know that they only follow their own lusts. And who is more astray than one who follows his own lusts, without guidance from Allâh? Verily Allâh guides not the people who are Zâlimûn (wrong-doers, disobedient to Allâh, and polytheists). (28:50)
ولقد وصلنا لهم القول لعلهم يتذكرون: 51. And indeed now We have conveyed the Word (this Qur’ân in which is the news of everything) to them, in order that they may remember (or receive admonition). (28:51)
الذين اتيناهم الكتاب من قبله هم به يؤمنو: 52. Those to whom We gave the Scripture [i.e. the Taurât (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] before it, they believe in it (the Qur’ân). (28:52)
واذا يتلى عليهم قالوا امنا به انه الحق م: 53. And when it is recited to them, they say: "We believe in it. Verily, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed even before it we have been from those who submit themselves to Allâh in Islâm as Muslims (like ‘Abdullâh bin Salâm and Salmân Al-Farisî).[1] (28:53)
اولئك يؤتون اجرهم مرتين بما صبروا ويدرءو: 54. These will be given their reward twice over,[2] because they are patient, and repel evil with good, and spend (in charity) out of what We have provided them. (28:54)
واذا سمعوا اللغو اعرضوا عنه وقالوا لنا ا: 55. And when they hear Al-Laghw (dirty, false, evil vain talk), they withdraw from it and say: "To us our deeds, and to you your deeds. Peace be to you. We seek not (the way of) the ignorant." (28:55)
انك لا تهدي من احببت ولكن الله يهدي من ي: 56. Verily you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) guide not whom you like, but Allâh guides whom He wills. And He knows best those who are the guided.[3] (28:56)
وقالوا ان نتبع الهدى معك نتخطف من ارضنا: 57. And they say: "If we follow the guidance with you, we would be snatched away from our land." Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary (Makkah), to which are brought fruits of all kinds, a provision from Ourselves, but most of them know not.[4] (28:57)
وكم اهلكنا من قرية بطرت معيشتها فتلك مسا: 58. And how many a town (population) have We destroyed, which was thankless for its means of livelihood (disobeyed Allâh, and His Messengers, by doing evil deeds and crimes)! And those are their dwellings, which have not been inhabited after them except a little. And verily! We have been the inheritors. (28:58)
وما كان ربك مهلك القرى حتى يبعث في امها: 59. And never will your Lord destroy the towns (populations) until He sends to their mother town a Messenger reciting to them Our Verses. And never would We destroy the towns unless the people thereof are Zâlimûn (polytheists, wrong-doers, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh, oppressors and tyrants). (28:59)
وما اوتيتم من شيء فمتاع الحياة الدنيا وز: 60. And whatever you have been given is an enjoyment of the life of (this) world and its adornment, and that (Hereafter) which is with Allâh is better and will remain forever. Have you then no sense? (28:60)
افمن وعدناه وعدا حسنا فهو لاقيه كمن متعن: 61. Is he whom We have promised an excellent promise (Paradise) - which he will find true - like him whom We have made to enjoy the luxuries of the life of (this) world, then on the Day of Resurrection, he will be among those brought up (to be punished in the Hell-fire)? (28:61)
ويوم يناديهم فيقول اين شركائي الذين كنتم: 62. And (remember) the Day when He will call to them and say: "Where are My (so-called) partners whom you used to assert?" (28:62)
قال الذين حق عليهم القول ربنا هؤلاء الذي: 63. Those about whom the Word will have come true (to be punished) will say: "Our Lord! These are they whom we led astray. We led them astray, as we were astray ourselves. We declare our innocence (from them) before You. It was not us they worshipped." (28:63)
وقيل ادعوا شركاءكم فدعوهم فلم يستجيبوا ل: 64. And it will be said (to them): "Call upon your (so-called) partners (of Allâh)", and they will call upon them, but they will give no answer to them, and they will see the torment. (They will then wish) if only they had been guided! (28:64)
ويوم يناديهم فيقول ماذا اجبتم المرسلين: 65. And (remember) the Day (Allâh) will call to them, and say: "What answer gave you to the Messengers?" (28:65)
فعميت عليهم الانباء يومئذ فهم لا يتساءلو: 66. Then the news of a good answer will be obscured to them on that day, and they will not be able to ask one another. (28:66)
فاما من تاب وامن وعمل صالحا فعسى ان يكون: 67. But as for him who repented (from polytheism and sins), believed (in the Oneness of Allâh, and in His Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), and did righteous deeds (in the life of this world), then he will be among those who are successful. (28:67)
وربك يخلق ما يشاء ويختار ما كان لهم الخي: 68. And your Lord creates whatsoever He wills and chooses: no choice have they (in any matter). Glorified be Allâh, and exalted above all that they associate (as partners with Him). (28:68)
وربك يعلم ما تكن صدورهم وما يعلنون: 69. And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal, and what they reveal. (28:69)
وهو الله لا اله الا هو له الحمد في الاول: 70. And He is Allâh: Lâ ilâha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), all praises and thanks be to Him (both) in the first (i.e. in this world) and in the last (i.e. in the Hereafter). And for Him is the Decision, and to Him shall you (all) be returned. (28:70)
قل ارايتم ان جعل الله عليكم الليل سرمدا: 71. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Tell me! If Allâh made the night continuous for you till the Day of Resurrection, which ilâh (god) besides Allâh could bring you light? Will you not then hear?" (28:71)
قل ارايتم ان جعل الله عليكم النهار سرمدا: 72. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Tell me! If Allâh made the day continuous for you till the Day of Resurrection, which ilâh (god) besides Allâh could bring you night wherein you rest? Will you not then see?" (28:72)
ومن رحمته جعل لكم الليل والنهار لتسكنوا: 73. It is out of His Mercy that He has made for you the night and the day that you may rest therein (i.e. during the night) and that you may seek of His Bounty (i.e. during the day) - and in order that you may be grateful. (28:73)
ونزعنا من كل امة شهيدا فقلنا هاتوا برهان: 75. And We shall take out from every nation a witness, and We shall say: "Bring your proof." Then they shall know that the truth is with Allâh (Alone), and the lies (false gods) which they invented will disappear from them. (28:75)
ان قارون كان من قوم موسى فبغى عليهم واتي: 76. Verily, Qârûn (Korah) was of Mûsâ’s (Moses) people, but he behaved arrogantly towards them. And We gave him of the treasures, that of which the keys would have been a burden to a body of strong men. Remember when his people said to him: "Do not exult (with riches, being ungrateful to Allâh). Verily Allâh likes not those who exult (with riches, being ungrateful to Allâh). (28:76)
وابتغ فيما اتاك الله الدار الاخرة ولا تن: 77. "But seek, with that (wealth) which Allâh has bestowed on you, the home of the Hereafter, and forget not your portion of lawful enjoyment in this world; and do good as Allâh has been good to you, and seek not mischief in the land. Verily, Allâh likes not the Mufsidûn (those who commit great crimes and sins, oppressors, tyrants, mischief-makers, corrupters)." (28:77)
قال انما اوتيته على علم عندي اولم يعلم ا: 78. He said: "This has been given to me only because of the knowledge I possess." Did he not know that Allâh had destroyed before him generations, men who were stronger than him in might and greater in the amount (of riches) they had collected? But the Mujrimûn (criminals, disbelievers, polytheists, sinners) will not be questioned of their sins (because Allâh knows them well, so they will be punished without being called to account). (28:78)
فخرج على قومه في زينته قال الذين يريدون: 79. So he went forth before his people in his pomp. Those who were desirous of the life of the world, said: "Ah, would that we had the like of what Qârûn (Korah) has been given! Verily he is the owner of a great fortune." (28:79)
وقال الذين اوتوا العلم ويلكم ثواب الله خ: 80. But those who had been given (religious) knowledge said: "Woe to you! The Reward of Allâh (in the Hereafter) is better for those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and this none shall attain except those who are As-Sâbirûn (the patient in following the truth)." (28:80)
فخسفنا به وبداره الارض فما كان له من فئة: 81. So We caused the earth to swallow him[5] and his dwelling place. Then he had no group or party to help him against Allâh, nor was he one of those who could save themselves. (28:81)
واصبح الذين تمنوا مكانه بالامس يقولون وي: 82. And those who had desired (for a position like) his position the day before, began to say: "Know you not that it is Allâh Who enlarges the provision or restricts it to whomsoever He pleases of His slaves. Had it not been that Allâh was Gracious to us, He could have caused the earth to swallow us up (also)! Know you not that the disbelievers will never be successful. (28:82)
تلك الدار الاخرة نجعلها للذين لا يريدون: 83. That home of the Hereafter (i.e. Paradise), We shall assign to those who rebel not against the truth with pride[6] and oppression in the land nor do mischief by committing crimes. And the good end is for the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2). (28:83)
