وجعلنا بينهم وبين القرى التي باركنا فيها: 18. And We placed, between them and the towns which We had blessed, towns easy to be seen, and We made the stages (of journey) between them easy (saying): "Travel in them safely both by night and day." (34:18)
فقالوا ربنا باعد بين اسفارنا وظلموا انفس: 19. But they said: "Our Lord! Make the stages between our journey longer," and they wronged themselves; so We made them as tales (in the land), and We dispersed them all totally. Verily, in this are indeed signs for every steadfast, grateful (person). (34:19)
ولقد صدق عليهم ابليس ظنه فاتبعوه الا فري: 20. And indeed Iblîs (Satan) did prove true his thought about them: and they followed him, all except a group of true believers (in the Oneness of Allâh). (34:20)
وما كان له عليهم من سلطان الا لنعلم من ي: 21. And he (Iblîs - Satan) had no authority over them, - except that We might test him who believes in the Hereafter from him who is in doubt about it. And your Lord is a Hafiz (Watchful) over everything. (All-Knower of everything i.e. He keeps record of every person as regards deeds, and then He will reward them accordingly). (34:21)
قل ادعوا الذين زعمتم من دون الله لا يملك: 22. Say: (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to polytheists, pagans) "Call upon those whom you assert (to be associate gods) besides Allâh, they possess not even an atom’s (or a small ant’s) weight either in the heavens or on the earth, nor have they any share in either, nor there is for Him any supporter from among them. (34:22)
ولا تنفع الشفاعة عنده الا لمن اذن له حتى: 23. Intercession with Him profits not except for him whom He permits. So much so that when fear is banished from their (angels’) hearts, they (angels) say: "What is it that your Lord has said?" They say: "The truth. And He is the Most High, the Most Great." (34:23)
قل من يرزقكم من السماوات والارض قل الله: 24. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to polytheists, pagans) "Who gives you provision from the heavens and the earth?" Say: "Allâh. And verily, (either) we or you are rightly guided or in plain error." (34:24)
قل لا تسالون عما اجرمنا ولا نسال عما تعم: 25. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to polytheists, pagans) "You will not be asked about our sins, nor shall we be asked of what you do." (34:25)
قل يجمع بيننا ربنا ثم يفتح بيننا بالحق و: 26. Say: "Our Lord will assemble us all together (on the Day of Resurrection), then He will judge between us with truth. And He is the Just Judge, the All-Knower of the true state of affairs [Tafsir Ibn Kathir] (34:26)
قل اروني الذين الحقتم به شركاء كلا بل هو: 27. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to polytheists and pagans): "Show me those whom you have joined with Him as partners. Nay (there are not at all any partners with Him)! But He is Allâh (Alone), the All-Mighty, the All-Wise." (34:27)
وما ارسلناك الا كافة للناس بشيرا ونذيرا: 28. And We have not sent you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) except as a giver of glad tidings and a warner to all mankind, but most of men know not.[2] (34:28)
قل لكم ميعاد يوم لا تستاخرون عنه ساعة ول: 30. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "The appointment to you is for a Day, which you cannot put back for an hour (or a moment) nor put forward." (34:30)
وقال الذين كفروا لن نؤمن بهذا القران ولا: 31. And those who disbelieve say: "We believe not in this Qur’ân nor in that which was before it." But if you could see when the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers) will be made to stand before their Lord, how they will cast the (blaming) word one to another! Those who were deemed weak will say to those who were arrogant: "Had it not been for you, we should certainly have been believers!" (34:31)
قال الذين استكبروا للذين استضعفوا انحن ص: 32. And those who were arrogant will say to those who were deemed weak: "Did we keep you back from guidance after it had come to you? Nay, but you were Mujrimûn (polytheists, sinners, disbelievers, criminals)." (34:32)
وقال الذين استضعفوا للذين استكبروا بل مك: 33. Those who were deemed weak will say to those who were arrogant: "Nay, but it was your plotting by night and day: when you ordered us to disbelieve in Allâh and set up rivals to Him!" And each of them (parties) will conceal their own regrets (for disobeying Allâh during this worldly life), when they behold the torment. And We shall put iron collars round the necks of those who disbelieved. Are they requited aught except what they used to do? (34:33)
وما ارسلنا في قرية من نذير الا قال مترفو: 34. And We did not send a warner to a township but those who were given the worldly wealth and luxuries among them said: "We believe not in the (Message) with which you have been sent." (34:34)
وقالوا نحن اكثر اموالا واولادا وما نحن ب: 35. And they say: "We are more in wealth and in children, and we are not going to be punished." (34:35)
قل ان ربي يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء ويقدر ولكن: 36. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Verily, my Lord enlarges the provision to whom He wills and restricts, but most men know not." (34:36)
وما اموالكم ولا اولادكم بالتي تقربكم عند: 37. And it is not your wealth, nor your children that bring you nearer to Us (i.e. please Allâh), but only he who believes (in the Islâmic Monotheism), and does righteous deeds (will please Us); as for such, there will be twofold reward for what they did, and they will reside in the high dwellings (Paradise) in peace and security. (34:37)
والذين يسعون في اياتنا معاجزين اولئك في: 38. And those who strive against Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), to frustrate them, they will be brought to the torment. (34:38)
قل ان ربي يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء من عباده و: 39. Say: "Truly, my Lord enlarges the provision for whom He wills of His slaves, and (also) restricts (it) for him, and whatsoever you spend of anything (in Allâh’s Cause), He will replace it. And He is the Best of providers." (34:39)
ويوم يحشرهم جميعا ثم يقول للملائكة اهؤلا: 40. And (remember) the Day when He will gather them all together, then He will say to the angels: "Was it you that these people used to worship?" (34:40)
قالوا سبحانك انت ولينا من دونهم بل كانوا: 41. They (the angels) will say: "Glorified be You! You are our Walî (Lord) instead of them. Nay, but they used to worship the jinn; most of them were believers in them." (34:41)
فاليوم لا يملك بعضكم لبعض نفعا ولا ضرا و: 42. So Today (i.e. the Day of Resurrection), none of you can profit or harm one another. And We shall say to those who did wrong [i.e. worshipped others (like the angels, jinn, prophets, saints, righteous persons) along with Allâh]: "Taste the torment of the Fire which you used to belie.[3] (34:42)
واذا تتلى عليهم اياتنا بينات قالوا ما هذ: 43. And when Our Clear Verses are recited to them, they say: "This (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is naught but a man who wishes to hinder you from that which your fathers used to worship." And they say: "This (the Qur’ân) is nothing but an invented lie." And those who disbelieve say of the truth when it has come to them (i.e. Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم when Allâh sent him as a Messenger with proofs, evidence, verses of this Qur’ân, lessons, signs, etc.): "This is nothing but evident magic!" (34:43)
وما اتيناهم من كتب يدرسونها وما ارسلنا ا: 44. And We had not given them Scriptures which they could study, nor sent to them before you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) any warner (Messenger). (34:44)
وكذب الذين من قبلهم وما بلغوا معشار ما ا: 45. And those before them belied; these have not received even a tenth of what We had granted to those (of old); yet they belied My Messengers. Then how (terrible) was My denial (punishment)! (34:45)
قل انما اعظكم بواحدة ان تقوموا لله مثنى: 46. Say (to them O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I exhort you to one (thing) only: that you stand up for Allâh’s sake in pairs and singly, and reflect (within yourselves the life history of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم): there is no madness in your companion (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). He is only a warner to you in face of a severe torment." (34:46)
قل ما سالتكم من اجر فهو لكم ان اجري الا: 47. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Whatever wage I might have asked of you is yours. My wage is from Allâh only, and He is a Witness over all things." (34:47)
قل ان ربي يقذف بالحق علام الغيوب: 48. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Verily my Lord sends down (Revelation and makes apparent) the truth (i.e. this Revelation that had come to me), the All-Knower of the Ghaib (Unseen). (34:48)
قل جاء الحق وما يبدئ الباطل وما يعيد: 49. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Al-Haqq (the truth i.e. the Qur’ân and Allâh’s Revelation) has come, and Al-Bâtil [falsehood - Iblîs (Satan)] can neither create anything nor resurrect (anything)." (34:49)
قل ان ضللت فانما اضل على نفسي وان اهتديت: 50. Say: "If (even) I go astray, I shall stray only to my own loss. But if I remain guided, it is because of the Revelation of my Lord to me. Truly, He is All-Hearer, Ever Near (to all things)." (34:50)
ولو ترى اذ فزعوا فلا فوت واخذوا من مكان: 51. And if you could but see, when they will be terrified with no escape (for them), and they will be seized from a near place. (34:51)
وقالوا امنا به وانى لهم التناوش من مكان: 52. And they will say (in the Hereafter): "We do believe (now):" but how could they receive (Faith and the acceptance of their repentance by Allâh) from a place so far off (i.e. to return to the worldly life again). (34:52)
وقد كفروا به من قبل ويقذفون بالغيب من مك: 53. Indeed they did disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh, Islâm, the Qur’ân and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) before (in this world), and they (used to) conjecture about the Unseen [i.e. the Hereafter, Hell, Paradise, Resurrection and the Promise of Allâh (by saying) all that is untrue], from a far place. (34:53)
وحيل بينهم وبين ما يشتهون كما فعل باشياع: 54. And a barrier will be set between them and that which they desire [i.e. At-Taubah (turning to Allâh in repentance) and the accepting of Faith], as was done in the past with the people of their kind. Verily, they have been in grave doubt. (34:54)
الحمد لله فاطر السماوات والارض جاعل المل: 1. All the praises and thanks be to Allâh, the (only) Originator [or the (Only) Creator] of the heavens and the earth, Who made the angels messengers with wings, two or three or four. He increases in creation what He wills. Verily, Allâh is Able to do all things. (35:1)
ما يفتح الله للناس من رحمة فلا ممسك لها: 2. Whatever of mercy (i.e. of good), Allâh may grant to mankind, none can withhold it; and whatever He may withhold, none can grant it thereafter. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. (35:2)
يا ايها الناس اذكروا نعمت الله عليكم هل: 3. O mankind! Remember the Grace of Allâh upon you! Is there any creator other than Allâh who provides for you from the sky (rain) and the earth? Lâ ilâha illâ Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). How then are you turning away (from Him)? (35:3)
وان يكذبوك فقد كذبت رسل من قبلك والى الل: 4. And if they belie you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), so were Messengers belied before you. And to Allâh return all matters (for decision). (35:4)
يا ايها الناس ان وعد الله حق فلا تغرنكم: 5. O mankind! Verily, the Promise of Allâh is true. So let not this present life deceive you, and let not the chief deceiver (Satan) deceive you about Allâh. (35:5)
ان الشيطان لكم عدو فاتخذوه عدوا انما يدع: 6. Surely, Shaitân (Satan) is an enemy to you, so take (treat) him as an enemy. He only invites his Hizb (followers) that they may become the dwellers of the blazing Fire. (35:6)
الذين كفروا لهم عذاب شديد والذين امنوا و: 7. Those who disbelieve, theirs will be a severe torment; and those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, theirs will be forgiveness and a great reward (i.e. Paradise). (35:7)
افمن زين له سوء عمله فراه حسنا فان الله: 8. Is he, then, to whom the evil of his deeds is made fair-seeming, so that he considers it as good (equal to one who is rightly guided)? Verily, Allâh sends astray whom He wills, and guides whom He wills. So destroy not yourself (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) in sorrow for them. Truly, Allâh is the All-Knower of what they do! (35:8)
والله الذي ارسل الرياح فتثير سحابا فسقنا: 9. And it is Allâh Who sends the winds, so that they raise up the clouds, and We drive them to a dead land, and revive therewith the earth after its death. As such (will be) the Resurrection! (35:9)
من كان يريد العزة فلله العزة جميعا اليه: 10. Whosoever desires honour, (power and glory), then to Allâh belong all honour, power and glory [and one can get honour, power and glory only by obeying and worshipping Allâh (Alone)]. To Him ascend (all) the goodly words, and the righteous deeds exalt it (i.e. the goodly words are not accepted by Allâh unless and until they are followed by good deeds), but those who plot evils, theirs will be severe torment. And the plotting of such will perish. (35:10)
والله خلقكم من تراب ثم من نطفة ثم جعلكم: 11. And Allâh did create you (Adam) from dust, then from Nutfah (male and female discharge semen drops i.e. Adam’s offspring), then He made you pairs (male and female). And no female conceives or gives birth but with His Knowledge. And no aged man is granted a length of life nor is a part cut off from his life (or another man’s life), but is in a Book (Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz ) Surely, that is easy for Allâh. (35:11)
وما يستوي البحران هذا عذب فرات سائغ شراب: 12. And the two seas (kinds of water) are not alike: this is palatable, sweet and pleasant to drink, and that is salt and bitter. And from them both you eat fresh tender meat (fish), and derive the ornaments that you wear. And you see the ships cleaving (the sea-water as they sail through it), that you may seek of His Bounty, and that you may give thanks. (35:12)
يولج الليل في النهار ويولج النهار في الل: 13. He merges the night into the day (i.e. the decrease in the hours of the night is added to the hours of the day), and He merges the day into the night (i.e. the decrease in the hours of the day is added to the hours of the night). And He has subjected the sun and the moon: each runs its course for a term appointed. Such is Allâh, your Lord; His is the kingdom. And those, whom you invoke or call upon instead of Him, own not even a Qitmîr (the thin membrane over the date-stone). (35:13)
ان تدعوهم لا يسمعوا دعاءكم ولو سمعوا ما: 14. If you invoke (or call upon) them, they hear not your call; and if (in case) they were to hear, they could not grant it (your request) to you. And on the Day of Resurrection, they will disown your worshipping them. And none can inform you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) like Him Who is the All-Knower (of everything).[1] (35:14)
يا ايها الناس انتم الفقراء الى الله والل: 15. O mankind! it is you who stand in need of Allâh. But Allâh is Rich (Free of all needs), Worthy of all praise. (35:15)
ان يشا يذهبكم ويات بخلق جديد: 16. If He willed, He could destroy you and bring about a new creation. (35:16)
ولا تزر وازرة وزر اخرى وان تدع مثقلة الى: 18. And no bearer of burdens shall bear another’s burden; and if one heavily laden calls another to (bear) his load, nothing of it will be lifted even though he be near of kin. You (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) can warn only those who fear their Lord unseen and perform As-Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât). And he who purifies himself (from all kinds of sins), then he purifies only for the benefit of his ownself. And to Allâh is the (final) Return (of all). (35:18)
وما يستوي الاعمى والبصير: 19. Not alike are the blind (disbelievers in Islâmic Monotheism) and the seeing (believers in Islâmic Monotheism). (35:19)
ولا الظلمات ولا النور: 20. Nor are (alike) darkness (disbelief) and light (Belief in Islâmic Monotheism). (35:20)
ولا الظل ولا الحرور: 21. Nor are (alike) the shade and the sun’s heat. (35:21)
وما يستوي الاحياء ولا الاموات ان الله يس: 22. Nor are (alike) the living (i.e. the believers) and the dead (i.e. the disbelievers). Verily, Allâh makes whom He wills to hear, but you cannot make hear those who are in graves. (35:22)
ان انت الا نذير: 23. You (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are only a warner (i.e. your duty is to convey Allâh’s Message to mankind but the guidance is Allâh’s). (35:23)
انا ارسلناك بالحق بشيرا ونذيرا وان من ام: 24. Verily We have sent you with the truth, a bearer of glad tidings and a warner. And there never was a nation but a warner had passed among them. (35:24)
وان يكذبوك فقد كذب الذين من قبلهم جاءتهم: 25. And if they belie you, those before them also belied. Their Messengers came to them with clear signs, and with the Scriptures, and the book giving light. (35:25)
ثم اخذت الذين كفروا فكيف كان نكير: 26. Then I took hold of those who disbelieved: and how terrible was My denial (punishment)! (35:26)
الم تر ان الله انزل من السماء ماء فاخرجن: 27. See you not that Allâh sends down water (rain) from the sky, and We produce therewith fruits of various colours, and among the mountains are streaks white and red, of varying colours and (others) very black. (35:27)
ومن الناس والدواب والانعام مختلف الوانه: 28. And likewise of men and Ad-Dawâbb [moving (living) creatures, beasts], and cattle, are of various colours. It is only those who have knowledge among His slaves that fear Allâh. Verily, Allâh is All-Mighty, Oft-Forgiving. (35:28)
ان الذين يتلون كتاب الله واقاموا الصلاة: 29. Verily, those who recite the Book of Allâh (this Qur’ân), and perform As-Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât), and spend (in charity) out of what We have provided for them, secretly and openly, they hope for a (sure) trade-gain that will never perish. (35:29)
ليوفيهم اجورهم ويزيدهم من فضله انه غفور: 30. That He may pay them their wages in full, and give them (even) more, out of His Grace. Verily He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Ready to appreciate (good deeds and to recompense). (35:30)
والذي اوحينا اليك من الكتاب هو الحق مصدق: 31. And what We have revealed to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), of the Book (the Qur’ân), it is the (very) truth [that you (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) and your followers must act on its instructions], confirming that which was (revealed) before it. Verily! Allâh is indeed All-Aware, and All-Seer of His slaves. (35:31)
ثم اورثنا الكتاب الذين اصطفينا من عبادنا: 32. Then We gave the Book (the Qur’ân) as inheritance to such of Our slaves whom We chose (the followers of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). Then of them are some who wrong their ownselves, and of them are some who follow a middle course, and of them are some who are, by Allâh’s Leave, foremost in good deeds. That (inheritance of the Qur’ân) - that is indeed a great grace. (35:32)
جنات عدن يدخلونها يحلون فيها من اساور من: 33. ‘Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens) will they enter, therein will they be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garments therein will be of silk. (35:33)
وقالوا الحمد لله الذي اذهب عنا الحزن ان: 34. And they will say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allâh Who has removed from us (all) grief. Verily, our Lord is indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Ready to appreciate (good deeds and to recompense). (35:34)
الذي احلنا دار المقامة من فضله لا يمسنا: 35. Who, out of His Grace, has lodged us in a home that will last forever, where toil will touch us not nor weariness will touch us." (35:35)
والذين كفروا لهم نار جهنم لا يقضى عليهم: 36. But those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism), for them will be the Fire of Hell. Neither will it have a complete killing effect on them so that they die, nor shall its torment be lightened for them. Thus do We requite every disbeliever! (35:36)
وهم يصطرخون فيها ربنا اخرجنا نعمل صالحا: 37. Therein they will cry: "Our Lord! Bring us out, we shall do righteous good deeds, not (the evil deeds) that we used to do." (Allâh will reply): "Did We not give you lives long enough, so that whosoever would receive admonition could receive it? And the warner came to you. So taste you (the evil of your deeds). For the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers) there is no helper." (35:37)
ان الله عالم غيب السماوات والارض انه علي: 38. Verily, Allâh is the All-Knower of the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. Verily! He is the All-Knower of that is in the breasts. (35:38)
هو الذي جعلكم خلائف في الارض فمن كفر فعل: 39. He it is Who has made you successors generations after generations in the earth, so whosoever disbelieves (in Islâmic Monotheism) on him will be his disbelief. And the disbelief of the disbelievers adds nothing but hatred of their Lord. And the disbelief of the disbelievers adds nothing but loss.[2] (35:39)
قل ارايتم شركاءكم الذين تدعون من دون الل: 40. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Tell me or inform me (what) do you think about your (so-called) partner-gods to whom you call upon besides Allâh? Show me, what they have created of the earth. Or have they any share in the heavens? Or have We given them a Book, so that they act on clear proof therefrom? Nay, the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers) promise one another nothing but delusions." (35:40)
ان الله يمسك السماوات والارض ان تزولا ول: 41. Verily! Allâh grasps the heavens and the earth lest they should move away from their places, and if they were to move away from their places, there is not one that could grasp them after Him. Truly, He is Ever Most Forbearing, Oft-Forgiving.[3] (35:41)
واقسموا بالله جهد ايمانهم لئن جاءهم نذير: 42. And they swore by Allâh their most binding oath that if a warner came to them, they would be more guided than any of the nations (before them); yet when a warner (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) came to them, it increased in them nothing but flight (from the truth) (35:42)
استكبارا في الارض ومكر السيئ ولا يحيق ال: 43. (They took to flight because of their) arrogance in the land and their plotting of evil. But the evil plot encompasses only him who makes it. Then, can they expect anything (else) but the Sunnah (way of dealing) of the peoples of old? So no change will you find in Allâh’s Sunnah (way of dealing), and no turning off will you find in Allâh’s Sunnah (way of dealing). (35:43)
اولم يسيروا في الارض فينظروا كيف كان عاق: 44. Have they not travelled in the land, and seen what was the end of those before them - though they were superior to them in power? Allâh is not such that anything in the heavens or in the earth escapes Him. Verily, He is All-Knowing, All-Omnipotent. (35:44)
ولو يؤاخذ الله الناس بما كسبوا ما ترك عل: 45. And if Allâh were to punish men for that which they earned, He would not leave a moving (living) creature on the surface of the earth; but He gives them respite to an appointed term: and when their term comes, then verily, Allâh is Ever All-Seer of His slaves. (35:45)
والقران الحكيم: 2. By the Qur’ân, full of wisdom (i.e. full of laws, evidence, and proofs), (36:2)
انك لمن المرسلين: 3. Truly, you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are one of the Messengers, (36:3)
على صراط مستقيم: 4. On the Straight Path (i.e. on Allâh’s religion of Islâmic Monotheism). (36:4)
تنزيل العزيز الرحيم: 5. (This is a Revelation) sent down by the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful, (36:5)
لتنذر قوما ما انذر اباؤهم فهم غافلون: 6. In order that you may warn a people whose forefathers were not warned, so they are heedless. (36:6)
لقد حق القول على اكثرهم فهم لا يؤمنون: 7. Indeed the Word (of punishment) has proved true against most of them, so they will not believe. (36:7)
انا جعلنا في اعناقهم اغلالا فهي الى الاذ: 8. Verily We have put on their necks iron collars reaching to the chins, so that their heads are raised up. (36:8)
وجعلنا من بين ايديهم سدا ومن خلفهم سدا ف: 9. And We have put a barrier before them, and a barrier behind them, and We have covered them up, so that they cannot see. (36:9)
وسواء عليهم اانذرتهم ام لم تنذرهم لا يؤم: 10. It is the same to them whether you warn them or you warn them not, they will not believe. (36:10)
انما تنذر من اتبع الذكر وخشي الرحمن بالغ: 11. You can only warn him who follows the Reminder (the Qur’ân), and fears the Most Gracious (Allâh) unseen. Bear you to such one the glad tidings of forgiveness, and a generous reward (i.e. Paradise). (36:11)
انا نحن نحيي الموتى ونكتب ما قدموا واثار: 12. Verily, We give life to the dead, and We record that which they send before (them), and their traces[1] and all things We have recorded with numbers (as a record) in a Clear Book. (36:12)
واضرب لهم مثلا اصحاب القرية اذ جاءها الم: 13. And put forward to them a similitude: the (story of the) dwellers of the town, [it is said that the town was Antioch (Antâkiya)], when there came Messengers to them. (36:13)
اذ ارسلنا اليهم اثنين فكذبوهما فعززنا بث: 14. When We sent to them two Messengers, they belied them both; so We reinforced them with a third, and they said: "Verily we have been sent to you as Messengers." (36:14)
قالوا ما انتم الا بشر مثلنا وما انزل الر: 15. They (people of the town) said: "You are only human beings like ourselves, and the Most Gracious (Allâh) has revealed nothing. You are only telling lies." (36:15)
قالوا ربنا يعلم انا اليكم لمرسلون: 16. The Messengers said: "Our Lord knows that we have been sent as Messengers to you, (36:16)
وما علينا الا البلاغ المبين: 17. "And our duty is only to convey plainly (the Message)." (36:17)
قالوا انا تطيرنا بكم لئن لم تنتهوا لنرجم: 18. They (people) said: "For us, we see an evil omen from you: if you cease not, we will surely stone you, and a painful torment will touch you from us." (36:18)
قالوا طائركم معكم ائن ذكرتم بل انتم قوم: 19. They (Messengers) said: "Your evil omens be with you! (Do you call it "evil omen") because you are admonished? Nay, but you are a people Musrifûn (transgressing all bounds by committing all kinds of great sins, and by disobeying Allâh). (36:19)
وجاء من اقصى المدينة رجل يسعى قال يا قوم: 20. And there came a man running from the farthest part of the town. He said: "O my people! Obey the Messengers. (36:20)
اتبعوا من لا يسالكم اجرا وهم مهتدون: 21. "Obey those who ask no wages of you (for themselves), and who are rightly guided. (36:21)
وما لي لا اعبد الذي فطرني واليه ترجعون: 22. "And why should I not worship Him (Allâh Alone) Who has created me and to Whom you shall be returned. (36:22)
ااتخذ من دونه الهة ان يردن الرحمن بضر لا: 23. "Shall I take besides Him âlihah (gods)? If the Most Gracious (Allâh) intends me any harm, their intercession will be of no use for me whatsoever, nor can they save me? (36:23)
اني اذا لفي ضلال مبين: 24. "Then verily, I should be in plain error. (36:24)
اني امنت بربكم فاسمعون: 25. "Verily! I have believed in your Lord, so listen to me!" (36:25)
قيل ادخل الجنة قال يا ليت قومي يعلمون: 26. It was said (to him when the disbelievers killed him): "Enter Paradise." He said: "Would that my people knew[2] (36:26)
بما غفر لي ربي وجعلني من المكرمين: 27. "That my Lord (Allâh) has forgiven me, and made me of the honoured ones!" (36:27)
وما انزلنا على قومه من بعده من جند من ال: 28. And We sent not against his people after him a host from the heaven, nor was it needful for Us to send (such a thing). (36:28)
ان كانت الا صيحة واحدة فاذا هم خامدون: 29. It was but one Saihah (shout) and lo! they (all) were still (silent,dead,destroyed). (36:29)
يا حسرة على العباد ما ياتيهم من رسول الا: 30. Alas for mankind! There never came a Messenger to them but they used to mock at him. (36:30)
الم يروا كم اهلكنا قبلهم من القرون انهم: 31. Do they not see how many of the generations We have destroyed before them? Verily, they will not return to them. (36:31)
وان كل لما جميع لدينا محضرون: 32. And surely, all - everyone of them will be brought before Us. (36:32)
واية لهم الارض الميتة احييناها واخرجنا م: 33. And a sign for them is the dead land. We give it life, and We bring forth from it grains, so that they eat thereof. (36:33)
وجعلنا فيها جنات من نخيل واعناب وفجرنا ف: 34. And We have made therein gardens of date-palms and grapes, and We have caused springs of water to gush forth therein. (36:34)
لياكلوا من ثمره وما عملته ايديهم افلا يش: 35. So that they may eat of the fruit thereof - and their hands made it not. Will they not, then, give thanks? (36:35)
سبحان الذي خلق الازواج كلها مما تنبت الا: 36. Glory be to Him Who has created all the pairs of that which the earth produces, as well as of their own (human) kind (male and female), and of that which they know not. (36:36)
واية لهم الليل نسلخ منه النهار فاذا هم م: 37. And a sign for them is the night. We withdraw therefrom the day, and behold, they are in darkness. (36:37)
والشمس تجري لمستقر لها ذلك تقدير العزيز: 38. And the sun runs on its fixed course for a term (appointed). That is the Decree of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing. (36:38)
والقمر قدرناه منازل حتى عاد كالعرجون الق: 39. And the moon, We have measured for it mansions (to traverse) till it returns like the old dried curved date stalk. (36:39)
لا الشمس ينبغي لها ان تدرك القمر ولا الل: 40. It is not for the sun to overtake the moon, nor does the night outstrip the day. They all float, each in an orbit. (36:40)
واية لهم انا حملنا ذريتهم في الفلك المشح: 41. And an Ayâh (sign) for them is that We bore their offspring in the laden ship [of Nûh (Noah)]. (36:41)
وخلقنا لهم من مثله ما يركبون: 42. And We have created for them of the like thereunto, on which they ride. (36:42)
وان نشا نغرقهم فلا صريخ لهم ولا هم ينقذو: 43. And if We will, We shall drown them, and there will be no shout (or helper) for them (to hear their cry for help), nor will they be saved. (36:43)
الا رحمة منا ومتاعا الى حين: 44. Unless it be a mercy from Us, and as an enjoyment for a while. (36:44)
واذا قيل لهم اتقوا ما بين ايديكم وما خلف: 45. And when it is said to them: "Fear of that which is before you (worldly torments), and that which is behind you (torments in the Hereafter), in order that you may receive Mercy (i.e. if you believe in Allâh’s religion - Islâmic Monotheism, and avoid polytheism, and obey Allâh with righteous deeds)." (36:45)
واذا قيل لهم انفقوا مما رزقكم الله قال ا: 47. And when it is said to them: "Spend of that with which Allâh has provided you," those who disbelieve say to those who believe: "Shall we feed those whom, if Allâh willed, He (Himself) would have fed? You are only in a plain error." (36:47)
ما ينظرون الا صيحة واحدة تاخذهم وهم يخصم: 49. They await only but a single Saihah (shout), which will seize them while they are disputing! (36:49)
فلا يستطيعون توصية ولا الى اهلهم يرجعون: 50. Then they will not be able to make bequest, nor they will return to their family. (36:50)
ونفخ في الصور فاذا هم من الاجداث الى ربه: 51. And the Trumpet will be blown (i.e. the second blowing) and behold from the graves they will come out quickly to their Lord. (36:51)
قالوا يا ويلنا من بعثنا من مرقدنا هذا ما: 52. They will say: "Woe to us! Who has raised us up from our place of sleep." (It will be said to them): "This is what the Most Gracious (Allâh) had promised, and the Messengers spoke truth!" (36:52)
ان كانت الا صيحة واحدة فاذا هم جميع لدين: 53. It will be but a single Saihah (shout), so behold they will all be brought up before Us! (36:53)
فاليوم لا تظلم نفس شيئا ولا تجزون الا ما: 54. This Day (Day of Resurrection), none will be wronged in anything, nor will you be requited anything except that which you used to do. (36:54)
ان اصحاب الجنة اليوم في شغل فاكهون: 55. Verily, the dwellers of the Paradise, that Day, will be busy with joyful things. (36:55)
هم وازواجهم في ظلال على الارائك متكئون: 56. They and their wives will be in pleasant shade, reclining on thrones. (36:56)
لهم فيها فاكهة ولهم ما يدعون: 57. They will have therein fruits (of all kinds) and all that they ask for. (36:57)
سلام قولا من رب رحيم: 58. (It will be said to them): Salâm (peace be on you) - a Word from the Lord (Allâh), Most Merciful. (36:58)
وامتازوا اليوم ايها المجرمون: 59. (It will be said): "And O you Mujrimûn (criminals, polytheists, sinners, disbelievers in the Islâmic Monotheism, wicked evil ones)! Get you apart this Day (from the believers). (36:59)
الم اعهد اليكم يا بني ادم ان لا تعبدوا ا: 60. Did I not command you, O Children of Adam, that you should not worship Shaitân (Satan). Verily, he is a plain enemy to you. (36:60)
وان اعبدوني هذا صراط مستقيم: 61. And that you should worship Me [Alone - Islâmic Monotheism, and set up not rivals, associate-gods with Me]. That is the Straight Path.[3] (36:61)
ولقد اضل منكم جبلا كثيرا افلم تكونوا تعق: 62. And indeed he (Satan) did lead astray a great multitude of you. Did you not, then, understand? (36:62)
هذه جهنم التي كنتم توعدون: 63. This is Hell which you were promised! (36:63)
اصلوها اليوم بما كنتم تكفرون: 64. Burn therein this Day, for that you used to disbelieve.[4] (36:64)
اليوم نختم على افواههم وتكلمنا ايديهم وت: 65. This Day, We shall seal up their mouths, and their hands will speak to Us, and their legs will bear witness to what they used to earn. (It is said that one’s left thigh will be the first to bear the witness). [Tafsir At-Tabarî,] (36:65)
ولو نشاء لطمسنا على اعينهم فاستبقوا الصر: 66. And if it had been Our Will, We would surely have wiped out (blinded) their eyes, so that they would struggle for the Path, how then would they see? (36:66)
ولو نشاء لمسخناهم على مكانتهم فما استطاع: 67. And if it had been Our Will, We could have transformed them (into animals or lifeless objects) in their places. Then they would have been unable to go forward (move about) nor they could have turned back.[5] (36:67)
ومن نعمره ننكسه في الخلق افلا يعقلون: 68. And he whom We grant long life - We reverse him in creation (weakness after strength). Will they not then understand? (36:68)
وما علمناه الشعر وما ينبغي له ان هو الا: 69. And We have not taught him (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) poetry, nor is it suitable for him. This is only a Reminder and a plain Qur’ân. (36:69)
لينذر من كان حيا ويحق القول على الكافرين: 70. That he or it (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم or the Qur’ân) may give warning to him who is living (a healthy minded - the believer), and that Word (charge) may be justified against the disbelievers (dead, as they reject the warnings). (36:70)
اولم يروا انا خلقنا لهم مما عملت ايدينا: 71. Do they not see that We have created for them of what Our Hands have created the cattle, so that they are their owners. (36:71)
وذللناها لهم فمنها ركوبهم ومنها ياكلون: 72. And We have subdued them unto them so that some of them they have for riding and some they eat. (36:72)
ولهم فيها منافع ومشارب افلا يشكرون: 73. And they have (other) benefits from them, and they get (milk) to drink. Will they not then be grateful? (36:73)
واتخذوا من دون الله الهة لعلهم ينصرون: 74. And they have taken besides Allâh âlihah (gods), hoping that they might be helped (by those so-called gods). (36:74)
لا يستطيعون نصرهم وهم لهم جند محضرون: 75. They cannot help them, but they will be brought forward as a troop against those who worshipped them (at the time of Reckoning). (36:75)
فلا يحزنك قولهم انا نعلم ما يسرون وما يع: 76. So let not their speech, then, grieve you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). Verily, We know what they conceal and what they reveal. (36:76)
اولم ير الانسان انا خلقناه من نطفة فاذا: 77. Does not man see that We have created him from Nutfah (mixed male and female sexual discharge - semen drops). Yet behold he (stands forth) as an open opponent. (36:77)
وضرب لنا مثلا ونسي خلقه قال من يحيي العظ: 78. And he puts forth for Us a parable, and forgets his own creation. He says: "Who will give life to these bones after they are rotten and have become dust?" (36:78)
قل يحييها الذي انشاها اول مرة وهو بكل خل: 79. Say: (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) "He will give life to them Who created them for the first time! And He is the All-Knower of every creation!" (36:79)
الذي جعل لكم من الشجر الاخضر نارا فاذا ا: 80. He Who produces for you fire out of the green tree, when behold you kindle therewith. (36:80)
اوليس الذي خلق السماوات والارض بقادر على: 81. Is not He Who created the heavens and the earth, Able to create the like of them? Yes, indeed! He is the All-Knowing Supreme Creator. (36:81)
انما امره اذا اراد شيئا ان يقول له كن في: 82. Verily, His Command, when He intends a thing, is only that He says to it, "Be!" - and it is! (36:82)
فسبحان الذي بيده ملكوت كل شيء واليه ترجع: 83. So glorified be He and exalted above all that they associate with Him, and in Whose Hands is the dominion of all things: and to Him you shall be returned. (36:83)
والصافات صفا: 1. By those (angels) ranged in ranks (or rows). (37:1)
فالزاجرات زجرا: 2. By those (angels) who drive the clouds in a good way. (37:2)
فالتاليات ذكرا: 3. By those (angels) who bring the Book and the Qur’ân from Allâh to mankind [Tafsir Ibn Kathîr]. (37:3)
ان الهكم لواحد: 4. Verily your Ilâh (God) is indeed One (i.e. Allâh): (37:4)
رب السماوات والارض وما بينهما ورب المشار: 5. Lord of the heavens and of the earth, and all that is between them, and Lord of every point of the sun’s risings.[1] (37:5)
انا زينا السماء الدنيا بزينة الكواكب: 6. Verily We have adorned the near heaven with the stars (for beauty).[2] (37:6)
وحفظا من كل شيطان مارد: 7. And to guard against every rebellious devil. (37:7)
لا يسمعون الى الملا الاعلى ويقذفون من كل: 8. They cannot listen to the higher group (angels) for they are pelted from every side. (37:8)
دحورا ولهم عذاب واصب: 9. Outcast, and theirs is a constant (or painful) torment. (37:9)
الا من خطف الخطفة فاتبعه شهاب ثاقب: 10. Except such as snatch away something by stealing, and they are pursued by a flaming fire of piercing brightness. (37:10)
فاستفتهم اهم اشد خلقا ام من خلقنا انا خل: 11. Then ask them (i.e. these polytheists, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Are they stronger as creation, or those (others like the heavens and the earth and the mountains) whom We have created?" Verily, We created them of a sticky clay. (37:11)
بل عجبت ويسخرون: 12. Nay, you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) wondered (at their insolence) while they mock (at you and at the Qur’ân). (37:12)
واذا ذكروا لا يذكرون: 13. And when they are reminded, they pay no attention. (37:13)
واذا راوا اية يستسخرون: 14. And when they see an Ayâh (a sign, or an evidence) from Allâh, they mock at it. (37:14)
وقالوا ان هذا الا سحر مبين: 15. And they say: "This is nothing but evident magic! (37:15)
ااذا متنا وكنا ترابا وعظاما اانا لمبعوثو: 16. "When we are dead and have become dust and bones, shall we (then) verily be resurrected? (37:16)
قل نعم وانتم داخرون: 18. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Yes, and you shall then be humiliated." (37:18)
فانما هي زجرة واحدة فاذا هم ينظرون: 19. It will be a single Zajrah [shout (i.e. the second blowing of the Trumpet)], and behold, they will be staring! (37:19)
وقالوا يا ويلنا هذا يوم الدين: 20. They will say: "Woe to us! This is the Day of Recompense!" (37:20)
هذا يوم الفصل الذي كنتم به تكذبون: 21. (It will be said): "This is the Day of Judgement which you used to deny." (37:21)
احشروا الذين ظلموا وازواجهم وما كانوا يع: 22. (It will be said to the angels): "Assemble those who did wrong, together with their companions (from the devils) and what they used to worship, (37:22)
من دون الله فاهدوهم الى صراط الجحيم: 23. "Instead of Allâh, and lead them on to the way of flaming Fire (Hell); (37:23)
وقفوهم انهم مسئولون: 24. "But stop them, verily they are to be questioned. (37:24)
ما لكم لا تناصرون: 25. "What is the matter with you? Why do you not help one another (as you used to do in the world)?" (37:25)
بل هم اليوم مستسلمون: 26. Nay, but that Day they shall surrender. (37:26)
واقبل بعضهم على بعض يتساءلون: 27. And they will turn to one another and question one another. (37:27)
قالوا انكم كنتم تاتوننا عن اليمين: 28. They will say: "It was you who used to come to us from the right side [i.e. from the right side of one of us and beautify for us every evil, enjoin on us polytheism, and stop us from the truth i.e. Islâmic Monotheism and from every good deed]." (37:28)
قالوا بل لم تكونوا مؤمنين: 29. They will reply: "Nay, you yourselves were not believers. (37:29)
وما كان لنا عليكم من سلطان بل كنتم قوما: 30. "And we had no authority over you. Nay! But you were Tâghûn (transgressing) people (polytheists, and disbelievers). (37:30)
فحق علينا قول ربنا انا لذائقون: 31. "So now the Word of our Lord has been justified against us, that we shall certainly (have to) taste (the torment). (37:31)
فاغويناكم انا كنا غاوين: 32. "So we led you astray because we were ourselves astray." (37:32)
فانهم يومئذ في العذاب مشتركون: 33. Then verily, that Day, they will (all) share in the torment. (37:33)
انا كذلك نفعل بالمجرمين: 34. Certainly, that is how We deal with Al-Mujrimûn (polytheists, sinners, disbelievers, criminals, the disobedient to Allâh). (37:34)
انهم كانوا اذا قيل لهم لا اله الا الله ي: 35. Truly, when it was said to them: Lâ ilâha illallâh "(none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh)," they puffed themselves up with pride[3] (i.e. denied it). (37:35)
ويقولون ائنا لتاركو الهتنا لشاعر مجنون: 36. And (they) said: "Are we going to abandon our âlihah (gods) for the sake of a mad poet?" (37:36)
بل جاء بالحق وصدق المرسلين: 37. Nay! he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) has come with the truth (i.e. Allâh’s religion - Islâmic Monotheism and this Qur’ân) and he confirms the Messengers (before him who brought Allâh’s religion - Islâmic Monotheism). (37:37)
انكم لذائقو العذاب الاليم: 38. Verily, you (pagans of Makkah) are going to taste the painful torment; (37:38)
وما تجزون الا ما كنتم تعملون: 39. And you will be requited nothing except for what you used to do (evil deeds, sins, and Allâh’s disobedience which you used to do in this world) (37:39)
الا عباد الله المخلصين: 40. Save the chosen slaves of Allâh (i.e. the true believers of Islâmic Monotheism). (37:40)
اولئك لهم رزق معلوم: 41. For them there will be a known provision (in Paradise), (37:41)
فواكه وهم مكرمون: 42. Fruits; and they shall be honoured, (37:42)
يطاف عليهم بكاس من معين: 45. Round them will be passed a cup of pure wine - (37:45)
لا فيها غول ولا هم عنها ينزفون: 47. Neither will they have Ghoul (any kind of hurt, abdominal pain, headache, a sin) from that, nor will they suffer intoxication therefrom. (37:47)
وعندهم قاصرات الطرف عين: 48. And beside them will be Qâsirât-at-Tarf [chaste females (wives), restraining their glances (desiring none except their husbands)], with wide and beautiful eyes.[4] (37:48)
كانهن بيض مكنون: 49. (Delicate and pure) as if they were (hidden) eggs (well) preserved. (37:49)
فاقبل بعضهم على بعض يتساءلون: 50. Then they will turn to one another, mutually questioning. (37:50)
قال قائل منهم اني كان لي قرين: 51. A speaker of them will say: "Verily, I had a companion (in the world), (37:51)
يقول اانك لمن المصدقين: 52. Who used to say: "Are you among those who believe (in resurrection after death). (37:52)
ااذا متنا وكنا ترابا وعظاما اانا لمدينون: 53. "(That) when we die and become dust and bones, shall we indeed (be raised up) to receive reward or punishment (according to our deeds)?" (37:53)
فاطلع فراه في سواء الجحيم: 55. So he looked down and saw him in the midst of the Fire. (37:55)
قال تالله ان كدت لتردين: 56. He said: "By Allâh! You have nearly ruined me. (37:56)
ولولا نعمة ربي لكنت من المحضرين: 57. "Had it not been for the Grace of my Lord, I would certainly have been among those brought forth (to Hell)." (37:57)
افما نحن بميتين: 58. (The dwellers of Paradise will say) "Are we then not to die (any more)? (37:58)
الا موتتنا الاولى وما نحن بمعذبين: 59. Except our first death, and we shall not be punished? (after we have entered Paradise)." (37:59)
ان هذا لهو الفوز العظيم: 60. Truly, this is the supreme success! (37:60)
لمثل هذا فليعمل العاملون: 61. For the like of this let the workers work. (37:61)
اذلك خير نزلا ام شجرة الزقوم: 62. Is that (Paradise) better entertainment or the tree of Zaqqûm (a horrible tree in Hell)? (37:62)
انا جعلناها فتنة للظالمين: 63. Truly We have made it (as) a trail for the Zâlimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, wrong-doers). (37:63)
انها شجرة تخرج في اصل الجحيم: 64. Verily, it is a tree that springs out of the bottom of Hell-fire, (37:64)
طلعها كانه رءوس الشياطين: 65. The shoots of its fruit-stalks are like the heads of Shayâtîn (devils); (37:65)
فانهم لاكلون منها فمالئون منها البطون: 66. Truly, they will eat thereof and fill their bellies therewith. (37:66)
ثم ان لهم عليها لشوبا من حميم: 67. Then on the top of that they will be given boiling water to drink so that it becomes a mixture (of boiling water and Zaqqûm in their bellies). (37:67)
ثم ان مرجعهم لالى الجحيم: 68. Then thereafter, verily, their return is to the flaming fire of Hell. (37:68)
انهم الفوا اباءهم ضالين: 69. Verily, they found their fathers on the wrong path; (37:69)
فهم على اثارهم يهرعون: 70. So they (too) hastened in their footsteps! (37:70)
ولقد ضل قبلهم اكثر الاولين: 71. And indeed most of the men of old went astray before them; (37:71)
ولقد ارسلنا فيهم منذرين: 72. And indeed We sent among them warners (Messengers); (37:72)
فانظر كيف كان عاقبة المنذرين: 73. Then see what was the end of those who were warned (but heeded not). (37:73)
ولقد نادانا نوح فلنعم المجيبون: 75. And indeed Nûh (Noah) invoked Us, and We are the Best of those who answer (the request). (37:75)
ونجيناه واهله من الكرب العظيم: 76. And We rescued him and his family from the great distress (i.e. drowning), (37:76)
وجعلنا ذريته هم الباقين: 77. And, his progeny, them We made the survivors (i.e. Shem, Ham and Japheth). (37:77)
وتركنا عليه في الاخرين: 78. And left for him (a goodly remembrance) among the later generations: (37:78)
سلام على نوح في العالمين: 79. "Salâm (peace) be upon Nûh (Noah) (from Us) among the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)!" (37:79)
انا كذلك نجزي المحسنين: 80. Verily, thus We reward the Muhsinûn (good-doers - See V.2:112). (37:80)
انه من عبادنا المؤمنين: 81. Verily, he [Nûh (Noah)] was one of Our believing slaves. (37:81)
وان من شيعته لابراهيم: 83. And, verily, among those who followed his [Nûh’s (Noah)] way (Islâmic Monotheism) was Ibrâhîm (Abraham). (37:83)
اذ جاء ربه بقلب سليم: 84. When he came to his Lord with a pure heart [attached to Allâh Alone and none else, worshipping none but Allâh Alone - true Islâmic Monotheism, pure from the filth of polytheism]. (37:84)
اذ قال لابيه وقومه ماذا تعبدون: 85. When he said to his father and to his people: "What is it that which you worship? (37:85)
ائفكا الهة دون الله تريدون: 86. "Is it a falsehood - âlihah (gods) other than Allâh - that you desire? (37:86)
فما ظنكم برب العالمين: 87. "Then what think you about the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn, and all that exists)?" (37:87)
فنظر نظرة في النجوم: 88. Then he cast a glance at the stars, (37:88)
فقال اني سقيم: 89. And he said: "Verily, I am sick[5] (with plague). [He did this trick to remain in their temple of idols to destroy them and not to accompany them to the pagan feast)]." (37:89)
فتولوا عنه مدبرين: 90. So they turned away from him, and departed (for fear of the disease). (37:90)
فراغ الى الهتهم فقال الا تاكلون: 91. Then he turned to their âlihah (gods) and said: "Will you not eat (of the offering before you)? (37:91)
ما لكم لا تنطقون: 92. "What is the matter with you that you speak not?" (37:92)
فراغ عليهم ضربا باليمين: 93. Then he turned upon them, striking (them) with (his) right hand. (37:93)
فاقبلوا اليه يزفون: 94. Then they (the worshippers of idols) came, towards him, hastening. (37:94)
قال اتعبدون ما تنحتون: 95. He said: "Worship you that which you (yourselves) carve? (37:95)
والله خلقكم وما تعملون: 96. "While Allâh has created you and what you make!" (37:96)
قالوا ابنوا له بنيانا فالقوه في الجحيم: 97. They said: "Build for him a building (it is said that the building was like a furnace) and throw him into the blazing fire!" (37:97)
فارادوا به كيدا فجعلناهم الاسفلين: 98. So they plotted a plot against him, but We made them the lowest. (37:98)
وقال اني ذاهب الى ربي سيهدين: 99. And he said (after his rescue from the fire): "Verily, I am going to my Lord. He will guide me!" (37:99)
رب هب لي من الصالحين: 100. "My Lord! Grant me (offspring) from the righteous." (37:100)
فبشرناه بغلام حليم: 101. So We gave him the glad tidings of a forbearing boy. (37:101)
فلما بلغ معه السعي قال يا بني اني ارى في: 102. And, when he (his son) was old enough to walk with him, he said: "O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you (offering you in sacrifice to Allâh). So look what you think!" He said: "O my father! Do that which you are commanded, Inshâ’ Allâh (if Allâh wills), you shall find me of As-Sâbirûn (the patient)." (37:102)
فلما اسلما وتله للجبين: 103. Then, when they had both submitted themselves (to the Will of Allâh), and he had laid him prostrate on his forehead (or on the side of his forehead for slaughtering); (37:103)
وناديناه ان يا ابراهيم: 104. We called out to him: "O Abraham! (37:104)
قد صدقت الرؤيا انا كذلك نجزي المحسنين: 105. You have fulfilled the dream!" Verily thus do We reward the Muhsinûn (good-doers - See 2:112). (37:105)
ان هذا لهو البلاء المبين: 106. Verily, that indeed was a manifest trial. (37:106)
وفديناه بذبح عظيم: 107. And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice (i.e. كبش - a ram); (37:107)
كذلك نجزي المحسنين: 110. Thus indeed do We reward the Muhsinûn (good-doers - See V.2:112). (37:110)
وبشرناه باسحاق نبيا من الصالحين: 112. And We gave him the glad tidings of Ishâq (Isaac) - a Prophet from the righteous. (37:112)
وباركنا عليه وعلى اسحاق ومن ذريتهما محسن: 113. We blessed him and Ishâq (Isaac). And of their progeny are (some) that do right, and some that plainly wrong themselves. (37:113)
ولقد مننا على موسى وهارون: 114. And, indeed We gave Our Grace to Mûsâ (Moses) and Hârûn (Aaron). (37:114)
ونجيناهما وقومهما من الكرب العظيم: 115. And We saved them and their people from the great distress, (37:115)
ونصرناهم فكانوا هم الغالبين: 116. And helped them, so that they became the victors; (37:116)
واتيناهما الكتاب المستبين: 117. And We gave them the clear Scripture; (37:117)
وهديناهما الصراط المستقيم: 118. And guided them to the Right Path. (37:118)
وتركنا عليهما في الاخرين: 119. And We left for them (a goodly remembrance) among the later generations. (37:119)
انهما من عبادنا المؤمنين: 122. Verily, they were two of Our believing slaves. (37:122)
وان الياس لمن المرسلين: 123. And verily, Iliyâs (Elias) was one of the Messengers. (37:123)
اذ قال لقومه الا تتقون: 124. When he said to his people: "Will you not fear Allâh? (37:124)
اتدعون بعلا وتذرون احسن الخالقين: 125. "Will you call upon Ba‘l (a well known idol of his nation whom they used to worship) and forsake the Best of creators, (37:125)
الله ربكم ورب ابائكم الاولين: 126. "Allâh, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?" (37:126)
فكذبوه فانهم لمحضرون: 127. But they denied him [Iliyâs (Elias)], so they will certainly be brought forth (to the punishment), (37:127)
وان لوطا لمن المرسلين: 133. And verily, Lût (Lot) was one of the Messengers. (37:133)
اذ نجيناه واهله اجمعين: 134. When We saved him and his family, all, (37:134)
وانكم لتمرون عليهم مصبحين: 137. Verily, you pass by them in the morning (37:137)
وبالليل افلا تعقلون: 138. And at night; will you not then reflect? (37:138)
وان يونس لمن المرسلين: 139. And, verily, Yûnus (Jonah) was one of the Messengers. (37:139)
اذ ابق الى الفلك المشحون: 140. When he ran to the laden ship: (37:140)
فساهم فكان من المدحضين: 141. Then he (agreed to) cast lots, and he was among the losers. (37:141)
فالتقمه الحوت وهو مليم: 142. Then a (big) fish swallowed him as he had done an act worthy of blame. (37:142)
فلولا انه كان من المسبحين: 143. Had he not been of them who glorify Allâh, (37:143)
للبث في بطنه الى يوم يبعثون: 144. He would have indeed remained inside its belly (the fish) till the Day of Resurrection. (37:144)
فنبذناه بالعراء وهو سقيم: 145. But We cast him forth on the naked shore while he was sick, (37:145)
وانبتنا عليه شجرة من يقطين: 146. And We caused a plant of gourd to grow over him. (37:146)
وارسلناه الى مائة الف او يزيدون: 147. And We sent him to a hundred thousand (people) or even more. (37:147)
فامنوا فمتعناهم الى حين: 148. And they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while. (37:148)
فاستفتهم الربك البنات ولهم البنون: 149. Now ask them (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Are there (only) daughters for your Lord and sons for them?" (37:149)
ام خلقنا الملائكة اناثا وهم شاهدون: 150. Or did We create the angels female while they were witnesses? (37:150)
الا انهم من افكهم ليقولون: 151. Verily, it is of their falsehood that they (Quraish pagans) say: (37:151)
ولد الله وانهم لكاذبون: 152. "Allâh has begotten [offspring - the angels being the daughters of Allâh)?" And, verily, they are liars! (37:152)
اصطفى البنات على البنين: 153. Has He (then) chosen daughters rather than sons? (37:153)
ما لكم كيف تحكمون: 154. What is the matter with you? How do you decide? (37:154)
ام لكم سلطان مبين: 156. Or is there for you a plain authority? (37:156)
فاتوا بكتابكم ان كنتم صادقين: 157. Then bring your Book if you are truthful! (37:157)
وجعلوا بينه وبين الجنة نسبا ولقد علمت ال: 158. And they have invented a kinship between Him and the jinn, but the jinn know well that they have indeed to appear (before Him) (i.e. they will be called to account). (37:158)
سبحان الله عما يصفون: 159. Glorified be Allâh! (He is free) from what they attribute unto Him! (37:159)
فانكم وما تعبدون: 161. So, verily you (pagans) and those whom you worship (idols) (37:161)
ما انتم عليه بفاتنين: 162. Cannot lead astray [turn away from Him (Allâh) anyone of the believers], (37:162)
الا من هو صال الجحيم: 163. Except those who are predestined to burn in Hell! (37:163)
وما منا الا له مقام معلوم: 164. And there is not one of us (angels) but has his known place (or position); (37:164)
وانا لنحن الصافون: 165. And verily, we (angels), we stand in rows (for the prayers as you Muslims stand in rows for your prayers); (37:165)
وانا لنحن المسبحون: 166. And verily, we (angels), indeed are those who glorify (Allâh’s Praises i.e. perform prayers). (37:166)
وان كانوا ليقولون: 167. And indeed they (Arab pagans) used to say: (37:167)
لو ان عندنا ذكرا من الاولين: 168. "If we had a reminder as had the men of old (before the coming of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم as a Messenger of Allâh), (37:168)
لكنا عباد الله المخلصين: 169. "We would have indeed been the chosen slaves of Allâh (true believers of Islâmic Monotheism)!" (37:169)
فكفروا به فسوف يعلمون: 170. But (now that the Qur’ân has come) they disbelieve therein (i.e. in the Qur’ân and in Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم , and all that he brought, - the Divine Revelation), so they will come to know![6] (37:170)
ولقد سبقت كلمتنا لعبادنا المرسلين: 171. And, verily, Our Word has gone forth of old for Our slaves, the Messengers, (37:171)
انهم لهم المنصورون: 172. That they verily would be made triumphant, (37:172)
وان جندنا لهم الغالبون: 173. And that Our hosts! they verily would be the victors. (37:173)
فتول عنهم حتى حين: 174. So turn away (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from them for a while, (37:174)
وابصرهم فسوف يبصرون: 175. And watch them and they shall see (the punishment)! (37:175)
فاذا نزل بساحتهم فساء صباح المنذرين: 177. Then, when it descends in their courtyard (i.e. near to them), evil will be the morning for those who had been warned! (37:177)
وتول عنهم حتى حين: 178. So turn (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) away from them for a while, (37:178)
وابصر فسوف يبصرون: 179. And watch and they shall see (the torment)! (37:179)
سبحان ربك رب العزة عما يصفون: 180. Glorified be your Lord, the Lord of Honour and Power! (He is free) from what they attribute unto Him! (37:180)
وسلام على المرسلين: 181. And peace be on the Messengers! (37:181)
والحمد لله رب العالمين: 182. And all the praises and thanks be to Allâh, Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists). (37:182)
ص والقران ذي الذكر:
1. Sâd.
[These letters (Sâd etc.) are one of the miracles of the Quran and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings]. By the Quran full of reminding. (38:1)
بل الذين كفروا في عزة وشقاق: 2. Nay, those who disbelieve are in false pride and opposition. (38:2)
كم اهلكنا من قبلهم من قرن فنادوا ولات حي: 3. How many a generation have We destroyed before them! And they cried out when there was no longer time for escape. (38:3)
وعجبوا ان جاءهم منذر منهم وقال الكافرون: 4. And they (Arab pagans) wonder that a warner (Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) has come to them from among themselves. And the disbelievers say: "This (Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is a sorcerer, a liar. (38:4)
اجعل الالهة الها واحدا ان هذا لشيء عجاب: 5. "Has he made the âlihah (gods) (all) into One Ilâh (God - Allâh). Verily, this is a curious thing!" (38:5)
وانطلق الملا منهم ان امشوا واصبروا على ا: 6. And the leaders among them went about (saying): "Go on, and remain constant to your âlihah (gods)! Verily, this is a thing designed (against you)! (38:6)
ما سمعنا بهذا في الملة الاخرة ان هذا الا: 7. "We have not heard (the like) of this in the religion of these later days (i.e. Christianity). This is nothing but an invention! [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi] (38:7)
اانزل عليه الذكر من بيننا بل هم في شك من: 8. "Has the Reminder been sent down to him (alone) from among us?" Nay, but they are in doubt about My Reminder (this Qur’ân)! Nay, but they have not tasted (My) Torment! (38:8)
ام عندهم خزائن رحمة ربك العزيز الوهاب: 9. Or have they the treasures of the Mercy of your Lord, the All-Mighty, the Real Bestower? (38:9)
ام لهم ملك السماوات والارض وما بينهما فل: 10. Or is it that the dominion of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them is theirs? If so, let them ascend up with means (to the heavens)! (38:10)
جند ما هنالك مهزوم من الاحزاب: 11. (As they denied Allâh’s Message) they will be a defeated host like the Confederates of the old times (who were defeated). (38:11)
كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح وعاد وفرعون ذو الاوتا: 12. Before them (were many who) belied (Messengers) - the people of Nûh (Noah); and ‘Âd; and Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) the man of stakes (with which he used to punish the people), (38:12)
وثمود وقوم لوط واصحاب الايكة اولئك الاحز: 13. And Thamûd, and the people of Lût (Lot), and the Dwellers of the Wood: such were the Confederates. (38:13)
ان كل الا كذب الرسل فحق عقاب: 14. Not one of them but belied the Messengers; therefore My Torment was justified, (38:14)
وما ينظر هؤلاء الا صيحة واحدة ما لها من: 15. And these only wait for a single Saihah [shout (i.e. the blowing of the Trumpet by the angel Isrâfil -)] there will be no pause or ending thereto [till everything will perish except Allâh (the only God full of Majesty, Bounty and Honour)]. (38:15)
وقالوا ربنا عجل لنا قطنا قبل يوم الحساب: 16. They say: "Our Lord! Hasten to us Qittanâ (i.e. our Record of good and bad deeds so that we may see it) before the Day of Reckoning!" (38:16)
اصبر على ما يقولون واذكر عبدنا داوود ذا: 17. Be patient (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) of what they say, and remember Our slave Dâwûd (David), endued with power. Verily, he was ever oft-returning in all matters and in repentance (toward Allâh). (38:17)
انا سخرنا الجبال معه يسبحن بالعشي والاشر: 18. Verily, We made the mountains to glorify Our Praises with him [Dâwûd (David)] in the ‘Ashî (i.e. after the mid-day till sunset) and Ishrâq (i.e. after the sunrise till mid-day). (38:18)
والطير محشورة كل له اواب: 19. And (so did) the birds assembled: all obedient to him [Dâwûd (David)] [i.e. they came and glorified Allâh’s Praises along with him]. (Tafsir Al-Qurtubi ). (38:19)
وشددنا ملكه واتيناه الحكمة وفصل الخطاب: 20. We made his kingdom strong and gave him Al-Hikmah (Prophethood) and sound judgement in speech and decision. (38:20)
وهل اتاك نبا الخصم اذ تسوروا المحراب: 21. And has the news of the litigants reached you? When they climbed over the wall into (his) Mihrâb (a praying place or a private room); (38:21)
اذ دخلوا على داوود ففزع منهم قالوا لا تخ: 22. When they entered in upon Dâwûd (David), he was terrified of them. They said: "Fear not! (We are) two litigants, one of whom has wronged the other, therefore judge between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, and guide us to the Right Way. (38:22)
ان هذا اخي له تسع وتسعون نعجة ولي نعجة و: 23. Verily, this my brother (in religion) has ninety-nine ewes, while I have (only) one ewe, and he says: "Hand it over to me, and he overpowered me in speech." (38:23)
قال لقد ظلمك بسؤال نعجتك الى نعاجه وان ك: 24. [Dâwûd (David)] said (immediately without listening to the opponent): "He has wronged you in demanding your ewe in addition to his ewes. And, verily, many partners oppress one another, except those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and they are few." And Dâwûd (David) guessed that We have tried him and he sought Forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down prostrate and turned (to Allâh) in repentance. (38:24)
فغفرنا له ذلك وان له عندنا لزلفى وحسن ما: 25. So We forgave him that, and verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good place of (final) return (Paradise). (38:25)
يا داوود انا جعلناك خليفة في الارض فاحكم: 26. O Dâwûd (David)! Verily! We have placed you as a successor on the earth; so judge you between men in truth (and justice) and follow not your desire - for it will mislead you from the Path of Allâh. Verily, those who wander astray from the Path of Allâh (shall) have a severe torment, because they forgot the Day of Reckoning. (38:26)
وما خلقنا السماء والارض وما بينهما باطلا: 27. And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them without purpose! That is the consideration of those who disbelieve! Then woe to those who disbelieve (in Islâmic Monotheism) from the Fire! (38:27)
ام نجعل الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات كالم: 28. Shall We treat those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds as Mufsidûn (those who associate partners in worship with Allâh and commit crimes) on earth? Or shall We treat the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2) as the Fujjâr (criminals, disbelievers, the wicked)? (38:28)
كتاب انزلناه اليك مبارك ليدبروا اياته ول: 29. (This is) a Book (the Qur’ân) which We have sent down to you, full of blessings, that they may ponder over its Verses, and that men of understanding may remember. (38:29)
ووهبنا لداوود سليمان نعم العبد انه اواب: 30. And to Dâwûd (David) We gave Sulaimân (Solomon). How excellent a slave! Verily, he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)! (38:30)
اذ عرض عليه بالعشي الصافنات الجياد: 31. When there were displayed before him, in the afternoon, well trained horses of the highest breed [for Jihâd (holy fighting in Allâh’s Cause)]. (38:31)
فقال اني احببت حب الخير عن ذكر ربي حتى ت: 32. He said: "I did love the good (these horses) instead of remembering my Lord (in my ‘Asr prayer)" till the time was over, and (the sun) had hidden in the veil (of night). (38:32)
ردوها علي فطفق مسحا بالسوق والاعناق: 33. Then he said "Bring them (horses) back to me." Then he began to pass his hand over their legs and their necks (till the end of the display). (38:33)
ولقد فتنا سليمان والقينا على كرسيه جسدا: 34. And indeed, We did try Sulaimân (Solomon) and We placed on his throne Jasad (a devil, so he lost his kingdom for a while) and he did return (to Allâh with obedience and in repentance, and to his throne and kingdom by the Grace of Allâh). (38:34)
قال رب اغفر لي وهب لي ملكا لا ينبغي لاحد: 35. He said: "My Lord! Forgive me, and bestow upon me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me: Verily, You are the Bestower." (38:35)
فسخرنا له الريح تجري بامره رخاء حيث اصاب: 36. So, We subjected to him the wind; it blew gently by his order whithersoever he willed, (38:36)
والشياطين كل بناء وغواص: 37. And also the Shayâtîn (devils) from the jinn (including) every kind of builder and diver, (38:37)
واخرين مقرنين في الاصفاد: 38. And also others bound in fetters. (38:38)
هذا عطاؤنا فامنن او امسك بغير حساب: 39. [Allâh said to Sulaimân (Solomon)]: "This is Our gift: so spend you or withhold, no account will be asked (of you)." (38:39)
وان له عندنا لزلفى وحسن ماب: 40. And verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good (final) return (Paradise). (38:40)
واذكر عبدنا ايوب اذ نادى ربه اني مسني ال: 41. And remember Our slave Ayyûb (Job), when he invoked his Lord (saying): "Verily Shaitân (Satan) has touched me with distress (by ruining my health) and torment (by ruining my wealth)! (38:41)
اركض برجلك هذا مغتسل بارد وشراب: 42. (Allâh said to him): "Strike the ground with your foot: This is (a spring of) water to wash in, cool and a (refreshing) drink." (38:42)
ووهبنا له اهله ومثلهم معهم رحمة منا وذكر: 43. And We gave him (back) his family, and along with them the like thereof, as a Mercy from Us, and a Reminder for those who understand. (38:43)
وخذ بيدك ضغثا فاضرب به ولا تحنث انا وجدن: 44. "And take in your hand a bundle of thin grass and strike therewith (your wife), and break not your oath.[1] Truly! We found him patient. How excellent a slave! Verily he was ever oft-returning in repentance (to Us)! (38:44)
واذكر عبادنا ابراهيم واسحاق ويعقوب اولي: 45. And remember Our slaves, Ibrâhîm (Abraham), Ishâq (Isaac), and Ya‘qûb (Jacob), (all) owners of strength (in worshipping Us) and (also) of religious understanding. (38:45)
انا اخلصناهم بخالصة ذكرى الدار: 46. Verily, We did choose them by granting them (a good thing, - i.e.) the remembrance of the Home [in the Hereafter and they used to make the people remember it, and also they used to invite the people to obey Allâh and to do good deeds for the Hereafter]. (38:46)
وانهم عندنا لمن المصطفين الاخيار: 47. And they are with Us, verily, of the chosen and the best! (38:47)
واذكر اسماعيل واليسع وذا الكفل وكل من ال: 48. And remember Ismâ‘îl (Ishmael), Alyasa‘ (Elisha), and Dhul-Kifl (Isaiah), all are among the best. (38:48)
هذا ذكر وان للمتقين لحسن ماب: 49. This is a Reminder. And verily, for the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2) is a good final return (Paradise), (38:49)
جنات عدن مفتحة لهم الابواب: 50. ‘Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), whose doors will be opened for them. (38:50)
متكئين فيها يدعون فيها بفاكهة كثيرة وشرا: 51. Therein they will recline; therein they will call for fruits in abundance and drinks; (38:51)
وعندهم قاصرات الطرف اتراب: 52. And beside them will be Qâsirât-at-Tarf [chaste females (wives) restraining their glances (desiring none except their husbands)], (and) of equal ages. (38:52)
هذا ما توعدون ليوم الحساب: 53. This it is what you (Al-Muttaqûn - the pious - see V.2:2) are promised for the Day of Reckoning! (38:53)
ان هذا لرزقنا ما له من نفاد: 54. (It will be said to them)! Verily, this is Our Provision which will never finish. (38:54)
هذا وان للطاغين لشر ماب: 55. This is so! And for the Tâghûn (transgressors, the disobedient to Allâh and His Messenger - disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh, criminals) will be an evil final return (Fire). (38:55)
جهنم يصلونها فبئس المهاد: 56. Hell! Where they will burn, and worst (indeed) is that place to rest! (38:56)
هذا فليذوقوه حميم وغساق: 57. This is so! Then let them taste it - a boiling fluid and dirty wound discharges. (38:57)
واخر من شكله ازواج: 58. And other (torments) of similar kind - all together! (38:58)
هذا فوج مقتحم معكم لا مرحبا بهم انهم صال: 59. This is a troop entering with you (in Hell), no welcome for them! Verily, they shall burn in the Fire! (38:59)
قالوا بل انتم لا مرحبا بكم انتم قدمتموه: 60. (The followers of the misleaders will say): "Nay, you (too)! No welcome for you! It is you (misleaders) who brought this upon us (because you misled us in the world), so evil is this place to stay in!" (38:60)
قالوا ربنا من قدم لنا هذا فزده عذابا ضعف: 61. They will say: "Our Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, add to him a double torment in the Fire!" (38:61)
وقالوا ما لنا لا نرى رجالا كنا نعدهم من: 62. And they will say: "What is the matter with us that we see not men whom we used to count among the bad ones?" (38:62)
اتخذناهم سخريا ام زاغت عنهم الابصار: 63. Did we take them as an object of mockery, or have (our) eyes failed to perceive them?" (38:63)
ان ذلك لحق تخاصم اهل النار: 64. Verily, that is the very truth - the mutual dispute of the people of the Fire! (38:64)
قل انما انا منذر وما من اله الا الله الو: 65. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I am only a warner and there is no Ilâh (God) except Allâh (none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh) the One, the Irresistible, (38:65)
رب السماوات والارض وما بينهما العزيز الغ: 66. "The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving." (38:66)
قل هو نبا عظيم: 67. Say: "That (this Qur’ân) is a great news, (38:67)
ما كان لي من علم بالملا الاعلى اذ يختصمو: 69. "I had no knowledge of the chiefs (angels) on high when they were disputing and discussing (about the creation of Adam). (38:69)
ان يوحى الي الا انما انا نذير مبين: 70. "Only this has been revealed to me, that I am a plain warner." (38:70)
اذ قال ربك للملائكة اني خالق بشرا من طين: 71. (Remember) when your Lord said to the angels: "Truly I am going to create man from clay". (38:71)
الا ابليس استكبر وكان من الكافرين: 74. Except Iblîs (Satan): he was proud[2] and was one of the disbelievers. (38:74)
قال يا ابليس ما منعك ان تسجد لما خلقت بي: 75. (Allâh) said: "O Iblîs (Satan)! What prevents you from prostrating yourself to one whom I have created with Both My Hands[3]. Are you too proud (to fall prostrate to Adam) or are you one of the high exalted?" (38:75)
قال انا خير منه خلقتني من نار وخلقته من: 76. [Iblîs (Satan)] said: "I am better than he. You created me from fire, and You created him from clay." (38:76)
وان عليك لعنتي الى يوم الدين: 78. "And verily My Curse is on you till the Day of Recompense." (38:78)
قال فبعزتك لاغوينهم اجمعين: 82. [Iblîs (Satan)] said: "By Your Might, then I will surely mislead them all, (38:82)
قال فالحق والحق اقول: 84. (Allâh) said: "The Truth is - and the Truth I say - (38:84)
لاملان جهنم منك وممن تبعك منهم اجمعين: 85. That I will fill Hell with you [Iblîs (Satan)] and those of them (mankind) that follow you, together." (38:85)
قل ما اسالكم عليه من اجر وما انا من المت: 86. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "No wage do I ask of you for this (the Qur’ân), nor am I one of the Mutakallifûn (those who pretend and fabricate things which do not exist). (38:86)
ان هو الا ذكر للعالمين: 87. "It (this Qur’ân) is only a Reminder for all the ‘Âlamîn (mankind and jinn). (38:87)
ولتعلمن نباه بعد حين: 88. "And you shall certainly know the truth of it after a while". (38:88)
تنزيل الكتاب من الله العزيز الحكيم: 1. The revelation of this Book (the Qur’ân) is from Allâh, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. (39:1)
انا انزلنا اليك الكتاب بالحق فاعبد الله: 2. Verily We have sent down the Book to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) in truth: So worship Allâh (Alone) by doing religious deeds sincerely for Allâh’s sake only. (39:2)
الا لله الدين الخالص والذين اتخذوا من دو: 3. Surely the religion (i.e. the worship and the obedience) is for Allâh only. And those who take Auliyâ’ (protectors, helpers, lords, gods) besides Him (say): "We worship them only that they may bring us near to Allâh." Verily Allâh will judge between them concerning that wherein they differ. Truly, Allâh guides not him who is a liar, and a disbeliever. (39:3)
لو اراد الله ان يتخذ ولدا لاصطفى مما يخل: 4. Had Allâh willed to take a son (or offspring), He could have chosen whom He willed out of those whom He created. But glory be to Him! (He is above such things). He is Allâh, the One, the Irresistible[1]. (39:4)
خلق السماوات والارض بالحق يكور الليل على: 5. He has created the heavens and the earth with truth. He makes the night to go in the day and makes the day to go in the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon. Each running (on a fixed course) for an appointed term. Verily, He is the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving. (39:5)
خلقكم من نفس واحدة ثم جعل منها زوجها وان: 6. He created you (all) from a single person (Adam); then made from him his wife [Hawwâ’ (Eve)]. And He has sent down for you of cattle eight pairs (of the sheep, two, male and female; of the goats, two, male and female; of the oxen, two, male and female; and of the camels, two, male and female). He creates you in the wombs of your mothers: creation after creation in three veils of darkness. Such is Allâh your Lord. His is the kingdom. Lâ ilâha illâ Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). How then are you turned away? (39:6)
ان تكفروا فان الله غني عنكم ولا يرضى لعب: 7. If you disbelieve, then verily, Allâh is not in need of you; He likes not disbelief for His slaves. And if you are grateful (by being believers), He is pleased therewith for you. No bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you what you used to do. Verily, He is the All-Knower of that which is in (men’s) breasts. (39:7)
واذا مس الانسان ضر دعا ربه منيبا اليه ثم: 8. And when some hurt touches man, he cries to his Lord (Allâh Alone), turning to Him in repentance. But when He bestows a favour upon him from Himself, he forgets that for which he cried for before, and he sets up rivals to Allâh, in order to mislead others from His Path. Say: "Take pleasure in your disbelief for a while: surely you are (one) of the dwellers of the Fire!" (39:8)
امن هو قانت اناء الليل ساجدا وقائما يحذر: 9. Is one who is obedient to Allâh, prostrating himself or standing (in prayer) during the hours of the night, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the Mercy of his Lord (like one who disbelieves)? Say: "Are those who know equal to those who know not?" It is only men of understanding who will remember (i.e. get a lesson from Allâh’s Signs and Verses). (39:9)
قل يا عباد الذين امنوا اتقوا ربكم للذين: 10. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "O My slaves who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism), be afraid of your Lord (Allâh) and keep your duty to Him. Good is (the reward) for those who do good in this world, and Allâh’s earth is spacious (so if you cannot worship Allâh at a place, then go to another)! Only those who are patient shall receive their reward in full, without reckoning.[2]" (39:10)
قل اني امرت ان اعبد الله مخلصا له الدين: 11. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Verily I am commanded to worship Allâh (Alone) by obeying Him and doing religious deeds sincerely for His sake only. (39:11)
وامرت لان اكون اول المسلمين: 12. "And I am commanded (this) in order that I may be the first of those who submit themselves to Allâh (in Islâm) as Muslims." (39:12)
قل الله اعبد مخلصا له ديني: 14. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Allâh Alone I worship by doing religious deeds sincerely for His sake only (and not to show off, and not to set up rivals with Him in worship.)" (39:14)
فاعبدوا ما شئتم من دونه قل ان الخاسرين ا: 15. So worship what you like besides Him. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "The losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Verily, that will be a manifest loss!" (39:15)
لهم من فوقهم ظلل من النار ومن تحتهم ظلل: 16. They shall have coverings of Fire, above them and covering (of Fire) beneath them. With this Allâh does frighten His slaves: "O My slaves, therefore fear Me!" (39:16)
والذين اجتنبوا الطاغوت ان يعبدوها وانابو: 17. Those who avoid At-Tâghût [3] (false deities) by not worshipping them and turn to Allâh (in repentance), for them are glad tidings; so announce the good news to My slaves - (39:17)
الذين يستمعون القول فيتبعون احسنه اولئك: 18. Those who listen to the Word [good advice Lâ ilâha illallâh - (none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh) and Islâmic Monotheism] and follow the best thereof (i.e. worship Allâh Alone, repent to Him and avoid Tâghût) those are (the ones) whom Allâh has guided and those are men of understanding.[4] (39:18)
افمن حق عليه كلمة العذاب افانت تنقذ من ف: 19. Is, then one against whom the Word of punishment is justified (equal to the one who avoids evil)? Will you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) rescue him who is in the Fire? (39:19)
لكن الذين اتقوا ربهم لهم غرف من فوقها غر: 20. But those who fear their Lord (Allâh) and keep their duty to Him, for them are built lofty rooms, one above another under which rivers flow (i.e. Paradise). (This is) the Promise of Allâh: and Allâh does not fail in (His) Promise. (39:20)
الم تر ان الله انزل من السماء ماء فسلكه: 21. See you not that Allâh sends down water (rain) from the sky, and causes it to penetrate the earth, (and then makes it to spring up) as water-springs, and afterward thereby produces crops of different colours, and afterward they wither and you see them turn yellow; then He makes them dry and broken pieces. Verily, in this is a Reminder for men of understanding. (39:21)
افمن شرح الله صدره للاسلام فهو على نور م: 22. Is he whose breast Allâh has opened to Islâm, so that he is in light from His Lord (as he who is a non-Muslim)? So woe to those whose hearts are hardened against remembrance of Allâh! They are in plain error! (39:22)
الله نزل احسن الحديث كتابا متشابها مثاني: 23. Allâh has sent down the Best Statement, a Book (this Qur’ân), its parts resembling each other (in goodness and truth) (and) oft-repeated. The skins of those who fear their Lord shiver from it (when they recite it or hear it). Then their skin and their heart soften to the remembrance of Allâh. That is the guidance of Allâh. He Guides therewith whom He wills; and whomever Allâh sends astray, for him there is no guide. (39:23)
افمن يتقي بوجهه سوء العذاب يوم القيامة و: 24. Is he then, who will confront with his face the awful torment on the Day of Resurrection (as he who enters peacefully in Paradise)? And it will be said to the Zâlimûn (polytheists and wrong-doers): "Taste what you used to earn!" (39:24)
كذب الذين من قبلهم فاتاهم العذاب من حيث: 25. Those before them belied, and so the torment came on them from directions they perceived not. (39:25)
فاذاقهم الله الخزي في الحياة الدنيا ولعذ: 26. So Allâh made them to taste the disgrace in the present life, but greater is the torment of the Hereafter if they only knew! (39:26)
ولقد ضربنا للناس في هذا القران من كل مثل: 27. And indeed We have put forth for men, in this Qur’ân every kind of similitude in order that they may remember. (39:27)
قرانا عربيا غير ذي عوج لعلهم يتقون: 28. An Arabic Qur’ân, without any crookedness (therein) in order that they may avoid all evil which Allâh has ordered them to avoid, fear Him and keep their duty to Him. (39:28)
ضرب الله مثلا رجلا فيه شركاء متشاكسون ور: 29. Allâh puts forth a similitude: a (slave) man belonging to many partners (like those who worship others along with Allâh) disputing with one another, and a (slave) man belonging entirely to one master (like those who worship Allâh Alone). Are those two equal in comparison? All the praises and thanks be to Allâh! But most of them know not. (39:29)
انك ميت وانهم ميتون: 30. Verily you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) will die, and verily they (too) will die. (39:30)
ثم انكم يوم القيامة عند ربكم تختصمون: 31. Then, on the Day of Resurrection, you will be disputing before your Lord. (39:31)
فمن اظلم ممن كذب على الله وكذب بالصدق اذ: 32. Then, who does more wrong than one who utters a lie against Allâh,[5] and denies the truth [this Qur’ân, the Prophet (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) and the Islâmic Monotheism] when it comes to him! Is there not in Hell an abode for the disbelievers? (39:32)
والذي جاء بالصدق وصدق به اولئك هم المتقو: 33. And he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) who has brought the truth (this Qur’ân and Islâmic Monotheism) and (those who) believed therein (i.e. the true believers of Islâmic Monotheism), those are Al-Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2). (39:33)
لهم ما يشاءون عند ربهم ذلك جزاء المحسنين: 34. They shall have all that they will desire with their Lord. That is the reward of Muhsinûn (good-doers - see the footnote of V.9:120). (39:34)
ليكفر الله عنهم اسوا الذي عملوا ويجزيهم: 35. So that Allâh may expiate from them the evil of what they did and give them the reward, according to the best of what they used to do.[6] (39:35)
اليس الله بكاف عبده ويخوفونك بالذين من د: 36. Is not Allâh Sufficient for His slave? Yet they try to frighten you with those (whom they worship) besides Him! And whom Allâh sends astray, for him there will be no guide. (39:36)
ومن يهد الله فما له من مضل اليس الله بعز: 37. And whomsoever Allâh guides, for him there will be no misleader. Is not Allâh All-Mighty, Possessor of Retribution? (39:37)
