وتبارك الذي له ملك السماوات والارض وما ب: 85. And Blessed be He to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, and with Whom is the knowledge of the Hour, and to Whom you (all) will be returned. (43:85)
ولا يملك الذين يدعون من دونه الشفاعة الا: 86. And those whom they invoke instead of Him have no power of intercession - except for those who bear witness to the truth knowingly (i.e. believed in the Oneness of Allâh, and obeyed His Orders), and they know (the facts about the Oneness of Allâh).[5] (43:86)
ولئن سالتهم من خلقهم ليقولن الله فانى يؤ: 87. And if you ask them who created them, they will surely say: "Allâh." How then are they turned away (from the worship of Allâh, Who created them)? (43:87)
وقيله يا رب ان هؤلاء قوم لا يؤمنون: 88. (And Allâh has the knowledge) of (Prophet Muhammad’s) saying: "O my Lord! Verily, these are a people who believe not!" (43:88)
فاصفح عنهم وقل سلام فسوف يعلمون: 89. So turn away from them (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه و سلم), and say: Salâm (peace)! But they will come to know.[6] (43:89)
انا انزلناه في ليلة مباركة انا كنا منذري: 3. We sent it (this Qur’ân) down on a blessed night [(i.e. night of Al-Qadr, Sûrah No. 97) in the month of Ramadân - the 9th month of the Islâmic calendar]. Verily, We are ever warning [mankind that Our Torment will reach those who disbelieve in Our Oneness of Lordship and in Our Oneness of worship]. (44:3)
فيها يفرق كل امر حكيم: 4. Therein (that night) is decreed every matter of ordainments.[1] (44:4)
امرا من عندنا انا كنا مرسلين: 5. As a Command (or this Qur’ân or the Decree of every matter) from Us. Verily, We are ever sending (the Messengers), (44:5)
رحمة من ربك انه هو السميع العليم: 6. (As) a Mercy from your Lord. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower. (44:6)
رب السماوات والارض وما بينهما ان كنتم مو: 7. The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, if you (but) have a faith with certainty. (44:7)
لا اله الا هو يحيي ويميت ربكم ورب ابائكم: 8. Lâ ilaha illâ Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). It is He Who gives life and causes death - your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers. (44:8)
بل هم في شك يلعبون: 9. Nay! They play about in doubt. (44:9)
فارتقب يوم تاتي السماء بدخان مبين: 10. Then wait you for the Day when the sky will bring forth a visible smoke, (44:10)
يغشى الناس هذا عذاب اليم: 11. Covering the people: this is a painful torment. (44:11)
ربنا اكشف عنا العذاب انا مؤمنون: 12. (They will say): "Our Lord! Remove the torment from us, really we shall become believers!" (44:12)
انى لهم الذكرى وقد جاءهم رسول مبين: 13. How can there be for them an admonition (at the time when the torment has reached them), when a Messenger explaining things clearly has already come to them. (44:13)
ثم تولوا عنه وقالوا معلم مجنون: 14. Then they had turned away from him (Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه و سلم) and said: (He is) one taught (by a human being), a madman!" (44:14)
انا كاشفو العذاب قليلا انكم عائدون: 15. Verily, We shall remove the torment for a while. Verily you will revert (to disbelief). (44:15)
يوم نبطش البطشة الكبرى انا منتقمون: 16. On the Day when We shall seize you with the greatest seizure (punishment). Verily, We will exact retribution. (44:16)
ولقد فتنا قبلهم قوم فرعون وجاءهم رسول كر: 17. And indeed We tried before them Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh) people, when there came to them a noble Messenger [i.e. Mûsâ (Moses) عليه السلام], (44:17)
ان ادوا الي عباد الله اني لكم رسول امين: 18. Saying: "Deliver to me the slaves of Allâh (i.e. the Children of Israel). Verily, I am to you a Messenger worthy of all trust. (44:18)
وان لا تعلوا على الله اني اتيكم بسلطان م: 19. "And exalt not yourselves against Allâh. Truly, I have come to you with a manifest authority. (44:19)
واني عذت بربي وربكم ان ترجمون: 20. "And truly, I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord, lest you should stone me (or call me a sorcerer or kill me). (44:20)
وان لم تؤمنوا لي فاعتزلون: 21. "But if you believe me not, then keep away from me and leave me alone." (44:21)
فدعا ربه ان هؤلاء قوم مجرمون: 22. (But they were aggressive), so he [Mûsâ (Moses)] called upon his Lord (saying): "These are indeed the people who are Mujrimûn (disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, criminals)." (44:22)
فاسر بعبادي ليلا انكم متبعون: 23. (Allâh said): "Depart you with My slaves by night. Surely, you will be pursued. (44:23)
واترك البحر رهوا انهم جند مغرقون: 24. "And leave the sea as it is (quiet and divided). Verily, they are a host to be drowned." (44:24)
كم تركوا من جنات وعيون: 25. How many of gardens and springs that they [Fir‘aun’s (Pharaoh) people] left behind, (44:25)
وزروع ومقام كريم: 26. And green crops (fields) and goodly places, (44:26)
ونعمة كانوا فيها فاكهين: 27. And comforts of life wherein they used to take delight! (44:27)
كذلك واورثناها قوما اخرين: 28. Thus (it was)! And We made other people inherit them (i.e. We made the Children of Israel to inherit the kingdom of Egypt). (44:28)
فما بكت عليهم السماء والارض وما كانوا من: 29. And the heavens and the earth wept not for them,[2] nor were they given a respite. (44:29)
ولقد نجينا بني اسرائيل من العذاب المهين: 30. And indeed We saved the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment: (44:30)
من فرعون انه كان عاليا من المسرفين: 31. From Fir‘aun (Pharaoh); verily he was arrogant and was of the Musrifûn (those who transgress beyond bound in spending and other things and commit great sins). (44:31)
ولقد اخترناهم على علم على العالمين: 32. And We chose them (the Children of Israel) above the ‘Âlamîn (mankind and jinn) [during the time of Mûsâ (Moses)] with knowledge, (44:32)
واتيناهم من الايات ما فيه بلاء مبين: 33. And granted them signs in which there was a plain trial. (44:33)
ان هي الا موتتنا الاولى وما نحن بمنشرين: 35. "There is nothing but our first death, and we shall not be resurrected. (44:35)
فاتوا بابائنا ان كنتم صادقين: 36. "Then bring back our forefathers, if you speak the truth!" (44:36)
اهم خير ام قوم تبع والذين من قبلهم اهلكن: 37. Are they better or the people of Tubba‘ and those before them? We destroyed them because they were indeed Mujrimûn (disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, criminals). (44:37)
وما خلقنا السماوات والارض وما بينهما لاع: 38. And We created not the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, for mere play. (44:38)
ما خلقناهما الا بالحق ولكن اكثرهم لا يعل: 39. We created them not except with truth (i.e. to examine and test those who are obedient and those who are disobedient and then reward the obedient ones and punish the disobedient ones), but most of them know not. (44:39)
ان يوم الفصل ميقاتهم اجمعين: 40. Verily, the Day of Judgement (when Allâh will judge between the creatures) is the time appointed for all of them - (44:40)
يوم لا يغني مولى عن مولى شيئا ولا هم ينص: 41. The Day when a Maulâ (a near relative) cannot avail a Maulâ (a near relative) in aught, and no help can they receive, (44:41)
الا من رحم الله انه هو العزيز الرحيم: 42. Except him on whom Allâh has Mercy. Verily, He is the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful. (44:42)
طعام الاثيم: 44. Will be the food of the sinners. (44:44)
كالمهل يغلي في البطون: 45. Like boiling oil, it will boil in the bellies, (44:45)
كغلي الحميم: 46. Like the boiling of scalding water. (44:46)
خذوه فاعتلوه الى سواء الجحيم: 47. (It will be said) "Seize him and drag him into the midst of blazing Fire, (44:47)
ثم صبوا فوق راسه من عذاب الحميم: 48. "Then pour over his head the torment of boiling water. (44:48)
ذق انك انت العزيز الكريم: 49. "Taste you (this)! Verily, you were (pretending to be) the mighty, the generous! (44:49)
ان هذا ما كنتم به تمترون: 50. "Verily, this is that whereof you used to doubt!" (44:50)
ان المتقين في مقام امين: 51. Verily, the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2), will be in place of Security (Paradise). (44:51)
يلبسون من سندس واستبرق متقابلين: 53. Dressed in fine silk and (also) in thick silk, facing each other, (44:53)
كذلك وزوجناهم بحور عين: 54. So (it will be). And We shall marry them to Hûr[3] (fair female) with wide, lovely eyes. (44:54)
يدعون فيها بكل فاكهة امنين: 55. They will call therein for every kind of fruit in peace and security; (44:55)
لا يذوقون فيها الموت الا الموتة الاولى و: 56. They will never taste death therein except the first death (of this world), and He will save them from the torment of the blazing Fire, (44:56)
فضلا من ربك ذلك هو الفوز العظيم: 57. As a Bounty from your Lord! That will be the supreme success! (44:57)
فانما يسرناه بلسانك لعلهم يتذكرون: 58. Certainly, We have made this (Qur’an) easy in your tongue, in order that they may remember. (44:58)
فارتقب انهم مرتقبون: 59. Wait then (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه و سلم); verily, they (too) are waiting. (44:59)
ان في السماوات والارض لايات للمؤمنين: 3. Verily, in the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers. (45:3)
وفي خلقكم وما يبث من دابة ايات لقوم يوقن: 4. And in your creation, and what He scattered (through the earth) of moving (living) creatures are signs for people who have Faith with certainty. (45:4)
واختلاف الليل والنهار وما انزل الله من ا: 5. And in the alternation of night and day, and the provision (rain) that Allâh sends down from the sky, and revives therewith the earth after its death, and in the turning about of the winds (i.e. sometimes towards the east or north, and sometimes towards the south or west sometimes bringing glad tidings of rain etc., and sometimes bringing the torment), are signs for a people who understand. (45:5)
تلك ايات الله نتلوها عليك بالحق فباي حدي: 6. These are the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, revelations, etc.) of Allâh, which We recite to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه و سلم) with truth. Then in which speech after Allâh and His Ayât will they believe? (45:6)
يسمع ايات الله تتلى عليه ثم يصر مستكبرا: 8. Who hears the Verses of Allâh (being) recited to him, yet persists with pride as if he heard them not. So announce to him a painful torment! (45:8)
واذا علم من اياتنا شيئا اتخذها هزوا اولئ: 9. And when he learns something of Our Verses (this Qur’ân), he makes them a jest. For such there will be a humiliating torment. (45:9)
من ورائهم جهنم ولا يغني عنهم ما كسبوا شي: 10. In front of them there is Hell. And that which they have earned will be of no profit to them, nor (will be of any profit to them) those whom they have taken as Auliyâ’ (protectors, helpers) besides Allâh. And theirs will be a great torment. (45:10)
هذا هدى والذين كفروا بايات ربهم لهم عذاب: 11. This (Qur’an) is a guidance. And those who disbelieve in the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations) of their Lord, for them there is a painful torment of Rijz (a severe kind of punishment). (45:11)
الله الذي سخر لكم البحر لتجري الفلك فيه: 12. Allâh, it is He Who has subjected to you the sea, that ships may sail through it by His Command, and that you may seek of His Bounty, and that you may be thankful. (45:12)
وسخر لكم ما في السماوات وما في الارض جمي: 13. And has subjected to you all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth; it is all as a favour and kindness from Him. Verily, in it are signs for a people who think deeply. (45:13)
قل للذين امنوا يغفروا للذين لا يرجون ايا: 14. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه و سلم) to the believers to forgive those who (harm them and) hope not for the Days of Allâh (i.e. His Recompense), that He may recompense people, according to what they have earned (i.e. to punish these disbelievers who harm the believers).[1] (45:14)
من عمل صالحا فلنفسه ومن اساء فعليها ثم ا: 15. Whosoever does a good deed, it is for his ownself, and whosoever does evil, it is against (his ownself). Then to your Lord you will be made to return. (45:15)
ولقد اتينا بني اسرائيل الكتاب والحكم وال: 16. And indeed We gave the Children of Israel the Scripture, and the understanding of the Scripture and its laws, and the Prophethood; and provided them with good things, and preferred them above the ‘Âlamîn (mankind and jinn of their time, during that period), (45:16)
واتيناهم بينات من الامر فما اختلفوا الا: 17. And gave them clear proofs in matters [by revealing to them the Taurât (Torah)]. And they differed not until after the knowledge came to them, through envy among themselves. Verily, Your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection about that wherein they used to differ. (45:17)
ثم جعلناك على شريعة من الامر فاتبعها ولا: 18. Then We have put you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه و سلم) on a (plain) way of (Our) commandment [like the one which We commanded Our Messengers before you (i.e. legal ways and laws of the Islâmic Monotheism)]. So follow you that (Islâmic Monotheism and its laws), and follow not the desires of those who know not. (Tafsir At-Tabarî). (45:18)
انهم لن يغنوا عنك من الله شيئا وان الظال: 19. Verily, they can avail you nothing against Allâh (if He wants to punish you). Verily, the Zâlimûn (polytheists, wrong-doers) are Auliyâ’ (protectors, helpers) of one another, but Allâh is the Walî (Helper, Protector) of the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2). (45:19)
هذا بصائر للناس وهدى ورحمة لقوم يوقنون: 20. This (Qur’an) is a clear insight and evidence for mankind, and a guidance and a mercy for people who have Faith with certainty.[2] (45:20)
ام حسب الذين اجترحوا السيئات ان نجعلهم ك: 21. Or do those who earn evil deeds think that We shall hold them equal with those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, in their present life and after their death? Worst is the judgement that they make. (45:21)
وخلق الله السماوات والارض بالحق ولتجزى ك: 22. And Allâh has created the heavens and the earth with truth, in order that each person may be recompensed what he has earned, and they will not be wronged.[3] (45:22)
افرايت من اتخذ الهه هواه واضله الله على: 23. Have you seen him who takes his own lust (vain desires) as his ilâh (god)? And Allâh knowing (him as such), left him astray, and sealed his hearing and his heart, and put a cover on his sight. Who then will guide him after Allâh? Will you not then remember? (45:23)
وقالوا ما هي الا حياتنا الدنيا نموت ونحي: 24. And they say: "There is nothing but our life of this world, we die and we live and nothing destroys us except Ad-Dahr (time).[4] And they have no knowledge of it: they only conjecture. (45:24)
واذا تتلى عليهم اياتنا بينات ما كان حجته: 25. And when Our Clear Verses are recited to them, their argument is no other than that they say: "Bring back our (dead) fathers, if you are truthful!" (45:25)
قل الله يحييكم ثم يميتكم ثم يجمعكم الى ي: 26. Say (to them): "Allâh gives you life, then causes you to die, then He will assemble you on the Day of Resurrection about which there is no doubt. But most of mankind know not." (45:26)
ولله ملك السماوات والارض ويوم تقوم الساع: 27. And to Allâh belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And on the Day that the Hour will be established - on that Day the followers of falsehood (polytheists, disbelievers, worshippers of false deities) shall lose (everything). (45:27)
وترى كل امة جاثية كل امة تدعى الى كتابها: 28. And you will see each nation humbled to their knees (kneeling): each nation will be called to its Record (of deeds). This Day you shall be recompensed for what you used to do. (45:28)
هذا كتابنا ينطق عليكم بالحق انا كنا نستن: 29. This Our Record speaks about you with truth. Verily, We were recording what you used to do (i.e. Our angels used to record your deeds). (45:29)
فاما الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات فيدخلهم: 30. Then, as for those who believed (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and did righteous good deeds, their Lord will admit them to His Mercy. That will be the evident success. (45:30)
واما الذين كفروا افلم تكن اياتي تتلى علي: 31. But as for those who disbelieved (it will be said to them): "Were not Our Verses recited to you? But you were proud, and you were a people who were Mujrimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals)." (45:31)
واذا قيل ان وعد الله حق والساعة لا ريب ف: 32. And when it was said: "Verily, Allâh’s Promise is the truth, and there is no doubt about the coming of the Hour," you said: "We know not what is the Hour: we do not think it but as a conjecture, and we have no firm convincing belief (therein)." (45:32)
وبدا لهم سيئات ما عملوا وحاق بهم ما كانو: 33. And the evil of what they did will appear to them, and that which they used to mock at will completely encircle them. (45:33)
وقيل اليوم ننساكم كما نسيتم لقاء يومكم ه: 34. And it will be said: "This Day We will forget you as you forgot the Meeting of this Day of yours. And your abode is the Fire, and there is none to help you." (45:34)
ذلكم بانكم اتخذتم ايات الله هزوا وغرتكم: 35. This, because you took the revelations of Allâh (this Qur’ân) in mockery, and the life of the world deceived you. So this Day, they shall not be taken out from there (Hell),[5] nor shall they be returned to the worldly life, (so that they repent to Allâh, and beg His Pardon for their sins). (45:35)
فلله الحمد رب السماوات ورب الارض رب العا: 36. So all the praises and thanks be to Allâh, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, and the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists). (45:36)
وله الكبرياء في السماوات والارض وهو العز: 37. And His (Alone) is the Majesty in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. (45:37)
ما خلقنا السماوات والارض وما بينهما الا: 3. We created not the heavens and the earth and all that is between them except with truth, and for an appointed term. But those who disbelieve turn away from that whereof they are warned. (46:3)
قل ارايتم ما تدعون من دون الله اروني ماذ: 4. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to these pagans): "Think you about all that you invoke besides Allâh? Show me. What have they created of the earth? Or have they a share in (the creation of) the heavens? Bring me a Book (revealed before this), or some trace of knowledge (in support of your claims), if you are truthful!" (46:4)
ومن اضل ممن يدعو من دون الله من لا يستجي: 5. And who is more astray than one who calls on (invokes) besides Allâh, such as will not answer him till the Day of Resurrection, and who are (even) unaware of their calls (invocations) to them?[1] (46:5)
واذا حشر الناس كانوا لهم اعداء وكانوا بع: 6. And when mankind are gathered (on the Day of Resurrection), they (false deities) will become their enemies and will deny their worshipping. (46:6)
واذا تتلى عليهم اياتنا بينات قال الذين ك: 7. And when Our Clear Verses are recited to them, the disbelievers say of the truth (this Qur’ân) when it reaches them: "This is plain magic!" (46:7)
ام يقولون افتراه قل ان افتريته فلا تملكو: 8. Or say they: "He (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) has fabricated it." Say: "If I have fabricated it? still you have no power to support me against Allâh. He knows best of what you say among yourselves concerning it (i.e. this Qur’ân)! Sufficient is He as a witness between me and you! And He is the Oft-Forgiving, the Most Merciful." (46:8)
قل ما كنت بدعا من الرسل وما ادري ما يفعل: 9. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I am not a new thing among the Messengers (of Allâh i.e. I am not the first Messenger) nor do I know what will be done with me or with you. I only follow that which is revealed to me, and I am but a plain warner." (46:9)
قل ارايتم ان كان من عند الله وكفرتم به و: 10. Say: "Tell me! If this (Qur’ân) is from Allâh and you deny it, and a witness from among the Children of Israel (‘Abdullâh bin Salâm رضي الله عنه) testifies that [this Qur’ân is from Allâh (like the Taurât (Torah)], and he believed (embraced Islâm)[2] while you are too proud (to believe)." Verily, Allâh guides not the people who are Zâlimûn (polytheists, disbelievers and wrong-doers). (46:10)
وقال الذين كفروا للذين امنوا لو كان خيرا: 11. And those who disbelieve (the strong and wealthy) say of those who believe (the weak and poor): "Had it (Islâmic Monotheism to which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم is inviting mankind) been a good thing, they (the weak and poor) would not have preceded us thereto!" And when they have not let themselves be guided by it (this Qur’ân), they say: "This is an ancient lie!" (46:11)
ومن قبله كتاب موسى اماما ورحمة وهذا كتاب: 12. And before this was the Scripture of Mûsâ (Moses) as a guide and a mercy. And this is a confirming Book (the Qur’ân) in the Arabic language, to warn those who do wrong, and as glad tidings to the Muhsinûn (good-doers. See the footnote of V.9:120). (46:12)
ان الذين قالوا ربنا الله ثم استقاموا فلا: 13. Verily, those who say: "Our Lord is (only) Allâh," and thereafter stand firm and straight on the Islâmic Faith of Monotheism,[3] on them shall be no fear, nor shall they grieve. (46:13)
اولئك اصحاب الجنة خالدين فيها جزاء بما ك: 14. Such shall be the dwellers of Paradise, abiding therein (forever) - a reward for what they used to do. (46:14)
ووصينا الانسان بوالديه احسانا حملته امه: 15. And We have enjoined on man to be dutiful and kind to his parents. His mother bears him with hardship. And she brings him forth with hardship, and the bearing of him, and the weaning of him is thirty months, till when he attains full strength and reaches forty years, he says: "My Lord! Grant me the power and ability that I may be grateful for Your Favour which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and that I may do righteous good deeds, such as please You, and make my offspring good. Truly, I have turned to You in repentance, and truly, I am one of the Muslims (submitting to Your Will)." (46:15)
اولئك الذين نتقبل عنهم احسن ما عملوا ونت: 16. They are those from whom We shall accept the best of their deeds and overlook their evil deeds. (They shall be) among the dwellers of Paradise - a promise of truth, which they have been promised. (46:16)
والذي قال لوالديه اف لكما اتعدانني ان اخ: 17. But he who says to his parents: "Fie upon you both! Do you hold out the promise to me that I shall be raised up (again) when generations before me have passed away (without rising)?" While they (father and mother) invoke Allâh for help (and rebuke their son): "Woe to you! Believe! Verily, the Promise of Allâh is true." But he says: "This is nothing but the tales of the ancient." (46:17)
اولئك الذين حق عليهم القول في امم قد خلت: 18. They are those against whom the Word (of torment) is justified among the previous generations of jinn and mankind that have passed away. Verily, they are ever the losers. (46:18)
ولكل درجات مما عملوا وليوفيهم اعمالهم وه: 19. And for all, there will be degrees according to that which they did, that He (Allâh) may recompense them in full for their deeds. And they will not be wronged. (46:19)
ويوم يعرض الذين كفروا على النار اذهبتم ط: 20. On the Day when those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) will be exposed to the Fire (it will be said): "You received your good things in the life of the world, and you took your pleasure therein. Now this Day you shall be recompensed with a torment of humiliation, because you were arrogant in the land without a right, and because you used to rebel against Allâh’s Command (disobey Allâh). (46:20)
واذكر اخا عاد اذ انذر قومه بالاحقاف وقد: 21. And remember (Hûd) the brother of ‘Âd, when he warned his people in Al-Ahqâf (the curved sand-hills in the southern part of Arabian Peninsula). And surely, there have passed away warners before him and after him (saying): "Worship none but Allâh; truly, I fear for you the torment of a mighty Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection)." (46:21)
قالوا اجئتنا لتافكنا عن الهتنا فاتنا بما: 22. They said: "Have you come to turn us away from our âlihah (gods)? Then bring us that with which you threaten us, if you are one of the truthful!" (46:22)
قال انما العلم عند الله وابلغكم ما ارسلت: 23. He said: "The knowledge (of the time of its coming) is with Allâh only. And I convey to you that wherewith I have been sent, but I see that you are a people given to ignorance!" (46:23)
فلما راوه عارضا مستقبل اوديتهم قالوا هذا: 24. Then, when they saw it as a dense cloud coming towards their valleys, they said: "This is a cloud bringing us rain!" Nay, but it is that (torment) which you were asking to be hastened - a wind wherein is a painful torment! (46:24)
تدمر كل شيء بامر ربها فاصبحوا لا يرى الا: 25. Destroying everything by the Command of its Lord! So they became such that nothing could be seen except their dwellings! Thus do We recompense the people who are Mujrimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners)! (46:25)
ولقد مكناهم فيما ان مكناكم فيه وجعلنا له: 26. And indeed We had firmly established them with that wherewith We have not established you (O Quraish)! And We had assigned them the (faculties of) hearing (ears), seeing (eyes), and hearts; but their hearing (ears), seeing (eyes), and their hearts availed them nothing since they used to deny the Ayât (Allâh’s Prophets and their Prophethood, proofs, evidence, verses, signs, revelations) of Allâh, and they were completely encircled by that which they used to mock at! (46:26)
ولقد اهلكنا ما حولكم من القرى وصرفنا الا: 27. And indeed We have destroyed towns (populations) round about you, and We have (repeatedly) shown (them) the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) in various ways that they might return (to the truth and believe in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism). (46:27)
فلولا نصرهم الذين اتخذوا من دون الله قرب: 28. Then why did those whom they had taken for âlihah (gods) besides Allâh, as a way of approach (to Allâh) not help them? Nay, but they vanished completely from them (when there came the torment). And that was their lie, and their inventions which they had been inventing (before their destruction). (46:28)
واذ صرفنا اليك نفرا من الجن يستمعون القر: 29. And (remember) when We sent towards you (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) a group (three to ten persons) of the jinn, (quietly) listening to the Qur’ân. When they stood in the presence thereof, they said: "Listen in silence!" And when it was finished, they returned to their people, as warners. (46:29)
قالوا يا قومنا انا سمعنا كتابا انزل من ب: 30. They said: "O our people! Verily, we have heard a Book (this Qur’ân) sent down after Mûsâ (Moses), confirming what came before it: it guides to the truth and to the Straight Path (i.e. Islâm). (46:30)
يا قومنا اجيبوا داعي الله وامنوا به يغفر: 31. O our people! Respond (with obedience) to Allâh’s Caller (i.e. Allâh’s Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), and believe in him (i.e. believe in that which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم has brought from Allâh and follow him). He (Allâh) will forgive you of your sins, and will save you from a painful torment (i.e. Hell-fire)[4] (46:31)
ومن لا يجب داعي الله فليس بمعجز في الارض: 32. And whosoever does not respond to Allâh’s Caller, he cannot escape on earth, and there will be no Auliyâ’ (lords, helpers, supporters, protectors) for him besides Allâh (from Allâh’s punishment). Those are in manifest error. (46:32)
اولم يروا ان الله الذي خلق السماوات والا: 33. Do they not see that Allâh, Who created the heavens and the earth, and was not wearied by their creation, is Able to give life to the dead? Yes, He surely is Able to do all things. (46:33)
ويوم يعرض الذين كفروا على النار اليس هذا: 34. And on the Day when those who disbelieve will be exposed to the Fire (it will be said to them): "Is this not the truth?" They will say: "Yes, By our Lord!" He will say: "Then taste the torment, because you used to disbelieve!" (46:34)
فاصبر كما صبر اولو العزم من الرسل ولا تس: 35. Therefore be patient (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) as did the Messengers of strong will[5] and be in no haste about them (disbelievers). On the Day when they will see that (torment) with which they are promised (i.e. threatened, it will be) as if they had not stayed more than an hour in a single day. (O mankind! this Qur’ân is sufficient as) a clear Message (or proclamation to save yourself from destruction). But shall any be destroyed except the people who are Al-Fâsiqûn (the rebellious against Allâh’s Command, the disobedient to Allâh)? (46:35)
الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله اضل اعمال: 1. Those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh, and in the Message of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), and hinder (men) from the Path of Allâh (Islâmic Monotheism), He will render their deeds vain.[1] (47:1)
والذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات وامنوا بما: 2. But those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and believe in that which is sent down to Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وسلم) - for it is the truth from their Lord - He will expiate from them their sins, and will make good their state. (47:2)
ذلك بان الذين كفروا اتبعوا الباطل وان ال: 3. That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, while those who believe follow the truth from their Lord. Thus does Allâh set forth for mankind their parables. (47:3)
فاذا لقيتم الذين كفروا فضرب الرقاب حتى ا: 4. So, when you meet (in fight - Jihâd in Allâh’s Cause) those who disbelieve, smite (their) necks till when you have killed and wounded many of them, then bind a bond firmly (on them, i.e. take them as captives). Thereafter (is the time) either for generosity (i.e. free them without ransom), or ransom (according to what benefits Islâm), until the war lays down its burden. Thus [you are ordered by Allâh to continue in carrying out Jihâd against the disbelievers till they embrace Islâm and are saved from the punishment in the Hell-fire or at least come under your protection], but if it had been Allâh’s Will, He Himself could certainly have punished them (without you). But (He lets you fight) in order to test some of you with others. But those who are killed in the Way of Allâh, He will never let their deeds be lost.[2] (47:4)
سيهديهم ويصلح بالهم: 5. He will guide them and set right their state. (47:5)
ويدخلهم الجنة عرفها لهم: 6. And admit them to Paradise which He has made known to them (i.e. they will know their places in Paradise better than they used to know their homes in the world).[3] [Tafsir Ibn Kathir ] (47:6)
يا ايها الذين امنوا ان تنصروا الله ينصرك: 7. O you who believe! If you help (in the cause of) Allâh, He will help you, and make your foothold firm. (47:7)
والذين كفروا فتعسا لهم واضل اعمالهم: 8. But those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism), for them is destruction, and (Allâh) will make their deeds vain. (47:8)
ذلك بانهم كرهوا ما انزل الله فاحبط اعمال: 9. That is because they hate that which Allâh has sent down (this Qur’ân and Islâmic laws, etc.); so He has made their deeds fruitless. (47:9)
افلم يسيروا في الارض فينظروا كيف كان عاق: 10. Have they not travelled through the earth, and seen what was the end of those before them? Allâh destroyed them completely, and a similar (fate awaits) the disbelievers. (47:10)
ذلك بان الله مولى الذين امنوا وان الكافر: 11. That is because Allâh is the Maulâ (Lord, Master, Helper, Protector, etc.) of those who believe, and the disbelievers have no Maulâ (lord, master, helper, protector, etc.). (47:11)
ان الله يدخل الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات: 12. Certainly Allâh will admit those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, to Gardens under which rivers flow (Paradise); while those who disbelieve enjoy themselves and eat as cattle eat; and the Fire will be their abode. (47:12)
وكاين من قرية هي اشد قوة من قريتك التي ا: 13. And many a town, stronger than your town (Makkah) (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) which has driven you out We have destroyed. And there was none to help them. (47:13)
افمن كان على بينة من ربه كمن زين له سوء: 14. Is he who is on a clear proof from his Lord, like those for whom their evil deeds that they do are beautified for them, while they follow their own lusts (evil desires)? (47:14)
مثل الجنة التي وعد المتقون فيها انهار من: 15. The description of Paradise which the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2) have been promised (is that) in it are rivers of water the taste and smell of which are not changed, rivers of milk of which the taste never changes, rivers of wine delicious to those who drink, and rivers of clarified honey (clear and pure) therein for them is every kind of fruit, and forgiveness from their Lord. (Are these) like those who shall dwell for ever in the Fire and be given to drink boiling water so that it cuts up their bowels? (47:15)
ومنهم من يستمع اليك حتى اذا خرجوا من عند: 16. And among them are some who listen to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) till when they go out from you, they say to those who have received knowledge: "What has he said just now? Such are men whose hearts Allâh has sealed, and they follow their lusts (evil desires). (47:16)
والذين اهتدوا زادهم هدى واتاهم تقواهم: 17. While as for those who accept guidance, He increases their guidance and bestows on them their piety. (47:17)
فهل ينظرون الا الساعة ان تاتيهم بغتة فقد: 18. Do they then await (anything) other than the Hour, that it should come upon them suddenly? But some of its portents (indications and signs) have already come; and when it (actually) is on them, how can they benefit then by their reminder?[4] (47:18)
فاعلم انه لا اله الا الله واستغفر لذنبك: 19. So know (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) that, Lâ ilâha illallâh (none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh)[5], and ask forgiveness for your sin, and also for (the sin of) believing men and believing women. And Allâh knows well your moving about, and your place of rest (in your homes). (47:19)
ويقول الذين امنوا لولا نزلت سورة فاذا ان: 20. Those who believe say: "Why is not a Sûrah (chapter of the Qur’ân) sent down (for us)? But when a decisive Sûrah (explaining and ordering things) is sent down, and fighting (Jihâd - holy fighting in Allâh’s cause) is mentioned (i.e. ordained) therein, you will see those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy) looking at you with a look of one fainting to death. But it was better for them (hypocrites, to listen to Allâh and to obey Him). (47:20)
طاعة وقول معروف فاذا عزم الامر فلو صدقوا: 21. Obedience (to Allâh) and good words (were better for them). And when the matter (preparation for Jihâd ) is resolved on, then if they had been true to Allâh, it would have been better for them. (47:21)
فهل عسيتم ان توليتم ان تفسدوا في الارض و: 22. Would you then, if you were given the authority, do mischief in the land, and sever your ties of kinship?[6] (47:22)
اولئك الذين لعنهم الله فاصمهم واعمى ابصا: 23. Such are they whom Allâh has cursed, so that He has made them deaf and blinded their sight. (47:23)
افلا يتدبرون القران ام على قلوب اقفالها: 24. Do they not then think deeply in the Qur’ân, or are their hearts locked up (from understanding it)? (47:24)
ان الذين ارتدوا على ادبارهم من بعد ما تب: 25. Verily, those who have turned back (have apostatised) as disbelievers after the guidance has been manifested to them - Shaitân (Satan) has beautified for them (their false hopes), and (Allâh) prolonged their term (age). (47:25)
ذلك بانهم قالوا للذين كرهوا ما نزل الله: 26. This is because they said to those who hate what Allâh has sent down: "We will obey you in part of the matter." But Allâh knows their secrets. (47:26)
فكيف اذا توفتهم الملائكة يضربون وجوههم و: 27. Then how (will it be) when the angels will take their souls at death, smiting their faces and their backs? (47:27)
ذلك بانهم اتبعوا ما اسخط الله وكرهوا رضو: 28. That is because they followed that which angered Allâh and hated that which pleased Him. So He made their deeds fruitless. (47:28)
ام حسب الذين في قلوبهم مرض ان لن يخرج ال: 29. Or do those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy), think that Allâh will not bring to light all their hidden ill-wills?[7] (47:29)
ولو نشاء لاريناكهم فلعرفتهم بسيماهم ولتع: 30. Had We willed, We could have shown them to you, and you should have known them by their marks; but surely, you will know them by the tone of their speech! And Allâh knows (all) your deeds. (47:30)
ولنبلونكم حتى نعلم المجاهدين منكم والصاب: 31. And surely, We shall try you till We test those who strive hard (for the Cause of Allâh) and As-Sâbirûn (the patient), and We shall test your facts (i.e. the one who is a liar, and the one who is truthful). (47:31)
ان الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله وشاقوا: 32. Verily, those who disbelieve, and hinder (men) from the Path of Allâh (i.e. Islâm), and oppose the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم) (by standing against him and hurting him), after the guidance[8] has been clearly shown to them, they will not harm Allâh in the least, but He will make their deeds fruitless, (47:32)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اطيعوا الله واطيعوا: 33. O you who believe! Obey Allâh, and obey the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) and render not vain your deeds. (47:33)
ان الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله ثم مات: 34. Verily, those who disbelieve, and hinder (men) from the Path of Allâh (i.e. Islâm); then die while they are disbelievers - Allâh will not forgive them.[9] (47:34)
فلا تهنوا وتدعوا الى السلم وانتم الاعلون: 35. So be not weak and ask not for peace (from the enemies of Islâm) while you are having the upper hand. Allâh is with you, and He will never decrease the reward of your good deeds. (47:35)
انما الحياة الدنيا لعب ولهو وان تؤمنوا و: 36. The life of this world is but play and pastime; but if you believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism), and fear Allâh, and avoid evil, He will grant you your wages, and will not ask you your wealth. (47:36)
ان يسالكموها فيحفكم تبخلوا ويخرج اضغانكم: 37. If He were to ask you of it, and press you, you would covetously withhold, and He will bring out all your (secret) ill-wills. (47:37)
ها انتم هؤلاء تدعون لتنفقوا في سبيل الله: 38. Behold! You are those who are called to spend in the Cause of Allâh, yet among you are some who are niggardly. And whoever is niggardly, it is only at the expense of his ownself. But Allâh is Rich (Free of all needs), and you (mankind) are poor. And if you turn away (from Islâm and the obedience to Allâh), He will exchange you for some other people and they will not be your likes. (47:38)
انا فتحنا لك فتحا مبينا: 1. Verily, We have given you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) a manifest victory. (48:1)
ليغفر لك الله ما تقدم من ذنبك وما تاخر و: 2. That Allâh may forgive you your sins of the past and the future,[1] and complete His Favour on you, and guide you on the Straight Path, (48:2)
وينصرك الله نصرا عزيزا: 3. And that Allâh may help you with strong help. (48:3)
هو الذي انزل السكينة في قلوب المؤمنين لي: 4. He it is Who sent down As-Sakînah (calmness and tranquillity) into the hearts of the believers, that they may grow more in Faith along with their (present) Faith. And to Allâh belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth, and Allâh is Ever All-Knower, All-Wise. (48:4)
ليدخل المؤمنين والمؤمنات جنات تجري من تح: 5. That He may admit the believing men and the believing women to Gardens under which rivers flow (i.e. Paradise), to abide therein forever, and He may expiate from them their sins; and that is with Allâh a supreme success, (48:5)
ويعذب المنافقين والمنافقات والمشركين وال: 6. And that He may punish the Munâfiqûn (hypocrites), men and women, and also the Mushrikûn[2] men and women, who think evil thoughts about Allâh: for them is a disgraceful torment. And the Anger of Allâh is upon them, and He has cursed them and prepared Hell for them - and worst indeed is that destination. (48:6)
ولله جنود السماوات والارض وكان الله عزيز: 7. And to Allâh belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth. And Allâh is Ever All-Powerful, All-Wise. (48:7)
انا ارسلناك شاهدا ومبشرا ونذيرا: 8. Verily, We have sent you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) as a witness, as a bearer of glad tidings, and as a warner[3]. (48:8)
لتؤمنوا بالله ورسوله وتعزروه وتوقروه وتس: 9. In order that you (O mankind) may believe in Allâh and His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم), and that you assist and honour him (صلى الله عليه وسلم), and (that you) glorify (Allâh’s) praises morning and afternoon. (48:9)
ان الذين يبايعونك انما يبايعون الله يد ا: 10. Verily, those who give Bai‘ah (pledge) to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) they are giving Bai‘ah (pledge) to Allâh. The Hand of Allâh is over their hands. Then whosoever breaks his pledge, breaks it only to his own harm; and whosoever fulfils what he has covenanted with Allâh, He will bestow on him a great reward. (48:10)
سيقول لك المخلفون من الاعراب شغلتنا اموا: 11. Those of the bedouins who lagged behind will say to you: "Our possessions and our families occupied us, so ask forgiveness for us." They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say: "Who then has any power at all (to intervene) on your behalf with Allâh, if He intends you hurt or intends you benefit? Nay, but Allâh is Ever All-Aware of what you do. (48:11)
بل ظننتم ان لن ينقلب الرسول والمؤمنون ال: 12. "Nay, but you thought that the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم) and the believers would never return to their families, and that was made fair-seeming in your hearts, and you did think an evil thought and you became a useless people going for destruction." (48:12)
ومن لم يؤمن بالله ورسوله فانا اعتدنا للك: 13. And whosoever does not believe in Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), then verily, We have prepared for the disbelievers a blazing Fire.[4] (48:13)
ولله ملك السماوات والارض يغفر لمن يشاء و: 14. And to Allâh belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whom He wills, and punishes whom He wills. And Allâh is Ever Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful. (48:14)
سيقول المخلفون اذا انطلقتم الى مغانم لتا: 15. Those who lagged behind will say, when you set forth to take the spoils, "Allow us to follow you." They want to change Allâh’s Words. Say: "You shall not follow us; thus Allâh has said beforehand." Then they will say: "Nay, you envy us." Nay, but they understand not except a little. (48:15)
قل للمخلفين من الاعراب ستدعون الى قوم او: 16. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) to the bedouins who lagged behind: "You shall be called to fight against a people given to great warfare, then you shall fight them, or they shall surrender. Then if you obey, Allâh will give you a fair reward; but if you turn away as you did turn away before, He will punish you with a painful torment." (48:16)
ليس على الاعمى حرج ولا على الاعرج حرج ول: 17. No blame or sin is there upon the blind, nor is there blame or sin upon the lame, nor is there blame or sin upon the sick (that they go not for fighting). And whosoever obeys Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), He will admit him to Gardens beneath which rivers flow (Paradise); and whosoever turns back, He will punish him with a painful torment. (48:17)
لقد رضي الله عن المؤمنين اذ يبايعونك تحت: 18. Indeed, Allâh was pleased with the believers when they gave the Bai‘ah (pledge) to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) under the tree: He knew what was in their hearts, and He sent down As-Sakînah (calmness and tranquillity) upon them, and He rewarded them with a near victory. (48:18)
ومغانم كثيرة ياخذونها وكان الله عزيزا حك: 19. And abundant spoils that they will capture. And Allâh is Ever All-Mighty, All-Wise. (48:19)
وعدكم الله مغانم كثيرة تاخذونها فعجل لكم: 20. Allâh has promised you abundant spoils that you will capture, and He has hastened for you this, and He has restrained the hands of men from you: that it may be a sign for the believers, and that He may guide you to the Straight Path. (48:20)
واخرى لم تقدروا عليها قد احاط الله بها و: 21. And other (victories and much booty He promises you) which are not yet within your power; indeed Allâh compasses them. And Allâh is Ever Able to do all things. (48:21)
ولو قاتلكم الذين كفروا لولوا الادبار ثم: 22. And if those who disbelieve fight against you, they certainly would have turned their backs; then they would have found neither a Walî (protector, guardian) nor a helper. (48:22)
سنة الله التي قد خلت من قبل ولن تجد لسنة: 23. That has been the Way of Allâh already with those who passed away before. And you will not find any change in the Way of Allâh. (48:23)
وهو الذي كف ايديهم عنكم وايديكم عنهم ببط: 24. And He it is Who has withheld their hands from you and your hands from them in the midst of Makkah, after He had made you victors over them. And Allâh is Ever the All-Seer of what you do. (48:24)
هم الذين كفروا وصدوكم عن المسجد الحرام و: 25. They are the ones who disbelieved (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and hindered you from Al-Masjid-al-Harâm (at Makkah) and detained the sacrificial animals from reaching their place of sacrifice. Had there not been believing men and believing women whom you did not know, that you may kill them and on whose account a sin would have been committed by you without (your) knowledge, that Allâh might bring into His Mercy whom He wills - if they (the believers and the disbelievers) had been apart, We verily would have punished those of them who disbelieved with painful torment. (48:25)
اذ جعل الذين كفروا في قلوبهم الحمية حمية: 26. When those who disbelieve had put in their hearts pride and haughtiness - the pride and haughtiness of the time of ignorance, - then Allâh sent down His Sakînah (calmness and tranquillity) upon His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم) and upon the believers, and made them stick to the word of piety (i.e. none has the right to be worshipped but Allâh); and they were well entitled to it and worthy of it. And Allâh is the All-Knower of everything. (48:26)
لقد صدق الله رسوله الرؤيا بالحق لتدخلن ا: 27. Indeed Allâh shall fulfil the true vision which He showed to His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم) [i.e. the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم saw a dream that he has entered Makkah along with his Companions, having their (head) hair shaved and cut short] in very truth. Certainly, you shall enter Al-Masjid-al-Harâm, if Allâh wills, secure, (some) having your heads shaved, and (some) having your head hair cut short, having no fear. He knew what you knew not, and He granted besides that a near victory. (48:27)
هو الذي ارسل رسوله بالهدى ودين الحق ليظه: 28. He it is Who has sent His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) with guidance and the religion of truth (Islâm), that He may make it (Islâm) superior to all religions. And All-Sufficient is Allâh as a Witness. (48:28)
محمد رسول الله والذين معه اشداء على الكف: 29. Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وسلم) is the Messenger of Allâh. And those who are with him are severe against disbelievers, and merciful among themselves. You see them bowing and falling down prostrate (in prayer), seeking Bounty from Allâh and (His) Good Pleasure. The mark of them (i.e. of their Faith) is on their faces (foreheads) from the traces of prostration (during prayers). This is their description in the Taurât (Torah). But their description in the Injeel (Gospel) is like a (sown) seed which sends forth its shoot, then makes it strong, and becomes thick and it stands straight on its stem, delighting the sowers, that He may enrage the disbelievers with them. Allâh has promised those among them who believe (i.e. all those who follow Islâmic Monotheism, the religion of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم till the Day of Resurrection) and do righteous good deeds, forgiveness and a mighty reward (i.e. Paradise). (48:29)
يا ايها الذين امنوا لا تقدموا بين يدي ال: 1. O you who believe! Make not (a decision) in advance[1] before Allâh and His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم), and fear Allâh. Verily! Allâh is All-Hearing, All-Knowing. (49:1)
يا ايها الذين امنوا لا ترفعوا اصواتكم فو: 2. O you who believe! Raise not your voices above the voice of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وسلم), nor speak aloud to him in talk as you speak aloud to one another, lest your deeds should be rendered fruitless while you perceive not. (49:2)
ان الذين يغضون اصواتهم عند رسول الله اول: 3. Verily, those who lower their voices in the presence of Allâh’s Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم), they are the ones whose hearts Allâh has tested for piety. For them is forgiveness and a great reward. (49:3)
ان الذين ينادونك من وراء الحجرات اكثرهم: 4. Verily those who call you from behind the dwellings, most of them have no sense. (49:4)
ولو انهم صبروا حتى تخرج اليهم لكان خيرا: 5. And if they had patience till you could come out to them, it would have been better for them. And Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful. (49:5)
يا ايها الذين امنوا ان جاءكم فاسق بنبا ف: 6. O you who believe! If a Fâsiq (liar - evil person) comes to you with any news, verify it, lest you should harm people in ignorance, and afterwards you become regretful for what you have done. (49:6)
واعلموا ان فيكم رسول الله لو يطيعكم في ك: 7. And know that among you there is the Messenger of Allâh (صلى الله عليه وسلم). If he were to obey you (i.e. follow your opinions and desires) in much of the matter, you would surely be in trouble. But Allâh has endeared the Faith to you and has beautified it in your hearts, and has made disbelief, wickedness and disobedience (to Allâh and His Messenger صلى الله عليه وسلم) hateful to you. Such are they who are the rightly guided. (49:7)
فضلا من الله ونعمة والله عليم حكيم: 8. (This is) a Grace from Allâh and His Favour. And Allâh is All-Knowing, All-Wise. (49:8)
وان طائفتان من المؤمنين اقتتلوا فاصلحوا: 9. And if two parties or groups among the believers fall to fighting, then make peace between them both. But if one of them outrages against the other, then fight you (all) against the one that which outrages till it complies with the Command of Allâh. Then if it complies, then make reconciliation between them justly, and be equitable. Verily! Allâh loves those who are the equitable. (49:9)
انما المؤمنون اخوة فاصلحوا بين اخويكم وا: 10. The believers are nothing else than brothers (in Islâmic religion). So make reconciliation between your brothers, and fear Allâh, that you may receive mercy. (49:10)
يا ايها الذين امنوا لا يسخر قوم من قوم ع: 11. O you who believe! Let not a group scoff at another group, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor let (some) women scoff at other women, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor defame one another, nor insult one another by nicknames. How bad is it to insult one’s brother after having Faith [i.e. to call your Muslim brother (a faithful believer) as: "O sinner", or "O wicked"]. And whosoever does not repent, then such are indeed Zâlimûn (wrong-doers, etc.). (49:11)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اجتنبوا كثيرا من الظ: 12. O you who believe! Avoid much suspicion; indeed some suspicions are sins. And spy not, neither backbite one another. Would one of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother? You would hate it (so hate backbiting)[2]. And fear Allâh. Verily, Allâh is the One Who forgives and accepts repentance, Most Merciful. (49:12)
يا ايها الناس انا خلقناكم من ذكر وانثى و: 13. O mankind! We have created you from a male and a female, and made you into nations and tribes, that you may know one another. Verily, the most honourable of you with Allâh is that (believer) who has At-Taqwâ [i.e. he is one of the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2)]. Verily, Allâh is All-Knowing, All-Aware. (49:13)
قالت الاعراب امنا قل لم تؤمنوا ولكن قولو: 14. The bedouins say: "We believe." Say: "You believe not but you only say, ‘We have surrendered (in Islâm),’ for Faith has not yet entered your hearts. But if you obey Allâh and His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم), He will not decrease anything in reward for your deeds. Verily, Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful." (49:14)
انما المؤمنون الذين امنوا بالله ورسوله ث: 15. Only those are the believers who have believed in Allâh and His Messenger, and afterward doubt not but strive with their wealth and their lives for the Cause of Allâh. Those! They are the truthful. (49:15)
قل اتعلمون الله بدينكم والله يعلم ما في: 16. Say: "Will you inform Allâh of your religion while Allâh knows all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth, and Allâh is All-Aware of everything. (49:16)
يمنون عليك ان اسلموا قل لا تمنوا علي اسل: 17. They regard as favour to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) that they have embraced Islâm. Say: "Count not your Islâm as a favour to me. Nay, but Allâh has conferred a favour upon you that He has guided you to the Faith if you indeed are true. (49:17)
ان الله يعلم غيب السماوات والارض والله ب: 18. Verily, Allâh knows the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. And Allâh is the All-Seer of what you do. (49:18)
ق والقران المجيد: 1. Qâf. [These letters (Qâf, etc.) are one of the miracles of the Qur’ân, and none but Allâh (Alone) knows their meanings]. By the Glorious Qur’ân. (50:1)
بل عجبوا ان جاءهم منذر منهم فقال الكافرو: 2. Nay, they wonder that there has come to them a warner (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from among themselves. So the disbelievers say: "This is a strange thing! (50:2)
ااذا متنا وكنا ترابا ذلك رجع بعيد: 3. "When we are dead and have become dust (shall we be resurrected?) That is a far return." (50:3)
قد علمنا ما تنقص الارض منهم وعندنا كتاب: 4. We know that which the earth takes of them (their dead bodies), and with Us is a Book preserved (i.e. the Book of Decrees). (50:4)
بل كذبوا بالحق لما جاءهم فهم في امر مريج: 5. Nay, but they have denied the truth (this Qur’ân) when it has come to them, so they are in a confused state (cannot differentiate between right and wrong). (50:5)
افلم ينظروا الى السماء فوقهم كيف بنيناها: 6. Have they not looked at the heaven above them, how We have made it and adorned it, and there are no rifts in it? (50:6)
والارض مددناها والقينا فيها رواسي وانبتن: 7. And the earth! We have spread it out, and set thereon mountains standing firm, and have produced therein every kind of lovely growth (plants). (50:7)
تبصرة وذكرى لكل عبد منيب: 8. An insight and a Reminder for every slave who turns to Allâh in repentance (i.e. the one who believes in the Oneness of Allâh and performs deeds of His obedience, and always begs His pardon). (50:8)
ونزلنا من السماء ماء مباركا فانبتنا به ج: 9. And We send down blessed water (rain) from the sky, then We produce therewith gardens and grain (every kind of harvests) that are reaped. (50:9)
والنخل باسقات لها طلع نضيد: 10. And tall date-palms, with ranged clusters. (50:10)
رزقا للعباد واحيينا به بلدة ميتا كذلك ال: 11. A provision for (Allâh’s) slaves. And We give life therewith to a dead land. Thus will be the resurrection (of the dead). (50:11)
كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح واصحاب الرس وثمود: 12. Denied before them (i.e. these pagans of Makkah) the people of Nûh (Noah), and the dwellers of Rass, and Thamûd; (50:12)
وعاد وفرعون واخوان لوط: 13. And ‘Âd, and Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), and the brethren of Lût (Lot); (50:13)
واصحاب الايكة وقوم تبع كل كذب الرسل فحق: 14. And the Dwellers of the Wood, and the people of Tubba‘. Everyone of them denied (their) Messengers, so My Threat took effect. (50:14)
افعيينا بالخلق الاول بل هم في لبس من خلق: 15. Were We then tired with the first creation? Nay, they are in confused doubt about a new creation (i.e. Resurrection). (50:15)
ولقد خلقنا الانسان ونعلم ما توسوس به نفس: 16. And indeed We have created man, and We know what his ownself whispers to him. And We are nearer to him than his jugular vein (by Our Knowledge). (50:16)
اذ يتلقى المتلقيان عن اليمين وعن الشمال: 17. (Remember) that the two receivers (recording angels) receive (each human being),[1] one sitting on the right and one on the left (to note his or her actions).[2] (50:17)
ما يلفظ من قول الا لديه رقيب عتيد: 18. Not a word does he (or she) utter but there is a watcher by him ready (to record it).[3] (50:18)
وجاءت سكرة الموت بالحق ذلك ما كنت منه تح: 19. And the stupor of death will come in truth: "This is what you have been avoiding!" (50:19)
ونفخ في الصور ذلك يوم الوعيد: 20. And the Trumpet will be blown - that will be the Day whereof warning (had been given) (i.e. the Day of Resurrection). (50:20)
وجاءت كل نفس معها سائق وشهيد: 21. And every person will come forth along with an (angel) to drive (him) and an (angel) to bear witness. (50:21)
لقد كنت في غفلة من هذا فكشفنا عنك غطاءك: 22. (It will be said to the sinners): "Indeed you were heedless of this. Now We have removed from you your covering, and sharp is your sight this Day!" (50:22)
وقال قرينه هذا ما لدي عتيد: 23. And his companion (angel) will say: "Here is (this Record) ready with me!" (50:23)
القيا في جهنم كل كفار عنيد: 24. (Allah will say to the angels): "Both of you throw into Hell every stubborn disbeliever (in the Oneness of Allâh, in His Messengers). (50:24)
الذي جعل مع الله الها اخر فالقياه في الع: 26. "Who set up another ilâh (god) with Allâh. Then both of you cast him in the severe torment." (50:26)
قال قرينه ربنا ما اطغيته ولكن كان في ضلا: 27. His companion (Satan - devil)] will say: "Our Lord! I did not push him to transgression, (in disbelief, oppression, and evil deeds) but he was himself in error far astray." (50:27)
قال لا تختصموا لدي وقد قدمت اليكم بالوعي: 28. Allâh will say: "Dispute not in front of Me, I had already in advance sent you the threat. (50:28)
ما يبدل القول لدي وما انا بظلام للعبيد: 29. The Sentence that comes from Me cannot be changed, and I am not unjust to the slaves." (50:29)
يوم نقول لجهنم هل امتلات وتقول هل من مزي: 30. On the Day when We will say to Hell: "Are you filled?" It will say: "Are there any more (to come)?"[4] (50:30)
وازلفت الجنة للمتقين غير بعيد: 31. And Paradise will be brought near to the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2), not far off. (50:31)
هذا ما توعدون لكل اواب حفيظ: 32. (It will be said): "This is what you were promised - (it is) for those oft-returning (to Allâh) in sincere repentance, and those who preserve their covenant with Allâh (by obeying Him in all what He has ordered, and worshipping none but Allâh Alone, i.e. follow Allâh’s religion - Islâmic Monotheism). (50:32)
من خشي الرحمن بالغيب وجاء بقلب منيب: 33. "Who feared the Most Gracious (Allâh) in the Ghaib (Unseen) and brought a heart turned in repentance (to Him and absolutely free from each and every kind of polytheism). (50:33)
ادخلوها بسلام ذلك يوم الخلود: 34. "Enter you therein in peace and security - this is a Day of eternal life!" (50:34)
لهم ما يشاءون فيها ولدينا مزيد: 35. There they will have all that they desire - and We have more (for them, i.e. a glance at the All-Mighty, All-Majestic جل جلاله). (50:35)
وكم اهلكنا قبلهم من قرن هم اشد منهم بطشا: 36. And how many a generation We have destroyed before them who were stronger in power than they. And (when Our Torment came), they ran for a refuge in the land! Could they find any place of refuge (for them to save themselves from destruction)? (50:36)
ان في ذلك لذكرى لمن كان له قلب او القى ا: 37. Verily, therein is indeed a reminder for him who has a heart or gives ear while he is heedful. (50:37)
ولقد خلقنا السماوات والارض وما بينهما في: 38. And indeed We created the heavens and the earth and all between them in six Days and nothing of fatigue touched Us. (50:38)
فاصبر على ما يقولون وسبح بحمد ربك قبل طل: 39. So bear with patience (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) all that they say, and glorify the Praises of your Lord, before the rising of the sun and before (its) setting (i.e. the Fajr, Zuhr, and ‘Asr prayers)[5]. (50:39)
ومن الليل فسبحه وادبار السجود: 40. And during a part of the night (also) glorify His praises (i.e. Maghrib and ‘Isha prayers) and (so likewise) after the prayers [As-Sunnah, Nawâfil - optional and additional prayers. And also glorify, praise and magnify Allâh - Subhân Allâh, Alhamdu lillâh, Allâhu-Akbar]. (50:40)
واستمع يوم يناد المناد من مكان قريب: 41. And listen on the Day when the caller will call from a near place. (50:41)
يوم يسمعون الصيحة بالحق ذلك يوم الخروج: 42. The Day when they will hear As-Saihah (shout) in truth: that will be the Day of coming out (from the graves, i.e. the Day of Resurrection). (50:42)
انا نحن نحيي ونميت والينا المصير: 43. Verily, We it is Who give life and cause death; and to Us is the final return. (50:43)
يوم تشقق الارض عنهم سراعا ذلك حشر علينا: 44. On the Day when the earth shall be cleft, from off them, (they will come out) hastening forth. That will be a gathering, quite easy for Us. (50:44)
نحن اعلم بما يقولون وما انت عليهم بجبار: 45. We know best what they say. And you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are not the one to force them (to Belief). But warn by the Qur’an; him who fears My Threat. (50:45)
فالحاملات وقرا: 2. And (the clouds) that bear heavy weight of water. (51:2)
فالجاريات يسرا: 3. And (the ships) that float with ease and gentleness. (51:3)
فالمقسمات امرا: 4. And those (angels) who distribute (provisions, rain, and other blessings) by (Allâh’s) Command. (51:4)
انما توعدون لصادق: 5. Verily, that which you are promised (i.e. Resurrection in the Hereafter and receiving the reward or punishment of good or bad deeds) is surely true. (51:5)
وان الدين لواقع: 6. And verily, the Recompense is sure to happen. (51:6)
والسماء ذات الحبك: 7. By the heaven full of paths, (51:7)
انكم لفي قول مختلف: 8. Certainly, you have different ideas (about Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم and the Qur’ân). (51:8)
يؤفك عنه من افك: 9. Turned aside therefrom (i.e. from Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم and the Qur’ân) is he who is turned aside (by the Decree and Preordainment القضاء والقدر of Allâh). (51:9)
الذين هم في غمرة ساهون: 11. Who are under a cover of heedlessness (think not about the gravity of the Hereafter), (51:11)
يسالون ايان يوم الدين: 12. They ask: "When will be the Day of Recompense?" (51:12)
يوم هم على النار يفتنون: 13. (It will be) a Day when they will be tried (punished i.e. burnt) over the Fire! (51:13)
ذوقوا فتنتكم هذا الذي كنتم به تستعجلون: 14. "Taste you your trial (punishment i.e. burning)! This is what you used to ask to be hastened!" (51:14)
اخذين ما اتاهم ربهم انهم كانوا قبل ذلك م: 16. Taking joy in the things which their Lord has given them. Verily, they were before this Muhsinûn (good-doers. See V.2:112). (51:16)
كانوا قليلا من الليل ما يهجعون: 17. They used to sleep but little by night [invoking their Lord (Allâh) and praying, with fear and hope]. (51:17)
وبالاسحار هم يستغفرون: 18. And in the hours before dawn, they were (found) asking (Allâh) for forgiveness. (51:18)
وفي اموالهم حق للسائل والمحروم: 19. And in their properties there was the right of the Sâ’il (the beggar who asks) and the Mahrûm (the poor who does not ask others)[1]. (51:19)
وفي الارض ايات للموقنين: 20. And on the earth are signs for those who have Faith with certainty. (51:20)
وفي انفسكم افلا تبصرون: 21. And also in your ownselves. Will you not then see? (51:21)
وفي السماء رزقكم وما توعدون: 22. And in the heaven is your provision, and that which you are promised. (51:22)
فورب السماء والارض انه لحق مثل ما انكم ت: 23. Then by the Lord of the heaven and the earth, it is the truth (i.e. what has been promised to you), just as it is the truth that you can speak. (51:23)
هل اتاك حديث ضيف ابراهيم المكرمين: 24. Has the story reached you, of the honoured guests [three angels; Jibrîl (Gabriel) along with another two] of Ibrâhîm (Abraham)? (51:24)
اذ دخلوا عليه فقالوا سلاما قال سلام قوم: 25. When they came in to him and said: "Salâm, (peace be upon you)!" He answered: "Salâm, (peace be upon you)," and said: "You are a people unknown to me." (51:25)
فراغ الى اهله فجاء بعجل سمين: 26. Then he turned to his household, and brought out a roasted calf [as the property of Ibrâhîm (Abraham) was mainly cows]. (51:26)
فقربه اليهم قال الا تاكلون: 27. And placed it before them, (saying): "Will you not eat?" (51:27)
فاوجس منهم خيفة قالوا لا تخف وبشروه بغلا: 28. Then he conceived fear of them (when they ate not). They said: "Fear not."[2] And they gave him glad tidings of a son having knowledge (about Allâh and His religion of True Monotheism). (51:28)
فاقبلت امراته في صرة فصكت وجهها وقالت عج: 29. Then his wife came forward with a loud voice: she smote her face, and said: "A barren old woman!"[3] (51:29)
قالوا كذلك قال ربك انه هو الحكيم العليم: 30. They said: "Even so says your Lord.[4] Verily, He is the All-Wise, the All-Knower." (51:30)
لنرسل عليهم حجارة من طين: 33. To send down upon them stones of baked clay. (51:33)
مسومة عند ربك للمسرفين: 34. Marked by your Lord for the Musrifûn (polytheists, criminals, sinners - those who trespass Allâh’s set limits in evil-doings by committing great sins). (51:34)
فاخرجنا من كان فيها من المؤمنين: 35. So We brought out from therein the believers. (51:35)
فما وجدنا فيها غير بيت من المسلمين: 36. But We found not there any household of the Muslims except one [of Lût (Lot) and his two daughters]. (51:36)
وتركنا فيها اية للذين يخافون العذاب الال: 37. And We have left there a sign (i.e. the place of the Dead Sea[5] in Palestine) for those who fear the painful torment. (51:37)
وفي موسى اذ ارسلناه الى فرعون بسلطان مبي: 38. And in Mûsâ (Moses) (too, there is a sign), when We sent him to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) with a manifest authority. (51:38)
فتولى بركنه وقال ساحر او مجنون: 39. But [Fir‘aun (Pharaoh)] turned away (from Belief in might) along with his hosts, and said: "A sorcerer, or a madman." (51:39)
فاخذناه وجنوده فنبذناهم في اليم وهو مليم: 40. So We took him and his hosts, and dumped them into the sea, for he was blameworthy. (51:40)
وفي عاد اذ ارسلنا عليهم الريح العقيم: 41. And in ‘Âd (there is also a sign) when We sent against them the barren wind; (51:41)
ما تذر من شيء اتت عليه الا جعلته كالرميم: 42. It spared nothing that it reached, but blew it into broken spreads of rotten ruins. (51:42)
وفي ثمود اذ قيل لهم تمتعوا حتى حين: 43. And in Thamûd (there is also a sign), when they were told: "Enjoy yourselves for a while!" (51:43)
فعتوا عن امر ربهم فاخذتهم الصاعقة وهم ين: 44. But they insolently defied the Command of their Lord, so the Sâ‘iqah[6] overtook them while they were looking. (51:44)
فما استطاعوا من قيام وما كانوا منتصرين: 45. Then they were unable to rise up, nor could they help themselves. (51:45)
وقوم نوح من قبل انهم كانوا قوما فاسقين: 46. (So were) the people of Nûh (Noah) before them. Verily, they were a people who were Fâsiqûn (rebellious, disobedient to Allâh). (51:46)
والسماء بنيناها بايد وانا لموسعون: 47. With power did We construct the heaven. Verily, We are Able to extend the vastness of space thereof. (51:47)
والارض فرشناها فنعم الماهدون: 48. And We have spread out the earth: how Excellent Spreader (thereof) are We! (51:48)
ومن كل شيء خلقنا زوجين لعلكم تذكرون: 49. And of everything We have created pairs, that you may remember (the Grace of Allâh). (51:49)
ففروا الى الله اني لكم منه نذير مبين: 50. So flee to Allâh (from His Torment to His Mercy - Islâmic Monotheism). Verily, I (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) am a plain warner to you from Him.[7] (51:50)
ولا تجعلوا مع الله الها اخر اني لكم منه: 51. And set not up (or worship not) any other ilâh (god) along with Allâh [Glorified be He (Alone), Exalted above all that they associate as partners with Him]. Verily, I (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) am a plain warner to you from Him. (51:51)
كذلك ما اتى الذين من قبلهم من رسول الا ق: 52. Likewise, no Messenger came to those before them but they said: "A sorcerer or a madman!" (51:52)
اتواصوا به بل هم قوم طاغون: 53. Have they (the people of the past) transmitted this saying to these (Quraish pagans)? Nay, they are themselves a people transgressing beyond bounds (in disbelief)! (51:53)
فتول عنهم فما انت بملوم: 54. So turn away (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from them (Quraish pagans), you are not blame-worthy (as you have conveyed Allâh’s Message). (51:54)
وذكر فان الذكرى تنفع المؤمنين: 55. And remind (by preaching the Qur’ân, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), for verily, the reminding profits the believers. (51:55)
وما خلقت الجن والانس الا ليعبدون: 56. And I (Allâh) created not the jinn and mankind except that they should worship Me (Alone). (51:56)
ما اريد منهم من رزق وما اريد ان يطعمون: 57. I seek not any provision from them (i.e. provision for themselves or for My creatures) nor do I ask that they should feed Me (i.e. feed themselves or My creatures). (51:57)
ان الله هو الرزاق ذو القوة المتين: 58. Verily, Allâh is the All-Provider, Owner of Power, the Most Strong. (51:58)
فان للذين ظلموا ذنوبا مثل ذنوب اصحابهم ف: 59. And verily, for those who do wrong, there is a portion of torment like to the evil portion of torment (which came for) their likes (of old); so let them not ask Me to hasten on! (51:59)
فويل للذين كفروا من يومهم الذي يوعدون: 60. Then woe to those who disbelieve (in Allâh and His Oneness - Islâmic Monotheism) from their Day which they have been promised (for their punishment).[8] (51:60)
والبيت المعمور: 4. And by Al-Bait-ul-Ma‘mûr[1] (the house over the heavens parallel to the Ka‘bah at Makkah, continuously visited by the angels). (52:4)
والسقف المرفوع: 5. And by the roof raised high (i.e. the heaven). (52:5)
والبحر المسجور: 6. And by the sea kept filled (or it will be fire kindled on the Day of Resurrection). (52:6)
ان عذاب ربك لواقع: 7. Verily, the Torment of your Lord will surely come to pass. (52:7)
ما له من دافع: 8. There is none that can avert it. (52:8)
يوم تمور السماء مورا: 9. On the Day when the heaven will shake with a dreadful shaking, (52:9)
وتسير الجبال سيرا: 10. And the mountains will move away with a (horrible) movement. (52:10)
فويل يومئذ للمكذبين: 11. Then woe that Day to the beliers.[2] (52:11)
يوم يدعون الى نار جهنم دعا: 13. The Day when they will be pushed down by force to the Fire of Hell, with a horrible, forceful pushing. (52:13)
هذه النار التي كنتم بها تكذبون: 14. This is the Fire which you used to belie. (52:14)
افسحر هذا ام انتم لا تبصرون: 15. Is this magic, or do you not see? (52:15)
اصلوها فاصبروا او لا تصبروا سواء عليكم ا: 16. Taste you therein its heat and whether you are patient of it or impatient of it, it is all the same. You are only being requited for what you used to do. (52:16)
ان المتقين في جنات ونعيم: 17. Verily, the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2) will be in Gardens (Paradise), and Delight. (52:17)
فاكهين بما اتاهم ربهم ووقاهم ربهم عذاب ا: 18. Enjoying in that which their Lord has bestowed on them, and (the fact that) their Lord saved them from the torment of the blazing Fire. (52:18)
كلوا واشربوا هنيئا بما كنتم تعملون: 19. "Eat and drink with happiness because of what you used to do." (52:19)
متكئين على سرر مصفوفة وزوجناهم بحور عين: 20. They will recline (with ease) on thrones arranged in ranks. And We shall marry them to Hûr: (female, fair ones) with wide lovely eyes.[4] (52:20)
والذين امنوا واتبعتهم ذريتهم بايمان الحق: 21. And those who believe and whose offspring follow them in Faith: to them shall We join their offspring, and We shall not decrease the reward of their deeds in anything. Every person is a pledge for that which he has earned. (52:21)
وامددناهم بفاكهة ولحم مما يشتهون: 22. And We shall provide them with fruit and meat such as they desire. (52:22)
يتنازعون فيها كاسا لا لغو فيها ولا تاثيم: 23. There they shall pass from hand to hand a (wine) cup, free from any Laghw (dirty, false, evil vain talk between them), and free from sin[5] (because it will be lawful for them to drink). (52:23)
ويطوف عليهم غلمان لهم كانهم لؤلؤ مكنون: 24. And there will go round boy-servants of theirs, to serve them as if they were preserved pearls. (52:24)
قالوا انا كنا قبل في اهلنا مشفقين: 26. Saying: "Aforetime, we were afraid (of the punishment of Allâh) in the midst of our families. (52:26)
فمن الله علينا ووقانا عذاب السموم: 27. "So Allâh has been gracious to us, and has saved us from the torment of the Fire. (52:27)
انا كنا من قبل ندعوه انه هو البر الرحيم: 28. "Verily, We used to invoke Him (Alone and none else) before. Verily, He is Al-Barr (the Most Subtle, Kind, Courteous, and Generous), the Most Merciful."[6] (52:28)
فذكر فما انت بنعمت ربك بكاهن ولا مجنون: 29. Therefore, remind (mankind of Islâmic Monotheism, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). By the Grace of Allâh, you are neither a soothsayer, nor a madman. (52:29)
ام يقولون شاعر نتربص به ريب المنون: 30. Or do they say: "(Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is a poet! We await for him some calamity by time!" (52:30)
قل تربصوا فاني معكم من المتربصين: 31. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to them): "Wait! I am with you among the waiters!" (52:31)
ام تامرهم احلامهم بهذا ام هم قوم طاغون: 32. Do their minds command them this [i.e. to tell a lie against you (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم)] or are they people transgressing all bounds? (52:32)
ام يقولون تقوله بل لا يؤمنون: 33. Or do they say: "He (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) has forged it (this Qur’ân)?" Nay! They believe not! (52:33)
فلياتوا بحديث مثله ان كانوا صادقين: 34. Let them then produce a recital like unto it (the Qur’ân) if they are truthful. (52:34)
ام خلقوا من غير شيء ام هم الخالقون: 35. Were they created by nothing? Or were they themselves the creators? (52:35)
ام خلقوا السماوات والارض بل لا يوقنون: 36. Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Nay, but they have no firm Belief. (52:36)
ام عندهم خزائن ربك ام هم المصيطرون: 37. Or are with them the treasures of your Lord? Or are they the tyrants with the authority to do as they like? (52:37)
ام لهم سلم يستمعون فيه فليات مستمعهم بسل: 38. Or have they a stairway (to heaven), by means of which they listen (to the talks of the angels)? Then let their listener produce some manifest proof. (52:38)
ام له البنات ولكم البنون: 39. Or has He (Allâh) only daughters and you have sons? (52:39)
ام تسالهم اجرا فهم من مغرم مثقلون: 40. Or is it that you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) ask a wage from them (for your preaching of Islâmic Monotheism) so that they are burdened with a load of debt? (52:40)
ام عندهم الغيب فهم يكتبون: 41. Or that the Ghaib (Unseen) is with them, and they write it down? (52:41)
ام يريدون كيدا فالذين كفروا هم المكيدون: 42. Or do they intend a plot (against you O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم)? But those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) are themselves plotted against! (52:42)
ام لهم اله غير الله سبحان الله عما يشركو: 43. Or have they an ilâh (a god) other than Allâh? Glorified be Allâh from all that they ascribe as partners (to Him)[7] (52:43)
وان يروا كسفا من السماء ساقطا يقولوا سحا: 44. And if they were to see a piece of the heaven falling down, they would say: "Clouds gathered in heaps!" (52:44)
فذرهم حتى يلاقوا يومهم الذي فيه يصعقون: 45. So leave them alone till they meet their Day, in which they will sink into a fainting (with horror). (52:45)
يوم لا يغني عنهم كيدهم شيئا ولا هم ينصرو: 46. The Day when their plotting shall not avail them at all nor will they be helped (i.e. they will receive their torment in Hell). (52:46)
وان للذين ظلموا عذابا دون ذلك ولكن اكثره: 47. And verily, for those who do wrong, there is another punishment (i.e. the torment in this world and in their graves)[8] before this; but most of them know not. (Tafsir At-Tabarî). (52:47)
واصبر لحكم ربك فانك باعيننا وسبح بحمد رب: 48. So wait patiently (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) for the Decision of your Lord, for verily, you are under Our Eyes[9]; and glorify the Praises of your Lord when you get up from sleep. (52:48)
ومن الليل فسبحه وادبار النجوم: 49. And in the nighttime also glorify His Praises - and at the setting of the stars.[10] (52:49)
والنجم اذا هوى: 1. By the star when it goes down (or vanishes). (53:1)
ما ضل صاحبكم وما غوى: 2. Your companion (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) has neither gone astray nor has erred. (53:2)
وما ينطق عن الهوى: 3. Nor does he speak of (his own) desire. (53:3)
ان هو الا وحي يوحى: 4. It is only a Revelation revealed. (53:4)
علمه شديد القوى: 5. He has been taught (this Qur’ân) by one mighty in power [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] (53:5)
ذو مرة فاستوى: 6. One free from any defect in body and mind then he (Jibrîl - Gabriel in his real shape as created by Allah) rose and became stable. (53:6)
وهو بالافق الاعلى: 7. While he [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] was in the highest part of the horizon, (Tafsir Ibn Kathir) (53:7)
ثم دنا فتدلى: 8. Then he [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] approached and came closer, (53:8)
فكان قاب قوسين او ادنى: 9. And was at a distance of two bows’ length or (even) nearer. (53:9)
فاوحى الى عبده ما اوحى: 10. So (Allâh) revealed to His slave [Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم through Jibrîl (Gabriel) عليه السلام] whatever He revealed. (53:10)
ما كذب الفؤاد ما راى: 11. The (Prophet’s) heart lied not in what he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) saw. (53:11)
افتمارونه على ما يرى: 12. Will you then dispute with him (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) about what he saw [during the Mi‘râj: (Ascent of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم to the seven heavens)][1] (53:12)
ولقد راه نزلة اخرى: 13. And indeed he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) saw him [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] at a second descent (i.e. another time). (53:13)
عند سدرة المنتهى: 14. Near Sidrat-ul-Muntaha (a lote-tree of the utmost boundary over the seventh heaven beyond which none can pass). (53:14)
عندها جنة الماوى: 15. Near it is the Paradise of Abode. (53:15)
اذ يغشى السدرة ما يغشى: 16. When that covered the lote-tree which did cover it![2] (53:16)
ما زاغ البصر وما طغى: 17. The sight (of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) turned not aside (right or left), nor it transgressed beyond the limit (ordained for it). (53:17)
لقد راى من ايات ربه الكبرى: 18. Indeed he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) did see of the Greatest Signs, of his Lord (Allâh). (53:18)
افرايتم اللات والعزى: 19. Have you then considered Al-Lât, and Al-‘Uzzâ (two idols of the pagan Arabs)[3] (53:19)
ومناة الثالثة الاخرى: 20. And Manât (another idol of the pagan Arabs), the other third? (53:20)
الكم الذكر وله الانثى: 21. Is it for you the males and for Him the females? (53:21)
تلك اذا قسمة ضيزى: 22. That indeed is a division most unfair! (53:22)
ان هي الا اسماء سميتموها انتم واباؤكم ما: 23. They are but names which you have named - you and your fathers - for which Allâh has sent down no authority. They follow but a guess and that which they themselves desire, whereas there has surely come to them the Guidance from their Lord! (53:23)
ام للانسان ما تمنى: 24. Or shall man have what he wishes? (53:24)
فلله الاخرة والاولى: 25. But to Allâh belongs the last (Hereafter) and the first (the world). (53:25)
وكم من ملك في السماوات لا تغني شفاعتهم ش: 26. And there are many angels in the heavens, whose intercession will avail nothing except after Allâh has given leave for whom He wills and is pleased with. (53:26)
ان الذين لا يؤمنون بالاخرة ليسمون الملائ: 27. Verily, those who believe not in the Hereafter, name the angels with female names. (53:27)
وما لهم به من علم ان يتبعون الا الظن وان: 28. But they have no knowledge thereof. They follow but a guess, and verily, guess is no substitute for the truth. (53:28)
فاعرض عن من تولى عن ذكرنا ولم يرد الا ال: 29. Therefore withdraw (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from him who turns away from Our Reminder (this Qur’ân) and desires nothing but the life of this world. (53:29)
ذلك مبلغهم من العلم ان ربك هو اعلم بمن ض: 30. That is what they could reach of knowledge. Verily, your Lord it is He Who knows best him who goes astray from His Path, and He knows best him who receives guidance. (53:30)
ولله ما في السماوات وما في الارض ليجزي ا: 31. And to Allâh belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth, that He may requite those who do evil with that which they have done (i.e. punish them in Hell), and reward those who do good, with what is best (i.e. Paradise). (53:31)
الذين يجتنبون كبائر الاثم والفواحش الا ا: 32. Those who avoid great sins (See the Qur’ân, Verses: 6:152,153) and Al-Fawâhish (illegal sexual intercourse) except the small faults, - verily, your Lord is of Vast Forgiveness. He knows you well when He created you from the earth (Adam), and when you were fetuses in your mothers’ wombs. So ascribe not purity to yourselves. He knows best him who fears Allâh and keeps his duty to Him [i.e. those who are Al-Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2)]. (53:32)
افرايت الذي تولى: 33. Did you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) observe him who turned away (from Islâm). (53:33)
واعطى قليلا واكدى: 34. And gave a little, then stopped (giving)? (53:34)
اعنده علم الغيب فهو يرى: 35. Is with him the knowledge of the Unseen so that he sees? (53:35)
ام لم ينبا بما في صحف موسى: 36. Or is he not informed with what is in the Pages (Scripture) of Mûsâ (Moses), (53:36)
وابراهيم الذي وفى: 37. And of Ibrâhîm (Abraham) who fulfilled (or conveyed) all that (Allâh ordered him to do or convey): (53:37)
الا تزر وازرة وزر اخرى: 38. That no burdened person (with sins) shall bear the burden (sins) of another. (53:38)
