وان ليس للانسان الا ما سعى: 39. And that man can have nothing but what he does (good or bad).[4] (53:39)
وان سعيه سوف يرى: 40. And that his deeds will be seen. (53:40)
ثم يجزاه الجزاء الاوفى: 41. Then he will be recompensed with a full and the best recompense.[5] (53:41)
وان الى ربك المنتهى: 42. And that to your Lord (Allâh) is the End (Return of everything). (53:42)
وانه هو اضحك وابكى: 43. And that it is He (Allâh) Who makes (whom He wills) laugh, and makes (whom He wills) weep. (53:43)
وانه هو امات واحيا: 44. And that it is He (Allâh) Who causes death and gives life. (53:44)
وانه خلق الزوجين الذكر والانثى: 45. And that He (Allâh) creates the pairs, male and female, (53:45)
من نطفة اذا تمنى: 46. From Nutfah (drops of semen - male and female discharges) when it is emitted. (53:46)
وان عليه النشاة الاخرى: 47. And that upon Him (Allâh) is another bringing forth (Resurrection). (53:47)
وانه هو اغنى واقنى: 48. And that it is He (Allâh) Who gives much or a little (of wealth and contentment) (53:48)
وانه هو رب الشعرى: 49. And that He (Allâh) is the Lord of Sirius (the star which the pagan Arabs used to worship). (53:49)
وانه اهلك عادا الاولى: 50. And that it is He (Allâh) Who destroyed the former ‘Âd (people), (53:50)
وثمود فما ابقى: 51. And Thamûd (people): He spared none of them. (53:51)
وقوم نوح من قبل انهم كانوا هم اظلم واطغى: 52. And the people of Nûh (Noah) aforetime. Verily, they were more unjust and more rebellious and transgressing [in disobeying Allâh and His Messenger Nûh (Noah) عليه السلام]. (53:52)
والمؤتفكة اهوى: 53. And He destroyed the overthrown cities [of Sodom to which Prophet Lût (Lot) was sent]. (53:53)
فغشاها ما غشى: 54. So there covered them that which did cover (i.e. torment with stones). (53:54)
فباي الاء ربك تتمارى: 55. Then which of the Graces of your Lord (O man!) will you doubt? (53:55)
هذا نذير من النذر الاولى: 56. This (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is a warner (Messenger) of the (series of) warners (Messengers) of old.[6] (53:56)
ازفت الازفة: 57. The Day of Resurrection draws near. (53:57)
ليس لها من دون الله كاشفة: 58. None besides Allâh can avert it (or advance it or delay it). (53:58)
افمن هذا الحديث تعجبون: 59. Do you then wonder at this recitation (the Qur’ân)? (53:59)
وتضحكون ولا تبكون: 60. And you laugh at it and weep not, (53:60)
وانتم سامدون: 61. Wasting your (precious) lifetime in pastime and amusements (singing, etc.). (53:61)
فاسجدوا لله واعبدوا: 62. So fall you down in prostration to Allâh and worship Him (Alone). (53:62)
اقتربت الساعة وانشق القمر: 1. The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has been cleft asunder (the people of Makkah requested Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to show them a miracle, so he showed them the splitting of the moon).[1] (54:1)
وان يروا اية يعرضوا ويقولوا سحر مستمر: 2. And if they see a sign, they turn away, and say: "This is continuous magic." (54:2)
وكذبوا واتبعوا اهواءهم وكل امر مستقر: 3. They belied (the Verses of Allâh - this Qur’ân), and followed their own lusts. And every matter will be settled (according to the kind of deeds: good deeds will take their doers to Paradise, and similarly evil deeds will take their doers to Hell). (54:3)
ولقد جاءهم من الانباء ما فيه مزدجر: 4. And indeed there has come to them news (in this Qur’ân) wherein there is (enough warning) to check (them from evil), (54:4)
حكمة بالغة فما تغن النذر: 5. Perfect wisdom (this Qur’ân), but (the preaching of) warners benefit them not. (54:5)
فتول عنهم يوم يدع الداع الى شيء نكر: 6. So (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) withdraw from them. The Day that the caller will call (them) to a terrible thing. (54:6)
خشعا ابصارهم يخرجون من الاجداث كانهم جرا: 7. They will come forth, with humbled eyes from (their) graves as if they were locusts spread abroad, (54:7)
مهطعين الى الداع يقول الكافرون هذا يوم ع: 8. Hastening towards the caller. The disbelievers will say: "This is a hard Day." (54:8)
كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح فكذبوا عبدنا وقالوا م: 9. The people of Nûh (Noah) denied (their Messenger) before them. They rejected Our slave, and said: "A madman!" and he was insolently rebuked and threatened. (54:9)
فدعا ربه اني مغلوب فانتصر: 10. Then he invoked his Lord (saying): "I have been overcome, so help (me)!" (54:10)
ففتحنا ابواب السماء بماء منهمر: 11. So We opened the gates of the heaven with water pouring forth. (54:11)
وفجرنا الارض عيونا فالتقى الماء على امر: 12. And We caused springs to gush forth from the earth. So the waters (of the heaven and the earth) met for a matter predestined. (54:12)
وحملناه على ذات الواح ودسر: 13. And We carried him on a (ship) made of planks and nails, (54:13)
تجري باعيننا جزاء لمن كان كفر: 14. Floating under Our Eyes: a reward for him who had been rejected! (54:14)
ولقد تركناها اية فهل من مدكر: 15. And indeed, We have left this as a sign. Then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)? (54:15)
فكيف كان عذابي ونذر: 16. Then how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings? (54:16)
ولقد يسرنا القران للذكر فهل من مدكر: 17. And We have indeed made the Qur’ân easy to understand and remember; then is there any one who will remember (or receive admonition)? (54:17)
كذبت عاد فكيف كان عذابي ونذر: 18. ‘Âd (people) belied (their Prophet, Hûd); then how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings? (54:18)
انا ارسلنا عليهم ريحا صرصرا في يوم نحس م: 19. Verily, We sent against them a furious wind of harsh voice on a day of evil omen and continuous calamity. (54:19)
تنزع الناس كانهم اعجاز نخل منقعر: 20. Plucking out men as if they were uprooted stems of date-palms. (54:20)
فقالوا ابشرا منا واحدا نتبعه انا اذا لفي: 24. And they said: "A man, alone among us - shall we follow him? Truly then we should be in error and distress (or madness)!" (54:24)
االقي الذكر عليه من بيننا بل هو كذاب اشر: 25. "Is it that the Reminder is sent to him [Prophet Sâlih عليه السلام] alone from among us? Nay, he is an insolent liar!" (54:25)
سيعلمون غدا من الكذاب الاشر: 26. Tomorrow they will come to know who is the liar, the insolent one! (54:26)
انا مرسلو الناقة فتنة لهم فارتقبهم واصطب: 27. Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them [O Sâlih عليه السلام], and be patient! (54:27)
ونبئهم ان الماء قسمة بينهم كل شرب محتضر: 28. And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them, each one’s right to drink being established (by turns). (54:28)
فنادوا صاحبهم فتعاطى فعقر: 29. But they called their comrade and he took (a sword) and killed (her). (54:29)
انا ارسلنا عليهم صيحة واحدة فكانوا كهشيم: 31. Verily, We sent against them a single Saihah (torment - awful cry), and they became like the stubble of a fold-builder. (54:31)
كذبت قوم لوط بالنذر: 33. The people of Lût (Lot) belied the warnings. (54:33)
انا ارسلنا عليهم حاصبا الا ال لوط نجيناه: 34. Verily, We sent against them a violent storm of stones (which destroyed them all), except the family of Lût (Lot), them We saved in the last hour of the night, (54:34)
نعمة من عندنا كذلك نجزي من شكر: 35. As a Favour from Us. Thus do We reward him who gives thanks (by obeying Us). (54:35)
ولقد انذرهم بطشتنا فتماروا بالنذر: 36. And he [Lût (Lot)] indeed had warned them of Our Seizure (punishment), but they did doubt the warnings! (54:36)
ولقد راودوه عن ضيفه فطمسنا اعينهم فذوقوا: 37. And they indeed sought to shame his guest (by asking to commit sodomy with them). So We blinded their eyes, (saying) "Then taste you My Torment and My Warnings." (54:37)
ولقد صبحهم بكرة عذاب مستقر: 38. And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning. (54:38)
فذوقوا عذابي ونذر: 39. "Then taste you My Torment and My Warnings." (54:39)
ولقد جاء ال فرعون النذر: 41. And indeed, warnings came to the people of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) [through Mûsâ (Moses) and Hârûn (Aaron)]. (54:41)
كذبوا باياتنا كلها فاخذناهم اخذ عزيز مقت: 42. (They) belied all Our Signs, so We seized them with a Seizure of the All-Mighty, All-Capable (Omnipotent). (54:42)
اكفاركم خير من اولئكم ام لكم براءة في ال: 43. Are your disbelievers (O Quraish!) better than these [nations of Nûh (Noah), Lût (Lot), Sâlih, and the people of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) who were destroyed)? Or have you an immunity (against Our Torment) in the Divine Scriptures? (54:43)
ام يقولون نحن جميع منتصر: 44. Or say they: "We are a great multitude, victorious?" (54:44)
سيهزم الجمع ويولون الدبر: 45. Their multitude will be put to flight, and they will show their backs. (54:45)
بل الساعة موعدهم والساعة ادهى وامر: 46. Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time (for their full recompense), and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter. (54:46)
ان المجرمين في ضلال وسعر: 47. Verily the Mujrimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals) are in error (in this world) and will burn (in the Hell-fire in the Hereafter). (54:47)
يوم يسحبون في النار على وجوههم ذوقوا مس: 48. The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire (it will be said to them): "Taste you the touch of Hell!"[2] (54:48)
انا كل شيء خلقناه بقدر: 49. Verily, We have created all things with Qadar (Divine Preordainments of all things before their creation as written in the Book of Decrees Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz). (54:49)
وما امرنا الا واحدة كلمح بالبصر: 50. And Our Commandment is but one as the twinkling of an eye. (54:50)
ولقد اهلكنا اشياعكم فهل من مدكر: 51. And indeed, We have destroyed your likes; then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)? (54:51)
وكل شيء فعلوه في الزبر: 52. And everything they have done is noted in (their) Records (of deeds). (54:52)
وكل صغير وكبير مستطر: 53. And everything, small and big, is written down (in Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz already beforehand i.e. before it befalls, or is done by its doer: الإيمان بالقدر) (See the Qur’ân V.57:22 and its footnote). (54:53)
ان المتقين في جنات ونهر: 54. Verily, The Muttaqûn (the pious - see V.2:2), will be in the midst of Gardens and Rivers (Paradise). (54:54)
في مقعد صدق عند مليك مقتدر: 55. In a seat of truth (i.e. Paradise), near the Omnipotent King (Allâh, the One, the All-Blessed, the Most High, the Owner of Majesty and Honour). (54:55)
علم القران: 2. He has taught (you mankind) the Qur’ân (by His Mercy). (55:2)
الشمس والقمر بحسبان: 5. The sun and the moon run on their fixed courses (exactly) calculated with measured out stages for each (for reckoning). (55:5)
والنجم والشجر يسجدان: 6. And the herbs (or stars) and the trees both prostrate themselves (to Allah - See V.22:18) (Tafsir Ibn Kathir) (55:6)
والسماء رفعها ووضع الميزان: 7. And the heaven: He has raised it high, and He has set up the Balance. (55:7)
الا تطغوا في الميزان: 8. In order that you may not transgress (due) balance. (55:8)
واقيموا الوزن بالقسط ولا تخسروا الميزان: 9. And observe the weight with equity and do not make the balance deficient. (55:9)
والارض وضعها للانام: 10. And the earth: He has put down (laid) for the creatures. (55:10)
فيها فاكهة والنخل ذات الاكمام: 11. Therein are fruits and date-palms producing sheathed fruit-stalks (enclosing dates). (55:11)
والحب ذو العصف والريحان: 12. And also corn, with (its) leaves and stalk for fodder, and sweet-scented plants. (55:12)
فباي الاء ربكما تكذبان: 13. Then which of the Blessings of your Lord will you both (jinn and men) deny? (55:13)
خلق الانسان من صلصال كالفخار: 14. He created man (Adam) from sounding clay like the clay of pottery. (55:14)
وخلق الجان من مارج من نار: 15. And the jinn: He created from a smokeless flame of fire. (55:15)
رب المشرقين ورب المغربين: 17. (He is) the Lord of the two easts (places of sunrise during early summer and early winter) and the Lord of the two wests (places of sunset during early summer and early winter). (55:17)
مرج البحرين يلتقيان: 19. He has let loose the two seas (the salt and fresh water) meeting together. (55:19)
بينهما برزخ لا يبغيان: 20. Between them is a barrier which none of them can transgress. (55:20)
يخرج منهما اللؤلؤ والمرجان: 22. Out of them both come out pearl and coral. (55:22)
وله الجوار المنشات في البحر كالاعلام: 24. And His are the ships going and coming in the seas, like mountains. (55:24)
كل من عليها فان: 26. Whatsoever is on it (the earth) will perish. (55:26)
ويبقى وجه ربك ذو الجلال والاكرام: 27. And the Face of your Lord full of Majesty and Honour will remain forever. (55:27)
يساله من في السماوات والارض كل يوم هو في: 29. Whosoever is in the heavens and on earth begs of Him (its needs from Him). Every day He is (engaged) in some affair (such as giving honour or disgrace to some, life or death to some, etc.)! (55:29)
سنفرغ لكم ايه الثقلان: 31. We shall attend to you, O you two classes (jinn and men)! (55:31)
يا معشر الجن والانس ان استطعتم ان تنفذوا: 33. O assembly of jinn and men! If you have power to pass beyond the zones of the heavens and the earth, then pass beyond (them)! But you will never be able to pass them, except with authority (from Allâh)! (55:33)
يرسل عليكما شواظ من نار ونحاس فلا تنتصرا: 35. There will be sent against you both, smokeless flames of fire and (molten) brass, and you will not be able to defend yourselves. (55:35)
فاذا انشقت السماء فكانت وردة كالدهان: 37. Then when the heaven is rent asunder, and it becomes rosy or red like red-oil, or red hide - [See V.70:8] (55:37)
فيومئذ لا يسال عن ذنبه انس ولا جان: 39. So on that Day no question will be asked of man or jinns as to his sin, [because they have already been known from their faces either white (dwellers of Paradise - true believers of Islamic Monotheism) or black (dwellers of Hell - polytheists; disbelievers, criminals)]. (55:39)
يعرف المجرمون بسيماهم فيؤخذ بالنواصي وال: 41. The Mujrimûn (polytheists, criminals, sinners) will be known by their marks (black faces), and they will be seized by their forelocks and their feet. (55:41)
هذه جهنم التي يكذب بها المجرمون: 43. This is the Hell which the Mujrimûn (polytheists, criminals, sinners) denied. (55:43)
يطوفون بينها وبين حميم ان: 44. They will go between it (Hell) and the fierce boiling water! (55:44)
ولمن خاف مقام ربه جنتان: 46. But for him who[1] fears the standing before his Lord, there will be two Gardens (i.e. in Paradise).[2] (55:46)
فيهما عينان تجريان: 50. In them (both) will be two springs flowing (free). (55:50)
فيهما من كل فاكهة زوجان: 52. In them (both) will be every kind of fruit in pairs. (55:52)
متكئين على فرش بطائنها من استبرق وجنى ال: 54. Reclining upon the couches lined with silk brocade, and the fruits of the two Gardens will be near at hand. (55:54)
فيهن قاصرات الطرف لم يطمثهن انس قبلهم ول: 56. Wherein both will be Qâsirât-ut-Tarf [chaste females (wives) restraining their glances, desiring none except their husbands], with whom no man or jinni has had tamth[3] before them. (55:56)
كانهن الياقوت والمرجان: 58. (In beauty) they are like rubies and coral. (55:58)
هل جزاء الاحسان الا الاحسان: 60. Is there any reward for good other than good? (55:60)
ومن دونهما جنتان: 62. And besides these two, there are two other Gardens (i.e. in Paradise). (55:62)
فيهما عينان نضاختان: 66. In them (both) will be two springs gushing forth. (55:66)
فيهما فاكهة ونخل ورمان: 68. In them (both) will be fruits, and date-palms and pomegranates. (55:68)
فيهن خيرات حسان: 70. Therein (Gardens) will be Khairâtun-Hisân [fair (wives) good and beautiful]; (55:70)
حور مقصورات في الخيام: 72. Hûr[4] (beautiful, fair females) guarded in pavilions; (55:72)
لم يطمثهن انس قبلهم ولا جان: 74. With whom no man or jinni has had tamth[5] before them. (55:74)
متكئين على رفرف خضر وعبقري حسان: 76. Reclining on green cushions and rich beautiful mattresses. (55:76)
تبارك اسم ربك ذي الجلال والاكرام: 78. Blessed be the Name of your Lord (Allâh), the Owner of Majesty and Honour. (55:78)
اذا وقعت الواقعة: 1. When the Event (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) befalls. (56:1)
ليس لوقعتها كاذبة: 2. And there can be no denial of its befalling. (56:2)
خافضة رافعة: 3. Bringing low (some - those who will enter Hell) Exalting (others - those who will enter Paradise). [Tafsir Ibn Kathir] (56:3)
اذا رجت الارض رجا: 4. When the earth will be shaken with a terrible shake. (56:4)
وبست الجبال بسا: 5. And the mountains will be powdered to dust, (56:5)
فكانت هباء منبثا: 6. So that they will become floating dust particles. (56:6)
وكنتم ازواجا ثلاثة: 7. And you (all) will be in three groups. (56:7)
فاصحاب الميمنة ما اصحاب الميمنة: 8. So those on the Right Hand (i.e. those who will be given their Records in their right hands) - how (fortunate) will be those on the Right Hand! (As a respect for them, because they will enter Paradise). (56:8)
واصحاب المشامة ما اصحاب المشامة: 9. And those on the Left Hand (i.e. those who will be given their Record in their left hands) - how (unfortunate) will be those on the Left Hand! (As a disgrace for them, because they will enter Hell). (56:9)
والسابقون السابقون: 10. And those foremost [(in Islâmic Faith of Monotheism and in performing righteous deeds) in the life of this world on the very first call for to embrace Islâm] will be foremost (in Paradise). (56:10)
اولئك المقربون: 11. These will be the nearest (to Allâh). (56:11)
ثلة من الاولين: 13. A multitude of those (foremost) will be from the first generations (who embraced Islâm). (56:13)
وقليل من الاخرين: 14. And a few of those (foremost) will be from the later generations. (56:14)
على سرر موضونة: 15. (They will be) on thrones woven with gold and precious stones. (56:15)
يطوف عليهم ولدان مخلدون: 17. Immortal boys will go around them (serving), (56:17)
باكواب واباريق وكاس من معين: 18. With cups, and jugs, and a glass of flowing wine, (56:18)
لا يصدعون عنها ولا ينزفون: 19. Wherefrom they will get neither any aching of the head, nor any intoxication. (56:19)
وفاكهة مما يتخيرون: 20. And with fruit that they may choose. (56:20)
ولحم طير مما يشتهون: 21. And with the flesh of fowls that they desire. (56:21)
وحور عين: 22. And (there will be) Hûr (fair females) with wide, lovely eyes (as wives for "Al-Muttaqûn" - the pious - See V. 2:2), (56:22)
جزاء بما كانوا يعملون: 24. A reward for what they used to do. (56:24)
لا يسمعون فيها لغوا ولا تاثيما: 25. No Laghw (dirty, false, evil vain talk) will they hear therein, nor any sinful speech (like backbiting). (56:25)
الا قيلا سلاما سلاما: 26. But only the saying of: Salâm! Salâm! (greetings with peace)![1] (56:26)
واصحاب اليمين ما اصحاب اليمين: 27. And those on the Right Hand - how (fortunate) will be those on the Right Hand? (56:27)
وطلح منضود: 29. And among Talh (banana-trees) with fruits piled one above another, (56:29)
لا مقطوعة ولا ممنوعة: 33. Whose supply is not cut off (by change of season), nor are they out of reach, (56:33)
وفرش مرفوعة: 34. And on couches or thrones, raised high. (56:34)
انا انشاناهن انشاء: 35. Verily, We have created them (maidens) of special creation. (56:35)
عربا اترابا: 37. Loving (their husbands only), (and) of equal age. (56:37)
لاصحاب اليمين: 38. For those on the Right Hand. (56:38)
وثلة من الاخرين: 40. And a multitude of those (on the Right Hand) will be from the later generations. (56:40)
واصحاب الشمال ما اصحاب الشمال: 41. And those on the Left Hand - how (unfortunate) will be those on the Left Hand? (56:41)
في سموم وحميم: 42. In fierce hot wind and boiling water, (56:42)
انهم كانوا قبل ذلك مترفين: 45. Verily, before that, they indulged in luxury, (56:45)
وكانوا يصرون على الحنث العظيم: 46. And were persisting in great sin (joining partners in worship along with Allâh, committing murder and other crimes)[2] (56:46)
وكانوا يقولون ائذا متنا وكنا ترابا وعظام: 47. And they used to say: "When we die and become dust and bones, shall we then indeed be resurrected? (56:47)
قل ان الاولين والاخرين: 49. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "(Yes) verily, those of old, and those of later times. (56:49)
لمجموعون الى ميقات يوم معلوم: 50. "All will surely be gathered together for appointed Meeting of a known Day. (56:50)
ثم انكم ايها الضالون المكذبون: 51. "Then moreover, verily you the erring-ones, the deniers (of Resurrection)! (56:51)
لاكلون من شجر من زقوم: 52. "You verily will eat of the trees of Zaqqûm. (56:52)
فمالئون منها البطون: 53. "Then you will fill your bellies therewith, (56:53)
فشاربون عليه من الحميم: 54. "And drink boiling water on top of it. (56:54)
فشاربون شرب الهيم: 55. "And you will drink (that) like thirsty camels!" (56:55)
هذا نزلهم يوم الدين: 56. That will be their entertainment on the Day of Recompense! (56:56)
نحن خلقناكم فلولا تصدقون: 57. We created you: then why do you believe not? (56:57)
افرايتم ما تمنون: 58. Then tell Me (about) the (human) semen that you emit. (56:58)
اانتم تخلقونه ام نحن الخالقون: 59. Is it you who create it (i.e. make this semen into a perfect human being), or are We the Creator? (56:59)
نحن قدرنا بينكم الموت وما نحن بمسبوقين: 60. We have decreed death to you all, and We are not outstripped, (56:60)
على ان نبدل امثالكم وننشئكم في ما لا تعل: 61. To transfigure you and create you in (forms) that you know not. (56:61)
ولقد علمتم النشاة الاولى فلولا تذكرون: 62. And indeed, you have already known the first form of creation (i.e. the creation of Adam): why then do you not remember (or take heed)? (56:62)
افرايتم ما تحرثون: 63. Then tell Me about the seed that you sow in the ground. (56:63)
اانتم تزرعونه ام نحن الزارعون: 64. Is it you that make it grow, or are We the Grower? (56:64)
لو نشاء لجعلناه حطاما فظلتم تفكهون: 65. Were it Our Will, We could crumble it to dry pieces, and you would be regretful (or left in wonderment) [Tafsir Ibn Kathîr]. (56:65)
انا لمغرمون: 66. (Saying): "We are indeed Mughramûn (i.e. ruined or have lost the money without any profit, or are punished by the loss of all that we spend for cultivation)! (Tafsir Al-Qurtubî) (56:66)
افرايتم الماء الذي تشربون: 68. Then tell Me about the water that you drink. (56:68)
اانتم انزلتموه من المزن ام نحن المنزلون: 69. Is it you who cause it from the rain-clouds to come down, or are We the Causer of it to come down? (56:69)
لو نشاء جعلناه اجاجا فلولا تشكرون: 70. If We willed, We verily could make it salt (and undrinkable): why then do you not give thanks (to Allâh)? (56:70)
افرايتم النار التي تورون: 71. Then tell Me about the fire which you kindle. (56:71)
اانتم انشاتم شجرتها ام نحن المنشئون: 72. Is it you who made the tree thereof to grow, or are We the Grower? (56:72)
نحن جعلناها تذكرة ومتاعا للمقوين: 73. We have made it a Reminder (of the Hell-fire in the Hereafter), and an article of use for the travellers (and all the others, in this world). (56:73)
فسبح باسم ربك العظيم: 74. Then glorify with praises the Name of your Lord, the Most Great. (56:74)
فلا اقسم بمواقع النجوم: 75. So I swear by the setting of the stars.[3] (56:75)
وانه لقسم لو تعلمون عظيم: 76. And verily that is indeed a great oath, if you but know. (56:76)
انه لقران كريم: 77. That (this) is indeed an honourable recitation (the Noble Qur’ân). (56:77)
في كتاب مكنون: 78. In a Book well-guarded (with Allâh in the heaven i.e. Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz ). (56:78)
لا يمسه الا المطهرون: 79. Which (that Book with Allâh) none can touch but the purified (i.e. the angels). (56:79)
تنزيل من رب العالمين: 80. A Revelation (this Qur’ân) from the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists). (56:80)
افبهذا الحديث انتم مدهنون: 81. Is it such a talk (this Qur’ân) that you (disbelievers) deny? (56:81)
وتجعلون رزقكم انكم تكذبون: 82. And instead (of thanking Allâh) for the provision He gives you, you deny (Him by disbelief)! (56:82)
فلولا اذا بلغت الحلقوم: 83. Then why do you not (intervene) when (the soul of a dying person) reaches the throat? (56:83)
وانتم حينئذ تنظرون: 84. And you at the moment are looking on, (56:84)
ونحن اقرب اليه منكم ولكن لا تبصرون: 85. But We (i.e. Our angels who take the soul) are nearer to him than you, but you see not, (Tafsir At-Tabarî) (56:85)
فلولا ان كنتم غير مدينين: 86. Then why do you not - if you are exempt from the reckoning and recompense (punishment) - (56:86)
ترجعونها ان كنتم صادقين: 87. Bring back the soul (to its body), if you are truthful? (56:87)
فاما ان كان من المقربين: 88. Then, if he (the dying person) be of the Muqarrabûn (those brought near to Allâh), (56:88)
فروح وريحان وجنت نعيم: 89. (There is for him) rest and provision, and a Garden of Delights (Paradise). (56:89)
واما ان كان من اصحاب اليمين: 90. And if he (the dying person) be of those on the Right Hand, (56:90)
فسلام لك من اصحاب اليمين: 91. Then there is safety and peace (from the Punishment of Allâh) for those on the right Hand. (56:91)
واما ان كان من المكذبين الضالين: 92. But if he (the dying person) be of the denying (of the Resurrection), the erring (away from the Right Path of Islâmic Monotheism), (56:92)
فنزل من حميم: 93. Then for him is an entertainment with boiling water. (56:93)
ان هذا لهو حق اليقين: 95. Verily, this! This is an absolute Truth with certainty. (56:95)
سبح لله ما في السماوات والارض وهو العزيز: 1. Whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allâh - and He is the All-Mighty, All-Wise. (57:1)
له ملك السماوات والارض يحيي ويميت وهو عل: 2. His is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. It is He Who gives life and causes death: and He is Able to do all things. (57:2)
هو الاول والاخر والظاهر والباطن وهو بكل: 3. He is the First (nothing is before Him) and the Last (nothing is after Him), the Most High (nothing is above Him) and the Most Near (nothing is nearer than Him). And He is the All-Knower of every thing. (57:3)
هو الذي خلق السماوات والارض في ستة ايام: 4. He it is Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days and then rose over (Istawâ) the Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty). He knows what goes into the earth and what comes forth from it, and what descends from the heaven and what ascends thereto. And He is with you (by His Knowledge) wheresoever you may be. And Allâh is the All-Seer of what you do. (57:4)
له ملك السماوات والارض والى الله ترجع ال: 5. His is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allâh return all the matters (for decision). (57:5)
يولج الليل في النهار ويولج النهار في الل: 6. He merges night into day (i.e. the decrease in the hours of the night is added into the hours of the day), and merges day into night (i.e. the decrease in the hours of the day is added into the hours of the night), and He has full knowledge of whatsoever is in the breasts. (57:6)
امنوا بالله ورسوله وانفقوا مما جعلكم مست: 7. Believe in Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), and spend of that whereof He has made you trustees.[1] And such of you as believe and spend (in Allâh’s Way), theirs will be a great reward. (57:7)
وما لكم لا تؤمنون بالله والرسول يدعوكم ل: 8. And what is the matter with you that you believe not in Allâh! While the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) invites you to believe in your Lord (Allâh); and He (Allâh) has indeed taken your covenant, if you are real believers. (57:8)
هو الذي ينزل على عبده ايات بينات ليخرجكم: 9. It is He Who sends down manifest Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) to His slave (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) that He may bring you out from darkness into light. And verily, Allâh is to you full of kindness, Most Merciful. (57:9)
وما لكم الا تنفقوا في سبيل الله ولله مير: 10. And what is the matter with you that you spend not in the Cause of Allâh? And to Allâh belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth. Not equal among you are those who spent and fought before the conquering (of Makkah, with those among you who did so later). Such are higher in degree than those who spent and fought afterwards. But to all Allâh has promised the best (reward). And Allâh is All-Aware of what you do. (57:10)
من ذا الذي يقرض الله قرضا حسنا فيضاعفه ل: 11. Who is he that will lend Allâh a goodly loan: then (Allâh) will increase it manifold to his credit (in repaying), and he will have (besides) a good reward (i.e. Paradise). (57:11)
يوم ترى المؤمنين والمؤمنات يسعى نورهم بي: 12. On the Day you shall see the believing men and the believing women: their light running forward before them and by their right hands. Glad tidings for you this Day! Gardens under which rivers flow (Paradise), to dwell therein forever! Truly, this is the great success! (57:12)
يوم يقول المنافقون والمنافقات للذين امنو: 13. On the Day when the hypocrites - men and women - will say to the believers: "Wait for us! Let us get something from your light!" It will be said: "Go back to your rear! Then seek a light!" So a wall will be put up between them, with a gate therein. Inside it will be mercy, and outside it will be torment." (57:13)
ينادونهم الم نكن معكم قالوا بلى ولكنكم ف: 14. (The hypocrites) will call the believers: "Were we not with you?" The believers will reply: "Yes! But you led yourselves into temptations, you looked forward for our destruction; you doubted (in Faith) and you were deceived by false desires, till the Command of Allâh came to pass. And the chief deceiver (Satan) deceived you in respect of Allâh." (57:14)
فاليوم لا يؤخذ منكم فدية ولا من الذين كف: 15. So this Day no ransom shall be taken from you (hypocrites), nor of those who disbelieved, (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism). Your abode is the Fire. That is your maulâ (friend - proper place), and worst indeed is that destination. (57:15)
الم يان للذين امنوا ان تخشع قلوبهم لذكر: 16. Has not the time come for the hearts of those who believe (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) to be affected by Allâh’s Reminder (this Qur’ân), and that which has been revealed of the truth, lest they become as those who received the Scripture [the Taurât (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] before (i.e. Jews and Christians), and the term was prolonged for them and so their hearts were hardened? And many of them were Fâsiqûn (the rebellious, the disobedient to Allâh). (57:16)
اعلموا ان الله يحيي الارض بعد موتها قد ب: 17. Know that Allâh gives life to the earth after its death! Indeed We have made clear the Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) to you, if you but understand. (57:17)
ان المصدقين والمصدقات واقرضوا الله قرضا: 18. Verily, those who give Sadaqât (i.e. Zakât and alms), men and women, and lend Allâh a goodly loan, it shall be increased manifold (to their credit), and theirs shall be an honourable good reward (i.e. Paradise). (57:18)
والذين امنوا بالله ورسله اولئك هم الصديق: 19. And those who believe in (the Oneness of) Allâh and His Messengers - they are the Siddiqûn (i.e. those followers of the Prophets who were first and foremost to believe in them), and the martyrs with their Lord, they shall have their reward and their light. But those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) and deny Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) - they shall be the dwellers of the blazing Fire. (57:19)
اعلموا انما الحياة الدنيا لعب ولهو وزينة: 20. Know that the life of this world is only play and amusement, pomp and mutual boasting among you, and rivalry in respect of wealth and children. (It is) as the likeness of vegetation after rain, thereof the growth is pleasing to the tiller; afterwards it dries up and you see it turning yellow; then it becomes straw. But in the Hereafter (there is) a severe torment (for the disbelievers - evil-doers), and (there is) Forgiveness from Allâh and (His) Good Pleasure (for the believers - good-doers). And the life of this world is only a deceiving enjoyment. (57:20)
سابقوا الى مغفرة من ربكم وجنة عرضها كعرض: 21. Race with one another in hastening towards forgiveness from your Lord (Allâh), and Paradise the width whereof is as the width of the heaven and the earth, prepared for those who believe in Allâh and His Messengers. That is the Grace of Allâh which He bestows on whom He is pleased with. And Allâh is the Owner of Great Bounty. (57:21)
ما اصاب من مصيبة في الارض ولا في انفسكم: 22. No calamity befalls on the earth or in yourselves but it is inscribed in the Book of Decrees (Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz) before We bring it into existence. Verily, that is easy for Allâh.[2] (57:22)
لكيلا تاسوا على ما فاتكم ولا تفرحوا بما: 23. In order that you may not grieve at the things that you fail to get, nor rejoice over that which has been given to you. And Allâh likes not prideful boasters. (57:23)
الذين يبخلون ويامرون الناس بالبخل ومن يت: 24. Those who are misers and enjoin upon people miserliness - (Allâh is not in need of their charity). And whosoever turns away (from Faith - Allâh’s Monotheism), then Allâh is Rich (Free of all needs), Worthy of all praise. (57:24)
لقد ارسلنا رسلنا بالبينات وانزلنا معهم ا: 25. Indeed We have sent Our Messengers with clear proofs, and revealed with them the Scripture and the Balance (justice) that mankind may keep up justice. And We brought forth iron wherein is mighty power (in matters of war[3]), as well as many benefits for mankind, that Allâh may test who it is that will help Him (His religion) and His Messengers in the unseen. Verily, Allâh is All-Strong, All-Mighty. (57:25)
ولقد ارسلنا نوحا وابراهيم وجعلنا في ذريت: 26. And indeed, We sent Nûh (Noah) and Ibrâhîm (Abraham), and placed in their offspring Prophethood and Scripture. And among them there are some who are guided; but many of them are Fâsiqûn (rebellious, disobedient to Allâh). (57:26)
ثم قفينا على اثارهم برسلنا وقفينا بعيسى: 27. Then, We sent after them Our Messengers, and We sent ‘Îsâ (Jesus) - son of Maryam (Mary), and gave him the Injeel (Gospel). And We ordained in the hearts of those who followed him compassion and mercy. But the monasticism which they invented for themselves, We did not prescribe for them, but (they sought it) only to please Allâh therewith, but they did not observe it with the right observance. So We gave those among them who believed their (due) reward; but many of them are Fâsiqûn (rebellious, disobedient to Allâh). (57:27)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اتقوا الله وامنوا بر: 28. O you who believe [in Mûsâ (Moses) (i.e. Jews) and ‘Îsâ (Jesus) (i.e. Christians)]! Fear Allâh, and believe in His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), He will give you a double portion of His Mercy, and He will give you a light by which you shall walk (straight). And He will forgive you. And Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.[4] (57:28)
لئلا يعلم اهل الكتاب الا يقدرون على شيء: 29. So that the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) may know that they have no power whatsoever over the Grace of Allâh, and that (His) Grace is (entirely) in His Hand to bestow it on whomsoever He wills. And Allâh is the Owner of Great Bounty. (57:29)
قد سمع الله قول التي تجادلك في زوجها وتش: 1. Indeed Allâh has heard the statement of her (Khaulah bint Tha‘labah) that disputes with you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) concerning her husband (Aus bin As-Sâmit), and complains to Allâh. And Allâh hears the argument between you both. Verily, Allâh is All-Hearer, All-Seer. (58:1)
الذين يظاهرون منكم من نسائهم ما هن امهات: 2. Those among you who make their wives unlawful to them by (Zihâr الظهار)[1] (i.e. by saying to them "You are like my mother’s back,") they cannot be their mothers. None can be their mothers except those who gave them birth. And verily, they utter an ill word and a lie. And verily, Allâh is Oft-Pardoning, Oft-Forgiving. (58:2)
والذين يظاهرون من نسائهم ثم يعودون لما ق: 3. And those who make unlawful to them their wives by Zihâr and wish to free themselves from what they uttered, (the penalty) in that case is the freeing of a slave before they touch each other. That is an admonition to you (so that you may not repeat such an ill thing). And Allâh is All-Aware of what you do. (58:3)
فمن لم يجد فصيام شهرين متتابعين من قبل ا: 4. And he who finds not (the money for freeing a slave) must fast two successive months before they both touch each other. And he who is unable to do so, should feed sixty Miskîns (poor). That is in order that you may have perfect Faith in Allâh and His Messenger. These are the limits set by Allâh. And for disbelievers, there is a painful torment. (58:4)
ان الذين يحادون الله ورسوله كبتوا كما كب: 5. Verily, those who oppose Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم)[2] will be disgraced, as those before them (among the past nation) were disgraced. And We have sent down clear Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). And for the disbelievers is a disgracing torment. (58:5)
يوم يبعثهم الله جميعا فينبئهم بما عملوا: 6. On the Day when Allâh will resurrect them all together (i.e. on the Day of Resurrection) and inform them of what they did. Allâh has kept account of it, while they have forgotten it. And Allâh is Witness over all things. (58:6)
الم تر ان الله يعلم ما في السماوات وما ف: 7. Have you not seen that Allâh knows whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is on the earth? There is no Najwâ[3] (secret counsel) of three but He is their fourth (with His Knowledge, while He Himself is over the Throne, over the seventh heaven), nor of five but He is their sixth (with His Knowledge), nor of less than that or more but He is with them (with His Knowledge) wheresoever they may be. And afterwards on the Day of Resurrection He will inform them of what they did. Verily, Allâh is the All-Knower of everything. (58:7)
الم تر الى الذين نهوا عن النجوى ثم يعودو: 8. Have you not seen those who were forbidden to hold secret counsels, and afterwards returned to that which they had been forbidden, and conspired together for sin and wrong doing and disobedience to the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). And when they come to you, they greet you with a greeting wherewith Allâh greets you not, and say within themselves: "Why should Allâh punish us not for what we say?" Hell will be sufficient for them; they will burn therein. And worst indeed is that destination! (58:8)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اذا تناجيتم فلا تتنا: 9. O you who believe! When you hold secret counsel, do it not for sin and wrong-doing, and disobedience towards the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), but do it for Al-Birr (righteousness) and Taqwâ (virtues and piety); and fear Allâh unto Whom you shall be gathered. (58:9)
انما النجوى من الشيطان ليحزن الذين امنوا: 10. Secret counsels (conspiracies) are only from Shaitân (Satan), in order that he may cause grief to the believers. But he cannot harm them in the least, except as Allâh permits. And in Allâh let the believers put their trust[4]. (58:10)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اذا قيل لكم تفسحوا ف: 11. O you who believe! When you are told to make room in the assemblies, (spread out and) make room. Allâh will give you (ample) room (from His Mercy). And when you are told to rise up [for prayers, or Jihâd (holy fighting in Allâh’s cause), or for any other good deed], rise up. Allâh will exalt in degree those of you who believe, and those who have been granted knowledge. And Allâh is Well-Acquainted with what you do. (58:11)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اذا ناجيتم الرسول فق: 12. O you who believe! When you (want to) consult the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) in private, spend something in charity before your private consultation. That will be better and purer for you. But if you find not (the means for it), then verily, Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful. (58:12)
ااشفقتم ان تقدموا بين يدي نجواكم صدقات ف: 13. Are you afraid of spending in charity before your private consultation (with him)? If then you do it not, and Allâh has forgiven you, then (at least) perform Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât ) and give Zakât and obey Allâh (i.e. do all that Allâh and His Messenger صلى الله عليه وسلم order you to do). And Allâh is All-Aware of what you do. (58:13)
الم تر الى الذين تولوا قوما غضب الله علي: 14. Have you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) not seen those (hypocrites) who take as friends a people upon whom is the Wrath of Allâh (i.e. Jews)? They are neither of you (Muslims) nor of them (Jews), and they swear to a lie while they know. (58:14)
اعد الله لهم عذابا شديدا انهم ساء ما كان: 15. Allâh has prepared for them a severe torment. Evil indeed is that which they used to do. (58:15)
اتخذوا ايمانهم جنة فصدوا عن سبيل الله فل: 16. They have made their oaths a screen (for their evil actions). Thus they hinder (men) from the Path of Allâh: so they shall have a humiliating torment. (58:16)
لن تغني عنهم اموالهم ولا اولادهم من الله: 17. Their children and their wealth will avail them nothing against Allâh. They will be the dwellers of the Fire to dwell therein forever. (58:17)
يوم يبعثهم الله جميعا فيحلفون له كما يحل: 18. On the Day when Allâh will resurrect them all together (for their account); then they will swear to Him as they swear to you (O Muslims). And they think that they have something (to stand upon). Verily, they are liars! (58:18)
استحوذ عليهم الشيطان فانساهم ذكر الله او: 19. Shaitân (Satan) has overpowered them. So he has made them forget the remembrance of Allâh. They are the party of Shaitân (Satan). Verily, it is the party of Shaitan (Satan) that will be the losers! (58:19)
ان الذين يحادون الله ورسوله اولئك في الا: 20. Those who oppose Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) they will be among the lowest (most humiliated). (58:20)
كتب الله لاغلبن انا ورسلي ان الله قوي عز: 21. Allâh has decreed: "Verily, it is I and My Messengers who shall be the victorious." Verily, Allâh is All-Powerful, All-Mighty. (58:21)
لا تجد قوما يؤمنون بالله واليوم الاخر يو: 22. You (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) will not find any people who believe in Allâh and the Last Day, making friendship with those who oppose Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), even though they were their fathers or their sons or their brothers or their kindred (people). For such He has written Faith in their hearts, and strengthened them with Rûh (proofs, light and true guidance) from Himself. And He will admit them to Gardens (Paradise) under which rivers flow to dwell therein (forever). Allâh is pleased with them, and they with Him. They are the Party of Allâh. Verily, it is the Party of Allâh that will be the successful. (58:22)
سبح لله ما في السماوات وما في الارض وهو: 1. Whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is on the earth glorifies Allâh. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. (59:1)
هو الذي اخرج الذين كفروا من اهل الكتاب م: 2. He it is Who drove out the disbelievers among the people of the Scripture (i.e. the Jews of the tribe of Banû An-Nadîr) from their homes at the first gathering. You did not think that they would get out. And they thought that their fortresses would defend them from Allâh! But Allâh’s (Torment) reached them from a place whereof they expected it not, and He cast terror into their hearts so that they destroyed their own dwellings with their own hands and the hands of the believers. Then take admonition, O you with eyes (to see). (59:2)
ولولا ان كتب الله عليهم الجلاء لعذبهم في: 3. And had it not been that Allâh had decreed exile for them, He would certainly have punished them in this world; and in the Hereafter theirs shall be the torment of the Fire. (59:3)
ذلك بانهم شاقوا الله ورسوله ومن يشاق الل: 4. That is because they opposed Allâh and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). And whosoever opposes Allâh, then verily, Allâh is Severe in punishment. (59:4)
ما قطعتم من لينة او تركتموها قائمة على ا: 5. What you (O Muslims) cut down of the palm-trees (of the enemy), or you left them standing on their stems, it was by Leave of Allâh, and in order that He might disgrace the Fâsiqûn (the rebellious, the disobedient to Allâh). (59:5)
وما افاء الله على رسوله منهم فما اوجفتم: 6. And what Allâh gave as booty (Fai’) to His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from them, for this you made no expedition with either cavalry or camelry. But Allâh gives power to His Messengers over whomsoever He wills. And Allâh is Able to do all things. (59:6)
ما افاء الله على رسوله من اهل القرى فلله: 7. What Allâh gave as booty (Fai’) to His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from the people of the townships - it is for Allâh, His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), the kindred (of Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), the orphans, Al-Masâkin (the poor), and the wayfarer, in order that it may not become a fortune used by the rich among you. And whatsoever the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) gives you, take it; and whatsoever he forbids you, abstain (from it).[1] And fear Allâh; verily, Allâh is Severe in punishment. (59:7)
للفقراء المهاجرين الذين اخرجوا من ديارهم: 8. (And there is also a share in this booty) for the poor emigrants, who were expelled from their homes and their property, seeking Bounties from Allâh and to please Him, and helping Allâh (i.e. helping His religion) and His Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم). Such are indeed the truthful (to what they say). (59:8)
والذين تبوءوا الدار والايمان من قبلهم يح: 9. And (it is also for) those who, before them, had homes (in Al-Madinah) and had adopted the Faith, love those who emigrate to them, and have no jealousy in their breasts for that which they have been given (from the booty of Banû An-Nadîr), and give them (emigrants) preference over themselves even though they were in need of that. And whosoever is saved from his own covetousness, such are they who will be the successful. (59:9)
والذين جاءوا من بعدهم يقولون ربنا اغفر ل: 10. And those who came after them say: "Our Lord! Forgive us and our brethren who have preceded us in Faith, and put not in our hearts any hatred against those who have believed. Our Lord! You are indeed full of kindness, Most Merciful. (59:10)
الم تر الى الذين نافقوا يقولون لاخوانهم: 11. Have you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) not observed the hypocrites who say to their friends among the people of the Scripture who disbelieve: "(By Allâh) If you are expelled, we (too) indeed will go out with you, and we shall never obey any one against you; and if you are attacked (in fight), we shall indeed help you." But Allâh is Witness that they verily are liars. (59:11)
لئن اخرجوا لا يخرجون معهم ولئن قوتلوا لا: 12. Surely, if they (the Jews) are expelled, never will they (hypocrites) go out with them; and if they are attacked, they will never help them. And (even) if they do help them, they (hypocrites) will turn their backs, and they will not be victorious. (59:12)
لانتم اشد رهبة في صدورهم من الله ذلك بان: 13. Verily, you (believers in the Oneness of Allâh - Islâmic Monotheism) are more fearful in their (Jews of Banû An-Nadîr) breasts than Allâh. That is because they are a people who comprehend not (the Majesty and Power of Allâh). (59:13)
لا يقاتلونكم جميعا الا في قرى محصنة او م: 14. They fight not against you even together, except in fortified townships, or from behind walls. Their enmity among themselves is very great. You would think they were united, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people who understand not. (59:14)
كمثل الذين من قبلهم قريبا ذاقوا وبال امر: 15. They are like their immediate predecessors (the Jews of Banû Qainûqâ‘, who suffered); they tasted the evil result of their conduct, and (in the Hereafter, there is) for them a painful torment. (59:15)
كمثل الشيطان اذ قال للانسان اكفر فلما كف: 16. (Their allies deceived them) like Shaitân (Satan), when he says to man: "Disbelieve in Allâh." But when (man) disbelieves in Allâh, Shaitân (Satan) says: "I am free of you, I fear Allâh, the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)!" (59:16)
فكان عاقبتهما انهما في النار خالدين فيها: 17. So the end of both will be that they will be in the Fire, abiding therein. Such is the recompense of the Zâlimûn (i.e. polytheists, wrong-doers, disbelievers in Allâh and in His Oneness). (59:17)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اتقوا الله ولتنظر نف: 18. O you who believe! Fear Allâh and keep your duty to Him. And let every person look to what he has sent forth for the morrow, and fear Allâh. Verily, Allâh is All-Aware of what you do.[2] (59:18)
ولا تكونوا كالذين نسوا الله فانساهم انفس: 19. And be not like those who forgot Allâh (i.e. became disobedient to Allâh), and He caused them to forget their ownselves, (let them to forget to do righteous deeds). Those are the Fâsiqûn (rebellious, disobedient to Allâh). (59:19)
لا يستوي اصحاب النار واصحاب الجنة اصحاب: 20. Not equal are the dwellers of the Fire and the dwellers of the Paradise. It is the dwellers of Paradise that will be successful. (59:20)
لو انزلنا هذا القران على جبل لرايته خاشع: 21. Had We sent down this Qur’ân on a mountain, you would surely have seen it humbling itself and rent asunder by the fear of Allâh. Such are the parables which We put forward to mankind that they may reflect.[3] (59:21)
هو الله الذي لا اله الا هو عالم الغيب وا: 22. He is Allâh, beside Whom Lâ ilâha illâ Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He) the All-Knower of the unseen and the seen. He is the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. (59:22)
هو الله الذي لا اله الا هو الملك القدوس: 23. He is Allâh beside Whom Lâ ilâha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), the King, the Holy, the One Free from all defects, the Giver of security, the Watcher over His creatures, the All-Mighty, the Compeller, the Supreme. Glory be to Allâh! (High is He) above all that they associate as partners with Him. (59:23)
هو الله الخالق البارئ المصور له الاسماء: 24. He is Allâh, the Creator, the Inventor of all things, the Bestower of forms. To Him belong the Best Names.[4] All that is in the heavens and the earth glorify Him. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise. (59:24)
يا ايها الذين امنوا لا تتخذوا عدوي وعدوك: 1. O you who believe! Take not My enemies and your enemies (i.e. disbelievers and polytheists) as friends, showing affection towards them, while they have disbelieved in what has come to you of the truth (i.e. Islâmic Monotheism, this Qur’ân, and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), and have driven out the Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) and yourselves (from your homeland) because you believe in Allâh your Lord! If you have come forth to strive in My Cause and to seek My Good Pleasure, (then take not these disbelievers and polytheists as your friends). You show friendship to them in secret, while I am All-Aware of what you conceal and what you reveal. And whosoever of you (Muslims) does that, then indeed he has gone (far) astray from the Straight Path[1]. (60:1)
ان يثقفوكم يكونوا لكم اعداء ويبسطوا اليك: 2. Should they gain the upper hand over you, they would behave to you as enemies, and stretch forth their hands and their tongues against you with evil, and they desire that you should disbelieve. (60:2)
لن تنفعكم ارحامكم ولا اولادكم يوم القيام: 3. Neither your relatives nor your children will benefit you on the Day of Resurrection (against Allâh). He will judge between you. And Allâh is the All-Seer of what you do. (60:3)
قد كانت لكم اسوة حسنة في ابراهيم والذين: 4. Indeed there has been an excellent example for you in Ibrâhîm (Abraham) and those with him, when they said to their people: "Verily, we are free from you and whatever you worship besides Allâh: we have rejected you, and there has started between us and you hostility and hatred for ever until you believe in Allâh Alone" - except the saying of Ibrâhîm (Abraham) to his father: "Verily, I will ask forgiveness (from Allâh) for you, but I have no power to do anything for you before Allâh.[2]" Our Lord! In You (Alone) we put our trust, and to You (Alone) we turn in repentance, and to You (Alone) is (our) final Return. (60:4)
ربنا لا تجعلنا فتنة للذين كفروا واغفر لن: 5. "Our Lord! Make us not a trial for the disbelievers, and forgive us, Our Lord! Verily, You, only You, are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise." (60:5)
لقد كان لكم فيهم اسوة حسنة لمن كان يرجو: 6. Certainly, there has been in them an excellent example for you to follow - for those who look forward to (the Meeting with) Allâh and the Last Day. And whosoever turns away, then verily, Allâh is Rich (Free of all needs), Worthy of all Praise. (60:6)
عسى الله ان يجعل بينكم وبين الذين عاديتم: 7. Perhaps Allâh will make friendship between you and those whom you hold as enemies. And Allâh has power (over all things), and Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful. (60:7)
لا ينهاكم الله عن الذين لم يقاتلوكم في ا: 8. Allâh does not forbid you to deal justly and kindly with those who fought not against you on account of religion nor drove you out of your homes. Verily, Allâh loves those who deal with equity. (60:8)
انما ينهاكم الله عن الذين قاتلوكم في الد: 9. It is only as regards those who fought against you on account of religion, and have driven you out of your homes, and helped to drive you out, that Allâh forbids you to befriend them. And whosoever will befriend them, then such are the Zâlimûn (wrong-doers - those who disobey Allâh). (60:9)
يا ايها الذين امنوا اذا جاءكم المؤمنات م: 10. O you who believe! When believing women come to you as emigrants, examine them; Allâh knows best as to their Faith, then if you ascertain that they are true believers send them not back to the disbelievers. They are not lawful (wives) for the disbelievers nor are the disbelievers lawful (husbands) for them. But give them (the disbelievers) that (amount of money) which they have spent [as their Mahr][3] to them. And there will be no sin on you to marry them if you have paid their Mahr to them. Likewise hold not the disbelieving women as wives, and ask for (the return of) that which you have spent (as Mahr ) and let them (the disbelievers) ask back for that which they have spent. That is the Judgement of Allâh, He judges between you. And Allâh is All-Knowing, All-Wise. (60:10)
وان فاتكم شيء من ازواجكم الى الكفار فعاق: 11. And if any of your wives have gone from you to the disbelievers, (as apostates and you asked them to return back your Mahr but they refused) - then you went out for a Ghazwah (military expedition) (against them and) gained booty; then pay from that booty to those whose wives have gone, the equivalent of what they had spent (on their Mahr ). And fear Allâh in Whom you believe. (60:11)
يا ايها النبي اذا جاءك المؤمنات يبايعنك: 12. O Prophet! When believing women come to you to give you the Bai‘ah (pledge), that they will not associate anything in worship with Allâh, that they will not steal, that they will not commit illegal sexual intercourse, that they will not kill their children, that they will not utter slander, intentionally forging falsehood (i.e. by making illegal children belonging to their husbands), and that they will not disobey you in Ma‘rûf (Islâmic Monotheism and all that which Islâm ordains), then accept their Bai‘ah (pledge), and ask Allâh to forgive them. Verily, Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful. (60:12)
يا ايها الذين امنوا لا تتولوا قوما غضب ا: 13. O you who believe! Take not as friends the people who incurred the Wrath of Allâh (i.e. the Jews). And those of your women as have passed the age of monthly courses, for them the ‘Iddah (prescribed period), if you have doubt (about their periods), is three months; and for those who have no courses [(i.e. they are still immature) their ‘Iddah (prescribed period) is three months likewise, except in case of death][2]. And for those who are pregnant (whether they are divorced or their husbands are dead), their ‘Iddah (prescribed period) is until they lay down their burden; and whosoever fears Allâh and keeps his duty to Him, He will make his matter easy for him. (66:5)
