ضرب الله مثلا للذين كفروا امرات نوح وامر: 10. Allâh sets forth an example for those who disbelieve: the wife of Nûh (Noah) and the wife of Lût (Lot). They were under two of our righteous slaves, but they both betrayed them (their husbands by rejecting their doctrine). So they [Nûh (Noah) and Lût (Lot) عليهما السلام] availed them (their respective wives) not against Allâh and it was said: "Enter the Fire along with those who enter!" (66:10)
وضرب الله مثلا للذين امنوا امرات فرعون ا: 11. And Allâh has set forth an example for those who believe: the wife of Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), when she said: "My Lord! Build for me a home with You in Paradise, and save me from Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) and his work, and save me from the people who are Zâlimûn (polytheists, wrong-doers and disbelievers in Allâh). (66:11)
ومريم ابنت عمران التي احصنت فرجها فنفخنا: 12. And Maryam (Mary), the daughter of ‘Imrân who guarded her chastity. And We breathed into (the sleeve of her shirt or her garment) through Our Rûh [i.e. Jibrîl (Gabriel)[4]], and she testified to the truth of the Words of her Lord [i.e. believed in the Words of Allâh: "Be!" - and he was; that is ‘Îsâ (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary) as a Messenger of Allâh], and (also believed in) His Scriptures, and she was of the Qânitûn (i.e. obedient to Allâh)[5]. (66:12)
تبارك الذي بيده الملك وهو على كل شيء قدي: 1. Blessed be He in Whose Hand is the dominion; and He is Able to do all things. (67:1)
الذي خلق الموت والحياة ليبلوكم ايكم احسن: 2. Who has created death and life that He may test you which of you is best in deed.[1] And He is the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving; (67:2)
الذي خلق سبع سماوات طباقا ما ترى في خلق: 3. Who has created the seven heavens one above another; you can see no fault in the creation of the Most Gracious. Then look again: "Can you see any rifts?" (67:3)
ثم ارجع البصر كرتين ينقلب اليك البصر خاس: 4. Then look again and yet again: your sight will return to you in a state of humiliation and worn out. (67:4)
ولقد زينا السماء الدنيا بمصابيح وجعلناها: 5. And indeed We have adorned the nearest heaven with lamps[2], and We have made such lamps (as) missiles to drive away the Shayâtîn (devils), and have prepared for them the torment of the blazing Fire. (67:5)
وللذين كفروا بربهم عذاب جهنم وبئس المصير: 6. And for those who disbelieve in their Lord (Allâh) is the torment of Hell, and worst indeed is that destination. (67:6)
اذا القوا فيها سمعوا لها شهيقا وهي تفور: 7. When they are cast therein, they will hear the (terrible) drawing in of its breath as it blazes forth. (67:7)
تكاد تميز من الغيظ كلما القي فيها فوج سا: 8. It almost bursts up with fury. Every time a group is cast therein, its keeper will ask: "Did no warner come to you?" (67:8)
قالوا بلى قد جاءنا نذير فكذبنا وقلنا ما: 9. They will say: "Yes, indeed a warner did come to us, but we belied him and said: ‘Allâh never sent down anything (of revelation); you are only in great error.’" (67:9)
وقالوا لو كنا نسمع او نعقل ما كنا في اصح: 10. And they will say: "Had we but listened or used our intelligence, we would not have been among the dwellers of the blazing Fire!" (67:10)
فاعترفوا بذنبهم فسحقا لاصحاب السعير: 11. Then they will confess their sin. So, away with the dwellers of the blazing Fire! (67:11)
ان الذين يخشون ربهم بالغيب لهم مغفرة واج: 12. Verily,, those who fear their Lord unseen (i.e. they do not see Him, nor His punishment in the Hereafter), theirs will be forgiveness and a great reward (i.e. Paradise). (67:12)
واسروا قولكم او اجهروا به انه عليم بذات: 13. And whether you keep your talk secret or disclose it, Verily, He is the All-Knower of what is in the breasts (of men). (67:13)
الا يعلم من خلق وهو اللطيف الخبير: 14. Should not He Who has created know? And He is the Most Kind and Courteous (to His slaves), All-Aware (of everything). (67:14)
هو الذي جعل لكم الارض ذلولا فامشوا في من: 15. He it is Who has made the earth subservient to you (i.e. easy for you to walk, to live and to do agriculture on it); so walk in the path thereof and eat of His provision. And to Him will be the Resurrection. (67:15)
اامنتم من في السماء ان يخسف بكم الارض فا: 16. Do you feel secure that He, Who is over the heaven (Allâh), will not cause the earth to sink with you, and then it should quake? (67:16)
ام امنتم من في السماء ان يرسل عليكم حاصب: 17. Or do you feel secure that He, Who is over the heaven (Allâh), will not send against you a violent whirlwind? Then you shall know how (terrible) has been My Warning. (67:17)
ولقد كذب الذين من قبلهم فكيف كان نكير: 18. And indeed those before them belied (the Messengers of Allâh), then how terrible was My denial (punishment)? (67:18)
اولم يروا الى الطير فوقهم صافات ويقبضن م: 19. Do they not see the birds above them, spreading out their wings and folding them in? None upholds them except the Most Gracious (Allâh). Verily, He is the All-Seer of everything. (67:19)
امن هذا الذي هو جند لكم ينصركم من دون ال: 20. Who is he besides the Most Gracious that can be an army to you to help you? The disbelievers are in nothing but delusion. (67:20)
امن هذا الذي يرزقكم ان امسك رزقه بل لجوا: 21. Who is he that can provide for you if He should withhold His provision? Nay, but they continue to be in pride, and (they) flee (from the truth). (67:21)
افمن يمشي مكبا على وجهه اهدى امن يمشي سو: 22. Is he who walks prone (without seeing) on his face, more rightly guided, or he who (sees and) walks upright on the Straight Way (i.e. Islâmic Monotheism)? (67:22)
قل هو الذي انشاكم وجعل لكم السمع والابصا: 23. Say it is He Who has created you, and endowed you with hearing (ears) and seeing (eyes), and hearts. Little thanks you give. (67:23)
قل هو الذي ذراكم في الارض واليه تحشرون: 24. Say: "It is He Who has created you on the earth, and to Him shall you be gathered (in the Hereafter)." (67:24)
قل انما العلم عند الله وانما انا نذير مب: 26. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "The knowledge (of its exact time) is with Allâh only, and I am only a plain warner." (67:26)
فلما راوه زلفة سيئت وجوه الذين كفروا وقي: 27. But when they will see it (the torment on the Day of Resurrection) approaching, the faces of those who disbelieve will change and turn black with sadness and in grief and it will be said (to them): "This is (the promise) which you were calling for!" (67:27)
قل ارايتم ان اهلكني الله ومن معي او رحمن: 28. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Tell me! If Allâh destroys me, and those with me, or He bestows His Mercy on us - who can save the disbelievers from a painful torment?" (67:28)
قل هو الرحمن امنا به وعليه توكلنا فستعلم: 29. Say: "He is the Most Gracious (Allâh), in Him we believe, and in Him we put our trust. So you will come to know who it is that is in manifest error." (67:29)
قل ارايتم ان اصبح ماؤكم غورا فمن ياتيكم: 30. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "Tell me! If (all) your water were to sink away, who then can supply you with flowing (spring) water?" (67:30)
ن والقلم وما يسطرون:
1. Nûn.
[These letters (Nun, etc.) are one of the miracles of the Qur’an, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings].
By the pen and by what they (the angels) write (in the Records of men). (68:1)
ما انت بنعمة ربك بمجنون: 2. You (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), by the Grace of your Lord, are not mad. (68:2)
وان لك لاجرا غير ممنون: 3. And Verily, for you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) will be an endless reward. (68:3)
وانك لعلى خلق عظيم: 4. And Verily, you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are on an exalted (standard of) character. (68:4)
فستبصر ويبصرون: 5. You will see, and they will see, (68:5)
باييكم المفتون: 6. Which of you is afflicted with madness. (68:6)
ان ربك هو اعلم بمن ضل عن سبيله وهو اعلم: 7. Verily, your Lord is the Best Knower of him who has gone astray from His Path, and He is the Best Knower of those who are guided. (68:7)
فلا تطع المكذبين: 8. So (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) obey you not the deniers [(of Islâmic Monotheism - those who belie the Verses of Allâh), the Oneness of Allâh, and the Messengership of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم][1] (68:8)
ودوا لو تدهن فيدهنون: 9. They wish that you should compromise (in religion out of courtesy) with them: so they (too) would compromise with you. (68:9)
ولا تطع كل حلاف مهين: 10. And (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) obey you not everyone Hallaf Mahîn (the one who swears much and is a liar or is worthless). [Tafsir At-Tabari] (68:10)
هماز مشاء بنميم: 11. A slanderer, going about with calumnies, (68:11)
مناع للخير معتد اثيم: 12. Hinderer of the good, transgressor, sinful, (68:12)
عتل بعد ذلك زنيم: 13. Cruel, and moreover base-born (of illegitimate birth). (68:13)
ان كان ذا مال وبنين: 14. (He was so) because he had wealth and children. (68:14)
اذا تتلى عليه اياتنا قال اساطير الاولين: 15. When Our Verses (of the Qur’ân) are recited to him, he says: "Tales of the men of old!" (68:15)
سنسمه على الخرطوم: 16. We shall brand him on the snout (nose)! (68:16)
انا بلوناهم كما بلونا اصحاب الجنة اذ اقس: 17. Verily, We have tried them as We tried the people of the garden, when they swore to pluck the fruits of the (garden) in the morning, (68:17)
ولا يستثنون: 18. Without saying: Inshâ’ Allâh (If Allâh wills). (68:18)
فطاف عليها طائف من ربك وهم نائمون: 19. Then there passed by on the (garden) a visitation (fire) from your Lord at night and burnt it while they were asleep. (68:19)
فاصبحت كالصريم: 20. So the (garden) became black by the morning, like a pitch dark night (in complete ruins). (68:20)
فتنادوا مصبحين: 21. Then they called out one to another as soon as the morning broke. (68:21)
ان اغدوا على حرثكم ان كنتم صارمين: 22. Saying: "Go to your tilth in the morning, if you would pluck the fruits." (68:22)
فانطلقوا وهم يتخافتون: 23. So they departed, conversing in secret low tones (saying): (68:23)
ان لا يدخلنها اليوم عليكم مسكين: 24. "No Miskîn (poor man) shall enter upon you into it today." (68:24)
وغدوا على حرد قادرين: 25. And they went in the morning with strong intention, thinking that they have power (to prevent the poor taking anything of the fruits therefrom). (68:25)
فلما راوها قالوا انا لضالون: 26. But when they saw the (garden), they said: "Verily, we have gone astray." (68:26)
قال اوسطهم الم اقل لكم لولا تسبحون: 28. The best among them said: "Did I not tell you: why say you not: Inshâ’ Allâh (If Allâh wills)." (68:28)
قالوا سبحان ربنا انا كنا ظالمين: 29. They said: "Glory to Our Lord! Verily, we have been Zâlimûn (wrong-doers)." (68:29)
فاقبل بعضهم على بعض يتلاومون: 30. Then they turned one against another, blaming. (68:30)
قالوا يا ويلنا انا كنا طاغين: 31. They said: "Woe to us! Verily, we were Tâghûn (transgressors and disobedient) (68:31)
عسى ربنا ان يبدلنا خيرا منها انا الى ربن: 32. We hope that our Lord will give us in exchange a better (garden) than this. Truly, we turn to our Lord (wishing for good that He may forgive our sins and reward us in the Hereafter). (68:32)
كذلك العذاب ولعذاب الاخرة اكبر لو كانوا: 33. Such is the punishment (in this life), but truly, the punishment of the Hereafter is greater if they but knew. (68:33)
ان للمتقين عند ربهم جنات النعيم: 34. Verily, for the Muttaqûn (the pious - See V.2:2) are Gardens of delight (Paradise) with their Lord. (68:34)
افنجعل المسلمين كالمجرمين: 35. Shall We then treat the Muslims (believers of Islamic Monotheism, doers of righteous deeds) like the Mujrimûn (criminals, polytheists and disbelievers, etc.)? (68:35)
ام لكم كتاب فيه تدرسون: 37. Or have you a Book wherein you learn, (68:37)
ان لكم فيه لما تخيرون: 38. That you shall therein have all that you choose? (68:38)
ام لكم ايمان علينا بالغة الى يوم القيامة: 39. Or have you oaths from Us, reaching to the Day of Resurrection, that yours will be what you judge? (68:39)
سلهم ايهم بذلك زعيم: 40. Ask them, which of them will stand surety for that! (68:40)
ام لهم شركاء فلياتوا بشركائهم ان كانوا ص: 41. Or have they "partners"? Then let them bring their "partners" if they are truthful! (68:41)
يوم يكشف عن ساق ويدعون الى السجود فلا يس: 42. (Remember) the Day when the Shin[2] shall be laid bare (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) and they shall be called to prostrate themselves (to Allâh), but they (hypocrites) shall not be able to do so. (68:42)
خاشعة ابصارهم ترهقهم ذلة وقد كانوا يدعون: 43. Their eyes will be cast down and ignominy will cover them; they used to be called to prostrate themselves (offer prayers), while they were healthy and good (in the life of the world, but they did not). (68:43)
فذرني ومن يكذب بهذا الحديث سنستدرجهم من: 44. Then leave Me Alone with such as belie this Qur’ân. We shall punish them gradually from directions they perceive not. (68:44)
فاصبر لحكم ربك ولا تكن كصاحب الحوت اذ نا: 48. So wait with patience for the Decision of your Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish - when he cried out (to Us) while he was in deep sorrow. (See the Qur’ân, Verse 21:87). (68:48)
لولا ان تداركه نعمة من ربه لنبذ بالعراء: 49. Had not a Grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been (left in the stomach of the fish, but We forgave him): so he was cast off on the naked shore, while he was to be blamed. (68:49)
فاجتباه ربه فجعله من الصالحين: 50. Then his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous. (68:50)
وان يكاد الذين كفروا ليزلقونك بابصارهم ل: 51. And verily, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes (through hatred) when they hear the Reminder (the Qur’ân), and they say: "Verily, he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is a madman!" (68:51)
وما هو الا ذكر للعالمين: 52. But it is nothing else than a Reminder to all the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, and jinn). (68:52)
الحاقة: 1. The Inevitable (i.e. the Day of Resurrection)! (69:1)
وما ادراك ما الحاقة: 3. And what will make you know what the Inevitable is? (69:3)
كذبت ثمود وعاد بالقارعة: 4. Thamûd and ‘Âd people denied the Qâri‘ah (the striking Hour of Judgement)! (69:4)
فاما ثمود فاهلكوا بالطاغية: 5. As for Thamûd, they were destroyed by the awful cry! (69:5)
واما عاد فاهلكوا بريح صرصر عاتية: 6. And as for ‘Âd, they were destroyed by a furious violent wind! (69:6)
سخرها عليهم سبع ليال وثمانية ايام حسوما: 7. Which Allâh imposed on them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so that you could see men lying overthrown (destroyed), as if they were hollow trunks of date-palms! (69:7)
فهل ترى لهم من باقية: 8. Do you see any remnants of them? (69:8)
وجاء فرعون ومن قبله والمؤتفكات بالخاطئة: 9. And Fir‘aun (Pharaoh), and those before him, and the cities overthrown [the towns of the people of [Lût (Lot)] committed sin. (69:9)
فعصوا رسول ربهم فاخذهم اخذة رابية: 10. And they disobeyed their Lord’s Messenger, so He seized them with a strong punishment. (69:10)
انا لما طغى الماء حملناكم في الجارية: 11. Verily, when the water rose beyond its limits [Nûh’s (Noah) Flood], We carried you (mankind) in the floating [ship that was constructed by Nûh (Noah)] (69:11)
لنجعلها لكم تذكرة وتعيها اذن واعية: 12. That We might make it (Noah’s ship) an admonition for you and that it might be retained by the retaining ears. (69:12)
فاذا نفخ في الصور نفخة واحدة: 13. Then when the Trumpet will be blown with one blowing (the first one). (69:13)
وحملت الارض والجبال فدكتا دكة واحدة: 14. And the earth and the mountains shall be removed from their places, and crushed with a single crushing. (69:14)
فيومئذ وقعت الواقعة: 15. Then on that Day shall the (Great) Event befall. (69:15)
وانشقت السماء فهي يومئذ واهية: 16. And the heaven will be rent asunder, for that Day it (the heaven) will be frail and torn up. (69:16)
والملك على ارجائها ويحمل عرش ربك فوقهم ي: 17. And the angels will be on its sides, and eight angels will, that Day, bear the Throne of your Lord above them. (69:17)
يومئذ تعرضون لا تخفى منكم خافية: 18. That Day shall you be brought to Judgement, not a secret of you will be hidden. (69:18)
فاما من اوتي كتابه بيمينه فيقول هاؤم اقر: 19. Then as for him who will be given his Record in his right hand will say: "Here! read my Record! (69:19)
اني ظننت اني ملاق حسابيه: 20. "Surely, I did believe that I shall meet my Account!" (69:20)
فهو في عيشة راضية: 21. So he shall be in a life, well-pleasing. (69:21)
قطوفها دانية: 23. The fruits in bunches whereof will be low and near at hand. (69:23)
كلوا واشربوا هنيئا بما اسلفتم في الايام: 24. Eat and drink at ease for that which you have sent on before you in days past! (69:24)
واما من اوتي كتابه بشماله فيقول يا ليتني: 25. But as for him who will be given his Record in his left hand, will say: "I wish that I had not been given my Record! (69:25)
ولم ادر ما حسابيه: 26. "And that I had never known how my Account is! (69:26)
يا ليتها كانت القاضية: 27. "Would that it had been my end (death)! (69:27)
هلك عني سلطانيه: 29. "My power (and arguments to defend myself) have gone from me!" (69:29)
خذوه فغلوه: 30. (It will be said): "Seize him and fetter him; (69:30)
ثم الجحيم صلوه: 31. Then throw him in the blazing Fire. (69:31)
ثم في سلسلة ذرعها سبعون ذراعا فاسلكوه: 32. "Then fasten him with a chain whereof the length is seventy cubits!" (69:32)
انه كان لا يؤمن بالله العظيم: 33. Verily, he used not to believe in Allâh, the Most Great, (69:33)
ولا يحض على طعام المسكين: 34. And urged not on the feeding of Al-Miskîn (the poor).[1] (69:34)
فليس له اليوم هاهنا حميم: 35. So no friend has he here this Day, (69:35)
ولا طعام الا من غسلين: 36. Nor any food except filth from the washing of wounds. (69:36)
لا ياكله الا الخاطئون: 37. None will eat it except the Khâti’ûn (sinners, disbelievers, polytheists). (69:37)
فلا اقسم بما تبصرون: 38. So I swear by whatsoever you see, (69:38)
وما لا تبصرون: 39. And by whatsoever you see not, (69:39)
انه لقول رسول كريم: 40. That this is verily, the word of an honoured Messenger [i.e. Jibrîl (Gabriel) or Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم which he has brought from Allâh]. (69:40)
وما هو بقول شاعر قليلا ما تؤمنون: 41. It is not the word of a poet: little is that you believe! (69:41)
ولا بقول كاهن قليلا ما تذكرون: 42. Nor is it the word of a soothsayer (or a foreteller): little is that you remember! (69:42)
ولو تقول علينا بعض الاقاويل: 44. And if he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) had forged a false saying concerning Us (Allâh جل جلاله), (69:44)
لاخذنا منه باليمين: 45. We surely would have seized him by his right hand (or with power and might), (69:45)
ثم لقطعنا منه الوتين: 46. And then We certainly would have cut off his life artery (aorta), (69:46)
فما منكم من احد عنه حاجزين: 47. And none of you could have withheld Us from (punishing) him. (69:47)
وانه لتذكرة للمتقين: 48. And verily, this (Qur’an) is a Reminder for the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2). (69:48)
وانا لنعلم ان منكم مكذبين: 49. And verily, We know that there are some among you that belie (this Qur’ân). (Tafsir At-Tabarî) (69:49)
وانه لحسرة على الكافرين: 50. And indeed it (this Qur’ân) will be an anguish for the disbelievers (on the Day of Resurrection).[2] (69:50)
وانه لحق اليقين: 51. And verily, it (this Qur’ân) is an absolute truth with certainty.[3] (69:51)
سال سائل بعذاب واقع: 1. A questioner asked concerning a torment about to befall (70:1)
للكافرين ليس له دافع: 2. Upon the disbelievers, which none can avert, (70:2)
من الله ذي المعارج: 3. From Allâh, the Lord of the ways of ascent. (70:3)
تعرج الملائكة والروح اليه في يوم كان مقد: 4. The angels and the Rûh [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] ascend to Him in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years. (70:4)
فاصبر صبرا جميلا: 5. So be patient (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), with a good patience. (70:5)
انهم يرونه بعيدا: 6. Verily, they see it (the torment) afar off. (70:6)
يوم تكون السماء كالمهل: 8. The Day that the sky will be like the boiling filth of oil, (or molten copper or silver or lead). (70:8)
وتكون الجبال كالعهن: 9. And the mountains will be like flakes of wool. (70:9)
ولا يسال حميم حميما: 10. And no friend will ask a friend (about his condition), (70:10)
يبصرونهم يود المجرم لو يفتدي من عذاب يوم: 11. Though they shall be made to see one another [(i.e. on the Day of Resurrection), there will be none but see his father, children and relatives, but he will neither speak to them nor will ask them for any help]. The Mujrim, (criminal, sinner, disbeliever) would desire to ransom himself from the punishment of that Day by his children, (70:11)
وصاحبته واخيه: 12. And his wife and his brother, (70:12)
وفصيلته التي تؤويه: 13. And his kindred who sheltered him, (70:13)
ومن في الارض جميعا ثم ينجيه: 14. And all that are in the earth, so that it might save him.[1] (70:14)
كلا انها لظى: 15. By no means! Verily, it will be the Fire of Hell, (70:15)
نزاعة للشوى: 16. Taking away (burning completely) the head skin! (70:16)
تدعو من ادبر وتولى: 17. Calling[2] (all) such as turn their backs and turn away their faces (from Faith) [picking and swallowing them up from that great gathering of mankind on the Day of Resurrection just as a bird picks up a food-grain from the earth with its beak and swallows it up] (Tafsir Al-Qurtubî) (70:17)
وجمع فاوعى: 18. And collect (wealth) and hide it (from spending it in the Cause of Allâh). (70:18)
ان الانسان خلق هلوعا: 19. Verily, man (disbeliever) was created very impatient; (70:19)
واذا مسه الخير منوعا: 21. And niggardly when good touches him. (70:21)
الا المصلين: 22. Except those who are devoted to Salât (prayers).[3] (70:22)
الذين هم على صلاتهم دائمون: 23. Those who remain constant in their Salât (prayers); (70:23)
والذين في اموالهم حق معلوم: 24. And those in whose wealth there is a recognised right (70:24)
للسائل والمحروم: 25. For the beggar who asks, and for the unlucky who has lost his property and wealth, (and his means of living has been straitened). (70:25)
والذين يصدقون بيوم الدين: 26. And those who believe in the Day of Recompense. (70:26)
والذين هم من عذاب ربهم مشفقون: 27. And those who fear the torment of their Lord. (70:27)
ان عذاب ربهم غير مامون: 28. Verily, the torment of their Lord is that before which none can feel secure. - (70:28)
والذين هم بشهاداتهم قائمون: 33. And those who stand firm in their testimonies. (70:33)
والذين هم على صلاتهم يحافظون: 34. And those who guard their Salât (prayers) well.[6] (70:34)
اولئك في جنات مكرمون: 35. Such shall dwell in the Gardens (i.e. Paradise), honoured. (70:35)
فمال الذين كفروا قبلك مهطعين: 36. So what is the matter with those who disbelieve that they hasten to listen from you [O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), in order to belie you and to mock at you, and at Allâh’s Book (this Qur’ân)]. (70:36)
عن اليمين وعن الشمال عزين: 37. (Sitting) in groups on the right and on the left (of you, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم)? (70:37)
ايطمع كل امرئ منهم ان يدخل جنة نعيم: 38. Does every man of them hope to enter the Paradise of Delight? (70:38)
كلا انا خلقناهم مما يعلمون: 39. No, that is not like that! Verily, We have created them out of that which they know! (70:39)
فلا اقسم برب المشارق والمغارب انا لقادرو: 40. So I swear by the Lord of all the [three hundred and sixty-five (365)] points of sunrise and sunset in the east and the west that surely We are Able (70:40)
على ان نبدل خيرا منهم وما نحن بمسبوقين: 41. To replace them by (others) better than them; and We are not to be outrun. (70:41)
يوم يخرجون من الاجداث سراعا كانهم الى نص: 43. The Day when they will come out of the graves quickly as racing to a goal, (70:43)
خاشعة ابصارهم ترهقهم ذلة ذلك اليوم الذي: 44. With their eyes lowered in fear and humility, ignominy covering them (all over)! That is the Day which they were promised! (70:44)
انا ارسلنا نوحا الى قومه ان انذر قومك من: 1. Verily, We sent Nûh (Noah) to his people (saying): "Warn your people before there comes to them a painful torment." (71:1)
قال يا قوم اني لكم نذير مبين: 2. He said: "O my people! Verily, I am a plain warner to you, (71:2)
ان اعبدوا الله واتقوه واطيعون: 3. "That you should worship Allâh (Alone), fear (be dutiful to) Him, and obey me, (71:3)
يغفر لكم من ذنوبكم ويؤخركم الى اجل مسمى: 4. "He (Allâh) will forgive you of your sins and respite you to an appointed term. Verily, the term of Allâh when it comes, cannot be delayed, if you but knew." (71:4)
قال رب اني دعوت قومي ليلا ونهارا: 5. He said: "O my Lord! Verily, I have called to my people night and day (i.e. secretly and openly to accept the doctrine of Islâmic Monotheism),[1] (71:5)
فلم يزدهم دعائي الا فرارا: 6. "But all my calling added nothing but to (their) flight (from the truth). (71:6)
واني كلما دعوتهم لتغفر لهم جعلوا اصابعهم: 7. And verily, every time I called unto them that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, and persisted (in their refusal), and magnified themselves in pride. (71:7)
ثم اني دعوتهم جهارا: 8. "Then verily, I called to them openly (aloud). (71:8)
ثم اني اعلنت لهم واسررت لهم اسرارا: 9. "Then verily, I proclaimed to them in public, and I have appealed to them in private. (71:9)
فقلت استغفروا ربكم انه كان غفارا: 10. "I said (to them): ‘Ask forgiveness from your Lord, verily, He is Oft-Forgiving; (71:10)
يرسل السماء عليكم مدرارا: 11. ‘He will send rain to you in abundance, (71:11)
ويمددكم باموال وبنين ويجعل لكم جنات ويجع: 12. ‘And give you increase in wealth and children, and bestow on you gardens and bestow on you rivers.’" (71:12)
ما لكم لا ترجون لله وقارا: 13. What is the matter with you, that [you fear not Allâh (His punishment), and] you hope not for reward (from Allâh or you believe not in His Oneness). (71:13)
وقد خلقكم اطوارا: 14. While He has created you in (different) stages [i.e. first Nutfah, then ‘Alaqah and then Mudghah, see (V.23:13,14)]. (71:14)
الم تروا كيف خلق الله سبع سماوات طباقا: 15. See you not how Allâh has created the seven heavens one above another? (71:15)
وجعل القمر فيهن نورا وجعل الشمس سراجا: 16. And has made the moon a light therein, and made the sun a lamp? (71:16)
والله انبتكم من الارض نباتا: 17. And Allâh has brought you forth from the (dust of) earth? (Tafsir At-Tabarî) (71:17)
ثم يعيدكم فيها ويخرجكم اخراجا: 18. Afterwards He will return you into it (the earth), and bring you forth (again on the Day of Resurrection)? (71:18)
والله جعل لكم الارض بساطا: 19. And Allâh has made for you the earth a wide expanse. (71:19)
لتسلكوا منها سبلا فجاجا: 20. That you may go about therein in broad roads. (71:20)
قال نوح رب انهم عصوني واتبعوا من لم يزده: 21. Nûh (Noah) said: "My Lord! They have disobeyed me, and followed one whose wealth and children give him no increase but loss. (71:21)
ومكروا مكرا كبارا: 22. "And they have plotted a mighty plot. (71:22)
وقالوا لا تذرن الهتكم ولا تذرن ودا ولا س: 23. "And they have said: ‘You shall not leave your gods: nor shall you leave Wadd, nor Suwâ‘, nor Yaghûth, nor Ya‘ûq nor Nasr’ (these are the names of their idols). (71:23)
وقد اضلوا كثيرا ولا تزد الظالمين الا ضلا: 24. "And indeed they have led many astray. And (O Allâh): ‘Grant no increase to the Zâlimûn (polytheists, wrong-doers, and disbelievers) save error.’" (71:24)
مما خطيئاتهم اغرقوا فادخلوا نارا فلم يجد: 25. Because of their sins they were drowned, then were made to enter the Fire. And they found none to help them instead of Allâh. (71:25)
وقال نوح رب لا تذر على الارض من الكافرين: 26. And Nûh (Noah) said: "My Lord! Leave not one of the disbelievers on the earth! (71:26)
انك ان تذرهم يضلوا عبادك ولا يلدوا الا ف: 27. "If You leave them, they will mislead Your slaves, and they will beget none but wicked disbelievers. (71:27)
رب اغفر لي ولوالدي ولمن دخل بيتي مؤمنا و: 28. "My Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and him who enters my home as a believer, and all the believing men and women. And to the Zâlimûn (polytheists, wrong-doers, and disbelievers) grant You no increase but destruction!" (71:28)
قل اوحي الي انه استمع نفر من الجن فقالوا: 1. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "It has been revealed to me that a group (from three to ten in number) of jinn[1] listened (to this Qur’ân). They said: ‘Verily, we have heard a wonderful Recitation (this Qur’ân)! (72:1)
يهدي الى الرشد فامنا به ولن نشرك بربنا ا: 2. ‘It guides to the Right Path, and we have believed therein, and we shall never join (in worship) anything with our Lord (Allâh). (72:2)
وانه تعالى جد ربنا ما اتخذ صاحبة ولا ولد: 3. ‘And He, exalted be the Majesty of our Lord, has taken neither a wife nor a son (or offspring or children).[2] (72:3)
وانه كان يقول سفيهنا على الله شططا: 4. ‘And that the foolish among us [i.e. Iblîs (Satan) or the polytheists amongst the jinn] used to utter against Allâh that which was an enormity in falsehood. (72:4)
وانا ظننا ان لن تقول الانس والجن على الل: 5. ‘And verily, we thought that men and jinn would not utter a lie against Allâh. (72:5)
وانه كان رجال من الانس يعوذون برجال من ا: 6. ‘And verily, there were men among mankind who took shelter with the males among the jinn, but they (jinn) increased them (mankind) in sin and transgression. (72:6)
وانهم ظنوا كما ظننتم ان لن يبعث الله احد: 7. ‘And they thought as you thought, that Allâh will not send any Messenger (to mankind or jinn). (72:7)
وانا لمسنا السماء فوجدناها ملئت حرسا شدي: 8. ‘And we have sought to reach the heaven; but found it filled with stern guards and flaming fires. (72:8)
وانا كنا نقعد منها مقاعد للسمع فمن يستمع: 9. ‘And verily, we used to sit there in stations, to (steal) a hearing, but any who listens now will find a flaming fire watching him in ambush. (72:9)
وانا لا ندري اشر اريد بمن في الارض ام ار: 10. ‘And we know not whether evil is intended for those on earth, or whether their Lord intends for them a Right Path. (72:10)
وانا منا الصالحون ومنا دون ذلك كنا طرائق: 11. ‘There are among us some that are righteous, and some the contrary; we are groups having different ways (religious sects). (72:11)
وانا ظننا ان لن نعجز الله في الارض ولن ن: 12. ‘And we think that we cannot escape (the punishment of) Allâh in the earth, nor can we escape Him by flight. (72:12)
وانا لما سمعنا الهدى امنا به فمن يؤمن بر: 13. ‘And indeed when we heard the Guidance (this Qur’ân), we believed therein (Islâmic Monotheism), and whosoever believes in his Lord shall have no fear, either of a decrease in the reward of his good deeds or an increase in the punishment for his sins. (72:13)
وانا منا المسلمون ومنا القاسطون فمن اسلم: 14. ‘And of us some are Muslims (who have submitted to Allâh, after listening to this Qur’ân), and of us some are Al-Qâsitûn (disbelievers - those who have deviated from the Right Path)’. And whosoever has embraced Islâm (i.e. has become a Muslim by submitting to Allâh), then such have sought the Right Path." (72:14)
واما القاسطون فكانوا لجهنم حطبا: 15. And as for the Qâsitûn (disbelievers who deviated from the Right Path), they shall be firewood for Hell, (72:15)
وان لو استقاموا على الطريقة لاسقيناهم ما: 16. If they (non-Muslims) had believed in Allâh, and went on the Right Way (i.e. Islâm) We would surely have bestowed on them water (rain) in abundance. (72:16)
لنفتنهم فيه ومن يعرض عن ذكر ربه يسلكه عذ: 17. That We might try them thereby. And whosoever turns away from the Reminder of his Lord (i.e. this Qur’ân, - and practise not its laws and orders), He will cause him to enter in a severe torment (i.e. Hell). (72:17)
وان المساجد لله فلا تدعوا مع الله احدا: 18. And the mosques are for Allâh (Alone): so invoke not anyone along with Allâh.[3] (72:18)
وانه لما قام عبد الله يدعوه كادوا يكونون: 19. And when the slave of Allâh (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) stood up invoking Him (his Lord - Allâh) in prayer they (the jinn) just made round him a dense crowd as if sticking one over the other (in order to listen to the Prophet’s recitation). (72:19)
قل انما ادعو ربي ولا اشرك به احدا: 20. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I invoke only my Lord (Allâh Alone), and I associate none as partners along with Him." (72:20)
قل اني لا املك لكم ضرا ولا رشدا: 21. Say: "It is not in my power to cause you harm, or to bring you to the Right Path." (72:21)
قل اني لن يجيرني من الله احد ولن اجد من: 22. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "None can protect me from Allâh’s punishment (if I were to disobey Him), nor can I find refuge except in Him. (72:22)
الا بلاغا من الله ورسالاته ومن يعص الله: 23. "(Mine is) but conveyance (of the truth) from Allâh and His Messages (of Islâmic Monotheism), and whosoever disobeys Allâh and His Messenger, then Verily, for him is the Fire of Hell, he shall dwell therein forever."[4] (72:23)
حتى اذا راوا ما يوعدون فسيعلمون من اضعف: 24. Till, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who it is that is weaker concerning helpers and less important concerning numbers. (72:24)
قل ان ادري اقريب ما توعدون ام يجعل له رب: 25. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "I know not whether (the punishment) which you are promised is near or whether my Lord will appoint for it a distant term. (72:25)
عالم الغيب فلا يظهر على غيبه احدا: 26. "(He Alone is) the All-Knower of the Ghaib (Unseen), and He reveals to none His Ghaib (Unseen)." (72:26)
الا من ارتضى من رسول فانه يسلك من بين يد: 27. Except to a Messenger (from mankind) whom He has chosen (He informs him of unseen as much as He likes), and then He makes a band of watching guards (angels) to march before him and behind him. (72:27)
ليعلم ان قد ابلغوا رسالات ربهم واحاط بما: 28. [He (Allâh) protects them (the Messengers)], till He sees that they (the Messengers) have conveyed the Messages of their Lord (Allâh). And He (Allâh) surrounds all that which is with them, and He (Allâh) keeps count of all things (i.e. He knows the exact number of everything). (72:28)
يا ايها المزمل: 1. O you wrapped in garments (i.e. Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم)! (73:1)
قم الليل الا قليلا: 2. Stand (to pray) all night, except a little - (73:2)
نصفه او انقص منه قليلا: 3. Half of it or a little less than that, (73:3)
او زد عليه ورتل القران ترتيلا: 4. Or a little more. And recite the Qur’ân (aloud) in a slow, (pleasant tone and) style.[1] (73:4)
انا سنلقي عليك قولا ثقيلا: 5. Verily, We shall send down to you a weighty Word (i.e. obligations, laws). (73:5)
ان ناشئة الليل هي اشد وطئا واقوم قيلا: 6. Verily, the rising by night (for Tahajjud prayer) is very hard and most potent and good for governing oneself, and most suitable for (understanding) the Word (of Allâh). (73:6)
ان لك في النهار سبحا طويلا: 7. Verily, there is for you by day prolonged occupation with ordinary duties. (73:7)
واذكر اسم ربك وتبتل اليه تبتيلا: 8. And remember the Name of your Lord and devote yourself to Him with a complete devotion. (73:8)
رب المشرق والمغرب لا اله الا هو فاتخذه و: 9. (He Alone is) the Lord of the east and the west; Lâ ilâha illâ Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). So take Him Alone as Wakîl (Disposer of your affairs).[2] (73:9)
واصبر على ما يقولون واهجرهم هجرا جميلا: 10. And be patient (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) with what they say, and keep away from them in a good way. (73:10)
وذرني والمكذبين اولي النعمة ومهلهم قليلا: 11. And leave Me Alone to deal with the beliers (those who deny My Verses), those who are in possession of good things of life. And give them respite for a little while. (73:11)
ان لدينا انكالا وجحيما: 12. Verily, with Us are fetters (to bind them), and a raging Fire. (73:12)
وطعاما ذا غصة وعذابا اليما: 13. And a food that chokes, and a painful torment. (73:13)
يوم ترجف الارض والجبال وكانت الجبال كثيب: 14. On the Day when the earth and the mountains will be in violent shake, and the mountains will be a heap of sand poured out. (73:14)
انا ارسلنا اليكم رسولا شاهدا عليكم كما ا: 15. Verily, We have sent to you (O men) a Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) to be a witness over you, as We did send a Messenger [Mûsâ (Moses)] to Fir‘aun (Pharaoh).[3] (73:15)
فعصى فرعون الرسول فاخذناه اخذا وبيلا: 16. But Fir‘aun (Pharaoh) disobeyed the Messenger [Mûsâ (Moses)]; so We seized him with a severe punishment. (73:16)
فكيف تتقون ان كفرتم يوما يجعل الولدان شي: 17. Then how can you avoid the punishment, if you disbelieve, on a Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) that will make the children grey-headed? (73:17)
السماء منفطر به كان وعده مفعولا: 18. Whereon the heaven will be cleft asunder? His Promise is certainly to be accomplished. (73:18)
ان هذه تذكرة فمن شاء اتخذ الى ربه سبيلا: 19. Verily, this is an admonition: therefore whosoever will, let him take a Path to His Lord! (73:19)
ان ربك يعلم انك تقوم ادنى من ثلثي الليل: 20. Verily, your Lord knows that you do stand (to pray at night) a little less than two thirds of the night, or half the night, or a third of the night, and also a party of those with you. And Allâh measures the night and the day. He knows that you are unable to pray the whole night, so He has turned to you (in mercy). So, recite you of the Qur’ân as much as may be easy for you. He knows that there will be some among you sick, others travelling through the land, seeking of Allâh’s Bounty, yet others fighting in Allâh’s Cause. So recite as much of the Qur’ân as may be easy (for you), and perform As-Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât ) and give Zakât, and lend to Allâh a goodly loan. And whatever good you send before you for yourselves (i.e. Nawâfil non-obligatory acts of worship: prayers, charity, fasting, Hajj and ‘Umrah), you will certainly find it with Allâh, better and greater in reward. And seek Forgiveness of Allâh. Verily, Allâh is Oft-Forgiving, Most-Merciful[4]. (73:20)
يا ايها المدثر: 1. O you (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) enveloped in garments! (74:1)
والرجز فاهجر: 5. And keep away from Ar-Rujz (the idols)! (74:5)
ولا تمنن تستكثر: 6. And give not a thing in order to have more (or consider not your deeds of obedience to Allâh as a favour to Him). (74:6)
ولربك فاصبر: 7. And be patient for the sake of your Lord (i.e. perform your duty to Allâh)! (74:7)
فاذا نقر في الناقور: 8. Then, when the Trumpet is sounded (i.e. the second blowing of the horn). (74:8)
فذلك يومئذ يوم عسير: 9. Truly, that Day will be a Hard Day - (74:9)
على الكافرين غير يسير: 10. Far from easy for the disbelievers. (74:10)
ذرني ومن خلقت وحيدا: 11. Leave Me Alone (to deal) with whom I created lonely (without any wealth or children etc, i.e. Al-Walîd bin Al-Mughîrah Al-Makhzûmî)! (74:11)
وجعلت له مالا ممدودا: 12. And then granted him resources in abundance. (74:12)
وبنين شهودا: 13. And children to be by his side. (74:13)
ومهدت له تمهيدا: 14. And made life smooth and comfortable for him. (74:14)
ثم يطمع ان ازيد: 15. After all that he desires that I should give more; (74:15)
كلا انه كان لاياتنا عنيدا: 16. Nay! Verily, he has been opposing Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations). (74:16)
سارهقه صعودا: 17. I shall oblige him to (climb a slippery mountain in the Hell-fire called As-Sa‘ûd, or) face a severe torment! (74:17)
فقتل كيف قدر: 19. So let him be cursed: how he plotted! (74:19)
ثم قتل كيف قدر: 20. And once more let him be cursed: how he plotted! (74:20)
ثم عبس وبسر: 22. Then he frowned and he looked in a bad tempered way; (74:22)
ثم ادبر واستكبر: 23. Then he turned back, and was proud. (74:23)
فقال ان هذا الا سحر يؤثر: 24. Then he said: "This is nothing but magic from that of old, (74:24)
ان هذا الا قول البشر: 25. "This is nothing but the word of a human being!" (74:25)
ساصليه سقر: 26. I will cast him into Hell-fire.[1] (74:26)
وما ادراك ما سقر: 27. And what will make you know (exactly) what Hell-fire is? (74:27)
لا تبقي ولا تذر: 28. It spares not (any sinner), nor does it leave (anything unburnt)! (74:28)
عليها تسعة عشر: 30. Over it are nineteen (angels as guardians and keepers of Hell). (74:30)
وما جعلنا اصحاب النار الا ملائكة وما جعل: 31. And We have set none but angels as guardians of the Fire. And We have fixed their number (19) only as a trial for the disbelievers, in order that the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) may arrive at a certainty [that this Qur’ân is the truth as it agrees with their Books regarding the number (19) which is written in the Taurât (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] and that the believers may increase in Faith (as this Qur’ân is the truth), and that no doubt may be left for the people of the Scripture and the believers, and that those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy) and the disbelievers may say: "What Allâh intends by this (curious) example ?" Thus Allâh leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And none can know the hosts of your Lord but He. And this (Hell) is nothing else than a (warning) reminder to mankind. (74:31)
والليل اذ ادبر: 33. And by the night when it withdraws. (74:33)
والصبح اذا اسفر: 34. And by the dawn when it brightens. (74:34)
انها لاحدى الكبر: 35. Verily, it (Hell, or their denial of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم, or the Day of Resurrection) is but one of the greatest (signs). (74:35)
لمن شاء منكم ان يتقدم او يتاخر: 37. To any of you that chooses to go forward (by working righteous deeds), or to remain behind (by committing sins). (74:37)
كل نفس بما كسبت رهينة: 38. Every person is a pledge for what he has earned, (74:38)
الا اصحاب اليمين: 39. Except those on the Right, (i.e. the pious true believers of Islâmic Monotheism). (74:39)
في جنات يتساءلون: 40. In Gardens (Paradise) they will ask one another, (74:40)
عن المجرمين: 41. About Al-Mujrimûn (polytheists, criminals, disbelievers), (And they will say to them): (74:41)
ما سلككم في سقر: 42. "What has caused you to enter Hell?" (74:42)
قالوا لم نك من المصلين: 43. They will say: "We were not of those who used to offer the Salât (prayers),[2] (74:43)
ولم نك نطعم المسكين: 44. "Nor we used to feed Al-Miskîn (the poor); (74:44)
وكنا نخوض مع الخائضين: 45. "And we used to talk falsehood (all that which Allâh hated) with vain talkers.[3] (74:45)
وكنا نكذب بيوم الدين: 46. And we used to belie the Day of Recompense,[4] (74:46)
حتى اتانا اليقين: 47. "Until there came to us (the death) that is certain." (74:47)
فما تنفعهم شفاعة الشافعين: 48. So no intercession of intercessors will be of any use to them. (74:48)
فما لهم عن التذكرة معرضين: 49. Then what is wrong with them (i.e. the polytheists, the disbelievers) that they turn away from (receiving) admonition? (74:49)
كانهم حمر مستنفرة: 50. As if they were (frightened) wild donkeys. (74:50)
فرت من قسورة: 51. Fleeing from a hunter, or a lion, or a beast of prey. (74:51)
بل يريد كل امرئ منهم ان يؤتى صحفا منشرة: 52. Nay, everyone of them desires that he should be given pages spread out (coming from Allâh with a writing that Islâm is the right religion, and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم has come with the truth from Allâh, the Lord of the heavens and earth). (74:52)
كلا بل لا يخافون الاخرة: 53. Nay! But they fear not the Hereafter (from Allâh’s punishment). (74:53)
كلا انه تذكرة: 54. Nay, verily, this (Qur’an) is an admonition, (74:54)
فمن شاء ذكره: 55. So whosoever will (let him read it), and receive admonition (from it)! (74:55)
وما يذكرون الا ان يشاء الله هو اهل التقو: 56. And they will not receive admonition unless Allâh wills; He (Allâh) is the One, deserving that mankind should be afraid of, and should be dutiful to Him, and should not take any Ilâh (god) along with Him, and He is the One Who forgives (sins). (74:56)
لا اقسم بيوم القيامة: 1. I swear by the Day of Resurrection. (75:1)
ولا اقسم بالنفس اللوامة: 2. And I swear by the self-reproaching person (a believer). (75:2)
ايحسب الانسان الن نجمع عظامه: 3. Does man (a disbeliever) think that We shall not assemble his bones? (75:3)
بلى قادرين على ان نسوي بنانه: 4. Yes, We are Able to put together in perfect order the tips of his fingers.[1] (75:4)
بل يريد الانسان ليفجر امامه: 5. Nay! Man (denies Resurrection and Reckoning. So he) desires to continue committing sins. (75:5)
يسال ايان يوم القيامة: 6. He asks: "When will be this Day of Resurrection?" (75:6)
فاذا برق البصر: 7. So, when the sight shall be dazed. (75:7)
وخسف القمر: 8. And the moon will be eclipsed. (75:8)
وجمع الشمس والقمر: 9. And the sun and moon will be joined together (by going one into the other or folded up or deprived of their light).[2] (75:9)
يقول الانسان يومئذ اين المفر: 10. On that Day man will say: "Where (is the refuge) to flee?" (75:10)
الى ربك يومئذ المستقر: 12. Unto your Lord (Alone) will be the place of rest that Day. (75:12)
ينبا الانسان يومئذ بما قدم واخر: 13. On that Day man will be informed of what he sent forward (of his evil good deeds), and what he left behind (of his good or evil traditions). (75:13)
بل الانسان على نفسه بصيرة: 14. Nay! Man will be a witness against himself [as his body parts (skin, hands, legs, etc.) will speak about his deeds], (75:14)
ولو القى معاذيره: 15. Though he may put forth his excuses (to cover his evil deeds). (75:15)
لا تحرك به لسانك لتعجل به: 16. Move not your tongue concerning (the Qur’ân, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) to make haste therewith. (75:16)
ان علينا جمعه وقرانه: 17. It is for Us to collect it and to give you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) the ability to recite it (the Qur’ân). (75:17)
فاذا قراناه فاتبع قرانه: 18. And when We have recited it to you [O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم through Jibrîl (Gabriel)], then follow its (the Qur’an’s) recital. (75:18)
ثم ان علينا بيانه: 19. Then it is for Us (Allâh) to make it clear (to you). (75:19)
كلا بل تحبون العاجلة: 20. Not [as you think, that you (mankind) will not be resurrected and recompensed for your deeds], but you (men) love the present life of this world, (75:20)
وجوه يومئذ ناضرة: 22. Some faces that Day shall be Nâdirah (shining and radiant). (75:22)
ووجوه يومئذ باسرة: 24. And some faces, that Day, will be Bâsirah (dark, gloomy, frowning, and sad), (75:24)
تظن ان يفعل بها فاقرة: 25. Thinking that some calamity is about to fall on them. (75:25)
كلا اذا بلغت التراقي: 26. Nay, when (the soul) reaches to the collar bone (i.e. up to the throat in its exit), (75:26)
وقيل من راق: 27. And it will be said: "Who can cure him (and save him from death)?" (75:27)
وظن انه الفراق: 28. And he (the dying person) will conclude that it was (the time) of parting (death); (75:28)
والتفت الساق بالساق: 29. And one leg will be joined with another leg (shrouded).[3] (75:29)
الى ربك يومئذ المساق: 30. The drive will be, on that Day, to your Lord (Allâh)! (75:30)
فلا صدق ولا صلى: 31. So he (the disbeliever) neither believed (in this Qur’ân and in the Message of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) nor prayed! (75:31)
ولكن كذب وتولى: 32. But on the contrary, he belied (this Qur’ân and the Message of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) and turned away! (75:32)
ثم ذهب الى اهله يتمطى: 33. Then he walked in conceit (full pride) to his family admiring himself! (75:33)
اولى لك فاولى: 34. Woe to you [O man (disbeliever)]! And then (again) woe to you! (75:34)
ثم اولى لك فاولى: 35. Again, woe to you [O man (disbeliever)]! And then (again) woe to you! (75:35)
ايحسب الانسان ان يترك سدى: 36. Does man think that he will be left neglected (without being punished or rewarded for the obligatory duties enjoined by his Lord Allâh on him)? (75:36)
الم يك نطفة من مني يمنى: 37. Was he not a Nutfah (mixed male and female sexual discharge) of semen emitted (poured forth)? (75:37)
ثم كان علقة فخلق فسوى: 38. Then he became an ‘Alaqah (a clot); then (Allâh) shaped and fashioned (him) in due proportion.[4] (75:38)
فجعل منه الزوجين الذكر والانثى: 39. And made of him two sexes, male and female. (75:39)
اليس ذلك بقادر على ان يحيي الموتى: 40. Is not He (Allâh Who does that) Able to give life to the dead? (Yes! He is Able to do all things). (75:40)
هل اتى على الانسان حين من الدهر لم يكن ش: 1. Has there not been over man a period of time, when he was not a thing worth mentioning? (76:1)
انا خلقنا الانسان من نطفة امشاج نبتليه ف: 2. Verily, We have created man from Nutfah (drops) of mixed semen (sexual discharge of man and woman), in order to try him: so We made him hearer and seer. (76:2)
انا هديناه السبيل اما شاكرا واما كفورا: 3. Verily, We showed him the way, whether he be grateful or ungrateful. (76:3)
انا اعتدنا للكافرين سلاسل واغلالا وسعيرا: 4. Verily, We have prepared for the disbelievers iron chains, iron collars, and a blazing Fire. (76:4)
ان الابرار يشربون من كاس كان مزاجها كافو: 5. Verily,, the Abrâr (the pious believers of Islamic Monotheism), shall drink of a cup (of wine) mixed with (water from a spring in Paradise called) Kâfûr (76:5)
عينا يشرب بها عباد الله يفجرونها تفجيرا: 6. A spring wherefrom the slaves of Allâh will drink, causing it to gush forth abundantly. (76:6)
يوفون بالنذر ويخافون يوما كان شره مستطير: 7. They (are those who) fulfil (their) vows, and they fear a Day whose evil will be wide-spreading. (76:7)
ويطعمون الطعام على حبه مسكينا ويتيما واس: 8. And they give food, inspite of their love for it (or for the love of Him), to the Miskîn[1] (the poor), the orphan, and the captive (76:8)
انما نطعمكم لوجه الله لا نريد منكم جزاء: 9. (Saying): "We feed you seeking Allâh’s Countenance only. We wish for no reward, nor thanks from you. (76:9)
انا نخاف من ربنا يوما عبوسا قمطريرا: 10. "Verily, We fear from our Lord a Day, hard and distressful, that will make the faces look horrible (from extreme dislike to it)." (76:10)
فوقاهم الله شر ذلك اليوم ولقاهم نضرة وسر: 11. So Allâh saved them from the evil of that Day, and gave them Nadrah (a light of beauty) and joy. (76:11)
وجزاهم بما صبروا جنة وحريرا: 12. And their recompense shall be Paradise, and silken garments, because they were patient. (76:12)
متكئين فيها على الارائك لا يرون فيها شمس: 13. Reclining therein on raised thrones, they will see there neither the excessive heat of the sun, nor the excessive bitter cold, (as in Paradise there is no sun and no moon). (76:13)
ودانية عليهم ظلالها وذللت قطوفها تذليلا: 14. And the shade thereof is close upon them, and the bunches of fruit thereof will hang low within their reach. (76:14)
ويطاف عليهم بانية من فضة واكواب كانت قوا: 15. And amongst them will be passed round vessels of silver and cups of crystal - (76:15)
قوارير من فضة قدروها تقديرا: 16. Crystal-clear, made of silver. They will determine the measure thereof (according to their wishes). (76:16)
ويسقون فيها كاسا كان مزاجها زنجبيلا: 17. And they will be given to drink there of a cup (of wine) mixed with Zanjabîl (ginger), (76:17)
ويطوف عليهم ولدان مخلدون اذا رايتهم حسبت: 19. And round about them will (serve) boys of everlasting youth. If you see them, you would think them scattered pearls. (76:19)
واذا رايت ثم رايت نعيما وملكا كبيرا: 20. And when you look there (in Paradise), you will see a delight (that cannot be imagined), and a great dominion. (76:20)
عاليهم ثياب سندس خضر واستبرق وحلوا اساور: 21. Their garments will be of fine green silk, and gold embroidery. They will be adorned with bracelets of silver, and their Lord will give them a pure drink. (76:21)
ان هذا كان لكم جزاء وكان سعيكم مشكورا: 22. (And it will be said to them): "Verily, this is a reward for you, and your endeavour has been accepted." (76:22)
انا نحن نزلنا عليك القران تنزيلا: 23. Verily, it is We Who have sent down the Qur’ân to you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) by stages. (76:23)
فاصبر لحكم ربك ولا تطع منهم اثما او كفور: 24. Therefore be patient (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) with constancy to the Command of your Lord (Allâh, by doing your duty to Him and by conveying His Message to mankind), and obey neither a sinner nor a disbeliever among them. (76:24)
واذكر اسم ربك بكرة واصيلا: 25. And remember the Name of your Lord every morning and afternoon [i.e. offering of the Morning (Fajr), Zuhr, and ‘Asr prayers]. (76:25)
ومن الليل فاسجد له وسبحه ليلا طويلا: 26. And during night, prostrate yourself to Him (i.e. the offering of Maghrib and ‘Ishâ’ prayers), and glorify Him a long night through (i.e. Tahajjud prayer). (76:26)
ان هؤلاء يحبون العاجلة ويذرون وراءهم يوم: 27. Verily, these (disbelievers) love the present life of this world, and put behind them a heavy Day (that will be hard). (76:27)
نحن خلقناهم وشددنا اسرهم واذا شئنا بدلنا: 28. It is We Who created them, and We have made them of strong built. And when We will, We can replace them with others like them with a complete replacement. (76:28)
وما تشاءون الا ان يشاء الله ان الله كان: 30. But you cannot will, unless Allâh wills. Verily, Allâh is Ever All-Knowing, All-Wise. (76:30)
يدخل من يشاء في رحمته والظالمين اعد لهم: 31. He will admit to His Mercy whom He wills and as for the Zâlimûn - (polytheists, wrong-doers) He has prepared a painful torment. (76:31)
والمرسلات عرفا: 1. By the winds (or angels or the Messengers of Allâh) sent forth one after another. (77:1)
فالعاصفات عصفا: 2. And by the winds that blow violently. (77:2)
والناشرات نشرا: 3. And by the winds that scatter clouds and rain. (77:3)
فالفارقات فرقا: 4. And by the Verses (of the Qur’ân) that separate the right from the wrong. (77:4)
فالملقيات ذكرا: 5. And by the angels that bring the revelations to the Messengers, (77:5)
عذرا او نذرا: 6. To cut off all excuses or to warn. (77:6)
انما توعدون لواقع: 7. Surely, what you are promised must come to pass. (77:7)
فاذا النجوم طمست: 8. Then when the stars lose their lights. (77:8)
واذا السماء فرجت: 9. And when the heaven is cleft asunder. (77:9)
واذا الجبال نسفت: 10. And when the mountains are blown away. (77:10)
واذا الرسل اقتت: 11. And when the Messengers are gathered to their time appointed. (77:11)
لاي يوم اجلت: 12. For what Day are these signs postponed? (77:12)
ليوم الفصل: 13. For the Day of Sorting Out (the men of Paradise from the men destined for Hell). (77:13)
وما ادراك ما يوم الفصل: 14. And what will explain to you what is the Day of Sorting Out? (77:14)
ويل يومئذ للمكذبين: 15. Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)! (77:15)
الم نهلك الاولين: 16. Did We not destroy the ancients? (77:16)
ثم نتبعهم الاخرين: 17. So shall We make later generations to follow them. (77:17)
كذلك نفعل بالمجرمين: 18. Thus do We deal with the Mujrimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals). (77:18)
الم نخلقكم من ماء مهين: 20. Did We not create you from a despised water (semen)? (77:20)
فجعلناه في قرار مكين: 21. Then We placed it in a place of safety (womb), (77:21)
الى قدر معلوم: 22. For a known period (determined by gestation)? (77:22)
فقدرنا فنعم القادرون: 23. So We did measure; and We are the Best to measure (the things). (77:23)
الم نجعل الارض كفاتا: 25. Have We not made the earth a receptacle (77:25)
احياء وامواتا: 26. For the living and the dead? (77:26)
وجعلنا فيها رواسي شامخات واسقيناكم ماء ف: 27. And have placed therein firm, and tall mountains, and have given you to drink sweet water? (77:27)
انطلقوا الى ما كنتم به تكذبون: 29. (It will be said to the disbelievers): "Depart you to that which you used to deny! (77:29)
انطلقوا الى ظل ذي ثلاث شعب: 30. "Depart you to a shadow (of Hell-Fire smoke ascending) in three columns, (77:30)
لا ظليل ولا يغني من اللهب: 31. Neither shady, nor of any use against the fierce flame of the Fire." (77:31)
انها ترمي بشرر كالقصر: 32. Verily, it (Hell) throws sparks (huge) as Al-Qasr (a fort or a huge log of wood), (77:32)
كانه جمالت صفر: 33. As if they were yellow camels or bundles of ropes. (77:33)
هذا يوم لا ينطقون: 35. That will be a Day when they shall not speak (during some part of it), (77:35)
ولا يؤذن لهم فيعتذرون: 36. And they will not be permitted to put forth any excuse. (77:36)
هذا يوم الفصل جمعناكم والاولين: 38. That will be a Day of Decision! We have brought you and the men of old together! (77:38)
فان كان لكم كيد فكيدون: 39. So if you have a plot, use it against Me (Allâh جل جلاله)! (77:39)
ان المتقين في ظلال وعيون: 41. Verily, the Muttaqûn (the pious. See V.2:2) shall be amidst shades and springs. (77:41)
وفواكه مما يشتهون: 42. And fruits, such as they desire. (77:42)
كلوا وتمتعوا قليلا انكم مجرمون: 46. (O you disbelievers)! Eat and enjoy yourselves (in this worldly life) for a little while. Verily, you are the Mujrimûn (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals). (77:46)
واذا قيل لهم اركعوا لا يركعون: 48. And when it is said to them: "Bow down yourself (in prayer)!" They bow not down (offer not their prayers).[1] (77:48)
فباي حديث بعده يؤمنون: 50. Then in what statement after this (the Qur’ân) will they believe? (77:50)
عم يتساءلون: 1. What are they asking (one another) about? (78:1)
عن النبا العظيم: 2. About the great news, (i.e. Islâmic Monotheism, the Qur’ân, which Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم brought and the Day of Resurrection) (78:2)
الذي هم فيه مختلفون: 3. About which they are in disagreement. (78:3)
كلا سيعلمون: 4. Nay, they will come to know! (78:4)
ثم كلا سيعلمون: 5. Nay, again, they will come to know! (78:5)
الم نجعل الارض مهادا: 6. Have We not made the earth as a bed, (78:6)
وخلقناكم ازواجا: 8. And We have created you in pairs (male and female, tall and short, good and bad, etc.). (78:8)
وجعلنا نومكم سباتا: 9. And We have made your sleep as a thing for rest. (78:9)
وجعلنا الليل لباسا: 10. And We have made the night as a covering (through its darkness), (78:10)
وجعلنا النهار معاشا: 11. And We have made the day for livelihood. (78:11)
وبنينا فوقكم سبعا شدادا: 12. And We have built above you seven strong (heavens), (78:12)
وجعلنا سراجا وهاجا: 13. And We have made (therein) a shining lamp (sun). (78:13)
وانزلنا من المعصرات ماء ثجاجا: 14. And We have sent down from the rainy clouds abundant water. (78:14)
لنخرج به حبا ونباتا: 15. That We may produce therewith corn and vegetations, (78:15)
ان يوم الفصل كان ميقاتا: 17. Verily, the Day of Decision is a fixed time, (78:17)
يوم ينفخ في الصور فتاتون افواجا: 18. The Day when the Trumpet will be blown, and you shall come forth in crowds (groups after groups). [Tafsir At-Tabari] (78:18)
وفتحت السماء فكانت ابوابا: 19. And the heaven shall be opened, and it will become as gates, (78:19)
وسيرت الجبال فكانت سرابا: 20. And the mountains shall be moved away from their places and they will be as if they were a mirage. (78:20)
ان جهنم كانت مرصادا: 21. Truly, Hell is a place of ambush - (78:21)
للطاغين مابا: 22. A dwelling place for the Tâghûn (those who transgress the boundary limits set by Allâh like polytheists, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allâh, hyprocrites, sinners, criminals), (78:22)
لابثين فيها احقابا: 23. They will abide therein for ages. (78:23)
لا يذوقون فيها بردا ولا شرابا: 24. Nothing cool shall they taste therein, nor any drink. (78:24)
الا حميما وغساقا: 25. Except boiling water, and dirty wound discharges - (78:25)
جزاء وفاقا: 26. An exact recompense (according to their evil crimes). (78:26)
انهم كانوا لا يرجون حسابا: 27. For verily, they used not to look for a reckoning. (78:27)
وكذبوا باياتنا كذابا: 28. But they belied Our Ayât (proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, and that which Our Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم brought) completely. (78:28)
وكل شيء احصيناه كتابا: 29. And all things We have recorded in a Book. (78:29)
فذوقوا فلن نزيدكم الا عذابا: 30. So taste you (the results of your evil actions). No increase shall We give you, except in torment. (78:30)
ان للمتقين مفازا: 31. Verily, for the Muttaqûn,[1] there will be a success (Paradise); (78:31)
وكواعب اترابا: 33. And young full-breasted (mature) maidens of equal age, (78:33)
لا يسمعون فيها لغوا ولا كذابا: 35. No Laghw (dirty, false, evil talk) shall they hear therein, nor lying; (78:35)
جزاء من ربك عطاء حسابا: 36. A reward from your Lord, an ample calculated gift (according to the best of their good deeds),[2] (78:36)
رب السماوات والارض وما بينهما الرحمن لا: 37. (From) the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatsoever is in between them, the Most Gracious, with Whom they cannot dare to speak (on the Day of Resurrection except by His Leave). (78:37)
يوم يقوم الروح والملائكة صفا لا يتكلمون: 38. The Day that Ar-Rûh [Jibrîl (Gabriel) or another angel] and the angels will stand forth in rows, they will not speak except him whom the Most Gracious (Allâh) allows, and he will speak what is right. (78:38)
ذلك اليوم الحق فمن شاء اتخذ الى ربه مابا: 39. That is (without doubt) the True Day. So, whosoever wills, let him seek a place with (or a way to) His Lord (by obeying Him in this worldly life)! (78:39)
انا انذرناكم عذابا قريبا يوم ينظر المرء: 40. Verily, We have warned you of a near torment - the Day when man will see that (the deeds) which his hands have sent forth, and the disbeliever will say: "Woe to me! Would that I were dust!" (78:40)
والنازعات غرقا: 1. By those (angels) who pull out (the souls of the disbelievers and the wicked) with great violence. (79:1)
والناشطات نشطا: 2. By those (angels) who gently take out (the souls of the believers). (79:2)
والسابحات سبحا: 3. And by those that swim along (i.e. angels or planets in their orbits). (79:3)
فالسابقات سبقا: 4. And by those that press forward as in a race (i.e. the angels or stars or the horses). (79:4)
فالمدبرات امرا: 5. And by those angels who arrange to do the Commands of their Lord, (so verily, you disbelievers will be called to account). (79:5)
يوم ترجف الراجفة: 6. On the Day (when the first blowing of the Trumpet is blown), the earth and the mountains will shake violently (and everybody will die). (79:6)
تتبعها الرادفة: 7. The second blowing of the Trumpet follows it (and everybody will be resurrected). (79:7)
قلوب يومئذ واجفة: 8. (Some) hearts that Day will shake with fear and anxiety. (79:8)
يقولون اانا لمردودون في الحافرة: 10. They say: "Shall we indeed be returned to (our) former state of life? (79:10)
قالوا تلك اذا كرة خاسرة: 12. They say: "It would in that case, be a return with loss!" (79:12)
فانما هي زجرة واحدة: 13. But it will be only a single Zajrah [shout (i.e., the second blowing of the Trumpet)], (See Verse 37:19). (79:13)
فاذا هم بالساهرة: 14. When behold, they find themselves on the surface of the earth alive after their death, (79:14)
هل اتاك حديث موسى: 15. Has there come to you the story of Mûsâ (Moses)? (79:15)
اذ ناداه ربه بالواد المقدس طوى: 16. When his Lord called him in the sacred valley of Tûwâ, (79:16)
فقل هل لك الى ان تزكى: 18. And say to him: "Would you purify yourself (from the sin of disbelief by becoming a believer)?" (79:18)
واهديك الى ربك فتخشى: 19. "And that I guide you to your Lord, so you should fear Him?" (79:19)
فاراه الاية الكبرى: 20. Then [Mûsâ (Moses)] showed him the great sign (miracles). (79:20)
ثم ادبر يسعى: 22. Then he turned his back, striving (against Allâh). (79:22)
فحشر فنادى: 23. Then he gathered (his people) and cried aloud, (79:23)
فقال انا ربكم الاعلى: 24. Saying: "I am your lord, most high." (79:24)
فاخذه الله نكال الاخرة والاولى: 25. So Allâh, seized him with punishment for his last[1] and first[2] transgression. [Tafsir At-Tabari] (79:25)
ان في ذلك لعبرة لمن يخشى: 26. Verily, in this is an instructive admonition for whosoever fears Allâh. (79:26)
اانتم اشد خلقا ام السماء بناها: 27. Are you more difficult to create or is the heaven that He constructed? (79:27)
رفع سمكها فسواها: 28. He raised its height, and has perfected it. (79:28)
واغطش ليلها واخرج ضحاها: 29. Its night He covers with darkness and its forenoon He brings out (with light) (79:29)
والارض بعد ذلك دحاها: 30. And after that He spread the earth, (79:30)
اخرج منها ماءها ومرعاها: 31. And brought forth therefrom its water and its pasture. (79:31)
والجبال ارساها: 32. And the mountains He has fixed firmly, (79:32)
متاعا لكم ولانعامكم: 33. (To be) a provision and benefit for you and your cattle. (79:33)
فاذا جاءت الطامة الكبرى: 34. But when there comes the greatest catastrophe (i.e. the Day of Recompense) - (79:34)
يوم يتذكر الانسان ما سعى: 35. The Day when man shall remember what he strove for. (79:35)
وبرزت الجحيم لمن يرى: 36. And Hell-fire shall be made apparent in full view for (every) one who sees. (79:36)
فاما من طغى: 37. Then for him who transgressed all bounds, (in disbelief, oppression and evil deeds of disobedience to Allâh). (79:37)
واثر الحياة الدنيا: 38. And preferred the life of this world (by following his evil desires and lusts), (79:38)
فان الجحيم هي الماوى: 39. Verily, his abode will be Hell-fire; (79:39)
واما من خاف مقام ربه ونهى النفس عن الهوى: 40. But as for him who feared standing before his Lord, and restrained himself from impure evil desires and lusts. (79:40)
فان الجنة هي الماوى: 41. Verily, Paradise will be his abode. (79:41)
يسالونك عن الساعة ايان مرساها: 42. They ask you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) about the Hour - when will be its appointed time? (79:42)
فيم انت من ذكراها: 43. You have no knowledge to say anything about it. (79:43)
الى ربك منتهاها: 44. To your Lord belongs (the knowledge of) the term thereof? (79:44)
انما انت منذر من يخشاها: 45. You (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) are only a warner for those who fear it, (79:45)
كانهم يوم يرونها لم يلبثوا الا عشية او ض: 46. The Day they see it, (it will be) as if they had not tarried (in this world) except an afternoon or a morning. (79:46)
عبس وتولى: 1. (The Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم) frowned and turned away. (80:1)
ان جاءه الاعمى: 2. Because there came to him the blind man (i.e. ‘Abdullâh bin Umm-Maktûm, who came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم while he was preaching to one or some of the Quraish chiefs). (80:2)
وما يدريك لعله يزكى: 3. And how can you know that he might become pure (from sins)? (80:3)
او يذكر فتنفعه الذكرى: 4. Or he might receive admonition, and the admonition might profit him? (80:4)
اما من استغنى: 5. As for him who thinks himself self-sufficient, (80:5)
وما عليك الا يزكى: 7. What does it matter to you if he will not become pure (from disbelief: you are only a Messenger, your duty is to convey the Message of Allâh). (80:7)
واما من جاءك يسعى: 8. But as to him who came to you running, (80:8)
وهو يخشى: 9. And is afraid (of Allâh and His punishment). (80:9)
فانت عنه تلهى: 10. Of him you are neglectful and divert your attention to another, (80:10)
كلا انها تذكرة: 11. Nay, (do not do like this); indeed it (this Qur’ân) is an admonition. (80:11)
في صحف مكرمة: 13. (It is) in Records held (greatly) in honour (Al-Lauh Al-Mahfûz), (80:13)
قتل الانسان ما اكفره: 17. Be cursed (the disbelieving) man! How ungrateful he is! (80:17)
من اي شيء خلقه: 18. From what thing did He create him? (80:18)
من نطفة خلقه فقدره: 19. From Nutfah (male and female semen drops) He created him and then set him in due proportion. (80:19)
ثم السبيل يسره: 20. Then He makes the Path easy for him. (80:20)
ثم اماته فاقبره: 21. Then He causes him to die and puts him in his grave. (80:21)
ثم اذا شاء انشره: 22. Then when it is His Will, He will resurrect him (again). (80:22)
كلا لما يقض ما امره: 23. Nay, but (man) has not done what He commanded him. (80:23)
فلينظر الانسان الى طعامه: 24. Then let man look at his food: (80:24)
انا صببنا الماء صبا: 25. We pour forth water in abundance. (80:25)
ثم شققنا الارض شقا: 26. And We split the earth in clefts. (80:26)
فانبتنا فيها حبا: 27. And We cause therein the grain to grow, (80:27)
وعنبا وقضبا: 28. And grapes and clover plants (i.e. green fodder for the cattle), (80:28)
وحدائق غلبا: 30. And gardens dense with many trees, (80:30)
فاذا جاءت الصاخة: 33. Then when there comes As-Sâkhkhah (the second blowing of the Trumpet on the Day of Resurrection) (80:33)
يوم يفر المرء من اخيه: 34. That Day shall a man flee from his brother, (80:34)
وامه وابيه: 35. And from his mother and his father, (80:35)
وصاحبته وبنيه: 36. And from his wife and his children. (80:36)
لكل امرئ منهم يومئذ شان يغنيه: 37. Everyman that Day will have enough to make him careless of others. (80:37)
وجوه يومئذ مسفرة: 38. Some faces that Day will be bright (true believers of Islâmic Monotheism), (80:38)
ضاحكة مستبشرة: 39. Laughing, rejoicing at good news (of Paradise). (80:39)
ووجوه يومئذ عليها غبرة: 40. And other faces, that Day, will be dust-stained. (80:40)
اولئك هم الكفرة الفجرة: 42. Such will be the Kafarah (disbelievers in Allâh, in His Oneness, and in His Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم, etc.), the Fajarah (wicked evil doers). (80:42)
اذا الشمس كورت: 1. When the sun is wound round and its light is lost and is overthrown.[1] (81:1)
واذا الجبال سيرت: 3. And when the mountains are made to pass away; (81:3)
واذا العشار عطلت: 4. And when the pregnant she-camels are neglected; (81:4)
واذا الوحوش حشرت: 5. And when the wild beasts are gathered together. (81:5)
واذا البحار سجرت: 6. And when the seas become as blazing Fire or overflow. (81:6)
واذا النفوس زوجت: 7. And when the souls are joined with their bodies, (the good with the good and the bad with the bad). (81:7)
واذا الموءودة سئلت: 8. And when the female (infant) buried alive (as the pagan Arabs used to do) is questioned:[2] (81:8)
باي ذنب قتلت: 9. For what sin, was she killed? (81:9)
واذا الصحف نشرت: 10. And when the (written) pages [of deeds (good and bad) of every person] are laid open. (81:10)
واذا السماء كشطت: 11. And when the heaven is stripped off and taken away from its place; (81:11)
واذا الجحيم سعرت: 12. And when Hell-fire is set ablaze. (81:12)
واذا الجنة ازلفت: 13. And when Paradise is brought near. (81:13)
علمت نفس ما احضرت: 14. (Then) every person will know what he has brought (of good and evil). (81:14)
فلا اقسم بالخنس: 15. So verily, I swear by the planets that recede (i.e. disappear during the day and appear during the night). (81:15)
الجوار الكنس: 16. And by the planets that move swiftly and hide themselves. (81:16)
والليل اذا عسعس: 17. And by the night as it departs. (81:17)
والصبح اذا تنفس: 18. And by the dawn as it brightens. (81:18)
ذي قوة عند ذي العرش مكين: 20. Owner of power, (and high rank) with (Allâh), the Lord of the Throne, (81:20)
مطاع ثم امين: 21. Obeyed (by the angels in the heavens), and trustworthy. (81:21)
وما صاحبكم بمجنون: 22. And (O people) your companion (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) is not a madman. (81:22)
ولقد راه بالافق المبين: 23. And indeed he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) saw him [Jibrîl (Gabriel)] in the clear horizon (towards the east). (81:23)
وما هو على الغيب بضنين: 24. And he (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) withholds not a knowledge of the Unseen. (81:24)
وما هو بقول شيطان رجيم: 25. And it (the Qur’ân) is not the word of the outcast Shaitân (Satan). (81:25)
لمن شاء منكم ان يستقيم: 28. To whomsoever among you who wills to walk straight. (81:28)
وما تشاءون الا ان يشاء الله رب العالمين: 29. And you cannot will unless (it be) that Allâh wills - the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists). (81:29)
اذا السماء انفطرت: 1. When the heaven is cleft asunder. (82:1)
واذا الكواكب انتثرت: 2. And when the stars have fallen and scattered. (82:2)
واذا البحار فجرت: 3. And when the seas are burst forth. (82:3)
واذا القبور بعثرت: 4. And when the graves are turned upside down (and bring out their contents) (82:4)
علمت نفس ما قدمت واخرت: 5. (Then) a person will know what he has sent forward and (what he has) left behind (of good or bad deeds). (82:5)
يا ايها الانسان ما غرك بربك الكريم: 6. O man! What has made you careless about your Lord, the Most Generous? (82:6)
الذي خلقك فسواك فعدلك: 7. Who created you, fashioned you perfectly, and gave you due proportion. (82:7)
في اي صورة ما شاء ركبك: 8. In whatever form He willed, He put you together. (82:8)
كلا بل تكذبون بالدين: 9. Nay! But you deny Ad-Dîn (i.e. the Day of Recompense). (82:9)
وان عليكم لحافظين: 10. But verily, over you (are appointed angels in charge of mankind) to watch you,[1] (82:10)
يعلمون ما تفعلون: 12. They know all that you do. (82:12)
ان الابرار لفي نعيم: 13. Verily, the Abrâr (the pious believers of Islamic Monotheism) will be in Delight (Paradise); (82:13)
وان الفجار لفي جحيم: 14. And verily, the Fujjâr (the wicked, disbelievers, polytheists, sinners and evil-doers) will be in the blazing Fire (Hell), (82:14)
يصلونها يوم الدين: 15. Therein they will enter, and taste its burning flame on the Day of Recompense, (82:15)
وما هم عنها بغائبين: 16. And they (Al-Fujjâr) will not be absent therefrom. (82:16)
وما ادراك ما يوم الدين: 17. And what will make you know what the Day of Recompense is? (82:17)
ثم ما ادراك ما يوم الدين: 18. Again, what will make you know what the Day of Recompense is? (82:18)
يوم لا تملك نفس لنفس شيئا والامر يومئذ ل: 19. (It will be) the Day when no person shall have power (to do) anything for another, and the Decision, that Day, will be (wholly) with Allâh. (82:19)
ويل للمطففين: 1. Woe to Al-Mutaffifûn (those who give less in measure and weight). (83:1)
الذين اذا اكتالوا على الناس يستوفون: 2. Those who, when they have to receive by measure from men, demand full measure, (83:2)
واذا كالوهم او وزنوهم يخسرون: 3. And when they have to give by measure or weight to (other) men, give less than due. (83:3)
الا يظن اولئك انهم مبعوثون: 4. Do they not think that they will be resurrected (for reckoning), (83:4)
يوم يقوم الناس لرب العالمين: 6. The Day when (all) mankind will stand before the Lord of the ‘Âlamîn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)? (83:6)
كلا ان كتاب الفجار لفي سجين: 7. Nay! Truly, the Record (writing of the deeds) of the Fujjâr (disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, evil-doers and the wicked) is (preserved) in Sijjîn. (83:7)
وما ادراك ما سجين: 8. And what will make you know what Sijjîn is? (83:8)
الذين يكذبون بيوم الدين: 11. Those who deny the Day of Recompense. (83:11)
وما يكذب به الا كل معتد اثيم: 12. And none can deny it except every transgressor beyond bounds, (in disbelief, oppression and disobedience to Allâh) the sinner! (83:12)
كلا بل ران على قلوبهم ما كانوا يكسبون: 14. Nay! But on their hearts is the Rân (covering of sins and evil deeds) which they used to earn.[1] (83:14)
كلا انهم عن ربهم يومئذ لمحجوبون: 15. Nay! Surely they (evil-doers) will be veiled from seeing their Lord that Day. (83:15)
ثم انهم لصالو الجحيم: 16. Then, verily, they will indeed enter (and taste) the burning flame of Hell. (83:16)
ثم يقال هذا الذي كنتم به تكذبون: 17. Then, it will be said to them: "This is what you used to deny!" (83:17)
كلا ان كتاب الابرار لفي عليين: 18. Nay! Verily, the Record (writing of the deeds) of Al-Abrâr (the pious believers of Islamic Monotheism) is (preserved) in ‘Illiyyûn (83:18)
وما ادراك ما عليون: 19. And what will make you know what ‘Illiyyûn is? (83:19)
يشهده المقربون: 21. To which bear witness those nearest (to Allâh, i.e. the angels). (83:21)
تعرف في وجوههم نضرة النعيم: 24. You will recognise in their faces the brightness of delight. (83:24)
يسقون من رحيق مختوم: 25. They will be given to drink of pure sealed wine. (83:25)
ختامه مسك وفي ذلك فليتنافس المتنافسون: 26. The last thereof (that wine) will be the smell of Musk, and for this let (all) those strive who want to strive (i.e. hasten earnestly to the obedience of Allah). (83:26)
ومزاجه من تسنيم: 27. It (that wine) will be mixed with Tasnîm: (83:27)
عينا يشرب بها المقربون: 28. A spring whereof drink those nearest to Allâh. (83:28)
ان الذين اجرموا كانوا من الذين امنوا يضح: 29. Verily, (during the worldly life) those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed. (83:29)
واذا مروا بهم يتغامزون: 30. And, whenever they passed by them, used to wink one to another (in mockery). (83:30)
واذا انقلبوا الى اهلهم انقلبوا فكهين: 31. And when they returned to their own people, they would return jesting; (83:31)
واذا راوهم قالوا ان هؤلاء لضالون: 32. And when they saw them, they said: "Verily these have indeed gone astray!" (83:32)
وما ارسلوا عليهم حافظين: 33. But they (disbelievers, sinners) had not been sent as watchers over them (the believers). (83:33)
فاليوم الذين امنوا من الكفار يضحكون: 34. But this Day (the Day of Resurrection) those who believe will laugh at the disbelievers[2] (83:34)
هل ثوب الكفار ما كانوا يفعلون: 36. Are not the disbelievers paid (fully) for what they used to do? (83:36)
اذا السماء انشقت: 1. When the heaven is split asunder, (84:1)
واذنت لربها وحقت: 2. And listens to and obeys its Lord - and it must do so. (84:2)
واذا الارض مدت: 3. And when the earth is stretched forth, (84:3)
والقت ما فيها وتخلت: 4. And has cast out all that was in it and became empty. (84:4)
يا ايها الانسان انك كادح الى ربك كدحا فم: 6. O man! Verily, you are returning towards your Lord with your deeds and actions (good or bad), a sure returning, and you will meet (the results of your deeds which you did). (84:6)
فاما من اوتي كتابه بيمينه: 7. Then as for him who will be given his Record in his right hand, (84:7)
فسوف يحاسب حسابا يسيرا: 8. He surely will receive an easy reckoning, (84:8)
وينقلب الى اهله مسرورا: 9. And will return to his family in joy! (84:9)
واما من اوتي كتابه وراء ظهره: 10. But whosoever is given his Record behind his back, (84:10)
ويصلى سعيرا: 12. And he shall enter a blazing Fire, and be made to taste its burning. (84:12)
انه كان في اهله مسرورا: 13. Verily, he was among his people in joy! (84:13)
انه ظن ان لن يحور: 14. Verily, he thought that he would never come back (to Us)! (84:14)
بلى ان ربه كان به بصيرا: 15. Yes! Verily, his Lord has been ever beholding him! (84:15)
فلا اقسم بالشفق: 16. So I swear by the afterglow of sunset; (84:16)
والليل وما وسق: 17. And by the night and whatever it gathers in its darkness, (84:17)
والقمر اذا اتسق: 18. And by the moon when it is at the full. (84:18)
لتركبن طبقا عن طبق: 19. You shall certainly travel from stage to stage (in this life and in the Hereafter). (84:19)
فما لهم لا يؤمنون: 20. What is the matter with them, that they believe not? (84:20)
واذا قرئ عليهم القران لا يسجدون: 21. And when the Qur’ân is recited to them, they fall not prostrate. (84:21)
بل الذين كفروا يكذبون: 22. Nay, those who disbelieve belie (Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم and whatever he brought, i.e. this Qur’ân and Islâmic Monotheism, etc.).[1] (84:22)
والله اعلم بما يوعون: 23. And Allâh knows best what they gather (of good and bad deeds), (84:23)
فبشرهم بعذاب اليم: 24. So announce to them a painful torment. (84:24)
الا الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات لهم اجر: 25. Save those who believe and do righteous good deeds, for them is a reward that will never come to an end (i.e. Paradise). (84:25)
والسماء ذات البروج: 1. By the heaven holding the big stars.[1] (85:1)
واليوم الموعود: 2. And by the Promised Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection). (85:2)
وشاهد ومشهود: 3. And by the Witnessing day (i.e. Friday), and by the Witnessed day [i.e. the day of ‘Arafât (Hajj) the ninth of Dhul-Hijjah]; (85:3)
قتل اصحاب الاخدود: 4. Cursed were the people of the Ditch (in the story of the Boy and the King).[2] (85:4)
وهم على ما يفعلون بالمؤمنين شهود: 7. And they witnessed what they were doing against the believers (i.e. burning them). (85:7)
وما نقموا منهم الا ان يؤمنوا بالله العزي: 8. And they had no fault except that they believed in Allâh, the All-Mighty, Worthy of all Praise! (85:8)
الذي له ملك السماوات والارض والله على كل: 9. To Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth! And Allâh is Witness over everything. (85:9)
ان الذين فتنوا المؤمنين والمؤمنات ثم لم: 10. Verily, those who put into trial the believing men and believing women (by torturing them and burning them), and then do not turn in repentance (to Allâh), then they will have the torment of Hell, and they will have the punishment of the burning Fire. (85:10)
ان الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات لهم جنات: 11. Verily, those who believe and do righteous good deeds, for them will be Gardens under which rivers flow (Paradise). That is the great success. (85:11)
ان بطش ربك لشديد: 12. Verily, (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) the Seizure (punishment) of your Lord is severe and painful. [See V.11:102]. (85:12)
انه هو يبدئ ويعيد: 13. Verily, He it is Who begins (punishment) and repeats (punishment in the Hereafter) (or originates the creation of everything, and then repeats it on the Day of Resurrection). (85:13)
وهو الغفور الودود: 14. And He is Oft-Forgiving, full of love (towards the pious who are real true believers of Islâmic Monotheism), (85:14)

ليلة القدر خير من الف شهر: 3. The night of Al-Qadr (Decree) is better than a thousand months (i.e. worshipping Allâh in that night is better than worshipping Him a thousand months, i.e. 83 years and 4 months). (97:3)
