سلام هي حتى مطلع الفجر: 5. (All that night), there is Peace (and Goodness from Allâh to His believing slaves) until the appearance of dawn.[2] (97:5)
لم يكن الذين كفروا من اهل الكتاب والمشرك: 1. Those who disbelieve from among the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) and Al-Mushrikûn,[1] were not going to leave (their disbelief) until there came to them clear evidence. (98:1)
رسول من الله يتلو صحفا مطهرة: 2. A Messenger (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from Allâh, reciting (the Qur’ân) purified pages [purified from Al-Bâtil (falsehood)]. (98:2)
فيها كتب قيمة: 3. Wherein are correct and straight laws from Allâh. (98:3)
وما تفرق الذين اوتوا الكتاب الا من بعد م: 4. And the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) differed not until after there came to them clear evidence (i.e. Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم and whatever was revealed to him). (98:4)
وما امروا الا ليعبدوا الله مخلصين له الد: 5. And they were commanded not, but that they should worship Allâh, and worship none but Him Alone (abstaining from ascribing partners to Him), and perform As-Salât (Iqâmat-as-Salât) and give Zakât, and that is the right religion. (98:5)
ان الذين كفروا من اهل الكتاب والمشركين ف: 6. Verily, those who disbelieve (in the religion of Islâm, the Qur’ân and Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) from among the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) and Al-Mushrikûn will abide in the Fire of Hell. They are the worst of creatures.[2] (98:6)
ان الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات اولئك هم: 7. Verily, those who believe [in the Oneness of Allâh, and in His Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) including all obligations ordered by Islâm] and do righteous good deeds, they are the best of creatures. (98:7)
جزاؤهم عند ربهم جنات عدن تجري من تحتها ا: 8. Their reward with their Lord is ‘Adn (Eden) Paradise (Gardens of Eternity), underneath which rivers flow. They will abide therein forever, Allâh will be pleased with them, and they with Him. That is for him who fears his Lord. (98:8)
اذا زلزلت الارض زلزالها: 1. When the earth is shaken with its (final) earthquake. (99:1)
واخرجت الارض اثقالها: 2. And when the earth throws out its burdens. (99:2)
وقال الانسان ما لها: 3. And man will say: "What is the matter with it?" (99:3)
يومئذ تحدث اخبارها: 4. That Day it will declare its information (about all that happened over it of good or evil). (99:4)
بان ربك اوحى لها: 5. Because your Lord will inspire it. (99:5)
يومئذ يصدر الناس اشتاتا ليروا اعمالهم: 6. That Day mankind will proceed in scattered groups that they may be shown their deeds.[1] (99:6)
فمن يعمل مثقال ذرة خيرا يره: 7. So whosoever does good equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant) shall see it. (99:7)
ومن يعمل مثقال ذرة شرا يره: 8. And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant) shall see it. (99:8)
والعاديات ضبحا: 1. By the (steeds) that run, with panting. (100:1)
فالموريات قدحا: 2. Striking sparks of fire (by their hooves). (100:2)
فالمغيرات صبحا: 3. And scouring to the raid at dawn. (100:3)
فاثرن به نقعا: 4. And raise the dust in clouds the while. (100:4)
فوسطن به جمعا: 5. And penetrating forthwith as one into the midst (of the foe). (100:5)
ان الانسان لربه لكنود: 6. Verily, man (disbeliever) is ungrateful to his Lord. (100:6)
وانه على ذلك لشهيد: 7. And to that he bears witness (by his deeds). (100:7)
وانه لحب الخير لشديد: 8. And verily, he is violent in the love of wealth. (100:8)
افلا يعلم اذا بعثر ما في القبور: 9. Knows he not that when the contents of the graves are poured forth (all mankind is resurrected)? (100:9)
وحصل ما في الصدور: 10. And that which is in the breasts (of men) is made known? (100:10)
ان ربهم بهم يومئذ لخبير: 11. Verily, that Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) their Lord will be Well-Acquainted with them (as to their deeds and will reward them for their deeds). (100:11)
القارعة: 1. Al-Qâri‘ah (the striking Hour i.e. the Day of Resurrection). (101:1)
وما ادراك ما القارعة: 3. And what will make you know what the striking (Hour) is? (101:3)
يوم يكون الناس كالفراش المبثوث: 4. It is a Day whereon mankind will be like moths scattered about. (101:4)
وتكون الجبال كالعهن المنفوش: 5. And the mountains will be like carded wool. (101:5)
فاما من ثقلت موازينه: 6. Then as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be heavy,[1] (101:6)
واما من خفت موازينه: 8. But as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be light, (101:8)
فامه هاوية: 9. He will have his home in Hâwiyah (pit, i.e. Hell). (101:9)
وما ادراك ما هيه: 10. And what will make you know what it is? (101:10)
الهاكم التكاثر: 1. The mutual rivalry (for piling up of worldly things) diverts you, (102:1)
حتى زرتم المقابر: 2. Until you visit the graves (i.e. till you die). (102:2)
كلا سوف تعلمون: 3. Nay! You shall come to know! (102:3)
ثم كلا سوف تعلمون: 4. Again nay! You shall come to know! (102:4)
كلا لو تعلمون علم اليقين: 5. Nay! If you knew with a sure knowledge (the end result of piling up, you would not have been occupied yourselves in worldly things). (102:5)
لترون الجحيم: 6. Verily, You shall see the blazing Fire (Hell)! (102:6)
ثم لترونها عين اليقين: 7. And again, you shall see it with certainty of sight! (102:7)
ثم لتسالن يومئذ عن النعيم: 8. Then on that Day you shall be asked about the delights[1] (you indulged in, in this world)! (102:8)
الا الذين امنوا وعملوا الصالحات وتواصوا: 3. Except those who believe (in Islâmic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, and recommend one another to the truth [i.e. order one another to perform all kinds of good deeds (Al-Ma‘ruf) which Allâh has ordained, and abstain from all kinds of sins and evil deeds (Al-Munkar which Allâh has forbidden], and recommend one another to patience (for the sufferings, harms, and injuries which one may encounter in Allâh’s Cause during preaching His religion of Islâmic Monotheism or Jihâd). (103:3)
ويل لكل همزة لمزة: 1. Woe to every slanderer and backbiter.[1] (104:1)
الذي جمع مالا وعدده: 2. Who has gathered wealth and counted it. (104:2)
يحسب ان ماله اخلده: 3. He thinks that his wealth will make him last forever! (104:3)
كلا لينبذن في الحطمة: 4. Nay! Verily, he will be thrown into the crushing Fire. (104:4)
وما ادراك ما الحطمة: 5. And what will make you know what the crushing Fire is? (104:5)
التي تطلع على الافئدة: 7. Which leaps up over the hearts, (104:7)
انها عليهم مؤصدة: 8. Verily, it shall be closed upon them, (104:8)
في عمد ممددة: 9. In pillars stretched forth (i.e. they will be punished in the Fire with pillars). (104:9)
الم تر كيف فعل ربك باصحاب الفيل: 1. Have you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) not seen how your Lord dealt with the owners of the Elephant? [The Elephant army which came from Yemen under the command of Abrahah Al-Ashram intending to destroy the Ka‘bah at Makkah]. (105:1)
الم يجعل كيدهم في تضليل: 2. Did He not make their plot go astray? (105:2)
وارسل عليهم طيرا ابابيل: 3. And He sent against them birds, in flocks, (105:3)
ترميهم بحجارة من سجيل: 4. Striking them with stones of Sijjîl (baked clay). (105:4)
فجعلهم كعصف ماكول: 5. And He made them like (an empty field of) stalks (of which the corn has been eaten up by cattle).[1] (105:5)
لايلاف قريش: 1. (It is a great Grace from Allâh) for the protection of the Quraish, (106:1)
ايلافهم رحلة الشتاء والصيف: 2. (And with all those Allâh’s Grace and Protections, We cause) the (Quraish) caravans to set forth safe in winter (to the south) and in summer (to the north without any fear), (106:2)
فليعبدوا رب هذا البيت: 3. So let them worship (Allâh) the Lord of this House (the Ka‘bah in Makkah), (106:3)
الذي اطعمهم من جوع وامنهم من خوف: 4. (He) Who has fed them against hunger, and has made them safe from fear. (106:4)
ارايت الذي يكذب بالدين: 1. Have you seen him who denies the Recompense? (107:1)
فذلك الذي يدع اليتيم: 2. That is he who repulses the orphan (harshly),[1] (107:2)
فويل للمصلين: 4. So woe unto those performers of Salât (prayers) (hypocrites), (107:4)
الذين هم عن صلاتهم ساهون: 5. Those who delay their Salât (prayer from their stated fixed times). (107:5)
الذين هم يراءون: 6. Those who do good deeds only to be seen (of men), (107:6)
ويمنعون الماعون: 7. And prevent Al-Mâ‘ûn (small kindnesses like salt, sugar, water). (107:7)
انا اعطيناك الكوثر: 1. Verily, We have granted you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم) Al-Kauthar (a river in Paradise).[1] (108:1)
فصل لربك وانحر: 2. Therefore turn in prayer to your Lord and sacrifice (to Him only). (108:2)
ان شانئك هو الابتر: 3. For he who hates you (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم), he will be cut off (from posterity and every good thing in this world and in the Hereafter).[2] (108:3)
قل يا ايها الكافرون: 1. Say: (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم to these Mushrikûn and Kâfirûn): "O Al-Kafirûn (disbelievers in Allâh, in His Oneness, in His Angels, in His Books, in His Messengers, in the Day of Resurrection, and in Al-Qadar)! (109:1)
لا اعبد ما تعبدون: 2. "I worship not that which you worship, (109:2)
ولا انتم عابدون ما اعبد: 3. "Nor will you worship that which I worship. (109:3)
ولا انا عابد ما عبدتم: 4. "And I shall not worship that which you are worshipping. (109:4)
لكم دينكم ولي دين: 6. "To you be your religion, and to me my religion (Islâmic Monotheism)." (109:6)
اذا جاء نصر الله والفتح: 1. When there comes the Help of Allâh (to you, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم against your enemies) and the conquest (of Makkah). (110:1)
ورايت الناس يدخلون في دين الله افواجا: 2. And you see that the people enter Allah’s religion (Islâm) in crowds. (110:2)
فسبح بحمد ربك واستغفره انه كان توابا: 3. So glorify the Praises of your Lord, and ask His Forgiveness. Verily, He is the One Who accepts the repentance and Who forgives. (110:3)
تبت يدا ابي لهب وتب: 1. Perish the two hands of Abû Lahab (an uncle of the Prophet) and perish he![1] (111:1)
ما اغنى عنه ماله وما كسب: 2. His wealth and his children will not benefit him! (111:2)
سيصلى نارا ذات لهب: 3. He will be burnt in a Fire of blazing flames! (111:3)
وامراته حمالة الحطب: 4. And his wife, too, who carries wood (thorns of Sa‘dân which she used to put on the way of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم, or use to slander him).[2] (111:4)
في جيدها حبل من مسد: 5. In her neck is a twisted rope of Masad (palm fibre).[3] (111:5)
قل هو الله احد: 1. Say (O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وسلم): "He is Allâh, (the) One.[1] (112:1)
الله الصمد: 2. "Allâh-us-Samad (السيد الذي يصمد إليه في الحاجات) [Allâh the Self-Sufficient Master, Whom all creatures need, (He neither eats nor drinks)]. (112:2)
لم يلد ولم يولد: 3. "He begets not, nor was He begotten.[2] (112:3)
ولم يكن له كفوا احد: 4. "And there is none co-equal or comparable unto Him." (112:4)
قل اعوذ برب الفلق: 1. Say: "I seek refuge with (Allâh), the Lord of the daybreak, (113:1)
من شر ما خلق: 2. "From the evil of what He has created, (113:2)
ومن شر غاسق اذا وقب: 3. "And from the evil of the darkening (night) as it comes with its darkness; (or the moon as it sets or goes away), (113:3)
ومن شر النفاثات في العقد: 4. "And from the evil of those who practise witchcraft when they blow in the knots, (113:4)
ومن شر حاسد اذا حسد: 5. "And from the evil of the envier when he envies." (113:5)
قل اعوذ برب الناس: 1. Say: "I seek refuge with (Allâh) the Lord of mankind,[1] (114:1)
من شر الوسواس الخناس: 4. "From the evil of the whisperer (devil who whispers evil in the hearts of men) who withdraws (from his whispering in one’s heart after one remembers Allâh).[3] (114:4)
الذي يوسوس في صدور الناس: 5. "Who whispers in the breasts of mankind. (114:5)
